## REVISION 1 — CHANGE LOG (applied 2026-05-02 per Cursor strict review)

Four targeted corrections applied to original Batch A, with no other changes:

1. **WF-064 scope (CSV catalog)** — expanded from "Manager-Action Owner Notification" to "Manager High-Risk Action Notifies Owner" with explicit enumeration of seven high-risk action types: accounting edits/posting overrides; settlement overrides; escrow forfeiture; deduction schedule overrides; manual journal entries / reclassifications; vendor or class remaps affecting accounting; retroactive allocation corrections. Forfeiture-blocked-by-agreement, write-off, and collections-send events now emit critical Owner notifications per WF-064. Full coverage of remaining high-risk events (settlement override, vendor remap, manual JE, retro correction) continues in Part 7 module specs.

2. **Escrow forfeiture legal gating (3.13.6.3)** — replaced "must reference clause; if not, escrow returned" with explicit three-state decision table (clause active / legacy / no agreement on file). Forfeiture flow is hard-blocked at API layer for legacy drivers via new error code `E_FORFEITURE_NOT_AUTHORIZED_BY_AGREEMENT`. Owner role cannot override agreement state. Added **remediation path** for legacy drivers (3.13.6.3.C) requiring updated agreement signature with non-retroactive effective date. Added **pre/post-clause balance split** (3.13.6.3.D) so forfeiture only debits balance accumulated AFTER clause acknowledgment. Added blocked-attempt audit event (3.13.6.5).

3. **Unrecovered driver receivable aging policy (3.13.5.4)** — replaced "collection per company policy" with normative aging state machine: 0–30 days `open` (active collection), 31–90 days `escalated` (priority on reports + Owner weekly digest), >90 days `disposition_required` (Owner must select disposition). Three disposition paths defined with explicit posting examples: write-off as bad debt, continue collections (max 4 extensions), send to third-party collections agency. Owner role specifically required for all dispositions (not Administrator/Manager). Aged receivables surface on Owner home page CRITICAL list.

4. **Allocation rounding fairness (3.14.4.3)** — replaced "first unit by display_id absorbs the penny" with deterministic **rotating allocator** keyed on `period_index mod N`. Each period rotates which unit receives the residual penny so no unit consistently absorbs the rounding penalty. Algorithm is pure/deterministic/reproducible. `rotation_offset` and `residual_recipient_unit_uuids[]` persisted on every allocation for audit. Reversal corrections use same rotation_offset as original (3.14.4.3.E). API responses updated to expose new rounding metadata. New parity tests T-034.9 through T-034.12 added.

No other changes to original Batch A. All other sections, WF mappings, posting rules, error codes, audit events, permission matrices, and parity tests are unchanged from the version delivered immediately prior.

---

## 3.12 — QBO AUTO-SYNC + OFFLINE QUEUE / REPLAY

**Maps to:** WF-031 (QBO Auto Sync on Accounting Writes), WF-032 (Offline Accounting Continuity), WF-022 (Integration Outage Handling), WF-033 (Driver Vendor and Unit Class Mapping Enforcement), WF-045 (Bill Payment Detail Visibility), WF-046 (Transfer and Credit Card Payment Support)

**Source basis:** Q1.1 (sync rules), Q18 (outage handling), Q1.7 (driver vendor mapping), §1.9 (banking), §1.12 (catalog mirroring), latest user requirements (auto-sync + offline continuity)

### 3.12.1 The local-write-first invariant

**INVARIANT 3.12.1.1** — Every accounting transaction MUST be written to the local PostgreSQL database before any attempt to send it to QuickBooks Online. The local write is durable; the QBO sync is queued.

**INVARIANT 3.12.1.2** — A transaction MUST NOT be considered failed because QBO is unavailable. QBO unavailability is an expected operational state. The transaction is local-committed, queued for sync, and surfaced as `qbo_pending` until QBO accepts it.

**INVARIANT 3.12.1.3** — The user-facing UI MUST treat a `qbo_pending` transaction as fully successful. The transaction has a status indicator (small QBO icon: green when synced, gold when pending, red when rejected), but no operation is blocked by sync state.

**INVARIANT 3.12.1.4** — The system MUST NOT write to QBO without a corresponding local row already in place. There is no "QBO-only" transaction.

**INVARIANT 3.12.1.5** — The system MUST NOT modify a local row to reflect a QBO-side change without recording the inbound sync as a separate event in the audit log.

**Rationale:** these invariants make the local DB the source of truth for accounting *operationally*, even though QBO remains the authoritative reporting source in v1 (Option C+, Part 2.5). When QBO is reachable, both stay in lockstep. When QBO is unreachable, operations continue and reconcile on reconnect. There is no broken state between the two.

### 3.12.2 Outbox table schema

**MUST 3.12.2.1** — Every external sync flows through a single `outbox.queue` table. Schema:

```sql
CREATE TABLE outbox.queue (
  uuid              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  target_system     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (target_system IN ('qbo','samsara','relay','plaid','twilio','resend')),
  operation         TEXT NOT NULL,
  entity_type       TEXT NOT NULL,
  entity_uuid       UUID NOT NULL,
  idempotency_key   TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  payload           JSONB NOT NULL,
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending'
                    CHECK (status IN ('pending','in_flight','succeeded','failed','dead_letter')),
  attempt_count     INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  next_attempt_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  last_attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  last_error_code   TEXT,
  last_error_msg    TEXT,
  succeeded_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  external_id       TEXT,
  external_version  TEXT,
  audit_user_id     UUID,
  audit_session_id  UUID
);

CREATE INDEX outbox_pending_target_idx ON outbox.queue (target_system, status, next_attempt_at)
  WHERE status IN ('pending','failed');
CREATE INDEX outbox_entity_idx ON outbox.queue (entity_type, entity_uuid);
```

**MUST 3.12.2.2** — The `idempotency_key` ensures retries do not duplicate. It MUST be deterministic from `{target_system}:{operation}:{entity_uuid}:{entity_version}`. Same key submitted twice is a no-op on the second submission.

**MUST 3.12.2.3** — Statuses transition strictly: `pending → in_flight → (succeeded | failed)`. From `failed` either back to `pending` (after backoff) or to `dead_letter` after policy threshold.

**MUST 3.12.2.4** — A row reaching `dead_letter` MUST trigger a critical notification per Part 3.6. Owner + Administrator + Manager email; no SMS unless marked operationally critical.

### 3.12.3 The drainer worker

**MUST 3.12.3.1** — A BullMQ worker named `outbox-drainer-qbo` runs continuously, polling `outbox.queue` for rows with `status IN ('pending','failed')` AND `next_attempt_at <= now()` AND `target_system = 'qbo'`. One worker per target system; QBO has its own.

**MUST 3.12.3.2** — The drainer claims a row by transitioning it to `in_flight` with a row-level lock. Only one worker may hold a row at a time.

**MUST 3.12.3.3** — On success: drainer marks `succeeded`, stores `external_id` and `external_version`, writes `qbo_id` and `qbo_synced_at` back to the source entity, writes an audit event.

**MUST 3.12.3.4** — On failure: drainer marks `failed`, increments `attempt_count`, records `last_error_code` + `last_error_msg`, sets `next_attempt_at = now() + backoff(attempt_count)`. Backoff schedule: 30s, 2min, 10min, 30min, 2h, 6h, 12h, then dead-letter.

**MUST 3.12.3.5** — Dead-letter threshold MUST be 8 failed attempts. After dead-letter, the drainer ignores the row until manual intervention via `/admin/outbox/{uuid}/requeue`.

**MUST 3.12.3.6** — The drainer MUST handle three classes of QBO errors distinctly:
- **Transient (retry):** HTTP 5xx, HTTP 429 rate-limit, network timeout, QBO `ServiceUnavailable` fault → backoff and retry
- **Validation (do not retry):** HTTP 400, QBO `BusinessValidationFault`, schema mismatch → mark `failed` with no further retry; flag for manual fix
- **Auth (block all QBO):** HTTP 401, QBO `AuthenticationFault`, expired token → pause all QBO drainer activity, fire critical notification, set system flag `qbo_auth_required=true` until refresh succeeds

### 3.12.4 Per-transaction-type sync rules

For each accounting transaction type the system MUST follow the rules below. Each subsection specifies: trigger, the QBO entity created, the field mapping (our → QBO), the response handling, and the idempotency key shape.

#### 3.12.4.1 Expense [WF-009, WF-031]

**Trigger:** any local write to `accounting.expenses` of status `posted`. Drafts do not sync.

**QBO entity:** `Purchase` (with `PaymentType` = `Cash` or `CreditCard` depending on payment account type).

**Field mapping:**

| Our field | QBO field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `expense.uuid` | (not sent; tracked in idempotency key) | |
| `expense.transaction_date` | `TxnDate` | ISO date |
| `expense.payment_account_uuid` → `accounts.qbo_id` | `AccountRef.value` | Bank or Credit Card account |
| `expense.vendor_uuid` → `vendors.qbo_id` | `EntityRef.value` (with type=Vendor) | Required; sync fails 4001 if absent |
| `expense.memo` | `PrivateNote` | |
| `expense.lines[].category_account_uuid` → `accounts.qbo_id` | `Line.AccountBasedExpenseLineDetail.AccountRef.value` | Per-line |
| `expense.lines[].class_uuid` → `classes.qbo_id` | `Line.AccountBasedExpenseLineDetail.ClassRef.value` | Per-line; unit number |
| `expense.lines[].billable_customer_uuid` → `customers.qbo_id` | `Line.AccountBasedExpenseLineDetail.CustomerRef.value` | Per-line; if billable |
| `expense.lines[].amount` | `Line.Amount` | Per-line |
| `expense.lines[].description` | `Line.Description` | Per-line |
| `expense.lines[].driver_vendor_uuid` → `vendors.qbo_id` | (custom field if QBO setup supports; else encoded in `Line.Description`) | Driver-vendor mapping per WF-033 |

**Response handling:** on 201 Created, `Purchase.Id` becomes our `expenses.qbo_id`, `Purchase.SyncToken` becomes our `expenses.qbo_sync_token`. Our row is updated; audit event `accounting.expense.qbo_synced` written.

**Idempotency key:** `qbo:create_purchase:{expense.uuid}:{expense.version}`

**Validation rules:**
- A. `vendor_uuid` MUST resolve to a vendor with non-null `qbo_id`. If null → `E_QBO_VENDOR_UNMAPPED` (4001).
- B. Every line's `class_uuid` MUST resolve to a class with non-null `qbo_id` if the line carries unit-attribution per WF-033 → `E_QBO_CLASS_UNMAPPED` (4002).
- C. Total of `lines[].amount` MUST equal `expense.total_amount` to 2 decimals.
- D. `transaction_date` MUST NOT be in the future beyond 1 day (today + 24h grace).
- E. `payment_account_uuid` MUST resolve to a bank or credit card account.
- F. Voided expenses MUST sync as `Purchase.void()` (QBO API), not as a delete.

#### 3.12.4.2 Bill [WF-008, WF-031]

**Trigger:** any local write to `accounting.bills` of status `posted`.

**QBO entity:** `Bill`.

**Field mapping:** same shape as Expense but uses `Bill` (no `PaymentType`, no `AccountRef` for payment account; bills are AP). Adds `DueDate` from `bills.due_date`.

**Special case — Driver Bill (auto-created on load creation per WF-039):** the bill's `vendor_uuid` is the **driver's vendor record** (per WF-033, every driver MUST have a corresponding QBO Vendor created at driver onboarding). Lines use the Driver Pay Catalog accounts (per WF-013). Class is the assigned unit.

**Idempotency key:** `qbo:create_bill:{bill.uuid}:{bill.version}`

**Validation rules:**
- All Expense rules A–F apply
- G. If `bill.is_driver_bill = true`, `vendor_uuid` MUST resolve to a vendor with `vendor_type = 'driver'`. Mismatch → `E_QBO_DRIVER_VENDOR_MISMATCH` (4003).
- H. Every Driver Bill MUST have at least one line referencing a load (`load_uuid` non-null on at least one line).

#### 3.12.4.3 Check (Bill Payment by Check) [WF-031, WF-046]

**Trigger:** any local write to `accounting.bill_payments` of `payment_method = 'check'` and status `posted`.

**QBO entity:** `BillPayment` with `PayType = "Check"`. QBO automatically generates the corresponding Check disbursement record.

**Field mapping:**

| Our field | QBO field |
|---|---|
| `bill_payment.payment_date` | `TxnDate` |
| `bill_payment.payment_account_uuid` → bank account `qbo_id` | `BankAccountRef.value` (under `CheckPayment`) |
| `bill_payment.vendor_uuid` → `qbo_id` | `VendorRef.value` |
| `bill_payment.check_number` | `DocNumber` |
| `bill_payment.memo` | `PrivateNote` |
| `bill_payment.lines[].bill_uuid` → bill `qbo_id` | `Line.LinkedTxn[].TxnId` (TxnType = Bill) |
| `bill_payment.lines[].amount_applied` | `Line.Amount` |

**Validation rules:**
- All Expense rules A–F apply
- I. Every line's `bill_uuid` MUST resolve to a bill with non-null `qbo_id` AND status `posted`. Mismatch → `E_QBO_BILL_UNMAPPED` (4004).
- J. Sum of `lines[].amount_applied` MUST equal `bill_payment.total_amount`.
- K. Each `amount_applied` MUST NOT exceed the referenced bill's open balance at payment time.
- L. `check_number` MUST be unique per bank account per fiscal year.

#### 3.12.4.4 Bill Payment (other methods: ACH, Wire, Credit Card) [WF-031, WF-046]

**Trigger:** any local write to `accounting.bill_payments` of `payment_method IN ('ach','wire','cc')` and status `posted`.

**QBO entity:** `BillPayment` with appropriate `PayType` (`CreditCard` for cc; `Check` with `PrintStatus = 'NeedToPrint'` and an annotated `PrivateNote` for ACH/wire — QBO doesn't natively model these so we annotate).

**Validation rules:** same as Check above except K does not apply for credit card payments where the credit card account may carry a balance.

#### 3.12.4.5 Invoice [WF-031, WF-040]

**Trigger:** any local write to `accounting.invoices` of status `posted`. Invoices auto-generate when a load is closed per WF-040.

**QBO entity:** `Invoice`.

**Field mapping:**

| Our field | QBO field |
|---|---|
| `invoice.invoice_date` | `TxnDate` |
| `invoice.due_date` | `DueDate` |
| `invoice.customer_uuid` → `qbo_id` | `CustomerRef.value` |
| `invoice.invoice_number` | `DocNumber` |
| `invoice.terms_uuid` → `qbo_id` | `SalesTermRef.value` |
| `invoice.lines[].item_uuid` → `qbo_id` | `Line.SalesItemLineDetail.ItemRef.value` |
| `invoice.lines[].class_uuid` → `qbo_id` | `Line.SalesItemLineDetail.ClassRef.value` |
| `invoice.lines[].quantity` | `Line.SalesItemLineDetail.Qty` |
| `invoice.lines[].unit_price` | `Line.SalesItemLineDetail.UnitPrice` |
| `invoice.lines[].amount` | `Line.Amount` |
| `invoice.lines[].description` | `Line.Description` |

**Validation rules:**
- M. `customer_uuid` MUST resolve to customer with non-null `qbo_id` → `E_QBO_CUSTOMER_UNMAPPED` (4005).
- N. Every `item_uuid` MUST resolve to item with non-null `qbo_id` → `E_QBO_ITEM_UNMAPPED` (4006).
- O. `invoice_number` MUST be unique within the customer-year.

#### 3.12.4.6 Customer Payment (Receive Payment) [WF-031]

**Trigger:** any local write to `accounting.customer_payments` of status `posted`.

**QBO entity:** `Payment`.

**Validation rules:** mirror BillPayment but customer-side. Similar `LinkedTxn` structure for invoice application.

#### 3.12.4.7 Journal Entry [WF-031]

**Trigger:** any local write to `accounting.journal_entries` of status `posted`. This includes manual journal entries (per Q1.9) AND auto-generated journal entries (e.g. for unit cost allocation per 3.14, factoring per 3.13.7, escrow movements per 3.13.4).

**QBO entity:** `JournalEntry`.

**Field mapping:**

| Our field | QBO field |
|---|---|
| `je.entry_date` | `TxnDate` |
| `je.memo` | `PrivateNote` |
| `je.postings[].account_uuid` → `qbo_id` | `Line.JournalEntryLineDetail.AccountRef.value` |
| `je.postings[].class_uuid` → `qbo_id` (nullable) | `Line.JournalEntryLineDetail.ClassRef.value` |
| `je.postings[].entity_uuid` (vendor/customer/employee) | `Line.JournalEntryLineDetail.Entity` |
| `je.postings[].debit_or_credit` | `Line.JournalEntryLineDetail.PostingType` ('Debit' or 'Credit') |
| `je.postings[].amount` | `Line.Amount` |
| `je.postings[].description` | `Line.Description` |

**Validation rules:**
- P. Sum of debit postings MUST equal sum of credit postings to 2 decimals → `E_JE_UNBALANCED` (4007).
- Q. Each posting's `account_uuid` MUST resolve to non-null `qbo_id` → `E_QBO_ACCOUNT_UNMAPPED` (4008).
- R. A journal entry MUST have at least 2 postings.

#### 3.12.4.8 Vendor / Customer / Item / Class / Account writes [WF-031, WF-063]

**Trigger:** any local write to `master_data.vendors`, `master_data.customers`, `master_data.items`, `master_data.classes`, `accounting.accounts`. For master data, sync MUST happen at create AND on every update.

**Bidirectional sync (per WF-063):** the QBO adapter ALSO consumes QBO webhooks (or polls the QBO `CDC` endpoint every 15 minutes) for changes made directly in QBO. Inbound changes match by `qbo_id`; if found, our row is updated; if not, a new local row is created. Inbound updates write an audit event `qbo.inbound_change` with the diff.

**Conflict rule:** if our `updated_at > qbo.MetaData.LastUpdatedTime` AND a webhook arrives, our version wins; we re-sync OUR record to QBO. If `qbo.MetaData.LastUpdatedTime > our updated_at`, QBO version wins; our row is updated. Both sides record audit events.

### 3.12.5 The pending-sync KPI [WF-022, WF-032]

**MUST 3.12.5.1** — The dashboard MUST display a "Pending QBO sync" KPI in the top status row of every page. The KPI shows the count of `outbox.queue` rows with `target_system = 'qbo'` AND `status IN ('pending','failed')`.

**MUST 3.12.5.2** — When count is 0: green dot, label "QBO synced". When count > 0 and < 50: gold dot, label "QBO pending: N". When count >= 50 OR any row in `dead_letter`: red dot, label "QBO sync issue", clickable to `/admin/outbox`.

**MUST 3.12.5.3** — Top bar status indicator (the dot next to "QuickBooks · Samsara · Relay connected") MUST reflect QBO connection state, not pending count. Connection state is derived from last-successful drainer attempt within 10 minutes.

### 3.12.6 Replay procedure on QBO reconnect [WF-022, WF-032]

**MUST 3.12.6.1** — On QBO reconnect (token refresh succeeds, manual reconnect succeeds, transient outage clears), the drainer MUST resume processing pending rows in `created_at` order within each target system.

**MUST 3.12.6.2** — Replay MUST preserve order for rows with the same `entity_uuid`. Two updates to the same entity MUST replay in the order they were created. The drainer enforces this by sorting `pending` rows by `(entity_uuid, created_at)` and processing one entity's queue serially even if multiple workers are running.

**MUST 3.12.6.3** — If a previously-`failed` row's retry now succeeds, the row MUST update its source entity normally. If it still fails for the same reason, it backs off again per 3.12.3.4.

**MUST 3.12.6.4** — Reconnect MUST NOT replay `succeeded` rows. Idempotency key already prevents duplicates, but the drainer MUST also filter by status.

### 3.12.7 Nightly reconciliation [Option C+ / Part 2.5]

**MUST 3.12.7.1** — A scheduled job runs every night at 02:00 America/Chicago that compares QBO and our database for: account balances per account, vendor counts, customer counts, open AP total, open AR total, and journal entry line counts for the prior 7 days.

**MUST 3.12.7.2** — Discrepancies MUST be written to `accounting.reconciliation_findings` with severity (critical / warning / info). Critical discrepancies (account balance off by > $0.01) MUST trigger the critical notification path.

**MUST 3.12.7.3** — The reconciliation job MUST NOT auto-correct discrepancies. It surfaces them. Resolution is manual via `/admin/reconciliation`.

### 3.12.8 Error code taxonomy (Module: accounting / sub: qbo_sync)

All QBO sync errors share the namespace `E_QBO_*`. The full taxonomy:

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning | Retry? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `E_QBO_AUTH_REQUIRED` | 401 | Token expired or revoked | After refresh |
| `E_QBO_RATE_LIMITED` | 429 | Throttle | Yes, with longer backoff |
| `E_QBO_UNAVAILABLE` | 5xx | Service unavailable | Yes |
| `E_QBO_VALIDATION_FAILED` | 400 | QBO rejected payload as invalid | No, manual fix |
| `E_QBO_VENDOR_UNMAPPED` | n/a | Local vendor missing `qbo_id` | After mapping |
| `E_QBO_CUSTOMER_UNMAPPED` | n/a | Local customer missing `qbo_id` | After mapping |
| `E_QBO_CLASS_UNMAPPED` | n/a | Local class missing `qbo_id` | After mapping |
| `E_QBO_ITEM_UNMAPPED` | n/a | Local item missing `qbo_id` | After mapping |
| `E_QBO_ACCOUNT_UNMAPPED` | n/a | Local account missing `qbo_id` | After mapping |
| `E_QBO_BILL_UNMAPPED` | n/a | Bill referenced in payment has no `qbo_id` yet | Yes (waits for bill sync) |
| `E_QBO_DRIVER_VENDOR_MISMATCH` | n/a | Driver bill vendor isn't a driver-typed vendor | No, manual fix |
| `E_JE_UNBALANCED` | n/a | Journal entry debits ≠ credits | No, code bug |
| `E_QBO_CONFLICT` | 409 | QBO SyncToken stale | Yes after re-fetch |
| `E_QBO_DUPLICATE` | n/a | QBO reports entity already exists | No, mark synced from existing |
| `E_QBO_DEAD_LETTER` | n/a | Exceeded retry threshold | No, manual requeue |

### 3.12.9 API contracts for outbox admin

#### `GET /api/admin/outbox`

List outbox rows with filtering.

**Permission:** `outbox.read` (Owner, Administrator, Manager, Accountant).

**Query parameters:**
- `target_system` (optional): `qbo` | `samsara` | `relay` | etc.
- `status` (optional): `pending` | `in_flight` | `succeeded` | `failed` | `dead_letter`
- `entity_type` (optional)
- `entity_uuid` (optional)
- `limit` (default 50, max 200)
- `offset` (default 0)

**Example request:**
```
GET /api/admin/outbox?target_system=qbo&status=failed&limit=20
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000001",
      "created_at": "2026-05-02T14:32:11Z",
      "target_system": "qbo",
      "operation": "create_bill",
      "entity_type": "bill",
      "entity_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000abc",
      "status": "failed",
      "attempt_count": 3,
      "next_attempt_at": "2026-05-02T14:42:11Z",
      "last_error_code": "E_QBO_VENDOR_UNMAPPED",
      "last_error_msg": "Vendor uuid=01923... has no qbo_id; cannot create Bill",
      "audit_user_id": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000def"
    }
  ],
  "total": 1,
  "limit": 20,
  "offset": 0
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Invalid query parameter",
    "details": [
      {"field": "limit", "reason": "must be <= 200"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923c8a"
  }
}
```

#### `POST /api/admin/outbox/{uuid}/requeue`

Manually requeue a `dead_letter` row.

**Permission:** `outbox.requeue` (Owner, Administrator only).

**Example request:**
```
POST /api/admin/outbox/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000001/requeue
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{"reason": "Vendor was missing qbo_id; mapped manually; please retry"}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000001",
    "status": "pending",
    "next_attempt_at": "2026-05-02T15:01:33Z",
    "requeued_at": "2026-05-02T15:01:33Z",
    "requeued_by_user_id": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000def",
    "requeue_reason": "Vendor was missing qbo_id; mapped manually; please retry"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error (409):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_OUTBOX_NOT_DEAD_LETTERED",
    "message": "Cannot requeue a row that is not in dead_letter status",
    "details": [
      {"field": "status", "current_value": "in_flight"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923c8b"
  }
}
```

### 3.12.10 Audit log events emitted

Every sync touches the audit log. The events:

| Event | Emitted when | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| `outbox.row_created` | New row queued | info |
| `outbox.row_succeeded` | Row marked succeeded | info |
| `outbox.row_failed` | Row marked failed (each attempt) | warning |
| `outbox.row_dead_lettered` | Row reaches dead_letter | critical |
| `outbox.row_requeued` | Manual requeue | warning |
| `qbo.inbound_change` | Webhook or CDC change applied | info |
| `qbo.auth_lost` | Token expired / revoked | critical |
| `qbo.auth_restored` | Token refreshed successfully | info |
| `qbo.reconciliation_finding` | Nightly recon found discrepancy | warning or critical |

### 3.12.11 Permission actions by role

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `outbox.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `outbox.requeue` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `qbo.reconcile` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `qbo.token_refresh` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — |
| `qbo.disconnect` | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |

### 3.12.12 Parity test IDs

This section satisfies WF-031, WF-032, WF-022, WF-033, WF-045, WF-046. Test cases (to be expanded in `IH35_REQUIREMENT_TRACE_MATRIX_V3.csv`):

- T-031.1: Create expense while QBO online → expense exists locally, `qbo_id` populated within 60s
- T-031.2: Create bill while QBO online → bill exists locally, `qbo_id` populated within 60s
- T-031.3: Create check while QBO online → bill_payment + check both sync
- T-032.1: Disconnect QBO → create expense → expense local-only, outbox row pending, KPI shows count
- T-032.2: Reconnect QBO → outbox drains in order → all rows succeed within 5min
- T-032.3: Disconnect QBO → 60 expenses created → KPI turns red
- T-022.1: QBO returns 5xx for 1 minute → drainer retries with backoff → eventual success
- T-022.2: QBO returns 401 → drainer halts all activity → critical notification fires
- T-022.3: QBO returns 400 validation error → row marked failed (no retry) → admin sees in queue
- T-033.1: Create expense with vendor missing qbo_id → sync fails E_QBO_VENDOR_UNMAPPED → vendor synced → retry succeeds
- T-033.2: Create driver bill with non-driver vendor → sync fails E_QBO_DRIVER_VENDOR_MISMATCH (no retry)
- T-045.1: Open bill with $1000, partial payment $400 → bill payment flow shows $600 open balance + $400 prior payment
- T-046.1: Transfer $5000 from Operating to Payroll → both bank accounts post correct entries → QBO Transfer entity created
- T-046.2: Pay credit card $2000 from operating → CC liability decreases, bank decreases → QBO posts correctly

---

## 3.13 — DRIVER LIABILITY / DEDUCTION / ESCROW ACCOUNTING

**Maps to:** WF-011 (Settlement with Debt Alert), WF-012 (Hold Deduction for Cycle), WF-014 (Border Crossing Charge Routing), WF-035 (Company Paid Driver Expense Recovery), WF-036 (Driver Terms Acceptance Capture), WF-013 (Driver Pay Catalog Application), WF-041 (Presettlement to Settlement Lifecycle)

**Source basis:** Q1.3 (driver financial control), Q2 (driver settlements), Q4 (driver pay types), §1.7 (dispatch / driver pay), §7 (settlements ready), latest user requirements (driver liability accounting)

### 3.13.1 The driver-as-vendor model

**MUST 3.13.1.1** — Every driver MUST have exactly one corresponding vendor record in `master_data.vendors` with `vendor_type = 'driver'` and `driver_uuid` foreign-key to `master_data.drivers`. This vendor record is what QBO sees; QBO uses it to post AP for settlements (per WF-033).

**MUST 3.13.1.2** — The driver-vendor MUST be created automatically on driver onboarding. It MUST NOT be possible to onboard a driver without their vendor record being created in the same database transaction.

**MUST 3.13.1.3** — Driver settlement totals are paid as bills against the driver-vendor. The bill is posted as a Driver Bill (WF-039) and paid as a Bill Payment (per 3.12.4.3 / 3.12.4.4).

### 3.13.2 The driver receivable model

**MUST 3.13.2.1** — Every driver also has a receivable account: `Driver Receivable` (chart of accounts, asset class). When the company pays an expense on behalf of a driver that the driver is liable for (fine, accident damage, license fee, equipment loss), the entry creates a debit to `Driver Receivable` per driver.

**MUST 3.13.2.2** — Driver Receivable MUST be sub-ledgered per driver. The sub-ledger is enforced by an `entity_uuid` reference on every posting to the Driver Receivable account, where `entity_uuid` references the driver's vendor record.

**MUST 3.13.2.3** — Driver debt = sum of debits to Driver Receivable for that driver minus sum of credits. This is computed on-demand by the `driver_finance.compute_debt_balance(driver_uuid)` function.

**MUST 3.13.2.4** — The debt-alert invariant (Part 3.4.9) reads from this function. Any UI that pays a driver MUST call this function and display the result before the payable amount.

### 3.13.3 Posting rules — full catalog

The system MUST post the following journal entries for each event. All entries MUST balance (debits = credits).

#### 3.13.3.1 Cash advance to driver [WF-035]

**Trigger:** dispatcher or accountant creates a cash advance.

**Journal entry:**

```
DR  Driver Receivable          (entity = driver-vendor, class = unit)        $X
    CR  Bank Account                                                                $X
```

**Local effect:** `bill_payments` row of payment_method=`advance`, `bills` row not created (advance has no bill); a `driver_finance.advances` row tracks recovery.

**QBO effect:** `Check` posted to driver-vendor, applied to "Driver Receivable" account (not AP).

**WF mapping:** WF-035, WF-057.

#### 3.13.3.2 Cash advance recovered via settlement [WF-035, WF-041]

**Trigger:** settlement processed; cash advance line included.

**Journal entry (within the settlement bill payment):**

```
DR  Accounts Payable - Driver  (driver-vendor)                              $X
    CR  Driver Receivable          (driver-vendor)                                    $X
```

**Local effect:** `settlement_lines` row of type `advance_recovery`; the advance is marked recovered.

**QBO effect:** included as a deduction line on the BillPayment.

#### 3.13.3.3 Civil fine paid by company, driver liable [WF-035]

**Trigger:** company pays a fine bill where the underlying violation was the driver's responsibility per Q2.

**Two-part journal entry:**

Part 1 — pay the fine (this is the existing expense posting per 3.12.4.1):
```
DR  Civil Fines Expense        (class = unit)                              $X
    CR  Bank Account                                                                $X
```

Part 2 — establish driver liability (auto-posted by the system; same transaction):
```
DR  Driver Receivable          (entity = driver-vendor, class = unit)      $X
    CR  Driver Fines Recovery    (income offset to expense)                          $X
```

**Net effect:** the company's P&L shows zero (fine expense offset by recovery income). The driver owes us $X.

**Local effect:** `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` row of type `civil_fine` with linked `expense_uuid`. A `driver_finance.deduction_schedule` row is created (next section).

**WF mapping:** WF-035, WF-036.

**MUST 3.13.3.3.A** — A driver-liable expense MUST require the driver's signed acknowledgment per WF-036 BEFORE the deduction schedule is auto-applied to settlements. Until acknowledgment, the deduction is `pending_acknowledgment` and does not auto-deduct.

#### 3.13.3.4 Internal company fine (policy violation) [WF-035]

**Trigger:** safety officer or owner creates an internal fine record.

**Journal entry:**

```
DR  Driver Receivable          (entity = driver-vendor, class = unit)      $X
    CR  Internal Fines Income                                                       $X
```

**Local effect:** `safety.internal_fines` row + `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` row + `deduction_schedule` row.

**WF mapping:** WF-035, WF-036.

**MUST 3.13.3.4.A** — Internal fines MUST also require signed acknowledgment per WF-036 before deducting.

#### 3.13.3.5 Accident damage — driver at fault, full responsibility [WF-035, WF-007]

**Trigger:** Accident WO closed (WF-004), `driver_liability_type = 'full'`, total damage cost = $Y.

**Journal entry (in addition to repair bills):**

```
DR  Driver Receivable          (entity = driver-vendor, class = unit)      $Y
    CR  Accident Damage Recovery (income)                                           $Y
```

**Local effect:** `safety.accidents` row links to `driver_finance.driver_liabilities`. WF-007 claim tracking continues separately.

#### 3.13.3.6 Accident damage — driver pays deductible only [WF-035, WF-007]

**Trigger:** Accident WO closed, `driver_liability_type = 'deductible_only'`, deductible amount = $D, total damage = $Y.

**Two-part journal entry:**

Part 1 — repair bills already posted normally per 3.12.4.2.

Part 2 — driver liability for deductible only:
```
DR  Driver Receivable          (entity = driver-vendor, class = unit)      $D
    CR  Accident Damage Recovery (income)                                           $D
```

#### 3.13.3.7 Equipment lost or damaged by driver [WF-035, WF-026, WF-047]

**Trigger:** driver reports equipment lost/damaged via PWA (Section 12) or office records loss with driver assignment; per Q2 driver is liable for the assigned equipment value.

**Journal entry:**

```
DR  Driver Receivable          (entity = driver-vendor, class = unit)      $V
    CR  Equipment Loss Recovery                                                     $V
```

Where $V = equipment book value at loss event.

**Local effect:** `master_data.equipment_log` records loss event; `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` of type `equipment_loss`.

#### 3.13.3.8 License plate, IRP, IFTA, antidoping paid by company [Q5.c, WF-034, WF-037]

**Trigger:** routine company-paid expenses where the driver is partially liable per Q1.5 (antidoping expensed initially, deducted from driver escrow when driver leaves).

**For antidoping specifically:**

When expense posted (per 3.12.4.1):
```
DR  Antidoping Expense         (class = unit; driver-vendor in memo)       $X
    CR  Bank or AP                                                                  $X
```

A linked safety record is auto-created per WF-037. No driver liability is created at this point.

When driver departs the company (separation event):
```
DR  Driver Escrow Liability    (entity = driver-vendor)                    $X
    CR  Antidoping Expense                                                          $X
```

This recovers the antidoping cost from the driver's escrow at separation. If escrow is insufficient, the unrecovered portion creates a `Driver Receivable` debit (driver continues to owe us; collection up to company policy).

**For IRP / license plate / IFTA:** posted as expense at time of purchase, allocated across units per 3.14 (the unit cost allocation engine). No driver liability — these are company costs.

### 3.13.4 Escrow accumulation

**MUST 3.13.4.1** — Each driver has an escrow target set in `master_data.drivers.escrow_target` (typical $1,000-$2,500). Escrow is built via a deduction on each settlement until target is reached.

**MUST 3.13.4.2** — When current escrow balance < target, settlement processing MUST include an escrow deduction line per the driver's `escrow_deduction_per_settlement` field (default $50/week).

**MUST 3.13.4.3** — Posting per settlement when escrow is being built:

```
DR  Accounts Payable - Driver  (driver-vendor)                              $E
    CR  Driver Escrow Liability  (entity = driver-vendor)                            $E
```

Where $E is the per-settlement escrow deduction.

**MUST 3.13.4.4** — When escrow balance reaches target, deduction stops automatically. Settlement no longer adds the escrow line.

**MUST 3.13.4.5** — Escrow balance is sub-ledgered per driver via `entity_uuid` on all postings to `Driver Escrow Liability`.

### 3.13.5 Escrow payout on driver departure

**MUST 3.13.5.1** — When a driver is marked separated (`driver.separation_date` set), the system MUST compute the final escrow position:

```
final_escrow = escrow_balance 
             - antidoping_recovery 
             - any_outstanding_driver_receivable
```

**MUST 3.13.5.2** — If `final_escrow > 0`: pay it out as a check to the driver-vendor.

Posting:
```
DR  Driver Escrow Liability                                                $F
    CR  Bank Account                                                                $F
```

**MUST 3.13.5.3** — If `final_escrow = 0` AND no outstanding receivable: close the driver's escrow record.

**MUST 3.13.5.4** — If `final_escrow < 0` (escrow insufficient): the unrecovered amount remains as Driver Receivable. The system MUST apply the following normative aging policy. Auto-write-off is forbidden; written-off receivables require Owner approval and a journal entry.

**Aging state machine:**

| Aging bucket | Days since departure | State | System behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| Active collection | 0–30 | `open` | Receivable visible on driver-debt reports; no escalation; standard collection contact per company practice |
| Escalated collection | 31–90 | `escalated` | System raises priority on debt-summary reports; Owner notified weekly digest; aged AR report flags this driver |
| Disposition required | >90 | `disposition_required` | System BLOCKS further passive aging; Owner MUST select disposition (write-off OR continue collections OR send to third-party collections) before any further automated handling |

**MUST 3.13.5.4.A** — On each driver-departure, the system MUST schedule the aging transitions: at day 30 the receivable's `aging_state` auto-transitions to `escalated`; at day 90 to `disposition_required` and a critical Owner notification fires.

**MUST 3.13.5.4.B** — While `aging_state = 'disposition_required'`, no automated transitions occur. The receivable remains on the books as an open asset until Owner selects disposition.

**MUST 3.13.5.4.C** — Three disposition paths are supported. Each requires Owner action and produces a specific posting:

**Path 1 — Write-off as bad debt:**

```
DR  Bad Debt Expense           (P&L expense)                             $R
    CR  Driver Receivable          (entity = driver-vendor)                       $R
```

The receivable is closed; P&L absorbs the loss. Audit event `driver_finance.receivable_written_off` (critical severity) with reason text.

**Path 2 — Continue collections (Owner extends):**

No new posting. Owner sets `aging_state` back to `escalated` and provides justification + a future review date (e.g. 60 days out). At that future date the system re-prompts for disposition. This path MAY be repeated up to 3 times; on the 4th review, write-off OR third-party collections is required (no further extensions).

Audit event `driver_finance.receivable_collection_extended` with reason text and review date.

**Path 3 — Send to third-party collections agency:**

```
DR  Collections Receivable      (entity = third-party agency)             $R
    CR  Driver Receivable          (entity = driver-vendor)                       $R
```

Optionally, if the contract with the agency takes a percentage:

```
DR  Collections Fee Expense                                              $F
    CR  Collections Receivable      (entity = third-party agency)                 $F
```

When the agency remits recovered funds:

```
DR  Bank Account                                                         $C
    CR  Collections Receivable      (entity = third-party agency)                 $C
```

If the agency reports the debt as uncollectable, write-off path applies to the residual `Collections Receivable` balance.

Audit event `driver_finance.receivable_sent_to_collections` with agency name + contract reference.

**MUST 3.13.5.4.D** — All three disposition paths require the Owner role specifically (not Administrator, not Manager). The disposition action is logged with timestamp, user, before/after state, and reason text. The audit entry is non-deletable.

**MUST 3.13.5.4.E** — While in `disposition_required` state, the receivable MUST appear on the Owner's home page attention list (`§1 HOME` per UI spec) as a CRITICAL item until disposition is selected.

### 3.13.6 Escrow forfeiture rules

**MUST 3.13.6.1** — Escrow forfeiture happens only under defined circumstances per Q2 (driver abandons load / leaves without notice / per company policy in the signed agreement). The system MUST NOT forfeit escrow without explicit Owner action.

**MUST 3.13.6.2** — Forfeiture posting:

```
DR  Driver Escrow Liability                                                $F
    CR  Escrow Forfeiture Income                                                    $F
```

**MUST 3.13.6.3** — Forfeiture MUST be logged with reason, original signed agreement reference (which authorized the forfeiture), and Owner approval audit event.

**Legal gating — three-state model:**

The system MUST evaluate the driver's signed-agreement state before permitting forfeiture, per the following decision table:

| Agreement state | Forfeiture allowed? | System behavior |
|---|---|---|
| Signed agreement INCLUDES forfeiture clause (current template) | **YES** | Owner can initiate forfeiture flow; all 3.13.6.1–3.13.6.4 rules apply |
| Signed agreement does NOT include forfeiture clause (legacy / pre-clause hires) | **NO — BLOCKED** | Forfeiture flow is hard-blocked at the API layer (`E_FORFEITURE_NOT_AUTHORIZED_BY_AGREEMENT`); escrow return rules per 3.13.5 apply instead |
| No signed agreement on file | **NO — BLOCKED** | Same as legacy; escrow MUST be returned per 3.13.5 |

**MUST 3.13.6.3.A** — Each driver record MUST carry a `forfeiture_clause_active` boolean derived from the signed agreement attached to the driver. This boolean is set when the agreement is uploaded/captured and recomputed on agreement updates.

**MUST 3.13.6.3.B** — The forfeiture flow's first server-side check MUST be `if (driver.forfeiture_clause_active === false) reject with E_FORFEITURE_NOT_AUTHORIZED_BY_AGREEMENT`. The Owner MUST NOT be able to override this — agreement state is the gate, not Owner role.

**MUST 3.13.6.3.C — Remediation path for legacy drivers:** to bring a legacy driver under the forfeiture clause going forward, the company MUST:
1. Generate an updated agreement (or addendum) that includes the forfeiture clause
2. Have the driver sign it (PWA in-app or printed PDF, captured per Part 3.17 chain-of-custody, Batch B)
3. The system records the new agreement; `forfeiture_clause_active` becomes `true` ONLY from the acknowledgment timestamp forward
4. Forfeiture MUST NOT apply retroactively to escrow accumulated before the acknowledgment timestamp; the pre-acknowledgment escrow balance is permanently exempt from forfeiture and MUST be returned per 3.13.5 even if a later forfeiture event triggers

**MUST 3.13.6.3.D** — The escrow record MUST track a `pre_clause_balance` and `post_clause_balance` split for any driver whose acknowledgment occurred mid-tenure. Forfeiture postings (per 3.13.6.2) MUST debit only `post_clause_balance`; `pre_clause_balance` is paid out per 3.13.5 regardless of forfeiture trigger.

**MUST 3.13.6.4** — A forfeiture event triggers a critical notification to Owner and creates a non-deletable audit trail entry.

**MUST 3.13.6.5** — A forfeiture **attempt** that is blocked by 3.13.6.3.B (agreement state) MUST itself be logged as a `driver_finance.forfeiture_blocked_by_agreement` audit event with the user who attempted, timestamp, and driver context. This protects the company from claims that forfeiture happened without legal grounds.

### 3.13.7 Deduction scheduling

**MUST 3.13.7.1** — Each driver liability creates one row in `driver_finance.deduction_schedule` with:
- `liability_uuid` — the underlying liability
- `schedule_type` — one of: `weekly`, `per_load`, `custom_quantity`
- `quantity_per_period` — dollar amount per period (if `weekly`) or per load (if `per_load`)
- `total_periods` — how many periods (if known)
- `start_period` — first settlement period to apply
- `current_balance` — remaining unpaid
- `hold_until_period` — null OR a period to skip past (per WF-012 hold deductions)
- `requires_acknowledgment` — boolean; if true and `acknowledgment_uuid IS NULL`, schedule is `pending_acknowledgment`

**MUST 3.13.7.2** — Per Q1.3: the system MUST offer all three schedule types when creating a liability:
- **Weekly**: deduct $X per settlement until balance is 0
- **Per-load**: deduct $X per load until balance is 0
- **Custom quantity**: deduct in N installments of $X each

**MUST 3.13.7.3** — Settlement processing iterates active deduction schedules for the driver and applies each one per its policy. Each applied deduction creates one settlement line.

**MUST 3.13.7.4** — Hold deduction (WF-012): a UI button on the settlement screen lets Owner / Admin / Manager / Accountant set `hold_until_period` for a specific schedule. Required reason text. Audit event written.

**MUST 3.13.7.5** — A held deduction MUST display on the settlement as `held` (visible, not silently skipped) with the held reason and who held it.

### 3.13.8 The debt-alert invariant in implementation

**MUST 3.13.8.1** — The settlement screen, the cash advance screen, the company-pays-driver-expense screen, and the PWA driver pay screen MUST each call `driver_finance.compute_debt_balance(driver_uuid)` before rendering the payable amount.

**MUST 3.13.8.2** — If `debt > 0`, the screen MUST display:
- A red banner at the top of the panel
- The debt amount in red, formatted currency
- A breakdown link (clickable) showing the source liabilities
- The proposed deductions for this period (already factored into payable)

**MUST 3.13.8.3** — The payable amount is shown SECOND, below the debt banner.

**MUST 3.13.8.4** — A settlement MUST NOT be advanced from `presettlement` to `final` until the user signing off has clicked the "Acknowledge debt summary" checkbox. This click is logged in the audit trail with timestamp and user.

### 3.13.9 Border crossing charge routing [WF-014]

**MUST 3.13.9.1** — When a load includes a border crossing event (Mexico per Q16), the dispatcher chooses the routing for the crossing charge from three options:

1. **Charge to customer** — adds a billable line to the invoice (per 3.12.4.5).
2. **Deduct from driver** — creates a `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` row of type `border_crossing` with appropriate posting (per 3.13.3.3 pattern).
3. **Pay to driver** — adds a pay line to the driver bill (per 3.12.4.2 driver bill mapping; pay catalog item `border_crossing_pay`).

**MUST 3.13.9.2** — The border crossing event MUST be logged in `dispatch.load_events` with the routing choice, who chose it, and timestamp. Audit event `dispatch.border_crossing_routed` written.

### 3.13.10 Signed acknowledgment requirement [WF-036]

**MUST 3.13.10.1** — Any deduction schedule with `requires_acknowledgment = true` (default true for fines, accident liability, equipment loss) MUST NOT auto-deduct from settlement until the driver has acknowledged via one of two methods:

1. **PWA in-app**: driver views terms in the app, taps Accept; system records server timestamp, IP, device fingerprint, generates SHA-256 hash of the terms document, stores acknowledgment record per Part 3.17 (chain of custody, Batch B).
2. **Printed and signed PDF**: terms PDF is printed, driver signs, scan is uploaded; the upload triggers acknowledgment with metadata.

**MUST 3.13.10.2** — Until acknowledgment is recorded, the deduction is `pending_acknowledgment`. It appears on the settlement screen as "pending acknowledgment" with no dollar effect.

**MUST 3.13.10.3** — The acknowledgment record MUST be immutable. Per Part 3.17 (Batch B) the record cannot be edited or deleted. Voiding (e.g. driver disputes legitimacy) is via a separate `acknowledgment_void` record.

### 3.13.11 Error code taxonomy (Module: driver_finance)

| Code | Meaning | Recovery |
|---|---|---|
| `E_DRIVER_VENDOR_MISSING` | Driver has no corresponding vendor record | Auto-create or fix manually |
| `E_DRIVER_DEBT_NOT_ACKNOWLEDGED` | Settlement attempted without debt acknowledgment | User must click the acknowledge checkbox |
| `E_DEDUCTION_PENDING_ACK` | Liability deduction not yet acknowledged | Wait for driver acknowledgment via PWA or PDF |
| `E_ESCROW_INSUFFICIENT` | Departure payout would result in negative; outstanding receivable created | Owner reviews; aging policy applies (3.13.5.4) |
| `E_FORFEITURE_NOT_AUTHORIZED` | Forfeiture attempted without Owner action | Owner must explicitly authorize |
| `E_FORFEITURE_NOT_AUTHORIZED_BY_AGREEMENT` | Driver's signed agreement does not include forfeiture clause; legacy hire | Remediation per 3.13.6.3.C; or pay out per 3.13.5 |
| `E_HOLD_REASON_MISSING` | Hold deduction attempted without reason text | User provides reason |
| `E_SETTLEMENT_LOCKED` | Edit attempted on `final` settlement | Void and re-issue |
| `E_BORDER_CHARGE_NOT_ROUTED` | Border event saved without routing choice | Dispatcher selects routing |
| `E_RECEIVABLE_DISPOSITION_REQUIRED` | Aged receivable beyond 90 days; awaiting Owner disposition | Owner selects write-off, extend, or collections (3.13.5.4.C) |
| `E_RECEIVABLE_EXTENSION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` | Receivable extended 4 times; further extension blocked | Owner must select write-off or collections |
| `E_WRITEOFF_REQUIRES_OWNER` | Write-off attempted by non-Owner role | Owner action required |

### 3.13.12 API contracts

#### `POST /api/driver-finance/liabilities`

Create a new driver liability.

**Permission:** `driver_finance.liability.create` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant, Safety).

**Example request:**
```json
{
  "driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000123",
  "liability_type": "civil_fine",
  "amount": 350.00,
  "linked_expense_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000456",
  "memo": "Civil fine — speeding citation 2026-04-22, US-77",
  "deduction_schedule": {
    "schedule_type": "custom_quantity",
    "quantity_per_period": 50.00,
    "total_periods": 7,
    "start_period": "2026-W18"
  },
  "requires_acknowledgment": true
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000789",
    "driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000123",
    "liability_type": "civil_fine",
    "amount": 350.00,
    "current_balance": 350.00,
    "status": "pending_acknowledgment",
    "deduction_schedule_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000079a",
    "journal_entry_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000079b",
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T15:14:22Z",
    "created_by_user_id": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000def"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Liability creation failed validation",
    "details": [
      {"field": "amount", "reason": "must be > 0"},
      {"field": "deduction_schedule.total_periods", "reason": "required when schedule_type=custom_quantity"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923c8c"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_DRIVER_VENDOR_MISSING",
    "message": "Driver 01923c8a... has no vendor record; cannot post liability",
    "details": [
      {"driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000123"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923c8c"
  }
}
```

#### `POST /api/driver-finance/deduction-schedules/{uuid}/hold`

Hold a deduction for the current period (WF-012).

**Permission:** `driver_finance.deduction.hold` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant).

**Example request:**
```json
{
  "hold_until_period": "2026-W19",
  "reason": "Driver had unexpected medical expense; deferring fine deduction one week"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000079a",
    "hold_until_period": "2026-W19",
    "hold_reason": "Driver had unexpected medical expense; deferring fine deduction one week",
    "held_by_user_id": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000def",
    "held_at": "2026-05-02T15:21:00Z"
  }
}
```

**Example error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_HOLD_REASON_MISSING",
    "message": "Hold reason is required",
    "details": [{"field": "reason", "reason": "required"}],
    "request_id": "req_01923c8d"
  }
}
```

#### `GET /api/driver-finance/drivers/{uuid}/debt-summary`

Compute current debt for the debt-alert invariant.

**Permission:** `driver_finance.debt.read` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant, Dispatcher, Safety, Driver-self-only).

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000123",
    "driver_name": "Juan Perez",
    "total_debt": 2400.00,
    "breakdown": [
      {"liability_uuid": "...", "type": "civil_fine", "balance": 350.00, "memo": "Speeding US-77"},
      {"liability_uuid": "...", "type": "accident_deductible", "balance": 1500.00, "memo": "I-20 collision 2026-03-14"},
      {"liability_uuid": "...", "type": "advance", "balance": 500.00, "memo": "Cash advance 2026-04-28"},
      {"liability_uuid": "...", "type": "equipment_loss", "balance": 50.00, "memo": "Locking jack RT-204"}
    ],
    "escrow_balance": 1840.00,
    "escrow_target": 2500.00,
    "computed_at": "2026-05-02T15:33:11Z"
  }
}
```

### 3.13.16 Parity test IDs

- T-011.1: Settlement attempted with $0 debt → no banner shown; payable shows correctly
- T-011.2: Settlement attempted with $2,400 debt → red banner shown first; payable shown after; deductions applied
- T-011.3: Settlement attempted with debt = 0 but ack checkbox not clicked → cannot lock settlement
- T-012.1: Active deduction → user holds for week → deduction not applied this week
- T-012.2: Hold deduction without reason → error E_HOLD_REASON_MISSING
- T-012.3: Held deduction → next week resumes automatically
- T-014.1: Border crossing — charge to customer → invoice has billable line
- T-014.2: Border crossing — deduct from driver → driver liability created
- T-014.3: Border crossing — pay to driver → driver bill has pay line
- T-035.1: Civil fine paid by company → driver receivable created → P&L net zero
- T-035.2: Equipment lost → driver liability for book value
- T-035.3: Internal fine → liability requires acknowledgment before deduction
- T-036.1: Liability with `requires_acknowledgment=true` → deduction does not auto-apply
- T-036.2: Driver accepts terms in PWA → ack record created → deduction begins
- T-036.3: Printed PDF signed and uploaded → ack record created
- T-041.1: First load delivered → presettlement created with that load
- T-041.2: Second load assigned to same driver → option to add to presettlement appears
- T-041.3: Truck back in Laredo → settlement composed with all loads + deductions
- T-064.1: Forfeiture initiated against driver with `forfeiture_clause_active=true` → flow proceeds; critical notification fires
- T-064.2: Forfeiture initiated against legacy driver with `forfeiture_clause_active=false` → blocked with E_FORFEITURE_NOT_AUTHORIZED_BY_AGREEMENT; audit event `forfeiture_blocked_by_agreement` written; escrow returned per 3.13.5
- T-064.3: Legacy driver signs updated agreement with forfeiture clause → `forfeiture_clause_active=true` from acknowledgment timestamp; `pre_clause_balance` permanently exempt from forfeiture
- T-064.4: Forfeiture event on driver with mid-tenure clause acknowledgment → only `post_clause_balance` debited; `pre_clause_balance` paid out per 3.13.5
- T-065.1: Driver departs with $200 unrecovered receivable, day 0 → aging_state='open'
- T-065.2: Day 30 reached → aging_state auto-transitions to 'escalated'; weekly Owner digest includes
- T-065.3: Day 90 reached → aging_state auto-transitions to 'disposition_required'; critical Owner notification fires; appears on Owner home page CRITICAL list
- T-065.4: Owner selects write-off → bad debt expense posted; receivable closed; audit event `receivable_written_off` (critical)
- T-065.5: Owner extends collections (1st time) → review date set 60 days out; aging_state back to 'escalated'
- T-065.6: Extensions count reaches 4 → further extension blocked with E_RECEIVABLE_EXTENSION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
- T-065.7: Owner sends to third-party collections → collections receivable posted; driver receivable closed; agency contract referenced

## 3.14 — UNIT COST ALLOCATION ENGINE

**Maps to:** WF-034 (Monthly Shared Cost Allocation), WF-033 (Driver Vendor and Unit Class Mapping Enforcement)

**Source basis:** Q1.2 (shared bills + filter to certain units), Q15 (insurance multiple policies), latest user requirements (insurance/IFTA/IRP/license plate allocation)

### 3.14.1 The allocation problem

Some bills cover the entire fleet or a subset of units. Examples per latest user requirements: insurance premiums, IFTA tax, Texas IRP, license plate fees, annual permits, fuel surcharge agreements. The bill arrives as a single dollar amount; the system MUST split it across the relevant units so per-unit P&L reflects true cost.

The naive approach (post the whole bill to one unit, or to no unit) breaks per-unit profitability and breaks the company-settlement profit-per-truck calculation per WF-042. The system MUST allocate.

### 3.14.2 Allocation policies

**MUST 3.14.2.1** — The system MUST support these allocation policies:

| Policy | Description | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| `single_unit` | Whole cost to one unit | Plate for one truck, single-vehicle insurance rider |
| `equal_split_all_active` | Equal split across all units active during the period | Insurance covering whole fleet |
| `equal_split_subset` | Equal split across a chosen subset | Insurance covering only refrigerated trailers |
| `equal_split_class` | Equal split across all units of a class (truck / refrigerated trailer / dry van / flatbed / company vehicle) | Trailer-class-specific insurance |
| `weighted_by_mileage` | Allocate proportionally to miles run during the period | IFTA tax (post-fact, when actual miles are known) |
| `weighted_by_revenue` | Allocate proportionally to revenue earned during the period | Variable cost agreements |

**MUST 3.14.2.2** — When creating a bill that needs allocation, the user MUST select a policy. The selection MUST be stored on the bill record as `allocation_policy` and `allocation_subset_uuids[]` (for subset/class policies).

**MUST 3.14.2.3** — The default policy for an account MAY be configured per `accounts.default_allocation_policy`, so creating a bill on the "Insurance Expense" account presents `equal_split_all_active` as the default.

### 3.14.3 The allocation period

**MUST 3.14.3.1** — Every allocation has an effective period: `period_start` and `period_end` (dates). The cost is conceptually spread across the period.

**MUST 3.14.3.2** — For monthly insurance, `period_start = 2026-04-01`, `period_end = 2026-04-30`. For an annual permit, `period_start = renewal_date`, `period_end = renewal_date + 1 year`.

**MUST 3.14.3.3** — Long periods (annual) MUST be amortized monthly via separate journal entries created automatically at month-end:
- One journal entry per month posting the unit-allocated portion to expense
- The remaining unposted portion sits in `Prepaid Expense` (asset)
- The original payment posts the full amount to `Prepaid Expense` and decreases `Bank` / `AP`

**MUST 3.14.3.4** — Short periods (monthly or one-time) MUST post the full amount to the expense account immediately, allocated across units in one journal entry with N postings (one per unit).

### 3.14.4 The allocation calculation

**MUST 3.14.4.1** — For `equal_split_all_active`: count the units active during the period. "Active" means: `unit.status = 'active'` AND `unit.acquired_date <= period_end` AND (`unit.retired_date IS NULL` OR `unit.retired_date >= period_start`).

**MUST 3.14.4.2** — For `equal_split_subset` and `equal_split_class`: same active rules within the chosen set.

**MUST 3.14.4.3** — Per-unit allocation = floor(total / N) for each unit. The remainder cents (`total - (floor(total/N) × N)`) MUST be distributed one-cent-per-unit to a rotating subset of eligible units, selected deterministically per period using the **rotating allocator** below.

**Rotating allocator — fairness over time:**

The allocator MUST distribute residual pennies fairly across periods such that no single unit consistently absorbs the rounding penalty. The algorithm:

1. Compute `base_per_unit = floor(total_cents / N)` and `residual_cents = total_cents - (base_per_unit × N)` where N = number of eligible units.
2. Build the eligible-unit list, sorted ascending by `unit.display_id` alphabetically (deterministic ordering).
3. Compute `rotation_offset = period_index mod N`, where `period_index` is a deterministic integer derived from `period_start`:
   - For monthly periods: `period_index = (year × 12) + month_zero_indexed` (e.g. Jan 2026 = 24312, Feb 2026 = 24313)
   - For weekly periods: `period_index = ISO_week_number_since_epoch_week_0`
   - For arbitrary periods: `period_index = days_since_epoch(period_start)`
4. The unit at index `rotation_offset` is the FIRST unit to receive a residual penny. The next `residual_cents - 1` units (wrapping around) each receive one additional penny.
5. Assign: `unit[i].amount = base_per_unit + (1 if i is in residual-penny-set, else 0)`.

**Worked example — $100 split across 3 units, monthly insurance:**

- Units (sorted by display_id): T032, T044, T056
- May 2026: `period_index = 24316`, `rotation_offset = 24316 mod 3 = 1` → T044 absorbs the penny → T032=$33.33, T044=$33.34, T056=$33.33
- Jun 2026: `period_index = 24317`, `rotation_offset = 24317 mod 3 = 2` → T056 absorbs the penny → T032=$33.33, T044=$33.33, T056=$33.34
- Jul 2026: `period_index = 24318`, `rotation_offset = 24318 mod 3 = 0` → T032 absorbs the penny → T032=$33.34, T044=$33.33, T056=$33.33
- Aug 2026: cycles back to T044, etc.

**MUST 3.14.4.3.A** — The allocator MUST be pure (deterministic): given the same inputs (period_start, total_cents, eligible-unit-set), it MUST produce identical output every time. No randomness, no time-of-day dependency.

**MUST 3.14.4.3.B** — The allocation record MUST persist `rotation_offset` and the residual-penny-recipient unit UUIDs in a `allocation_rounding_audit` JSON field on the journal entry. This makes the rotation reproducible and auditable. Reports MAY surface "this unit absorbed a $0.01 rounding penalty in 4 of last 12 months" to demonstrate fairness.

**MUST 3.14.4.3.C** — When residual_cents > N (extremely rare; only on unusually-shaped totals), every unit gets `+1` and the cycle continues. In practice residual_cents is always 0 to N-1.

**MUST 3.14.4.3.D** — When the eligible-unit-set changes between periods (units added or retired), `period_index mod N` is recomputed against the new N. Continuity of "next unit's turn" is not preserved across set changes; the allocator restarts within the new set. This is acceptable because the long-run distribution remains uniform within each stable set.

**MUST 3.14.4.3.E** — A correction (per 3.14.6) MUST use the same `rotation_offset` as the original posting; it does not recompute. This guarantees the reversal exactly cancels the original.

**MUST 3.14.4.4** — For `weighted_by_mileage`: per-unit miles for the period are computed from `dispatch.load_legs.miles_actual` summed by `unit_uuid`. Per-unit allocation = total × (unit_miles / total_miles). Same rounding rule for the residual penny.

**MUST 3.14.4.5** — For `weighted_by_revenue`: per-unit revenue = sum of invoices for loads where `dispatch.loads.assigned_unit_uuid = unit_uuid` AND `invoice_date IN [period_start, period_end]`. Same allocation logic.

### 3.14.5 Mid-period changes

**MUST 3.14.5.1** — A unit added mid-period (acquired during the period): receives a pro-rated allocation proportional to days in service during the period.

**Example:** Monthly bill $1,000 split across 10 units. Unit T100 acquired on April 15. April has 30 days. T100's pro-rate factor = 16/30 = 0.5333. The 9 full-period units get higher per-unit shares; T100 gets a smaller share. Math:

- Effective unit-days total = (9 × 30) + (1 × 16) = 286
- T100's share = $1,000 × 16 / 286 = $55.94
- Each full-period unit's share = $1,000 × 30 / 286 = $104.90
- 9 × $104.90 + $55.94 = $999.04 ... rounding residual $0.96 distributed via rotating allocator (3.14.4.3) one cent each to 96 unit-period slots (in this case all units get +$0.01 multiple times since residual > N; see 3.14.4.3.C)

**MUST 3.14.5.2** — A unit retired mid-period: same pro-rated logic, days = period_start to retired_date.

**MUST 3.14.5.3** — A unit reactivated mid-period: count from reactivation date.

### 3.14.6 Effective dates and corrections

**MUST 3.14.6.1** — An allocation can be corrected after posting only by:
1. Voiding the original bill and journal entries (reversal posting), AND
2. Re-creating with corrected parameters

**MUST 3.14.6.2** — Voids MUST be tracked per Q17 (5-year retention). Both the original and reversal remain on the books. Net effect on the period is zero, but the audit trail shows what happened.

**MUST 3.14.6.3** — A retroactive change (e.g. unit T100's acquired_date was wrong — should be April 20 not April 15) MUST be handled by an Allocation Correction journal entry that adjusts the prior month's allocations. The correction MUST be approved by Owner or Administrator.

### 3.14.7 The journal entry shape

A single allocation produces one journal entry with N+1 postings:

- 1 credit to the source account (Bank, AP, or Prepaid Expense, depending on payment method and amortization)
- N debits to the expense account, each tagged with the unit's class, summing to the credit

**Example posting for $1,500 monthly insurance bill, 50 units active, equal split:**

```
DR  Insurance Expense    class=T032    $30.00
DR  Insurance Expense    class=T044    $30.00
DR  Insurance Expense    class=T056    $30.00
... (50 lines total, residual pennies if any distributed via rotating allocator per 3.14.4.3)
DR  Insurance Expense    class=T999    $30.00
    CR  Accounts Payable                        $1500.00
```

**MUST 3.14.7.1** — The journal entry's QBO sync MUST preserve the per-line class assignments per 3.12.4.7 mapping.

**MUST 3.14.7.2** — If QBO has a maximum line count for a single journal entry that we exceed (rare; QBO supports 100s of lines), the allocation MUST split into multiple linked journal entries with a shared `allocation_batch_uuid` for traceability.

### 3.14.8 Per-unit P&L impact

**MUST 3.14.8.1** — The Reports module's "Profit per truck" report (Section 10) reads expenses by class (= unit). Allocation produces classed postings, so per-unit P&L includes the unit's share of allocated costs. Without allocation, per-unit P&L would be wrong.

**MUST 3.14.8.2** — A per-unit cost view MUST show allocated costs separately from direct costs (so user can see "of $X total cost, $Y was direct fuel/maintenance and $Z was allocated insurance/permit/etc.").

### 3.14.9 Filter-to-certain-units behavior [Q1.2]

**MUST 3.14.9.1** — Per Q1.2, when creating a shared bill the user MUST be able to select an arbitrary subset of units, not only "all active". The UI presents:
1. Allocate to: ( ) one unit · ( ) all active units · ( ) units of a class · (●) selected units
2. If selected: a multi-select list with all active units, with class filters and search

**MUST 3.14.9.2** — The selection MUST be stored as a list of unit UUIDs. Allocation respects only the selected units; non-selected active units do not participate.

### 3.14.10 Allocation in dispatch maintenance alerts [WF-044]

While WF-044 is primarily about maintenance-due alerts before assignment, it touches allocation in this way: a unit's `next_pm_due_at` and `pm_overdue_days` are derived in part from PM cost allocation. If a PM is allocated across multiple units (rare; most PMs are unit-specific), the alert references the unit-specific portion only. This is an edge case; most PMs are single-unit.

### 3.14.11 Error code taxonomy (Module: accounting / sub: allocation)

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `E_ALLOCATION_NO_UNITS_ACTIVE` | Period has no active units; cannot allocate |
| `E_ALLOCATION_SUBSET_EMPTY` | Subset policy chosen but list is empty |
| `E_ALLOCATION_PERIOD_INVALID` | period_end < period_start |
| `E_ALLOCATION_BALANCE_DRIFT` | Sum of unit allocations ≠ total (rounding bug) |
| `E_ALLOCATION_QBO_LINE_LIMIT` | More than QBO's max lines; split required |
| `E_ALLOCATION_RETRO_REQUIRES_OWNER` | Retroactive correction without Owner approval |
| `E_ALLOCATION_INVALID_POLICY` | Unknown allocation_policy value |

### 3.14.12 API contracts

#### `POST /api/accounting/bills` (with allocation)

Create a bill with allocation policy.

**Permission:** `accounting.bill.create` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant, Safety per 3.13.14 + Dispatch where applicable per Section 3.5).

**Example request:**
```json
{
  "vendor_uuid": "01923c8a-...vendor-insurance...",
  "transaction_date": "2026-05-01",
  "due_date": "2026-05-31",
  "memo": "Commercial auto liability — May 2026",
  "lines": [
    {
      "category_account_uuid": "01923c8a-...account-insurance...",
      "amount": 1500.00,
      "description": "Monthly premium",
      "allocation": {
        "policy": "equal_split_class",
        "class_filter": "truck",
        "period_start": "2026-05-01",
        "period_end": "2026-05-31"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-...bill...",
    "display_id": "BILL-2026-05-0142",
    "status": "posted",
    "total_amount": 1500.00,
    "allocation_summary": {
      "policy": "equal_split_class",
      "units_count": 50,
      "per_unit_amount": 30.00,
      "rounding": {
        "residual_cents": 0,
        "rotation_offset": 16,
        "period_index": 24316,
        "residual_recipient_unit_uuids": []
      }
    },
    "journal_entry_uuid": "01923c8a-...je...",
    "outbox_uuid": "01923c8a-...outbox..."
  }
}
```

**Example validation error (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_ALLOCATION_PERIOD_INVALID",
    "message": "period_end must be >= period_start",
    "details": [{"field": "lines[0].allocation.period_end", "value": "2026-04-30"}],
    "request_id": "req_01923c8e"
  }
}
```

#### `GET /api/accounting/allocations/preview`

Preview an allocation before posting.

**Permission:** `accounting.bill.create`.

**Example request:**
```
GET /api/accounting/allocations/preview?policy=equal_split_all_active&period_start=2026-05-01&period_end=2026-05-31&total=1500
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "policy": "equal_split_all_active",
    "period_start": "2026-05-01",
    "period_end": "2026-05-31",
    "total": 1500.00,
    "units_count": 142,
    "base_per_unit_amount": 10.56,
    "rounding": {
      "residual_cents": 4,
      "rotation_offset": 32,
      "period_index": 24316,
      "residual_recipient_unit_uuids": [
        "01923c8a-...unit-T088...",
        "01923c8a-...unit-T091...",
        "01923c8a-...unit-T094...",
        "01923c8a-...unit-T097..."
      ]
    },
    "preview_lines": [
      {"unit_display_id": "T032", "class_uuid": "...", "amount": 10.56},
      {"unit_display_id": "T044", "class_uuid": "...", "amount": 10.56},
      {"unit_display_id": "T088", "class_uuid": "...", "amount": 10.57},
      {"unit_display_id": "T091", "class_uuid": "...", "amount": 10.57}
    ]
  }
}
```

| Event | Severity |

| `allocation.bill_allocated` | info |
| `allocation.bill_voided` | warning |
| `allocation.retro_correction` | warning (Owner-only) |
| `allocation.amortization_posted` | info (monthly cron) |
| `allocation.unit_added_mid_period` | info |
| `allocation.unit_retired_mid_period` | info |

### 3.14.14 Permission actions by role

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `allocation.bill.create` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `allocation.preview` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |

### 3.14.15 Parity test IDs

- T-034.1: Monthly insurance $1,500 / 50 trucks, equal_split_class=truck → each truck gets $30.00, none get refrigerated/flatbed/dry-van
- T-034.2: Monthly insurance $1,000 / 3 units, equal_split_all_active across 3 consecutive months → rotating allocator assigns residual penny to a different unit each month (May=T044, Jun=T056, Jul=T032 per 3.14.4.3 worked example); over 12 months each unit absorbs the penny exactly 4 times
- T-034.3: Annual permit $1,200 / 12 units, equal_split_all_active, period_start=2026-01-01 period_end=2026-12-31 → posts to Prepaid Expense; monthly cron amortizes $100/12 units = $8.33/unit/month
- T-034.4: Unit added April 15 to monthly bill April 1-30 → pro-rata calculation; full units get higher share
- T-034.5: Subset selection of 5 specific units → only those 5 receive allocation
- T-034.6: Mileage-weighted allocation — 3 units run 1,000/2,000/3,000 miles → allocations 16.67% / 33.33% / 50.00%
- T-034.7: Voided allocation → reversal entries created → audit trail preserved; reversal uses same rotation_offset as original (3.14.4.3.E)
- T-034.8: Retroactive correction — Owner approves → correcting JE posted with audit reason
- T-034.9: Rotating allocator fairness — same $100 / 3 units / 12 consecutive monthly periods → each unit absorbs the residual penny exactly 4 times; long-run distribution is uniform
- T-034.10: Allocator determinism — running the same allocation twice (same period_index, same eligible-unit-set, same total) → produces byte-identical output
- T-034.11: Eligible-unit-set changes mid-period (unit retired April 15) → allocator recomputes against new N for following period; previously-stored `rotation_offset` is recorded for audit
- T-034.12: Residual cents > N (rare; total $1000 / 3 units = $333.33 each, residual 1¢; vs total $10000 / 3 = $3333.33 each, residual 1¢) → all residual cents distributed correctly

# END OF BATCH A — CHECKPOINT

This batch covers three high-risk sections of v3:

- **3.12** — QBO Auto-Sync + Offline Queue / Replay (the integration spine)
- **3.13** — Driver Liability / Deduction / Escrow Accounting (the most legally sensitive accounting domain)
- **3.14** — Unit Cost Allocation Engine (the engine that makes per-unit P&L correct)

**Coverage check against WF catalog:**

| Workflow | Section coverage |
|---|---|
| WF-008 Create AP Bill | 3.12.4.2 |
| WF-009 Create AP Expense | 3.12.4.1 |
| WF-011 Settlement with Debt Alert | 3.13.8 |
| WF-012 Hold Deduction | 3.13.7.4, 3.13.7.5 |
| WF-014 Border Crossing Charge Routing | 3.13.9 |
| WF-022 Integration Outage Handling | 3.12.5, 3.12.6 |
| WF-031 QBO Auto Sync | 3.12 (entire) |
| WF-032 Offline Accounting Continuity | 3.12.1, 3.12.5, 3.12.6 |
| WF-033 Driver Vendor + Unit Class Mapping | 3.13.1, 3.12.4 (validation rules A, B, G) |
| WF-034 Monthly Shared Cost Allocation | 3.14 (entire) |
| WF-035 Company Paid Driver Expense Recovery | 3.13.3 |
| WF-036 Driver Terms Acceptance Capture | 3.13.10 (depth in 3.17 Batch B) |
| WF-045 Bill Payment Detail Visibility | 3.12.4.3, 3.12.4.4 |
| WF-046 Transfer + Credit Card Payment | 3.12.4.4 |
| WF-064 Manager High-Risk Action Notifies Owner | partial (forfeiture-blocked, write-off, collections-send all emit critical Owner notifications); full coverage in Part 7 module specs (settlement override, vendor remap, manual JE, retro correction) |

**What's still pending in Batch B (sections 3.15-3.18):**
- Samsara HOS live ingest + stale-data fallback + SLA (WF-038, WF-022, WF-043)

- **approve** — I write Batch B (3.15-3.18) next
- **change X** — specifically what to change in Batch A
- **gap Y** — what Cursor or you spot as missing

# IH35 Master Blueprint v3 — Part 3 Extension · Batch B

**Sections covered:** 3.15 Samsara HOS Live Ingest + Stale-Data Fallback + SLA · 3.16 Geofence Timing Model · 3.17 Signature/Evidence Chain-of-Custody · 3.18 Chart-of-Accounts Governance + Posting Catalog

**Reading order:** read after Parts 1-3 base + Batch A revised. This batch completes Part 3 of v3.

**Normative language:** MUST · MUST NOT · SHOULD · SHOULD NOT · MAY (RFC 2119 conventions). Every requirement that affects financial integrity, audit, safety, or external system contracts is **MUST** unless explicitly marked **SHOULD**.

**WF coverage in this batch:** WF-021, WF-022, WF-036, WF-038, WF-043, WF-050, WF-058, WF-064 (full 7-action enforcement map below), plus cross-cutting CoA governance touching all accounting WFs (WF-008, WF-009, WF-010, WF-031, WF-033, WF-034, WF-035, WF-040, WF-046, WF-060)

**Test ID continuation:** this batch uses T-038.x, T-043.x, T-021.x, T-036.x (extending Batch A series), T-066.x through T-070.x. Verified against Batch A Revision 1 for non-collision.

---

## REVISION 1 — CHANGE LOG (applied 2026-05-02 per Cursor strict pass on Batch B)

Four targeted corrections applied to original Batch B, with no other functional changes:

1. **API DoD completion for three under-specified 3.18 endpoints** — `POST /api/admin/accounting/accounts/{uuid}/lifecycle`, `POST /api/admin/accounting/accounts/merge`, and `GET /api/admin/accounting/posting-templates` were missing full success + validation/error payload examples (violation of Definition of Done gate #4: "every API contract has request/success/validation error/error taxonomy"). All three endpoints now have: example success response (200), example validation error response (400), example business error response (409 where applicable), example permission error response (403), and explicit error code linkage. Two endpoints' success responses also note WF-064 Owner notification fan-out.

2. **WF-064 enforcement completeness** — original Batch B coverage table mapped WF-064 narrowly to "manual JE notification + merge notification" only. Replaced with explicit **7-action cross-reference table** mapping all seven high-risk action classes (accounting edits/posting overrides; settlement overrides; escrow forfeiture; deduction schedule overrides; manual JE/reclassifications; vendor/class remaps; retroactive allocation corrections) to their enforcement points across v3. Added four **WF-064 enforcement invariants** (WF-064.1 through WF-064.4) covering 60-second notification SLA, durable retry on dispatch failure, Owner suppression preferences (max 7-day window), and the Part 7 completion plan for actions whose foundations are in Batches A/B but whose per-module specs land later. Coverage status table shows 2 of 7 classes complete in Batches A/B (escrow forfeiture, retroactive allocation corrections); 5 of 7 reach completion in Part 7.

3. **Summary count corrections** — original Batch B end-of-file summary stated "24 error codes, 47 audit events, 61 parity tests." Recount during Cursor strict pass found these were inconsistent with the subsection counts. Corrected totals: **38 error codes** (10+7+9+12), **45 audit events** (14+10+11+10), **73 parity tests** (5+3+10+10+6+6+4+9+16+4). Reconciliation note added so any future doc referencing the prior totals can be corrected.

4. **Manual JE error code semantic fix** — original Batch B used `E_MANUAL_JE_REQUIRES_OWNER_APPROVAL` but spec correctly allows Owner/Admin/Accountant per Q1.9 / WF-060 / 3.18.12 permission matrix. Code name was misleading. Renamed to `E_MANUAL_JE_ROLE_FORBIDDEN` with role-based forbidden semantics ("attempted by role that lacks `accounting.manual_je.create` permission, i.e. not Owner, Administrator, or Accountant"). Permission matrix unchanged — Manager and Accountant access for manual JE remains as originally specified, since blocking Accountant would break normal month-end workflow. Test T-069.5 updated to reference renamed code.

No other changes to original Batch B. All other sections, WF mappings, posting rules, error codes, audit events, permission matrices, and parity tests are unchanged from the version delivered immediately prior.

## 3.15 — SAMSARA HOS LIVE INGEST + STALE-DATA FALLBACK + SLA
