**Maps to:** WF-038 (Active Driver Set Determination), WF-022 (Integration Outage Handling), WF-043 (Geofence and Delay Analytics — Samsara provides the position feed)

**Source basis:** Q19 (Samsara connected, 100 vehicles, real-time vehicle/driver/HOS), Q1.4 (active driver filter — exclude drivers inactive >15 days), §1 HOME (live HOS in attention list), §7 Drivers (active drivers status board with live HOS), §8 Dispatch (HOS visible on each row), §12 Driver App PWA (HOS visible to driver), latest user requirements (per-screen freshness budgets)

### 3.15.1 What Samsara provides — data classes

Samsara's API provides four classes of data the system MUST ingest:

1. **Vehicle position** — lat/lng, speed, heading, odometer, fuel level. Updated every ~30 seconds while the vehicle's gateway is powered.
2. **Driver HOS status** — current duty status (`driving` / `on_duty_not_driving` / `off_duty` / `sleeper_berth`), available driving hours under 11-hour rule, available on-duty hours under 14-hour rule, available cycle hours under 60/70-hour rule, time to next break, current driver assigned to vehicle.
3. **Vehicle diagnostics (DTC codes)** — engine fault codes from the J1939/OBD-II bus. Surfaced when a driver reports an in-transit issue (per WF-005, WF-048).
4. **Geofence events** — entry/exit events for configured geofences (per 3.16).

**MUST 3.15.1.1** — All four data classes MUST be ingested through a single `samsara` adapter module with a uniform polling/webhook interface. The adapter MUST NOT be split across modules; one team owns the Samsara contract.

### 3.15.2 Per-screen freshness budget

Different UI surfaces tolerate different staleness. The system MUST honor the following freshness budgets:

| Screen / context | Max staleness | Cache tier on hit | Behavior on miss |
|---|---|---|---|
| Driver App PWA — driver's own HOS card | 30 seconds | in-memory + Redis | Fall back to last DB snapshot, banner displays last-updated timestamp |
| Dispatch board (§8) — HOS column per row | 60 seconds | Redis | Fall back to DB; row shows gold "stale" indicator |
| Home page (§1) — attention list HOS items | 60 seconds | Redis | Fall back to DB; item still shown but marked stale |
| Drivers page (§7) — active drivers status board | 90 seconds | Redis | Fall back to DB; banner if any row stale |
| Fuel Planner (§5) — HOS-aware route diagram | 5 minutes | DB | Use cached snapshot; route recomputation deferred until fresh data |
| Safety dashboard (§6) — HOS rule warnings | 5 minutes | DB | Use cached snapshot; warnings recompute on next poll |
| Reports (§10) — historical HOS analysis | n/a | DB | Reads only persisted snapshots; never queries Samsara directly |
| Form 425C (§11) — projections | 1 hour | DB | Daily aggregate snapshots are sufficient |

**MUST 3.15.2.1** — Each screen MUST declare its freshness budget at the API endpoint level. The endpoint metadata (`hos_freshness_budget_seconds`) is consumed by the cache layer to decide whether to serve cached or fetch fresh.

**MUST 3.15.2.2** — A screen MUST NOT silently serve data older than its budget without surfacing the staleness to the user. UI MUST display a stale-data indicator (gold dot + tooltip with last-updated timestamp) whenever the served data is older than 50% of the budget AND a fresh fetch failed.

**MUST 3.15.2.3** — A screen MUST display data older than 100% of the budget only when a fresh fetch fails AND the cached data is younger than the absolute maximum staleness defined in 3.15.6 (the outage cutoff). Beyond the outage cutoff the screen displays the outage banner per 3.15.7.

### 3.15.3 The cache tier model

**MUST 3.15.3.1** — The system MUST implement a four-tier cache hierarchy for Samsara data:

1. **Tier 0 — In-memory (per-process):** 5-second TTL. Used by the Driver App PWA HOS endpoint. Skipped for office screens.
2. **Tier 1 — Redis (Upstash):** TTL per data class (30s vehicle position, 60s HOS status, 5min DTC codes, no TTL for geofence events). Shared across all backend processes.
3. **Tier 2 — PostgreSQL persisted snapshots:** every fetched record from Samsara is stored in `samsara.position_snapshots`, `samsara.hos_snapshots`, `samsara.dtc_snapshots`, `samsara.geofence_events`. Retention 5 years for positions and HOS (per Q17), unlimited for DTC + geofence events.
4. **Tier 3 — Samsara API:** the source of truth, but rate-limited and with cost.

**MUST 3.15.3.2** — Reads MUST query in tier order: in-memory → Redis → DB → Samsara API. The first tier with data younger than the freshness budget wins. If no tier has data younger than budget, the system fetches from Samsara API; on success the result populates all tiers; on failure the system falls back to the freshest tier within the absolute maximum staleness.

**MUST 3.15.3.3** — Writes (Samsara → us) MUST always populate Tier 2 (DB) first as durable persistence, then Tier 1 (Redis), then Tier 0 (per-process). Tier 2 is the recovery anchor.

### 3.15.4 The polling and webhook strategy

Samsara supports both polling and webhooks. The system MUST use:

**MUST 3.15.4.1** — **Polling for steady-state data (vehicle position, HOS status):**
- Vehicle position polled every 30 seconds across the fleet via the bulk position endpoint. One request returns positions for all 100 vehicles. The poller MUST handle pagination if Samsara's response is paginated.
- HOS status polled every 60 seconds across the active driver set (per WF-038, drivers active in the last 15 days).
- Polls run in BullMQ workers (`samsara-position-poller`, `samsara-hos-poller`) with overrun protection: a poll MUST NOT start if the previous poll for the same data class is still running; the worker logs an overrun warning and skips the cycle.

**MUST 3.15.4.2** — **Webhooks for event-driven data (DTC codes, geofence events, driver login/logout):**
- Configured Samsara webhook delivers events to `POST /api/webhooks/samsara` with HMAC signature verification.
- The endpoint MUST return 200 within 1 second; processing happens asynchronously via the `samsara-webhook-processor` queue.
- Failed signature verification MUST return 401 and log a security event.

**MUST 3.15.4.3** — **Rate-limit handling:**
- Samsara enforces ~150 requests/min per API token. The poller MUST enforce a self-imposed cap of 100 req/min to leave headroom for ad-hoc fetches.
- HTTP 429 responses MUST trigger exponential backoff matching the QBO backoff schedule (3.12.3.4): 30s → 2min → 10min → 30min, etc., with the same dead-letter threshold of 8 attempts.
- Rate-limit hits MUST be recorded as `samsara.rate_limited` audit events. When rate-limit hits exceed 3 in 5 minutes, the system MUST notify Administrator (warning, not critical).

**MUST 3.15.4.4** — **Samsara API auth:**
- The Samsara API token is stored in Cloudflare R2 secrets / environment variables, never in DB.
- Token rotation: when token is rotated by Owner via `/admin/integrations/samsara/rotate`, the old token continues to work for a 24-hour grace period during which both are tried.
- 401 from Samsara MUST trigger a critical notification to Owner + Administrator and pause the pollers (mirrors QBO 401 handling per 3.12.3.6).

### 3.15.5 The active driver filter [WF-038]

Per Q1.4 and WF-038, the active driver set is the basis for HOS polling, settlements visibility, and several other workflows.

**MUST 3.15.5.1** — A driver is "active" if and only if:
1. `driver.status = 'active'` (not separated, not on long-term leave) AND
2. The driver has had at least one Samsara HOS status update within the last 15 days OR has a load assignment in the next 7 days OR has an open settlement.

**MUST 3.15.5.2** — The active driver set MUST be recomputed every 15 minutes by the `samsara-active-driver-recompute` worker. The set is materialized in `master_data.driver_active_set` for fast lookup.

**MUST 3.15.5.3** — The HOS poller MUST query Samsara only for vehicles assigned to drivers in the active set. This caps Samsara request volume regardless of fleet size.

**MUST 3.15.5.4** — A driver who falls off the active set (>15 days no activity, no future loads, no open settlement) MUST be flagged on the Drivers page with a "stale" indicator. The Owner MUST receive a weekly digest of stale drivers for review (separation, return-to-work, etc.).

**MUST 3.15.5.5** — A driver who is on the active set but has had no Samsara updates in the last 24 hours (despite the active flag) MUST trigger a `samsara.driver_signal_lost` warning notification to Dispatcher. This protects against the case where a driver's tablet/Samsara device is offline but the driver is supposed to be on the road.

### 3.15.6 Outage cutoff and degraded modes

**MUST 3.15.6.1** — The absolute maximum staleness for any HOS data displayed in the UI is **2 hours**. Beyond 2 hours, the system MUST treat the data as unusable and switch to degraded mode.

**MUST 3.15.6.2** — In degraded mode, the affected screens MUST:
- Display a red banner: "Samsara connection degraded — HOS data older than 2 hours not shown"
- Hide HOS columns/widgets entirely (do not show stale numbers)
- Continue to show non-Samsara-derived information (load assignments, dispatch state, etc.)
- Disable workflows that require fresh HOS (e.g. fuel route recomputation that depends on remaining drive time)

**MUST 3.15.6.3** — When Samsara recovery is detected (a successful poll within the outage cutoff), the banner MUST clear automatically; affected widgets re-display.

### 3.15.7 The integration outage banner [WF-022]

**MUST 3.15.7.1** — The top bar's connection status indicator (next to "Samsara · QuickBooks · Relay connected") reflects Samsara health. Colors:
- Green — last successful poll within 2 minutes
- Gold — last successful poll within 10 minutes (warning)
- Red — last successful poll older than 10 minutes (critical) OR auth error active

**MUST 3.15.7.2** — Clicking the indicator opens `/admin/integrations/samsara` with diagnostic info: last poll timestamps per data class, error history (last 50 events), token rotation history, current rate-limit headroom.

**MUST 3.15.7.3** — Critical-state Samsara outage MUST trigger a notification per Part 3.6: Owner + Administrator + Manager via email; SMS only if the outage persists >30 minutes (so transient blips don't text everyone).

### 3.15.8 Persistent snapshots and reporting [Reports module]

**MUST 3.15.8.1** — Every Samsara HOS poll MUST persist the result to `samsara.hos_snapshots` regardless of whether the data changed since the previous poll. Retention: 5 years (per Q17). This builds a complete time-series for retrospective analysis and FMCSA audit defense.

**MUST 3.15.8.2** — The Reports module's "HOS history" report (per Section 10) reads from `samsara.hos_snapshots`. It MUST NOT query Samsara API directly — the snapshots are the report basis.

**MUST 3.15.8.3** — Snapshots MUST capture: `driver_uuid`, `vehicle_uuid` (if assigned), `duty_status`, `driving_hours_remaining`, `on_duty_hours_remaining`, `cycle_hours_remaining`, `time_to_next_break_minutes`, `samsara_payload` (full JSON for audit), `polled_at`, `samsara_event_at` (Samsara's own timestamp). Both timestamps are recorded so we can detect propagation delay.

**MUST 3.15.8.4** — The `samsara_event_at` timestamp is what's displayed to the user as "last updated"; `polled_at` is for audit only.

### 3.15.9 Time zone handling

**MUST 3.15.9.1** — All Samsara timestamps are received in UTC. The system MUST persist them in UTC and convert to America/Chicago for UI display (matching Q3 timezone preference).

**MUST 3.15.9.2** — HOS rule windows (11-hour, 14-hour, 60/70-hour) are computed by Samsara per FMCSA rules; the system MUST NOT recompute these. The system displays Samsara's computed values verbatim.

**MUST 3.15.9.3** — When a driver crosses time zones (common for Laredo-based fleet running US loads), Samsara handles the rule continuity; the system displays Samsara's view with the driver's home timezone (America/Chicago) as the default UI clock.

### 3.15.10 Error code taxonomy (Module: integrations / sub: samsara)

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning | Retry? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `E_SAMSARA_AUTH_REQUIRED` | 401 | Token expired or revoked | After rotation |
| `E_SAMSARA_RATE_LIMITED` | 429 | Throttle | Yes, longer backoff |
| `E_SAMSARA_UNAVAILABLE` | 5xx | Service unavailable | Yes |
| `E_SAMSARA_VEHICLE_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Vehicle UUID not in Samsara fleet | No, manual fix |
| `E_SAMSARA_DRIVER_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Driver UUID not in Samsara | No, manual fix |
| `E_SAMSARA_STALE_BEYOND_BUDGET` | n/a | Data older than freshness budget; no fresh fetch possible | UI shows stale banner |
| `E_SAMSARA_OUTAGE_CUTOFF_EXCEEDED` | n/a | Data older than 2-hour cutoff | UI switches to degraded mode |
| `E_SAMSARA_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_INVALID` | 401 | HMAC verification failed | No (security event logged) |
| `E_SAMSARA_POLL_OVERRUN` | n/a | Previous poll still running when next scheduled | No (skip cycle, log warning) |
| `E_SAMSARA_DEAD_LETTER` | n/a | Exceeded retry threshold | No, manual requeue |

### 3.15.11 API contracts

#### `GET /api/samsara/hos/{driver_uuid}`

Get current HOS status for a driver, with freshness budget honored.

**Permission:** `samsara.hos.read` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Dispatcher, Safety, Driver-self-only).

**Query parameters:**
- `freshness_budget_seconds` (optional, defaults to 60s for office; 30s for PWA)

**Example request:**
```
GET /api/samsara/hos/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000123?freshness_budget_seconds=60
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
```

**Example success response (200, fresh):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000123",
    "duty_status": "driving",
    "driving_hours_remaining": 7.5,
    "on_duty_hours_remaining": 9.25,
    "cycle_hours_remaining": 42.0,
    "time_to_next_break_minutes": 180,
    "vehicle_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000abc",
    "samsara_event_at": "2026-05-02T16:32:14Z",
    "polled_at": "2026-05-02T16:32:18Z",
    "data_age_seconds": 22,
    "freshness_state": "fresh"
  }
}
```

**Example success response (200, stale-but-within-cutoff):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000123",
    "duty_status": "driving",
    "driving_hours_remaining": 7.5,
    "on_duty_hours_remaining": 9.25,
    "cycle_hours_remaining": 42.0,
    "time_to_next_break_minutes": 180,
    "vehicle_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000abc",
    "samsara_event_at": "2026-05-02T16:25:11Z",
    "polled_at": "2026-05-02T16:25:18Z",
    "data_age_seconds": 421,
    "freshness_state": "stale",
    "stale_reason": "Last fresh fetch failed; serving from DB snapshot"
  }
}
```

**Example degraded response (503):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_SAMSARA_OUTAGE_CUTOFF_EXCEEDED",
    "message": "HOS data older than 2 hours; entering degraded mode",
    "details": {
      "last_known_event_at": "2026-05-02T14:00:00Z",
      "data_age_seconds": 9134,
      "outage_cutoff_seconds": 7200
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923c8e"
  }
}
```

#### `GET /api/samsara/active-drivers`

Get the currently active driver set (per WF-038).

**Permission:** `samsara.active_drivers.read` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Dispatcher, Safety).

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "active_driver_uuids": [
      "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000123",
      "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000124",
      "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000125"
    ],
    "computed_at": "2026-05-02T16:30:00Z",
    "next_recompute_at": "2026-05-02T16:45:00Z",
    "active_count": 47,
    "total_drivers": 70,
    "stale_drivers_count": 3
  }
}
```

#### `POST /api/webhooks/samsara`

Inbound Samsara webhook (DTC codes, geofence events, driver login/logout).

**Authentication:** HMAC signature in `X-Samsara-Signature` header.

**Example request body:**
```json
{
  "eventType": "DiagnosticTroubleCode",
  "eventTime": "2026-05-02T16:32:14Z",
  "data": {
    "vehicleId": "samsara_vehicle_id_here",
    "dtcCode": "P0420",
    "dtcDescription": "Catalyst System Efficiency Below Threshold (Bank 1)"
  }
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{"status": "received"}
```

**Example error (401, invalid signature):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_SAMSARA_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_INVALID",
    "message": "HMAC signature verification failed"
  }
}
```

### 3.15.12 Audit log events emitted

| Event | Severity |
|---|---|
| `samsara.poll_succeeded` | info (sampled, not every poll) |
| `samsara.poll_failed` | warning |
| `samsara.rate_limited` | warning |
| `samsara.auth_lost` | critical |
| `samsara.auth_restored` | info |
| `samsara.webhook_received` | info (sampled) |
| `samsara.webhook_signature_invalid` | critical (security) |
| `samsara.driver_signal_lost` | warning |
| `samsara.outage_started` | critical |
| `samsara.outage_resolved` | info |
| `samsara.degraded_mode_entered` | critical |
| `samsara.degraded_mode_exited` | info |
| `samsara.active_driver_set_recomputed` | info |
| `samsara.stale_driver_flagged` | info |

### 3.15.13 Permission actions by role

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `samsara.hos.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | ✓ | own only | — |
| `samsara.position.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | ✓ | own only | — |
| `samsara.dtc.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | ✓ | own only | ✓ |
| `samsara.active_drivers.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
| `samsara.token.rotate` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `samsara.token.disconnect` | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `samsara.poll.force_refresh` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — |

### 3.15.14 Parity test IDs

- T-038.1: Driver active in last 15 days → included in active set
- T-038.2: Driver inactive >15 days, no future loads, no open settlement → excluded from active set; flagged stale on Drivers page
- T-038.3: Driver inactive >15 days BUT has future load assignment → included in active set
- T-038.4: Active driver with no Samsara updates in last 24 hours → `samsara.driver_signal_lost` warning fires
- T-038.5: Active driver set recomputed every 15 minutes → cron job runs on schedule
- T-022.4: Samsara returns 5xx for 1 minute → poller retries with backoff → eventual success; no UI degradation
- T-022.5: Samsara returns 401 → poller halts; critical notification fires; top-bar indicator turns red
- T-022.6: Samsara unreachable for 30 minutes → SMS notifications fire to Owner + Admin
- T-066.1: Dispatch board HOS column with data <60s old → green; row shows fresh data
- T-066.2: Dispatch board HOS column with data 60-7200s old (between budget and cutoff) → gold "stale" indicator with last-updated tooltip
- T-066.3: Dispatch board HOS column with data >7200s old → degraded mode banner; HOS column hidden
- T-066.4: Driver PWA HOS card with data <30s old → fresh display
- T-066.5: Driver PWA HOS card data 30-7200s old → stale indicator on card
- T-066.6: Webhook signature invalid → 401 returned; security audit event written
- T-066.7: Poll overrun (previous poll still running) → next cycle skipped with warning log; no duplicate fetch
- T-066.8: Rate-limit hit (HTTP 429) → exponential backoff; logged
- T-066.9: 4 rate-limit hits in 5 minutes → Administrator warning notification fires
- T-066.10: Token rotation → 24-hour grace period, both tokens accepted

---

## 3.16 — GEOFENCE TIMING MODEL

**Maps to:** WF-043 (Geofence and Delay Analytics), WF-038 (Active Driver Set), WF-005 (In-Transit Issue Capture — geofence is one source of issue context)

**Source basis:** Q19 (Samsara provides geofence events), §1 HOME (detention-flagged loads in attention list), §8 Dispatch (per-row trip status), latest user requirements (dwell, detention, layover, dispatcher booking-gap analytics)

### 3.16.1 The stop lifecycle state machine

Every load stop (pickup or delivery) has a state machine the system MUST track:

```
  scheduled → en_route → arrived → checked_in → loading → loaded → departed
                                                ↓ (delivery variant)
                              arrived → checked_in → unloading → unloaded → departed
```

**MUST 3.16.1.1** — The stop record (`dispatch.load_stops`) MUST persist all state transitions with timestamps. Each transition writes to `dispatch.load_stop_events` with `from_state`, `to_state`, `transitioned_at`, `transitioned_by_source` (one of: `geofence`, `driver_pwa`, `dispatcher_manual`), `transitioned_by_user_id` (null when source=geofence).

**MUST 3.16.1.2** — The state MUST advance forward only. Backward transitions are forbidden except via explicit dispatcher correction (audit event `dispatch.stop_state_corrected`, requires reason text).

### 3.16.2 Geofence configuration per stop

**MUST 3.16.2.1** — Each stop's `geofence` is configured at load creation:
- `center_lat`, `center_lng` — derived from the customer/location address (geocoded via mapping service)
- `radius_meters` — default 200m for warehouses, 500m for ports/yards, configurable per location (`master_data.locations.geofence_radius_meters`)
- `arrival_dwell_threshold_seconds` — minimum continuous time inside the geofence to count as "arrived" (default 60 seconds, prevents drive-by false-arrivals)
- `departure_dwell_threshold_seconds` — minimum continuous time outside the geofence to count as "departed" (default 120 seconds, prevents brief exit/re-entry from triggering false departure)

**MUST 3.16.2.2** — The `master_data.locations` table MUST allow per-location overrides for geofence radius and dwell thresholds. Border crossing locations (per Q16) typically need 1000m radius with 300s arrival dwell.

**MUST 3.16.2.3** — A location MUST be reusable across loads. When a customer's facility is geocoded once, every future load to that customer reuses the same geofence configuration. The geocoding MUST persist with timestamp; re-geocoding requires Manager+ permission.

### 3.16.3 The state-transition resolution rules

Three input sources can advance the state: Samsara geofence events, driver PWA actions, dispatcher manual overrides. When multiple sources fire, the system MUST resolve per the following rules.

**MUST 3.16.3.1** — **Source priority for `arrived` transition:**
1. Samsara geofence-entry event with dwell threshold met → automatic transition
2. Driver PWA "arrived at stop" tap → transition (does NOT require geofence confirmation; driver may be slightly outside the configured geofence)
3. Dispatcher manual mark → transition (audit event with reason; least-trusted source)

When source 1 and source 2 both fire, the EARLIER `transitioned_at` wins; the later source records as a correlation event but does not re-transition. When source 1 and source 3 conflict (geofence says no, dispatcher says yes), source 3 wins and an audit event `dispatch.stop_arrived_dispatcher_override` is written.

**MUST 3.16.3.2** — **Source priority for `departed` transition:**
1. Samsara geofence-exit event with departure dwell threshold met → automatic transition
2. Driver PWA "departed stop" tap → transition
3. Dispatcher manual mark → transition

Same priority rules as above.

**MUST 3.16.3.3** — **`checked_in`, `loading`/`unloading`, `loaded`/`unloaded` states:** these are NOT geofence-driven; they require explicit driver PWA action OR dispatcher mark. Geofence cannot infer them.

**MUST 3.16.3.4** — When a driver is inside the geofence but has not tapped `arrived` in PWA AND no dispatcher mark exists, the system MUST NOT advance to `arrived` until the dwell threshold is met by geofence OR one of the other sources fires. The grace period prevents false-positives from drive-bys.

### 3.16.4 KPI derivations [WF-043]

The system MUST compute the following KPIs from stop event timestamps. Each KPI is computed per-stop, then aggregated per-load, per-driver, per-customer, per-week as needed by reports.

**MUST 3.16.4.1** — **Detention time:** `detention_minutes = max(0, (departed_at - arrived_at).total_minutes - free_time_minutes)`. Free time is per-customer agreement (default 120 minutes for pickup, 120 for delivery; configurable per `master_data.customers.free_time_pickup_minutes` etc.). Detention beyond free time is billable per Q9.

**MUST 3.16.4.2** — **Dwell time:** `dwell_minutes = (departed_at - arrived_at).total_minutes`. Total time at the stop, regardless of free-time threshold. Used for fleet utilization analytics.

**MUST 3.16.4.3** — **Layover time:** when a driver is on a multi-day load and stops overnight at a non-customer location (truck stop, hotel), `layover_minutes` is computed from the gap between consecutive stop departures and arrivals where the gap exceeds 8 hours. Layover beyond customer-provided lodging triggers layover compensation per the driver pay catalog (per WF-013).

**MUST 3.16.4.4** — **Dispatcher booking-gap time:** `booking_gap_hours = (next_load.assigned_at - prior_load.departed_at).total_hours`. Measures how long a unit sits empty between loads. Aggregated per dispatcher, per week, surfaced in dispatch reports.

**MUST 3.16.4.5** — **Late-arrival rate:** percentage of stops where `arrived_at > scheduled_arrival_at + grace_period`. Grace period default 30 minutes, configurable. Aggregated per driver and per customer (some customers run more loose schedules than others).

### 3.16.5 The detention-billable trigger

**MUST 3.16.5.1** — When a stop's detention exceeds free time, the system MUST flag the load for detention billing review. The flag appears on the load's billing screen with proposed detention amount per the customer's agreement (`master_data.customers.detention_rate_per_hour`).

**MUST 3.16.5.2** — Detention billing is NOT auto-added to invoices; it requires Manager or Owner approval per load. Once approved, an invoice line is added per 3.12.4.5.

**MUST 3.16.5.3** — Detention rejection (Manager declines to bill) MUST be logged with reason. Common reasons: customer agreement overrides default, dispute pending, complimentary waiver.

### 3.16.6 The data-quality reconciliation

**MUST 3.16.6.1** — When the geofence-inferred `arrived_at` and the driver-reported `arrived_at` differ by more than 5 minutes, the system MUST log a `dispatch.stop_timestamp_mismatch` event. Both timestamps are persisted; reports use the earlier one as canonical.

**MUST 3.16.6.2** — When the dispatcher overrides a state transition that contradicts geofence data, the audit event MUST capture both the geofence state and the dispatcher action so the audit trail can be reconstructed.

**MUST 3.16.6.3** — A daily reconciliation report (per Reports module) MUST surface stops where geofence data is missing (Samsara device offline at the time) so the operations team can fill in driver-PWA or dispatcher-manual data after the fact.

### 3.16.7 Cross-border geofences [Q16]

**MUST 3.16.7.1** — Border crossings (Laredo / World Trade Bridge / Pharr / etc.) are configured as special geofences with `is_border_crossing = true` and larger radius (1000m default). Crossing entry/exit times are captured for: customs-clearance time analytics, border crossing charge routing per WF-014, IFTA mileage attribution.

**MUST 3.16.7.2** — A border crossing event MUST trigger a `dispatch.border_crossing_detected` audit event AND, if the load has `border_crossing_routing` unset, a notification to the dispatcher to choose the routing per 3.13.9.

### 3.16.8 Error code taxonomy (Module: dispatch / sub: geofence)

| Code | Meaning | Recovery |
|---|---|---|
| `E_GEOFENCE_NOT_CONFIGURED` | Stop has no geofence; can't auto-track | Geocode location, set radius |
| `E_GEOFENCE_RADIUS_INVALID` | Radius <= 0 or > 50000m | Manual fix |
| `E_STOP_STATE_BACKWARD_TRANSITION` | Attempted to move state backward without correction flag | Use dispatcher correction flow |
| `E_STOP_STATE_INVALID_TRANSITION` | Skip-ahead state transition (e.g. scheduled → loaded) | Sequence transitions properly |
| `E_DETENTION_RATE_NOT_SET` | Customer has no detention rate configured | Set in master data |
| `E_BORDER_ROUTING_REQUIRED` | Border crossing detected; routing not set | Dispatcher selects routing per 3.13.9 |
| `E_GEOFENCE_GEOCODE_FAILED` | Address could not be geocoded | Manual lat/lng entry |

### 3.16.9 API contracts

#### `POST /api/dispatch/load-stops/{uuid}/transition`

Manually transition a stop's state (dispatcher override or PWA-initiated).

**Permission:** `dispatch.stop.transition` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Dispatcher) for dispatcher source; `dispatch.stop.transition.driver` (Driver-self) for PWA source.

**Example request:**
```json
{
  "to_state": "arrived",
  "source": "driver_pwa",
  "occurred_at": "2026-05-02T17:14:22Z",
  "device_metadata": {
    "device_uuid": "01923c8a-...device...",
    "lat": 27.5036,
    "lng": -99.5075
  }
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "stop_uuid": "01923c8a-...stop...",
    "from_state": "en_route",
    "to_state": "arrived",
    "transitioned_at": "2026-05-02T17:14:22Z",
    "source": "driver_pwa",
    "geofence_correlation": {
      "geofence_inferred_at": "2026-05-02T17:14:18Z",
      "delta_seconds": 4,
      "match": true
    }
  }
}
```

**Example error (409):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_STOP_STATE_INVALID_TRANSITION",
    "message": "Cannot transition from 'scheduled' to 'loaded'; intermediate states required",
    "details": {
      "current_state": "scheduled",
      "requested_state": "loaded",
      "valid_next_states": ["en_route"]
    }
  }
}
```

#### `GET /api/dispatch/load-stops/{uuid}/timing`

Get the full timing analytics for a stop.

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "stop_uuid": "01923c8a-...stop...",
    "scheduled_arrival_at": "2026-05-02T16:00:00Z",
    "arrived_at": "2026-05-02T16:38:00Z",
    "checked_in_at": "2026-05-02T16:42:00Z",
    "loading_started_at": "2026-05-02T17:05:00Z",
    "loaded_at": "2026-05-02T19:22:00Z",
    "departed_at": "2026-05-02T19:35:00Z",
    "metrics": {
      "late_arrival_minutes": 38,
      "dwell_minutes": 177,
      "free_time_minutes": 120,
      "detention_minutes": 57,
      "detention_billable": true,
      "detention_rate_per_hour": 75.00,
      "detention_proposed_amount": 71.25
    },
    "data_sources": {
      "arrived_at_source": "geofence",
      "departed_at_source": "geofence"
    }
  }
}
```

### 3.16.10 Audit log events emitted

| Event | Severity |
|---|---|
| `dispatch.stop_state_transitioned` | info |
| `dispatch.stop_state_corrected` | warning |
| `dispatch.stop_arrived_dispatcher_override` | warning |
| `dispatch.stop_timestamp_mismatch` | info |
| `dispatch.detention_flagged` | info |
| `dispatch.detention_billed` | info |
| `dispatch.detention_waived` | warning |
| `dispatch.border_crossing_detected` | info |
| `dispatch.geofence_geocode_failed` | warning |
| `dispatch.geofence_data_missing` | info |

### 3.16.11 Permission actions by role

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `dispatch.stop.transition` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `dispatch.stop.transition.driver` | — | — | — | — | — | — | own only | — |
| `dispatch.stop.correct` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `dispatch.detention.flag` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `dispatch.detention.bill` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — |
| `dispatch.detention.waive` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — |
| `dispatch.geofence.configure` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — |

### 3.16.12 Parity test IDs

- T-043.1: Driver enters geofence with 60s dwell → state advances to `arrived` automatically
- T-043.2: Driver enters geofence for 30s then exits → no state advance (dwell threshold not met)
- T-043.3: Driver taps `arrived` in PWA before geofence fires → PWA timestamp wins; geofence event logged as correlation
- T-043.4: Geofence fires arrival, driver hasn't tapped → state advances on geofence dwell
- T-043.5: Dispatcher manually marks arrived contradicting geofence (driver still in transit) → dispatcher wins; audit event written with reason
- T-043.6: Driver departs stop, geofence-exit dwell met (120s) → state advances to `departed`
- T-043.7: Driver briefly exits geofence (<120s) and re-enters → no state advance (false-departure prevented)
- T-043.8: Stop with 3-hour dwell, customer free time 2 hours → detention 60 min flagged; load billing screen shows proposed amount
- T-043.9: Manager approves detention billing → invoice line added
- T-043.10: Manager waives detention with reason → audit event written; no invoice line
- T-067.1: Border crossing detected, routing not set → dispatcher notification fires; load flagged
- T-067.2: Backward state transition attempted without correction flag → E_STOP_STATE_BACKWARD_TRANSITION
- T-067.3: Skip-ahead transition (scheduled → loaded) → E_STOP_STATE_INVALID_TRANSITION; valid next states returned
- T-067.4: Geocoding failure on new location → E_GEOFENCE_GEOCODE_FAILED; manual lat/lng accepted
- T-067.5: Booking gap calculation — unit departs 16:00, next load assigned 09:00 next day → 17h gap captured for dispatcher report
- T-067.6: Late-arrival rate aggregation — driver with 8 of 10 stops within grace → 80% on-time rate

---

## 3.17 — SIGNATURE / EVIDENCE CHAIN-OF-CUSTODY

**Maps to:** WF-021 (Driver Document Capture), WF-036 (Driver Terms Acceptance Capture), WF-050 (Pre-Trip / Post-Trip DVIR Capture), WF-058 (Equipment Damage Photo Comparison Storage)

**Source basis:** Q17 (5-year retention), Q1.10 (image storage), §12 PWA (signature capture), 3.13.10 (acknowledgment requirement deferred to this section), latest user requirements (legal-grade evidence chain)

### 3.17.1 What requires chain-of-custody

The system MUST apply chain-of-custody handling to the following evidence types:

1. **Driver-signed acknowledgments** of liability terms (per 3.13.10) — fines, accident liability, equipment loss, escrow forfeiture clause acknowledgment, settlement debt summary acknowledgment
2. **Signed BOL (Bill of Lading)** / proof of delivery captures (per WF-021)
3. **DVIR forms** signed at pre-trip and post-trip (per WF-050)
4. **Equipment damage photos** (per WF-058)
5. **Accident scene photos** and witness statements (per WF-002, WF-007)
6. **Bills uploaded by drivers** (gas/parking/scale receipts) for reimbursement
7. **Hire agreements** and addenda (per 3.13.6.3.C remediation path)

**MUST 3.17.1.1** — Each evidence type MUST resolve to a record in `documents.evidence_records` with the chain-of-custody fields specified in 3.17.2.

### 3.17.2 The evidence record schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE documents.evidence_records (
  uuid                    UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  evidence_type           TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (evidence_type IN (
    'liability_acknowledgment','bol_pod','dvir','damage_photo',
    'accident_photo','witness_statement','reimbursement_receipt',
    'hire_agreement','agreement_addendum'
  )),
  related_entity_type     TEXT NOT NULL,
  related_entity_uuid     UUID NOT NULL,
  driver_uuid             UUID,
  
  -- Capture metadata
  captured_at             TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  captured_via            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (captured_via IN ('pwa_in_app','pdf_upload','office_scan','samsara_attachment')),
  device_uuid             UUID,
  device_fingerprint      TEXT,
  capture_lat             DECIMAL(10,7),
  capture_lng             DECIMAL(10,7),
  capture_ip              INET,
  
  -- Server-side recording (the "record of record")
  server_received_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  server_recording_user_id UUID,
  
  -- Tamper-evidence
  payload_storage_url     TEXT NOT NULL,            -- Cloudflare R2 path
  payload_sha256_hash     TEXT NOT NULL,            -- hex-encoded SHA-256
  payload_size_bytes      BIGINT NOT NULL,
  payload_mime_type       TEXT NOT NULL,
  
  -- Signature (when applicable)
  signature_image_url     TEXT,                     -- separate R2 object
  signature_image_hash    TEXT,
  signature_typed_name    TEXT,
  signature_method        TEXT CHECK (signature_method IN ('drawn','typed','clicked_accept','wet_signature_scanned')),
  
  -- Tamper-evident PDF
  signed_pdf_url          TEXT,
  signed_pdf_hash         TEXT,
  
  -- Retention and legal hold
  retention_expires_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  legal_hold              BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  legal_hold_reason       TEXT,
  legal_hold_set_by_user_id UUID,
  legal_hold_set_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
  
  -- Voiding (cannot delete)
  voided_at               TIMESTAMPTZ,
  voided_by_user_id       UUID,
  voided_reason           TEXT,
  voiding_record_uuid     UUID                      -- links to the void record
);

CREATE INDEX evidence_related_idx ON documents.evidence_records (related_entity_type, related_entity_uuid);
CREATE INDEX evidence_driver_idx ON documents.evidence_records (driver_uuid) WHERE driver_uuid IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX evidence_legal_hold_idx ON documents.evidence_records (legal_hold) WHERE legal_hold = true;
CREATE INDEX evidence_retention_expires_idx ON documents.evidence_records (retention_expires_at) WHERE legal_hold = false AND voided_at IS NULL;
```

**MUST 3.17.2.1** — Every evidence record MUST be created via the `documents.create_evidence` server function. Direct INSERT into the table is forbidden by RLS policies.

**MUST 3.17.2.2** — `captured_at` is the device's claimed timestamp; `server_received_at` is the server's authoritative timestamp. They MAY differ. The server MUST NEVER use the device timestamp for legal/audit purposes; `server_received_at` is the canonical event time.

**MUST 3.17.2.3** — `payload_sha256_hash` is computed server-side from the uploaded payload bytes BEFORE the payload is stored. The hash is the canonical fingerprint; any mismatch on later retrieval means tampering.

### 3.17.3 The signed-PDF generator

**MUST 3.17.3.1** — For evidence types `liability_acknowledgment`, `dvir`, `bol_pod`, `hire_agreement`, `agreement_addendum`: the system MUST generate a tamper-evident PDF that embeds:
- The original document content (contract terms, BOL, DVIR checklist, etc.)
- The signature image (drawn / typed / wet-scanned)
- The chain-of-custody metadata: server timestamp, IP, device fingerprint, lat/lng, capture method
- The SHA-256 hash of the entire payload, printed on the last page in human-readable form

**MUST 3.17.3.2** — The signed PDF itself MUST be hashed (`signed_pdf_hash`) and that hash persisted. Any modification to the PDF after generation invalidates the chain.

**MUST 3.17.3.3** — The signed PDF SHOULD use PDF/A-2b format for long-term archival compliance.

**MUST 3.17.3.4** — Cryptographic signing of the PDF (via certificate) is OUT of scope for v1 (Phase 2 if FMCSA or insurance demands it). The hash-on-record approach is sufficient for v1's evidentiary needs.

### 3.17.4 The retention model

**MUST 3.17.4.1** — Default retention is 5 years from `server_received_at` (per Q17). Retention MAY be extended per evidence type:

| Evidence type | Default retention |
|---|---|
| `liability_acknowledgment` | 7 years (statute of limitations buffer) |
| `bol_pod` | 5 years |
| `dvir` | 6 months (FMCSA minimum) BUT raised to 5 years if any defect was reported |
| `damage_photo` | 5 years |
| `accident_photo` | 7 years (litigation risk) |
| `witness_statement` | 7 years |
| `reimbursement_receipt` | 5 years (IRS) |
| `hire_agreement` | 7 years from driver separation |
| `agreement_addendum` | 7 years from driver separation |

**MUST 3.17.4.2** — A nightly cron MUST scan `documents.evidence_records` for records past `retention_expires_at` AND `legal_hold = false` AND `voided_at IS NULL` and queue them for purge (see 3.17.5).

### 3.17.5 The purge process

**MUST 3.17.5.1** — Purge MUST be a two-phase process:
1. **Soft purge:** record marked `purge_pending_at`; payload moved to a quarantine R2 bucket with 90-day retention; record metadata stays in DB
2. **Hard purge:** after 90 days, payload deleted from quarantine; record's payload URL set to NULL; metadata kept indefinitely as audit trail

**MUST 3.17.5.2** — During the 90-day soft-purge window, an Owner MAY restore the record by clearing `purge_pending_at`. Restoration moves the payload back to the active R2 bucket and re-extends `retention_expires_at`.

**MUST 3.17.5.3** — Hard purge is irreversible. The audit trail keeps the metadata (who created, when, type) but the payload is gone.

### 3.17.6 Legal hold

**MUST 3.17.6.1** — A `legal_hold = true` flag overrides ALL retention expiration. Records on legal hold MUST NOT be purged regardless of `retention_expires_at`.

**MUST 3.17.6.2** — Setting legal hold requires Owner or Administrator role; reason text is required; audit event `documents.legal_hold_set` (critical) is emitted.

**MUST 3.17.6.3** — Releasing legal hold also requires Owner or Administrator and reason text; audit event `documents.legal_hold_released` (critical) emitted.

**MUST 3.17.6.4** — When a driver is named in pending litigation, the system MUST surface a one-click "Apply legal hold to all records for driver X" Owner action that sets `legal_hold = true` on every evidence record where `driver_uuid = X`.

### 3.17.7 Retrieval and tamper detection

**MUST 3.17.7.1** — On retrieval (any read of the payload), the system MUST recompute SHA-256 from the stored bytes and compare against `payload_sha256_hash`. Mismatch MUST:
- Block the retrieval response
- Return `E_EVIDENCE_HASH_MISMATCH` error
- Emit critical audit event `documents.evidence_tampered_detected`
- Trigger immediate notification to Owner + Administrator

**MUST 3.17.7.2** — Retrieval URLs MUST be short-lived signed R2 URLs (15-minute expiry). The signed URL encodes the expected hash so any swap of the underlying object invalidates the URL.

**MUST 3.17.7.3** — Retrieval MUST be logged in `documents.evidence_access_log` with: who retrieved, when, why (purpose code: `audit`, `legal`, `routine_view`, `dispute`, `support`), source IP. Aggregated retrieval reports surface unusual access patterns.

### 3.17.8 Voiding (when evidence is disputed)

**MUST 3.17.8.1** — Evidence records MUST NOT be deleted, even when their content is disputed (e.g. driver claims they did not actually sign). Disputes are handled via voiding.

**MUST 3.17.8.2** — Voiding creates a NEW evidence record (`evidence_type = 'void_record'`) that links to the original via `voiding_record_uuid` and contains:
- The void reason (free text)
- Who voided (user_id, role)
- When voided (server timestamp)
- A signed acknowledgment that the void was authorized (Owner or Administrator role required)

**MUST 3.17.8.3** — The original record's `voided_at`, `voided_by_user_id`, and `voiding_record_uuid` are populated, but the original payload, hash, and metadata REMAIN unchanged. Both records (original and void) survive purge until both retention dates pass.

**MUST 3.17.8.4** — A voided record's payload MUST still be retrievable for legal/audit purposes; the voiding does not hide the original — it annotates it.

### 3.17.9 The acknowledgment-specific flow [WF-036, 3.13.10]

This subsection details the acknowledgment-capture flow that 3.13.10 deferred to here.

**MUST 3.17.9.1** — When a deduction schedule has `requires_acknowledgment = true` and `acknowledgment_uuid IS NULL`, the system MUST present the terms to the driver via one of two paths:

**Path A — PWA in-app:**
1. Driver opens PWA, sees "Pending acknowledgment" banner
2. Tapping the banner opens a full-screen modal showing the terms
3. Driver scrolls through (system MUST require scroll-to-bottom before Accept button enables)
4. Driver taps Accept
5. PWA sends `POST /api/driver-finance/acknowledgments` with capture metadata (device fingerprint, lat/lng, timestamp, scroll-completion flag)
6. Server creates evidence record (`evidence_type = 'liability_acknowledgment'`), generates signed PDF, hashes both payload and PDF, returns success
7. Deduction schedule's `acknowledgment_uuid` populated; deduction now eligible for next settlement

**Path B — Printed PDF:**
1. Office prints the terms PDF from `/admin/driver-finance/liabilities/{uuid}/print`
2. Driver signs by hand
3. Office scans the signed PDF and uploads via `POST /api/driver-finance/acknowledgments` with `captured_via = 'pdf_upload'`
4. Server creates evidence record with the scanned image as payload, hashes it, and (if signature region detected via image processing) extracts a `signature_image_url`
5. Deduction schedule's `acknowledgment_uuid` populated

**MUST 3.17.9.2** — The acknowledgment record MUST capture the EXACT terms text shown to the driver. If the company changes terms language between the time terms were generated and the time the driver acknowledges, the acknowledgment is voided and a new acknowledgment cycle begins. This prevents the "we changed the terms after they signed" failure mode.

**MUST 3.17.9.3** — The acknowledgment record MUST NOT carry `requires_acknowledgment` recursively — i.e. acknowledging terms doesn't itself require acknowledgment. It's the leaf of the chain.

### 3.17.10 Cloudflare R2 storage configuration

**MUST 3.17.10.1** — Evidence payloads are stored in Cloudflare R2 (per Part 6 architecture). The bucket layout:
- `ih35-evidence-active/` — active evidence
- `ih35-evidence-quarantine/` — soft-purge holding
- `ih35-evidence-legal-hold/` — segregated bucket for legal hold (separate access controls)

**MUST 3.17.10.2** — R2 object names MUST include `{evidence_type}/{driver_uuid_or_load_uuid}/{evidence_uuid}.{ext}` for human-debuggable structure.

**MUST 3.17.10.3** — R2 bucket configuration: object lock for legal-hold bucket; versioning enabled on active bucket (catches accidental overwrites; old versions retained 30 days); lifecycle rules MUST NOT auto-delete (purge is application-controlled).

### 3.17.11 Error code taxonomy (Module: documents / sub: evidence)

| Code | Meaning | Recovery |
|---|---|---|
| `E_EVIDENCE_HASH_MISMATCH` | SHA-256 mismatch on retrieval; tampering detected | Critical incident; isolate record; investigate |
| `E_EVIDENCE_PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE` | Upload exceeds size limit (50 MB default) | Resize / split |
| `E_EVIDENCE_INVALID_MIME` | MIME type not in allowlist | Use approved formats |
| `E_EVIDENCE_TERMS_CHANGED` | Terms changed between presentation and acknowledgment | New acknowledgment cycle required |
| `E_EVIDENCE_SCROLL_INCOMPLETE` | Driver didn't scroll through full terms (PWA path) | Driver scrolls to bottom |
| `E_EVIDENCE_LEGAL_HOLD_PURGE_BLOCKED` | Purge attempted on legal-hold record | Cannot purge while held |
| `E_EVIDENCE_VOID_REQUIRES_OWNER` | Voiding attempted by non-Owner/Admin | Owner action required |
| `E_EVIDENCE_RETRIEVAL_EXPIRED` | Signed R2 URL expired | Request new retrieval |
| `E_EVIDENCE_DEVICE_FINGERPRINT_MISSING` | PWA didn't send device fingerprint | Re-capture required |

### 3.17.12 API contracts

#### `POST /api/driver-finance/acknowledgments`

Capture a driver acknowledgment of liability terms.

**Permission:** `evidence.acknowledgment.create.driver` (Driver-self for PWA path); `evidence.acknowledgment.create.office` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant for PDF upload path).

**Example request (PWA path):**
```json
{
  "deduction_schedule_uuid": "01923c8a-...schedule...",
  "captured_via": "pwa_in_app",
  "captured_at": "2026-05-02T17:42:00Z",
  "device_uuid": "01923c8a-...device...",
  "device_fingerprint": "fp_a1b2c3d4e5f6...",
  "capture_lat": 27.5036,
  "capture_lng": -99.5075,
  "signature_method": "drawn",
  "signature_image_base64": "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAA...",
  "scroll_completion": true,
  "terms_hash_at_presentation": "abc123def456..."
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-...evidence...",
    "deduction_schedule_uuid": "01923c8a-...schedule...",
    "server_received_at": "2026-05-02T17:42:03Z",
    "payload_sha256_hash": "9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08",
    "signed_pdf_hash": "2c26b46b68ffc68ff99b453c1d30413413422d706483bfa0f98a5e886266e7ae",
    "signed_pdf_url": "https://r2-signed-url.../evidence/.../signed.pdf?expires=...",
    "retention_expires_at": "2033-05-02T17:42:03Z"
  }
}
```

**Example error (409, terms changed):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_EVIDENCE_TERMS_CHANGED",
    "message": "Terms changed since presentation; please re-read and re-acknowledge",
    "details": {
      "presented_terms_hash": "abc123def456...",
      "current_terms_hash": "xyz789uvw012..."
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923c8f"
  }
}
```

**Example error (400, scroll incomplete, PWA only):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_EVIDENCE_SCROLL_INCOMPLETE",
    "message": "Driver must scroll through full terms before accepting",
    "details": {"scroll_completion": false}
  }
}
```

#### `GET /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/retrieve`

Get a short-lived signed URL to retrieve an evidence payload.

**Permission:** `evidence.retrieve` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant always; Driver-self for own; Safety for accident/DVIR; Mechanic for damage photos).

**Query parameters:**
- `purpose` (required): `audit` | `legal` | `routine_view` | `dispute` | `support`

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-...evidence...",
    "signed_url": "https://r2-signed-url.../evidence/...?signature=...",
    "signed_url_expires_at": "2026-05-02T17:57:00Z",
    "payload_sha256_hash": "9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08",
    "retrieved_at": "2026-05-02T17:42:00Z",
    "retrieved_by_user_id": "01923c8a-...user...",
    "purpose": "audit"
  }
}
```

**Example error (500, tamper detected):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_EVIDENCE_HASH_MISMATCH",
    "message": "Stored payload hash does not match expected; tampering suspected",
    "details": {
      "expected_hash": "9f86d081884c7d659a2feaa0c55ad015a3bf4f1b2b0b822cd15d6c15b0f00a08",
      "actual_hash": "deadbeef0000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000dead",
      "incident_id": "incident_01923c90"
    }
  }
}
```

#### `POST /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/legal-hold`

Set or release legal hold on an evidence record.

**Permission:** `evidence.legal_hold.set` (Owner, Admin only).

**Example request:**
```json
{
  "action": "set",
  "reason": "Pending litigation: Doe v. IH35 Transportation, filed 2026-04-15"
}
```

### 3.17.13 Audit log events emitted

| Event | Severity |
|---|---|
| `documents.evidence_created` | info |
| `documents.evidence_retrieved` | info |
| `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` | critical |
| `documents.evidence_voided` | warning |
| `documents.evidence_purge_pending` | info |
| `documents.evidence_purge_completed` | info |
| `documents.evidence_purge_restored` | warning |
| `documents.legal_hold_set` | critical |
| `documents.legal_hold_released` | critical |
| `documents.terms_changed_acknowledgment_invalidated` | warning |
| `documents.signed_pdf_generated` | info |

### 3.17.14 Permission actions by role

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `evidence.create.driver` | — | — | — | — | — | — | own only | — |
| `evidence.create.office` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ |
| `evidence.retrieve` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | by load | by safety scope | own only | by damage scope |
| `evidence.void` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `evidence.legal_hold.set` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `evidence.legal_hold.release` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `evidence.purge.review` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `evidence.purge.restore` | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |

### 3.17.15 Parity test IDs

- T-021.1: Driver scans BOL via PWA → evidence record created; SHA-256 hash stored; signed PDF generated; chain-of-custody metadata captured
- T-021.2: BOL retrieval → SHA-256 verified; signed URL returned with 15-min expiry
- T-021.3: BOL stored payload tampered (test by altering R2 object directly) → retrieval fails with `E_EVIDENCE_HASH_MISMATCH`; critical incident created; Owner notified
- T-021.4: Retention reaches 5 years on BOL with no legal hold → soft purge; payload moved to quarantine
- T-021.5: 90 days after soft purge → hard purge; payload deleted; metadata retained
- T-021.6: Legal hold set on BOL → record exempt from purge regardless of age
- T-036.4: PWA acknowledgment without scroll-to-bottom → `E_EVIDENCE_SCROLL_INCOMPLETE`
- T-036.5: PWA acknowledgment with scroll complete → evidence record created; deduction unlocked
- T-036.6: Terms changed between presentation and acknowledgment → `E_EVIDENCE_TERMS_CHANGED`; new cycle required
- T-036.7: PDF upload path acknowledgment → evidence record created with `captured_via='pdf_upload'`; same hash + retention rules
- T-068.1: Evidence voided by Owner → void record created; original record annotated but retained
- T-068.2: Evidence voided by Manager → `E_EVIDENCE_VOID_REQUIRES_OWNER`
- T-068.3: Evidence retrieval logged with purpose code → access log entry created
- T-068.4: Driver retrieves another driver's evidence → permission denied
- T-068.5: One-click legal hold for driver-X → all driver-X evidence flagged simultaneously
- T-068.6: Legal-hold release with reason → audit event critical; held=false
- T-068.7: DVIR with no defects → 6-month retention; expires; soft purge
- T-068.8: DVIR with reported defect → 5-year retention applied; not purged at 6 months
- T-068.9: Hire agreement; driver separates; 7 years from separation date applied to retention

---

## 3.18 — CHART-OF-ACCOUNTS GOVERNANCE + POSTING CATALOG

**Maps to:** WF-008, WF-009, WF-010, WF-031, WF-033, WF-034, WF-035, WF-040, WF-046, WF-060, WF-064 (cross-cutting; touches every accounting workflow)

**Source basis:** Option C+ architecture (Part 2.5 — local accounting authoritative + QBO mirror), Cursor scope expansion, latest user requirements (prevent account drift across modules)

### 3.18.1 The drift problem this section prevents

When multiple modules post to the chart of accounts ad-hoc, three failure modes appear:

1. **Account proliferation**: dispatch creates "Fuel Expense" while accounting creates "Fuel - Expense" while fuel module creates "Fuel Costs" — three accounts for the same concept; reports break.
2. **Inconsistent debit/credit routing**: civil fine posts to "Civil Fines Expense" in one place and "Penalties Expense" in another; P&L net-zero invariant per 3.13.3.3 fails.
3. **Account renames break references**: someone renames "Driver Receivable" to "Driver Loans Receivable" and historical postings now reference a non-existent account.

This section MUST prevent all three.

### 3.18.2 Canonical account ownership

**MUST 3.18.2.1** — Every account in `accounting.accounts` MUST have exactly one **owning module** in `account.owning_module` (one of: `accounting`, `dispatch`, `driver_finance`, `maintenance`, `safety`, `fuel`, `banking`, `documents`, `notifications`, `master_data`, `identity`, `catalogs`).

**MUST 3.18.2.2** — Only the owning module's code MAY create new accounts of that type. Other modules MUST resolve to existing accounts via the posting catalog (3.18.5) — they MUST NOT create accounts.

**MUST 3.18.2.3** — The default ownership map (revisable only by Owner via /admin):

| Account family | Owning module |
|---|---|
| Bank accounts, Credit Card accounts, Operating bank accounts | `banking` |
| AP, AR, Sales income, Cost of sales, Tax accounts | `accounting` |
| Driver Receivable, Driver Escrow Liability, Driver Pay accounts | `driver_finance` |
| Fuel Expense, Fuel Tax (IFTA), Fuel Discount Income | `fuel` |
| Insurance Expense (vehicle), Permit Expense, License Plate Expense, Antidoping Expense | `safety` |
| R&M Expense, Tire Expense, Repair Parts COGS | `maintenance` |
| Detention Income, Layover Pay Expense, Border Crossing Income/Expense | `dispatch` |
| Factoring Fees Expense, Factoring Reserve Asset, Factoring Chargeback | `accounting` (with `banking` integration) |

**MUST 3.18.2.4** — Cross-module postings (e.g. dispatch posting an invoice that touches `Sales` (accounting-owned) and `Detention Income` (dispatch-owned)) MUST go through the posting catalog (3.18.5), which validates that each posting line's account is reachable from the calling module.

### 3.18.3 Account-code lifecycle

Every account moves through a defined lifecycle:

```
draft → active → frozen → archived
```

**MUST 3.18.3.1** — **`draft`**: account exists in DB but is NOT available for posting. Created during config/migration. Cannot be QBO-synced. Owner-only state.

**MUST 3.18.3.2** — **`active`**: account is available for posting. Default state for production accounts. QBO-synced.

**MUST 3.18.3.3** — **`frozen`**: account is unavailable for new postings BUT existing postings remain valid. Used when transitioning from one account to another (phase-out).

**MUST 3.18.3.4** — **`archived`**: account is hidden from all UI dropdowns and reports by default (visible only when explicitly requested). Postings remain in the journal. Used after a frozen account has had no new posting attempts for 90+ days.

**MUST 3.18.3.5** — Lifecycle transitions:
- `draft → active`: Owner or Administrator action; QBO sync triggered
- `active → frozen`: Owner action with reason; new postings blocked from this point
- `frozen → archived`: automatic after 90 days of no posting attempts, OR Owner manual
- `frozen → active`: Owner action with reason (un-freeze, e.g. restored after migration)
- `archived → frozen`: Owner action with reason (restore from archive)
- Backward transitions to `draft` are forbidden once an account has had any posting

**MUST 3.18.3.6** — Each lifecycle transition writes audit event `accounting.account_lifecycle_transitioned` (warning severity) with from-state, to-state, reason, user.

### 3.18.4 Versioning, renames, and merges

**MUST 3.18.4.1** — **Account renames**: changing `account.display_name` MUST NOT change the account's `code` (immutable). The display name is what users see; the code is what postings reference. Rename writes audit event `accounting.account_renamed`.

**MUST 3.18.4.2** — **Account-code changes are forbidden after first posting.** If a code change is required (e.g. migration to a new chart structure), the system MUST use the merge process (3.18.4.3) rather than mutating the code.

**MUST 3.18.4.3** — **Account merges** (consolidating account A into account B):
1. Owner initiates merge with reason text
2. System validates: both accounts are `active` or `frozen`; both have same `account_type` (asset/liability/income/expense/equity); both have same currency
3. All postings on account A are kept (the journal entries remain unchanged for audit integrity)
4. Account A's status transitions to `frozen` then `archived`
5. A `accounting.account_merge_record` row is written linking A → B with timestamp, user, reason
6. Reports that aggregate "current chart of accounts" treat merged accounts as one (account A's historical postings still aggregate under A in historical reports; current period uses B)
7. QBO sync: account A's QBO entity is marked Inactive; account B continues; historical QBO postings are NOT migrated (they remain on QBO's account A indefinitely)

**MUST 3.18.4.4** — The merge process is **never a delete**. Account A continues to exist forever; it just stops being available for new postings.

### 3.18.5 The posting catalog

**MUST 3.18.5.1** — Every accounting event in any module MUST resolve to a registered **posting template** in `accounting.posting_templates`. The system MUST NOT permit ad-hoc journal entries from operational flows; manual journal entries via the UI (per Q1.9, WF-060) are the only exception, gated to Owner/Admin/Accountant.

**MUST 3.18.5.2** — A posting template defines:
- `template_code` — e.g. `cash_advance_to_driver`, `civil_fine_paid_company_driver_liable`, `monthly_insurance_allocation`
- `description` — human-readable
- `owning_module` — which module emits this template
- `version` — template version (incremented on schema changes)
- `lines[]` — array of:
  - `role` — semantic role (e.g. `driver_receivable_debit`, `bank_credit`)
  - `account_role` — references a registered account role (3.18.5.3)
  - `debit_or_credit` — fixed; `debit` or `credit`
  - `amount_source` — formula or input parameter (e.g. `params.amount`, `params.amount * 0.85`)
  - `class_source` — formula (e.g. `params.unit_uuid`, `null`)
  - `entity_source` — formula (e.g. `params.driver_vendor_uuid`)
  - `description_template` — string template (e.g. `Cash advance to {driver_name}`)
- `validation_rules` — additional checks (e.g. `params.amount > 0`)
- `effective_from`, `effective_to` — version-effectivity dates

**MUST 3.18.5.3** — **Account roles** are abstract bindings between posting templates and chart-of-accounts. Rather than hard-coding `account_uuid` in templates, templates reference `account_role` (e.g. `bank_operating`, `driver_receivable`, `civil_fines_expense`). The role is resolved to a concrete account at posting time via `accounting.account_role_bindings` (per company per period — supports re-mapping without changing every template).

**MUST 3.18.5.4** — Each module's posting catalog (the templates it owns) MUST be defined in code (TypeScript constant) AND mirrored to `accounting.posting_templates`. The code is the source of truth; the DB row is for audit and for the admin UI's discoverability. Mismatch between code and DB is a deploy-time validation failure.

**MUST 3.18.5.5** — At posting time:
1. Module calls `accounting.post(template_code, params)` 
2. System looks up the template, resolves account roles to concrete accounts, evaluates amount/class/entity sources
3. System validates: debits = credits, all accounts active, all required class assignments present, all validation rules pass
4. System creates `accounting.journal_entries` row + `accounting.journal_entry_lines` rows
5. System queues outbox row for QBO sync per 3.12

**MUST 3.18.5.6** — Direct INSERT into `accounting.journal_entries` is forbidden by RLS policies. Even Owner-level admin tooling MUST go through `accounting.post`. The only escape hatch is the explicit "manual JE" flow (WF-060) which uses a special template `manual_journal_entry` that allows arbitrary line definitions but records `is_manual = true` for reporting and Owner notification per WF-064.

### 3.18.6 Mapping enforcement

**MUST 3.18.6.1** — Every operational flow that needs to post MUST declare its templates upfront. The lint rule (per Part 2 architecture — `enforce-module-boundaries`) MUST extend to `enforce-posting-template-registration`: any call to `accounting.post()` with a template code not registered for the calling module fails the build.

**MUST 3.18.6.2** — Adding a new template requires:
1. Define template in calling module's `posting_templates.ts`
2. Migration to insert template row in DB
3. Define account role bindings if new roles are introduced
4. Test case proving balanced debits/credits across representative inputs
5. Owner approval (via PR review for code, via /admin UI for DB-only changes)

**MUST 3.18.6.3** — Removing or modifying an existing template requires versioning: bump `version` on the template, set `effective_to` on the old version, create new version with `effective_from` of today. Old version's historical postings remain valid; new postings use new version.

### 3.18.7 Migration and backward compatibility

**MUST 3.18.7.1** — When the chart of accounts changes (account renamed, merged, or new accounts added), prior period postings MUST remain valid. The system MUST NOT migrate historical postings to new accounts; history is immutable.

**MUST 3.18.7.2** — **Reports across migration boundaries** MUST handle the historical/current split:
- Reports for periods entirely before a merge: use historical accounts as-is
- Reports for periods entirely after a merge: use new accounts
- Reports spanning a merge: surface a footnote explaining the consolidation; the aggregated number is correct (because account merges keep totals) but the per-account drill-down may need explanation

**MUST 3.18.7.3** — **QBO chart-of-accounts mirroring**: when our local chart changes (new account, rename, merge), the change MUST sync to QBO via the outbox per 3.12.4.8. QBO is the downstream mirror; our chart is the source.

**MUST 3.18.7.4** — **Bidirectional sync rule for accounts (per WF-063)**: if a new account appears in QBO (someone added it directly in QBO), the inbound CDC creates a local row with `owning_module = 'unmapped'` and `lifecycle_state = 'frozen'` (cannot post until reviewed). Owner reviews via `/admin/accounting/account-mapping-review` and either: assigns owning_module + activates, or merges into an existing account.

### 3.18.8 The posting-template catalog (initial set)

The system ships with the following template families. Each is defined fully in the relevant module's spec (Parts 7-13). This is the index:

**Driver Finance templates** (defined in 3.13):
- `cash_advance_to_driver`
- `cash_advance_recovery_via_settlement`
- `civil_fine_paid_company_driver_liable`
- `internal_company_fine`
- `accident_damage_driver_full_fault`
- `accident_damage_driver_deductible_only`
- `equipment_lost_or_damaged_by_driver`
- `antidoping_expense_initial`
- `antidoping_recovery_at_separation`
- `escrow_accumulation_per_settlement`
- `escrow_payout_at_departure`
- `escrow_forfeiture`
- `bad_debt_writeoff` (3.13.5.4 Path 1)
- `send_to_third_party_collections` (3.13.5.4 Path 3)
- `border_crossing_charge_to_customer`
- `border_crossing_deduct_from_driver`
- `border_crossing_pay_to_driver`

**Accounting / AP / AR templates** (defined in Part 7 accounting module):
- `create_bill`
- `pay_bill_check`
- `pay_bill_ach_or_wire`
- `pay_bill_credit_card`
- `create_expense_cash`
- `create_expense_credit_card`
- `create_invoice`
- `receive_customer_payment`
- `bank_transfer_internal`
- `credit_card_payment_from_bank`
- `manual_journal_entry`

**Fuel templates** (defined in Part 7 fuel module):
- `fuel_purchase_company_card`
- `fuel_purchase_driver_pay`
- `fuel_tax_ifta_quarterly`
- `fuel_discount_income`

**Maintenance templates** (defined in Part 7 maintenance module):
- `repair_bill_inhouse_parts_labor`
- `repair_bill_external_vendor`
- `tire_purchase`
- `pm_service_completed`

**Allocation templates** (defined in 3.14):
- `monthly_insurance_allocation`
- `annual_permit_allocation_amortized`
- `ifta_allocation_mileage_weighted`
- `irp_allocation`
- `license_plate_allocation`

**Factoring templates** (defined in Part 7 banking module):
- `factoring_advance_received`
- `factoring_reserve_held`
- `factoring_chargeback`
- `factoring_release`

This index is normative: every template listed MUST exist in the system at v1 launch.

### 3.18.9 Error code taxonomy (Module: accounting / sub: governance)

| Code | Meaning | Recovery |
|---|---|---|
| `E_ACCOUNT_NOT_OWNED_BY_MODULE` | Module attempted to create/modify account it doesn't own | Use posting template instead |
| `E_ACCOUNT_LIFECYCLE_INVALID_TRANSITION` | Lifecycle transition not allowed | Use valid path (3.18.3.5) |
| `E_ACCOUNT_CODE_IMMUTABLE` | Attempt to change account code after first posting | Use merge (3.18.4.3) |
| `E_ACCOUNT_MERGE_TYPE_MISMATCH` | Merge attempted between accounts of different types | Cannot merge across types |
| `E_POSTING_TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND` | Module called `post()` with unregistered template code | Register template |
| `E_POSTING_TEMPLATE_NOT_OWNED_BY_MODULE` | Module called `post()` with template owned by different module | Use the right template or own one |
| `E_POSTING_VALIDATION_FAILED` | Template's validation rules rejected the inputs | Fix inputs |
| `E_POSTING_ROLE_UNRESOLVED` | Account role has no binding | Configure role binding |
| `E_POSTING_DEBITS_CREDITS_UNBALANCED` | Generated postings don't balance | Fix template or amounts |
| `E_POSTING_ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVE` | Template resolved to a frozen/archived account | Update role binding |
| `E_MANUAL_JE_ROLE_FORBIDDEN` | Manual JE attempted by role that lacks `accounting.manual_je.create` permission (i.e. not Owner, Administrator, or Accountant) | Use authorized role; or have Owner/Admin/Accountant perform the action |
| `E_QBO_INBOUND_ACCOUNT_UNMAPPED` | New QBO account synced with no owning_module | Owner reviews via admin |

### 3.18.10 API contracts

#### `POST /api/accounting/post`

Internal endpoint called by modules to post via a template. NOT a public API; this is the seam for cross-module accounting calls.

**Permission:** internal-service-token only; rejected for user JWT.

**Example request:**
```json
{
  "template_code": "cash_advance_to_driver",
  "params": {
    "amount": 500.00,
    "driver_uuid": "01923c8a-...driver...",
    "driver_vendor_uuid": "01923c8a-...vendor...",
    "unit_uuid": "01923c8a-...unit...",
    "bank_account_uuid": "01923c8a-...bank...",
    "memo": "Cash advance for fuel and meals — load 2026-LD-3344"
  },
  "calling_module": "driver_finance",
  "audit_user_id": "01923c8a-...user..."
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "journal_entry_uuid": "01923c8a-...je...",
    "outbox_uuid": "01923c8a-...outbox...",
    "lines": [
      {"role": "driver_receivable_debit", "account_code": "1320", "amount": 500.00, "debit_or_credit": "debit"},
      {"role": "bank_credit", "account_code": "1010", "amount": 500.00, "debit_or_credit": "credit"}
    ]
  }
}
```

**Example error (400, validation):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_POSTING_VALIDATION_FAILED",
    "message": "Template 'cash_advance_to_driver' validation rejected inputs",
    "details": [
      {"rule": "amount > 0", "actual_value": -50.00}
    ]
  }
}
```

**Example error (409, role unresolved):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_POSTING_ROLE_UNRESOLVED",
    "message": "Account role 'driver_receivable' has no binding configured",
    "details": {
      "role": "driver_receivable",
      "calling_module": "driver_finance",
      "fix_url": "/admin/accounting/account-role-bindings"
    }
  }
}
```

#### `POST /api/admin/accounting/accounts/{uuid}/lifecycle`

Lifecycle transition for an account.

**Permission:** `accounting.account.lifecycle` (Owner, Admin only).

**Example request:**
```json
{
  "to_state": "frozen",
  "reason": "Migrating to new structure; freeze pending merge into account 5210"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "account_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000000005a1",
    "code": "5210",
    "display_name": "Fuel Expense - Legacy",
    "from_state": "active",
    "to_state": "frozen",
    "transitioned_at": "2026-05-02T18:14:00Z",
    "transitioned_by_user_id": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000def",
    "reason": "Migrating to new structure; freeze pending merge into account 5210",
    "qbo_sync_outbox_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000fab"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Lifecycle transition request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 10 characters"},
      {"field": "to_state", "reason": "must be one of: draft, active, frozen, archived"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923c91"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (409, invalid transition):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_ACCOUNT_LIFECYCLE_INVALID_TRANSITION",
    "message": "Cannot transition from 'active' to 'draft'; backward transitions forbidden once account has had any posting",
    "details": {
      "current_state": "active",
      "requested_state": "draft",
      "valid_next_states": ["frozen"],
      "first_posting_at": "2024-08-15T09:30:00Z"
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923c91"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.account.lifecycle' requires Owner or Administrator role",
    "details": {
      "caller_role": "manager",
      "required_roles": ["owner", "administrator"]
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923c91"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_ACCOUNT_LIFECYCLE_INVALID_TRANSITION` (3.18.9), `E_VALIDATION` (baseline), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (baseline). On success, emits `accounting.account_lifecycle_transitioned` (warning) per 3.18.11.

---

#### `POST /api/admin/accounting/accounts/merge`

Merge account A into account B.

**Permission:** `accounting.account.merge` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```json
{
  "from_account_uuid": "01923c8a-...accountA...",
  "into_account_uuid": "01923c8a-...accountB...",
  "reason": "Consolidating duplicate fuel expense accounts created during 2024 migration"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "merge_record_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000m111",
    "from_account": {
      "uuid": "01923c8a-...accountA...",
      "code": "5211",
      "display_name": "Fuel Expense - Old",
      "lifecycle_state_after_merge": "frozen",
      "scheduled_archive_at": "2026-08-02T18:14:00Z"
    },
    "into_account": {
      "uuid": "01923c8a-...accountB...",
      "code": "5210",
      "display_name": "Fuel Expense",
      "lifecycle_state": "active"
    },
    "merged_at": "2026-05-02T18:14:00Z",
    "merged_by_user_id": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000def",
    "reason": "Consolidating duplicate fuel expense accounts created during 2024 migration",
    "qbo_sync_outbox_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000fac9",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {
      "fired": true,
      "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000n091"
    }
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Merge request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "from_account_uuid", "reason": "required"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 20 characters for merge operations"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923c92"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (409, type mismatch):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_ACCOUNT_MERGE_TYPE_MISMATCH",
    "message": "Cannot merge accounts of different types",
    "details": {
      "from_account_type": "expense",
      "into_account_type": "asset",
      "constraint": "from and into accounts must share account_type and currency"
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923c92"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.account.merge' requires Owner role",
    "details": {
      "caller_role": "administrator",
      "required_roles": ["owner"]
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923c92"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_ACCOUNT_MERGE_TYPE_MISMATCH` (3.18.9), `E_VALIDATION` (baseline), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (baseline). On success, emits `accounting.account_merged` (critical) per 3.18.11 AND fires WF-064 Owner notification (vendor/class remap is a high-risk action; merge is the same family).

---

#### `GET /api/admin/accounting/posting-templates`

List all registered posting templates.

**Permission:** `accounting.template.read` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant).

**Query parameters:**
- `owning_module` (optional): filter by module
- `effective_at` (optional, ISO date): show only templates effective at given date
- `include_archived` (optional, default false): include retired template versions
- `limit` (default 50, max 200), `offset` (default 0)

**Example request:**
```
GET /api/admin/accounting/posting-templates?owning_module=driver_finance&effective_at=2026-05-02
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "template_code": "cash_advance_to_driver",
      "version": 1,
      "owning_module": "driver_finance",
      "description": "Cash advance to driver-vendor; debits Driver Receivable, credits Bank",
      "effective_from": "2026-01-01",
      "effective_to": null,
      "lines": [
        {"role": "driver_receivable_debit", "account_role": "driver_receivable", "debit_or_credit": "debit", "amount_source": "params.amount"},
        {"role": "bank_credit", "account_role": "bank_operating", "debit_or_credit": "credit", "amount_source": "params.amount"}
      ],
      "validation_rules": ["params.amount > 0", "params.driver_vendor_uuid IS NOT NULL"],
      "wf_ids": ["WF-035", "WF-057"]
    },
    {
      "template_code": "civil_fine_paid_company_driver_liable",
      "version": 1,
      "owning_module": "driver_finance",
      "description": "Civil fine paid by company; driver liability established; P&L net zero",
      "effective_from": "2026-01-01",
      "effective_to": null,
      "lines": [
        {"role": "civil_fines_expense_debit", "account_role": "civil_fines_expense", "debit_or_credit": "debit", "amount_source": "params.amount"},
        {"role": "bank_credit", "account_role": "bank_operating", "debit_or_credit": "credit", "amount_source": "params.amount"},
        {"role": "driver_receivable_debit", "account_role": "driver_receivable", "debit_or_credit": "debit", "amount_source": "params.amount"},
        {"role": "fines_recovery_credit", "account_role": "driver_fines_recovery", "debit_or_credit": "credit", "amount_source": "params.amount"}
      ],
      "validation_rules": ["params.amount > 0", "params.driver_vendor_uuid IS NOT NULL", "params.requires_acknowledgment IS NOT NULL"],
      "wf_ids": ["WF-035", "WF-036"]
    }
  ],
  "total": 17,
  "limit": 50,
  "offset": 0
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Invalid query parameter",
    "details": [
      {"field": "limit", "reason": "must be <= 200"},
      {"field": "effective_at", "reason": "must be ISO date format YYYY-MM-DD"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923c93"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.template.read' requires Owner, Administrator, Manager, or Accountant role",
    "details": {
      "caller_role": "dispatcher",
      "required_roles": ["owner", "administrator", "manager", "accountant"]
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923c93"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION` (baseline), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (baseline). Read-only endpoint; no audit events emitted on read (sampled access logging only per Part 3.7).

### 3.18.11 Audit log events emitted

| Event | Severity |
|---|---|
| `accounting.account_created` | info |
| `accounting.account_lifecycle_transitioned` | warning |
| `accounting.account_renamed` | info |
| `accounting.account_merged` | critical |
| `accounting.posting_template_created` | warning |
| `accounting.posting_template_versioned` | warning |
| `accounting.posting_template_invoked` | info (sampled, not every post) |
| `accounting.posting_role_binding_changed` | warning |
| `accounting.qbo_inbound_account_unmapped` | warning |
| `accounting.manual_journal_entry_created` | warning (also fires WF-064 Owner notification) |

### 3.18.12 Permission actions by role

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `accounting.account.create` (via owning module) | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
| `accounting.account.lifecycle` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
| `accounting.account.rename` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
| `accounting.account.merge` | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `accounting.template.create` | via PR | via PR | — | — |
| `accounting.template.version` | via PR | via PR | — | — |
| `accounting.role_binding.set` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
| `accounting.template.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `accounting.manual_je.create` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ |
| `accounting.qbo_inbound.review` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ |

### 3.18.13 Parity test IDs

- T-069.1: Module attempts to post via unregistered template → `E_POSTING_TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND`
