- T-069.2: Module attempts to post via template owned by different module → `E_POSTING_TEMPLATE_NOT_OWNED_BY_MODULE`
- T-069.3: Module attempts to post; account role has no binding → `E_POSTING_ROLE_UNRESOLVED` with admin URL in details
- T-069.4: Module attempts to post; resolved account is `frozen` → `E_POSTING_ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVE`
- T-069.5: Manual JE via UI by Manager → `E_MANUAL_JE_ROLE_FORBIDDEN` (Manager not in {Owner, Administrator, Accountant})
- T-069.6: Manual JE by Owner → succeeds; `is_manual=true`; WF-064 Owner notification fires
- T-069.7: Manual JE by Accountant → succeeds; WF-064 Owner notification fires
- T-069.8: Account lifecycle `draft → active` → succeeds; QBO sync triggered
- T-069.9: Account lifecycle `active → draft` (backward) → forbidden
- T-069.10: Account code change after first posting → `E_ACCOUNT_CODE_IMMUTABLE`
- T-069.11: Account rename (display_name only) → succeeds; code unchanged; audit event written
- T-069.12: Merge two accounts of same type → succeeds; A→frozen→archived; B continues; merge record written
- T-069.13: Merge accounts of different types → `E_ACCOUNT_MERGE_TYPE_MISMATCH`
- T-069.14: Frozen account 90 days no posting attempts → auto-archived
- T-069.15: New account appears in QBO via webhook → local row created with `owning_module='unmapped'`, lifecycle `frozen`; Owner notification
- T-069.16: Owner reviews unmapped account, assigns owning_module, activates → posting now allowed
- T-070.1: Posting template version 1 used for periods Jan-Mar → historical postings remain on v1
- T-070.2: Template version bumped to v2 in April → April postings use v2; reports show split correctly
- T-070.3: Code constant for posting templates does not match DB rows → deploy-time validation fails build
- T-070.4: Cross-module call `dispatch.post('cash_advance_to_driver')` → fails because template owned by `driver_finance` (T-069.2 reproduction)

## WF-064 EXPANDED ENFORCEMENT MAP

Per Cursor scope expansion, WF-064 covers seven high-risk Manager (and same-tier role) action classes. Each MUST trigger Owner notification with full action context. The following table maps every class to its enforcement point in v3:

| # | High-risk action class | Enforcement section | Notification trigger | Audit event | Channel |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Accounting edits / posting overrides** | 3.18.5 (posting catalog enforces template-only posting); 3.18.5.6 (manual JE escape hatch); Part 7 accounting module spec will detail per-template override rules | Any non-Owner role posts via manual JE template, OR any role attempts a non-template posting via admin override | `accounting.manual_journal_entry_created` (warning, fires WF-064) | Email to Owner |
| 2 | **Settlement overrides** | 3.13.7.4 (hold deduction); 3.13.7.5 (held visible on settlement); 3.13.8.4 (debt acknowledgment); Part 7 driver_finance module spec will detail settlement.lock / settlement.void flows | Manager/Admin/Accountant uses settlement override (void final settlement, lock without all checks, override deduction) | `driver_finance.settlement_voided` (critical, fires WF-064); `driver_finance.settlement_lock_overridden` (warning, fires WF-064) — second event will be added in Part 7 driver_finance module spec | Email + SMS to Owner (settlement_voided is critical per 3.13.15) |
| 3 | **Escrow forfeiture** | 3.13.6 (entire); 3.13.6.5 (blocked-attempt audit) | Owner-authorized forfeiture succeeds OR Manager-attempted forfeiture is blocked | `driver_finance.escrow_forfeited` (critical, fires WF-064); `driver_finance.forfeiture_blocked_by_agreement` (critical, fires WF-064) | Email + SMS to Owner (already critical per 3.13.15) |
| 4 | **Deduction schedule overrides** | 3.13.7.4 (hold deduction with reason); 3.13.7.5 (held visible); Part 7 driver_finance module spec will detail schedule.modify / schedule.void / schedule.requires_ack_override | Manager/Admin/Accountant holds, modifies amount, voids, or overrides the requires_acknowledgment flag on a deduction schedule | `driver_finance.deduction_held` (warning, fires WF-064); `driver_finance.deduction_schedule_modified` (warning, fires WF-064) — second event added in Part 7 | Email to Owner |
| 5 | **Manual journal entries / reclassifications** | 3.18.5.6 (manual JE only via explicit template flow, marked `is_manual=true`); Q1.9 / WF-060 (reclassify-transaction button) | Any successful manual JE OR reclassify-transaction action by any role (Owner/Admin/Accountant) | `accounting.manual_journal_entry_created` (warning, fires WF-064); `accounting.transaction_reclassified` (warning, fires WF-064) — second event added in Part 7 accounting module spec | Email to Owner |
| 6 | **Vendor or class remaps affecting accounting** | 3.18.4.3 (account merge); 3.18.7.4 (QBO inbound unmapped account review); Part 7 master_data module spec will detail vendor.remap, class.remap, customer.remap | Owner-only account merge succeeds OR Owner reassigns owning_module on an unmapped account OR Manager/Admin remaps a vendor's qbo_id or class binding | `accounting.account_merged` (critical, fires WF-064); `accounting.qbo_inbound_account_unmapped` (warning, fires WF-064 on Owner review); `master_data.vendor_remapped` (warning, fires WF-064) — third event added in Part 7 master_data spec | Email to Owner (SMS for account_merged since it's critical) |
| 7 | **Retroactive allocation corrections** | 3.14.6.3 (retro requires Owner); 3.14.13 (`allocation.retro_correction` audit event Owner-only) | Owner-approved retroactive correction posted OR Manager attempts retro and is blocked | `allocation.retro_correction` (warning, fires WF-064); `allocation.retro_correction_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064) — second event added in Part 7 accounting module spec | Email to Owner |

**Enforcement invariants for WF-064:**

**MUST WF-064.1** — Every audit event marked above as "fires WF-064" MUST emit a notification to Owner (and Administrator if Owner is the actor) within 60 seconds of the action committing. The notification MUST include: actor user_id and role, action type, action context (deduction_uuid / settlement_uuid / journal_entry_uuid / etc.), reason text if captured, before/after state where applicable, link to the audit log entry.

**MUST WF-064.2** — A high-risk action MUST NOT commit silently. If the WF-064 notification dispatch fails (Resend outage, etc.), the failure MUST be queued in `outbox.queue` for retry per 3.12 mechanics, and the action's audit event MUST record `wf_064_notification_status = 'pending'` until dispatch succeeds. The action itself is not blocked by notification failure (operations continue), but the notification is durable.

**MUST WF-064.3** — Owner MAY suppress WF-064 notifications for specific action classes via `/admin/notifications/wf-064-preferences` (e.g. quiet manual JE notifications during month-end close when high volume is expected). Suppression has a maximum window of 7 days; expires automatically; suppression itself is logged as a critical audit event.

**MUST WF-064.4** — Sections of v3 marked "added in Part 7 [module] spec" above represent enforcement points that complete in Parts 7-13. The base notification fan-out and audit event family is established in Batch B (3.18); per-module event names and exact API surfaces finalize in Part 7.

**Coverage status table (revised from "narrow"):**

| Action class | Covered in Batch A/B | Completion in Part 7 |
|---|---|---|
| 1. Accounting edits / posting overrides | Foundation (3.18.5, 3.18.5.6) | Per-template override rules in Part 7 accounting |
| 2. Settlement overrides | Foundation (3.13.7-3.13.8) | settlement.lock/void flow specs in Part 7 driver_finance |
| 3. Escrow forfeiture | **Complete** (3.13.6, 3.13.6.5) | n/a |
| 4. Deduction schedule overrides | Foundation (3.13.7) | schedule.modify/void specs in Part 7 driver_finance |
| 5. Manual JE / reclassifications | Foundation (3.18.5.6); Q1.9/WF-060 | reclassify endpoint spec in Part 7 accounting |
| 6. Vendor / class remaps | Foundation (3.18.4.3, 3.18.7.4) | vendor/class remap specs in Part 7 master_data |
| 7. Retroactive allocation corrections | **Complete** (3.14.6.3) | n/a |

Two of seven classes have full WF-064 coverage in Batches A/B; five reach completion in Part 7. The trace matrix CSV (Parts 7-13 deliverable) will close all seven mappings before final Definition of Done sign-off.

# IH35 Master Blueprint v3 — Part 4: Data Model

**Section in v3 hierarchy:** Part 4 of 13. Reads after Parts 1-3 (base + Batch A Rev 1 + Batch B Rev 1). Read before Parts 5 (Integrations) and Part 6 (Design System), both of which reference entities defined here.

**Normative language:** MUST · MUST NOT · SHOULD · SHOULD NOT · MAY (RFC 2119 conventions). Every requirement that affects schema integrity, audit, or cross-module coupling is **MUST** unless explicitly marked **SHOULD**.

**WF coverage in this part:** WF-008, WF-009, WF-010, WF-013, WF-026, WF-031, WF-032, WF-033, WF-039, WF-040, WF-041, WF-047, WF-058, WF-063, WF-064 (and cross-cuts every other WF via the entity-storage layer)

**Test ID continuation:** this part uses T-080.x through T-099.x. Verified non-colliding with Parts 1-3 series (T-001..T-070).

**Architecture decisions referenced:** modular monolith with per-module Postgres schemas (Part 2.4); TypeScript + Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL 16 on Neon (Part 2.6); Option C+ local-authoritative accounting with QBO mirror (Part 2.5); UUID v7 primary keys; RLS-first authorization; outbox pattern for external sync (Part 3.12).

## REVISION 2 — CHANGE LOG (applied 2026-05-02 per Cursor final mini-change pass)

Three additional corrections applied on top of Revision 1, with no other functional changes:

1. **Schema-count consistency restored** — Revision 1 introduced `system.cross_leaf_ref_registry` and referenced an existing `system.schema_version` table, both of which created an implicit 15th schema not declared in the 4.0.1 schema list. Per Cursor's preferred option, all governance/integrity tooling tables moved INTO the existing `audit` schema: `audit.cross_leaf_ref_registry` (was `system.cross_leaf_ref_registry`), `audit.broken_ref_findings` (was `integrity.broken_ref_findings`), `audit.reconcile_cross_leaf_refs` (the cron function name; was `integrity.reconcile_cross_leaf_refs`), `audit.schema_version` (was `system.schema_version`). The `integrity.*` audit-event class names (e.g. `integrity.cross_leaf_ref_set`) remain unchanged — those are event class strings on `audit_events.event_class`, not schema namespaces. The 4.0.1 schema row for `audit` updated to explicitly enumerate the governance tooling tables. New text in 4.0.1.2 makes clear that these tooling tables share the `audit` schema's special RLS policy model.

2. **Transactional debt authority hard-locked (new 4.5.4 subsection)** — Cursor flagged that the denormalized debt cache on `master_data.drivers` could create a 6-hour staleness window between cache and source-of-truth, which is unacceptable for transactional contexts (settlement debt-alert, cash advance, liability acknowledgment, etc.). New section 4.5.4 with 6 MUST clauses establishes: 4.5.4.1 cache is authoritative for reporting/list-view contexts only; 4.5.4.2 enumerates 9 forbidden uses (settlement, cash advance, debt-alert flows, etc.); 4.5.4.3 mandates synchronous `driver_finance.recompute_driver_debt(driver_uuid)` call at transaction time with row locks; 4.5.4.4 forbids any "use_cache" fast path on transactional API endpoints; 4.5.4.5 UI MUST display `computed_at` timestamp and re-fetch if >5s stale; 4.5.4.6 audit event sampling and drift detection escalation. Closes the cache-staleness window completely for high-stakes paths.

3. **Parity tests added (T-085.5 through T-085.9)** — five new tests covering the cache-vs-live authority rules: T-085.5 stale cache differs from live; settlement uses live $1,500 not cached $1,000; T-085.6 cache IS used for reporting context (legitimate use); T-085.7 API endpoint has no fast-path bypass for transactional contexts; T-085.8 UI 5s-staleness lock blocks Confirm until refresh; T-085.9 drift detection triggers critical Owner notification on persistent miss.

No other changes to Part 4 Rev 1. All other sections, schema definitions, RLS policies, migration policy, and previously-defined parity tests are unchanged from the version delivered immediately prior.

---

## REVISION 1 — CHANGE LOG (applied 2026-05-02 per Cursor strict schema-integrity pass)

Four blocker corrections applied to original Part 4, with no other functional changes:

1. **Cross-leaf FK violations removed (4.0.3.3 enforcement)** — original Part 4 SQL DDL contained two FK declarations targeting leaf schemas: `accounting.journal_entries.source_liability_uuid REFERENCES driver_finance.driver_liabilities` and `safety.accidents.related_wo_uuid REFERENCES maintenance.work_orders`. Both REFERENCES clauses removed; columns now `UUID` with no FK and inline comments noting "polymorphic ref to leaf; integrity per 4.0.7". The integrity-without-FK contract (new 4.0.7) provides write-path validation, periodic reconciliation, and repair workflow to compensate for the absence of DB-level FK enforcement.

2. **Views rule consistency (4.11.1.3 enforcement, option A chosen)** — original `views.driver_debt_summary` joined `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` (leaf), violating 4.11.1.3. Fixed via option A: kept the rule strict and rewrote the view. Three new denormalized columns added to `master_data.drivers` (canonical reference table): `total_active_debt`, `pending_ack_liability_count`, `debt_last_recomputed_at`. These are maintained by `driver_finance` triggers via the `driver_finance.recompute_driver_debt(driver_uuid)` service function (per new MUST 4.2.2.7). The view now reads only from `master_data.drivers` — no leaf joins. Reconciliation per 4.0.7 detects drift between cache and source-of-truth.

3. **RLS wording clarified (4.0.5.1 + 4.0.5.4)** — original 4.0.5.1 said "Every table... except `audit.audit_events` and `outbox.queue` MUST have RLS enabled," which was ambiguous (could be read as "those two have no RLS"). Rewritten to make explicit that **every table including audit and outbox has RLS enabled**, with audit/outbox using a special policy model (described in 4.0.5.4) rather than being exempt. The actual security behavior is unchanged from the original intent — audit/outbox have always had RLS-enforced restrictions — but the wording now matches the implementation.

4. **New 4.0.7 "Reference-without-FK integrity contract" subsection added** — codifies how leaf-schema references work without FK enforcement. Specifies: write-path validation rules (MUST go through service function with target verification), 4 new error codes (`E_REF_TARGET_NOT_FOUND`, `E_REF_TARGET_DELETED`, `E_REF_TARGET_INVALID_STATE`, `E_REF_BROKEN_DETECTED_RECONCILIATION`), reconciliation job cadence (`audit.reconcile_cross_leaf_refs` every 6 hours), repair workflow (Owner/Admin manual disposition; 7-day escalation), 6 new audit events (`integrity.*` event class), 10 new parity tests (T-092.1 through T-092.10), and the `audit.cross_leaf_ref_registry` configuration table that enumerates every reference-without-FK column with initial population for v1's 9 cross-leaf references.

No other changes to original Part 4. All other sections, schema definitions, RLS policies, migration policy, and previously-defined parity tests are unchanged from the version delivered immediately prior.

---

## 4.0 — DATA MODEL OVERVIEW

### 4.0.1 The 14 schemas

Per Part 2 architecture, the system uses **one PostgreSQL database** (single Neon instance, branch `production`) with **14 logical schemas**. Each schema corresponds to one module and is the storage boundary for that module's domain.

**MUST 4.0.1.1** — The database MUST contain exactly these 14 schemas:

| Schema | Module | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `identity` | identity | Users, sessions, roles, permissions |
| `master_data` | master_data | Drivers, units (trucks/trailers), customers, vendors, locations, equipment |
| `catalogs` | catalogs | Lookup tables (states, counties, fuel grades, payment terms, classes, items, accounts) |
| `dispatch` | dispatch | Loads, stops, load_legs, load_events, settlements, presettlements |
| `driver_finance` | driver_finance | Driver liabilities, deduction schedules, advances, escrow ledger, settlement_lines |
| `maintenance` | maintenance | Work orders (PM/Repair/Tire/Accident), parts, labor, vendors, intransit_issues |
| `safety` | safety | Accidents, internal_fines, training, drug_tests, antidoping_records, citations |
| `fuel` | fuel | Fuel transactions, fuel cards, recommendations, route_plans, station_prices |
| `accounting` | accounting | Bills, expenses, invoices, customer_payments, bill_payments, journal_entries, posting_templates, accounts |
| `banking` | banking | Bank accounts, bank_transactions, factoring (advances/reserves/chargebacks), CC accounts |
| `documents` | documents | Evidence records, evidence_access_log, signed PDFs metadata |
| `notifications` | notifications | Notification preferences, notification_log, suppression rules |
| `audit` | (shared) | Append-only `audit_events` table; governance tooling (`cross_leaf_ref_registry`, `broken_ref_findings`, `schema_version`); audit indexes |
| `outbox` | (shared) | External-sync queue per Part 3.12 |

**MUST 4.0.1.2** — The two shared schemas (`audit`, `outbox`) MUST be writable from every module but read-restricted per role; they are infrastructure, not module-owned. The `audit` schema additionally houses governance/integrity tooling tables (`cross_leaf_ref_registry` per 4.0.7.7, `broken_ref_findings` per 4.0.7.3, `schema_version` per 4.10.4.1). These tooling tables are NOT separate schemas — they live inside `audit` to keep the schema count at exactly 14 per 4.0.1.1. Their RLS uses the same special policy model as `audit_events` (4.0.5.4): SELECT permitted to Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant; INSERT/UPDATE permitted only via SECURITY DEFINER functions; DELETE forbidden absolutely.

**MUST 4.0.1.3** — A module MUST NOT create tables outside its schema. The schema is the module's data boundary; cross-module reads happen via API, not via cross-schema queries (with the narrow exceptions in 4.0.4).

### 4.0.2 Universal column conventions

**MUST 4.0.2.1** — Every table in every schema MUST include:

```sql
uuid              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
created_by_user_id UUID,                                -- nullable; NULL = system-generated
updated_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
updated_by_user_id UUID,
version           INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
deleted_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,                          -- soft delete; null = active
deleted_by_user_id UUID,
deleted_reason    TEXT
```

**MUST 4.0.2.2** — `version` MUST increment by 1 on every UPDATE (enforced by trigger). This supports optimistic concurrency control: clients send `if-match` header with the version they read; mismatch returns 409.

**MUST 4.0.2.3** — Hard DELETE MUST NOT be used in application code on any business-domain table (audit_events, outbox.queue, evidence_records all have stricter rules per their owning sections). Soft delete sets `deleted_at`; queries filter `deleted_at IS NULL` by default. Hard delete is reserved for purge cron jobs and explicit Owner admin operations.

**MUST 4.0.2.4** — UUID v7 is the universal PRIMARY KEY format. The `gen_uuid_v7()` function is a Postgres extension installed at DB initialization. UUID v7's time-ordering property gives index-locality similar to bigint sequences while remaining globally unique. The DB MUST NOT use bigint identity columns or UUID v4 for new tables.

**MUST 4.0.2.5** — A separate `display_id` column MAY be added to user-visible entities (e.g. `BILL-2026-05-0142`, `WO-2026-04-1781`, `T032`). Display IDs are human-readable, generated by application code, immutable after creation. UUIDs are the FK target; display_ids are for UI only.

### 4.0.3 Cross-module foreign key rules

This is the central anti-coupling rule. Without it, the modular monolith degrades into a tightly-coupled monolith.

**MUST 4.0.3.1** — A foreign key MUST NOT cross schema boundaries except via the **canonical reference table** of the target schema. Each schema designates a small set of tables as "canonical references" — entities that other modules legitimately need to reference.

**MUST 4.0.3.2** — The canonical reference tables (the only allowed cross-schema FK targets):

| Schema | Canonical reference tables |
|---|---|
| `identity` | `users` |
| `master_data` | `drivers`, `units`, `customers`, `vendors`, `locations`, `equipment` |
| `catalogs` | `accounts`, `classes`, `items`, `payment_terms` (these mirror QBO master data) |
| `dispatch` | `loads`, `load_stops`, `settlements` |
| `accounting` | `bills`, `expenses`, `invoices`, `bill_payments`, `customer_payments`, `journal_entries` |
| `documents` | `evidence_records` |
| `audit` | (none — never FK to audit; audit_events FK INTO other schemas, not the reverse) |
| `outbox` | (none — outbox.queue uses `entity_uuid` as a polymorphic reference, not enforced FK) |

**MUST 4.0.3.3** — Schemas without canonical reference tables listed above (`driver_finance`, `maintenance`, `safety`, `fuel`, `banking`, `notifications`) are **leaf schemas**: their tables MAY be referenced from API responses, but other schemas MUST NOT FK INTO them. Driver finance liabilities, work orders, fines, fuel transactions, factoring records, notification logs — none of these are FK targets from outside their schema. Cross-module reference happens via the relevant master_data or accounting canonical entity (e.g. driver_finance.driver_liabilities references master_data.drivers, not the other way around).

**MUST 4.0.3.4** — The `outbox.queue.entity_uuid` polymorphic reference is a **deliberate exception** to FK rules. Outbox rows reference any entity via `(entity_type, entity_uuid)` without enforced FK. This is required because outbox is target-system-agnostic (QBO, Samsara, Relay, etc.) and the entity types vary widely. Integrity is enforced by the drainer at processing time, not by the DB.

**MUST 4.0.3.5** — A lint rule (`enforce-cross-schema-fk`) MUST run in CI and FAIL the build if any migration introduces an FK that violates 4.0.3.1-4.0.3.3. The lint reads all migrations, parses CONSTRAINT clauses, and validates each FK target against the allowed list.

### 4.0.4 Cross-schema read patterns

**MUST 4.0.4.1** — Reads that need data from multiple schemas MUST use one of these patterns:

1. **API composition** — caller hits two endpoints, joins in application code. Default for UI requests.
2. **Read view** — a Postgres VIEW that lives in a designated `views` schema (NOT in any module's schema) and joins across canonical reference tables. Used for reports.
3. **Materialized view** — for expensive aggregations (e.g. `views.driver_debt_summary`). Refreshed by cron.

**MUST 4.0.4.2** — A module MUST NOT directly SELECT from another module's schema in operational code paths. Only the `views` schema or API composition is allowed. Direct cross-schema SELECT is grounds for a CI failure via the `enforce-module-boundaries` lint rule (extended to cover SQL query targets).

**MUST 4.0.4.3** — The `views` schema MUST contain only views; it MUST NOT contain tables. Views in this schema are the seam for cross-module reads and reports.

### 4.0.5 Row-level security (RLS) strategy

**MUST 4.0.5.1** — **Every table in every schema MUST have RLS enabled.** This includes the shared schemas `audit.audit_events` and `outbox.queue` — the original draft's "except audit/outbox" wording was a clarity gap and is corrected here. The two shared schemas have a **special policy model** (described in 4.0.5.4) that differs from the per-module model below, but RLS is still enabled and still enforced. There is no table in the production database with `ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`.

**MUST 4.0.5.2** — RLS policies use the session-set `current_user_id` and `current_user_role` (set by the API layer at the start of every transaction). Policies enforce:
- `SELECT` — based on role + ownership rules per Part 3.5
- `INSERT` — based on role + module-of-call (set in session as `current_calling_module`)
- `UPDATE` — based on role + ownership; updates to `created_by_user_id` and `created_at` are forbidden
- `DELETE` — most modules forbid DELETE entirely; soft-delete via UPDATE `deleted_at` is the only path

**MUST 4.0.5.3** — Drivers have `own only` access to their own records (their `driver_uuid` matches `current_user.linked_driver_uuid`). Driver-self queries MUST filter at the RLS policy, not in the API layer; this is defense-in-depth.

**MUST 4.0.5.4** — The two shared schemas have a **special policy model** (RLS still enabled, but rules differ from per-module schemas):
- `audit.audit_events`: SELECT permitted to Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant per role; INSERT permitted to all module service roles **only via the `audit.append_event()` SECURITY DEFINER function** (direct INSERT blocked by RLS policy `audit_events_direct_insert_denied`); UPDATE and DELETE forbidden absolutely (RLS policies `audit_events_no_update` and `audit_events_no_delete` reject all callers including service roles).
- `outbox.queue`: SELECT permitted to Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant; INSERT permitted to all module service roles **only via the `outbox.enqueue()` SECURITY DEFINER function**; UPDATE permitted only to the drainer service role via policy `outbox_drainer_update_only`; DELETE forbidden.

**MUST 4.0.5.5** — There is NO superuser bypass for RLS in application code. The DB connection used by the application uses a non-superuser role. Direct DB access (e.g. via psql for incident response) requires a separate Owner-only role with audit logging at the DB level.

### 4.0.6 Module ownership map

**MUST 4.0.6.1** — Each schema has exactly one **owning module**. The owning module is responsible for the schema's migrations, RLS policies, and integrity.

**MUST 4.0.6.2** — Code in module M MAY write to schema M directly. Code in any other module N MUST NOT write to schema M; it MUST call M's API or service function.

**MUST 4.0.6.3** — The lint rule `enforce-module-boundaries` MUST verify this at build time by checking import graphs. A module M's source MUST NOT import or invoke schema-specific code from schema N.

### 4.0.7 Reference-without-FK integrity contract

When a column references an entity in a **leaf schema** (per 4.0.3.3), the column MUST be a UUID without a `REFERENCES` constraint. The DB-level FK is intentionally absent because adding it would create cross-leaf coupling. But the absence of the FK does not weaken integrity — it relocates integrity enforcement from the DB to the application layer plus periodic reconciliation.

This subsection defines the contract that every reference-without-FK column MUST satisfy.

**MUST 4.0.7.1 — Write-path validation:**
Every INSERT or UPDATE that sets a reference-without-FK column MUST pass through the owning module's API or service function. The service function MUST:
1. Verify the referenced UUID exists in the target leaf schema (by calling the leaf schema's API/service function or by querying the leaf table directly within the same database transaction).
2. Verify the referenced row is in a valid state for the reference (e.g. `deleted_at IS NULL`, `status NOT IN ('voided','canceled')` where applicable).
3. If validation fails, the calling transaction MUST abort with the appropriate error code (4.0.7.2) before the write commits.

Direct database INSERTs that bypass the service function are blocked by RLS policy `enforce_service_function_only` on every table that has reference-without-FK columns. The RLS policy verifies `current_calling_module IS NOT NULL` and matches the column's owning module.

**MUST 4.0.7.2 — Failure behavior and error codes:**

| Code | Meaning | Recovery |
|---|---|---|
| `E_REF_TARGET_NOT_FOUND` | Reference UUID does not exist in target schema | Caller fixes UUID or removes reference |
| `E_REF_TARGET_DELETED` | Reference UUID exists but `deleted_at IS NOT NULL` | Caller fixes UUID; or operation requires undelete |
| `E_REF_TARGET_INVALID_STATE` | Reference UUID exists but target row's status forbids reference (e.g. voided liability) | Caller fixes UUID or waits for state change |
| `E_REF_BROKEN_DETECTED_RECONCILIATION` | Reconciliation cron found a previously-valid reference now broken | Repair workflow per 4.0.7.4 |

These errors MUST be surfaced via the standard API error envelope (per Definition of Done gate #4) with full context including the column name, the broken UUID, and the target schema/table.

**MUST 4.0.7.3 — Reconciliation job cadence:**
A scheduled job named `audit.reconcile_cross_leaf_refs` runs every 6 hours. The job:
1. Iterates every reference-without-FK column documented in `audit.cross_leaf_ref_registry` (see 4.0.7.7).
2. For each column, JOINs the source table to the target table on the UUID column and identifies rows where the source has a non-NULL UUID but the target row is missing OR `deleted_at IS NOT NULL`.
3. For each broken reference found, writes a row to `audit.broken_ref_findings` with severity (`warning` for soft-deleted target, `critical` for missing target).
4. Critical findings trigger an Owner + Administrator notification per Part 3.6.

**MUST 4.0.7.4 — Repair workflow:**
Broken references are NOT auto-repaired. Each finding requires manual disposition by Owner or Administrator via `/admin/integrity/findings/{uuid}`:
- **Repair** — set the source UUID to NULL (when the reference was optional) or update to a valid UUID (when a replacement target exists). The repair writes audit event `integrity.broken_ref_repaired` (warning).
- **Acknowledge** — mark the finding as known/intentional (rare; e.g. a legacy data state being cleaned up by a migration). Audit event `integrity.broken_ref_acknowledged` (warning).
- **Reopen target** — when the target row was soft-deleted in error, restore it (`deleted_at = NULL`). Audit event `integrity.target_restored_for_ref` (warning).

Findings that remain unresolved >7 days escalate to a critical Owner notification.

**MUST 4.0.7.5 — Audit events for reference-without-FK lifecycle:**

| Event | Severity | Emitted when |
|---|---|---|
| `integrity.cross_leaf_ref_set` | info (sampled, not every write) | Reference column set on INSERT/UPDATE |
| `integrity.broken_ref_detected` | warning or critical | Reconciliation cron finds broken ref |
| `integrity.broken_ref_repaired` | warning | Manual repair completed |
| `integrity.broken_ref_acknowledged` | warning | Manual acknowledgment of finding |
| `integrity.target_restored_for_ref` | warning | Soft-deleted target restored |
| `integrity.ref_target_not_found_at_write` | warning | Write-path validation rejected an INSERT/UPDATE |

**MUST 4.0.7.6 — Parity tests for reference-without-FK integrity:**

| Test ID | Description |
|---|---|
| `T-092.1` | INSERT to `accounting.journal_entries` with `source_liability_uuid` referencing a non-existent UUID → write-path validation rejects with `E_REF_TARGET_NOT_FOUND` |
| `T-092.2` | INSERT with `source_liability_uuid` referencing a soft-deleted liability → rejection with `E_REF_TARGET_DELETED` |
| `T-092.3` | INSERT with `source_liability_uuid` referencing a `voided` liability → rejection with `E_REF_TARGET_INVALID_STATE` |
| `T-092.4` | INSERT to `safety.accidents` with `related_wo_uuid` referencing non-existent WO → write-path rejection |
| `T-092.5` | After valid INSERT, target liability is soft-deleted via UPDATE → reconciliation cron detects broken ref within 6 hours; finding written with severity=warning |
| `T-092.6` | After valid INSERT, target liability is hard-deleted (purge) → reconciliation cron detects with severity=critical |
| `T-092.7` | Owner repairs broken finding via `/admin/integrity/findings/{uuid}` → audit event `integrity.broken_ref_repaired` written; finding marked resolved |
| `T-092.8` | Finding unresolved >7 days → escalation Owner notification fires |
| `T-092.9` | Direct INSERT bypassing service function → RLS policy `enforce_service_function_only` rejects |
| `T-092.10` | `audit.cross_leaf_ref_registry` enumerates every reference-without-FK column in the schema; CI lint validates registry completeness |

**MUST 4.0.7.7 — The cross-leaf reference registry:**
A configuration table `audit.cross_leaf_ref_registry` MUST enumerate every reference-without-FK column. Each row specifies: source schema/table/column, target schema/table, validation function name, on-delete behavior (`restrict`/`set_null`/`cascade_audit`). The reconciliation job and CI lint both read from this registry. Adding a new reference-without-FK column requires adding a row to the registry in the same migration; CI lint fails if a column matching the pattern is added without a registry row.

The registry table lives in the `audit` schema (alongside `audit.audit_events`, `audit.broken_ref_findings`, `audit.schema_version`) because it is governance/integrity tooling, not domain data. This keeps the schema count at the 14 already locked in 4.0.1 — no new schemas are introduced for governance tooling.

**Initial registry population for v1:**

| Source | Target | Validation function | On target delete |
|---|---|---|---|
| `accounting.journal_entries.source_liability_uuid` | `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` | `driver_finance.validate_liability_ref()` | restrict |
| `safety.accidents.related_wo_uuid` | `maintenance.work_orders` | `maintenance.validate_wo_ref()` | restrict |
| `master_data.equipment_log.driver_liability_uuid` | `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` | `driver_finance.validate_liability_ref()` | set_null |
| `dispatch.intransit_issues.converted_to_wo_uuid` | `maintenance.work_orders` | `maintenance.validate_wo_ref()` | set_null |
| `dispatch.loads.driver_bill_uuid` | `accounting.bills` | `accounting.validate_bill_ref()` (canonical, but recorded for completeness) | restrict |
| `driver_finance.driver_liabilities.linked_accident_uuid` | `safety.accidents` | `safety.validate_accident_ref()` | set_null |
| `driver_finance.driver_liabilities.linked_equipment_log_uuid` | `master_data.equipment_log` | `master_data.validate_equipment_log_ref()` | set_null |
| `safety.accidents.related_liability_uuid` | `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` | `driver_finance.validate_liability_ref()` | set_null |
| `safety.internal_fines.related_liability_uuid` | `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` | `driver_finance.validate_liability_ref()` | set_null |

The registry MUST be the single source of truth for all reference-without-FK columns; if a column exists in the schema but not in the registry, CI lint fails the build.

---

## 4.1 — IDENTITY SCHEMA

### 4.1.1 Tables

```sql
-- Users (one row per human + system user)
CREATE TABLE identity.users (
  -- universal columns per 4.0.2.1 (uuid, created_at, ..., deleted_at)
  email             TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  email_normalized  TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,           -- lowercase
  full_name         TEXT NOT NULL,
  display_name      TEXT,
  phone             TEXT,
  role              TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (role IN (
    'owner','administrator','manager','accountant',
    'dispatcher','safety','driver','mechanic','system'
  )),
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active','suspended','deactivated')),
  
  -- Driver linkage (when role='driver')
  linked_driver_uuid UUID,                          -- FK to master_data.drivers (canonical reference)
  
  -- Auth (Lucia Auth backed)
  password_hash     TEXT,                           -- nullable; SSO-only users have null
  totp_secret       TEXT,                           -- nullable; 2FA optional v1, mandatory v1.1
  last_login_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
  last_login_ip     INET,
  failed_login_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  locked_until      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  
  -- Preferences
  timezone          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'America/Chicago',
  locale            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'en-US'
);

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX users_email_normalized_unique ON identity.users (email_normalized) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX users_role_idx ON identity.users (role) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX users_linked_driver_idx ON identity.users (linked_driver_uuid) WHERE linked_driver_uuid IS NOT NULL;

-- Sessions (Lucia-managed)
CREATE TABLE identity.sessions (
  uuid              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  user_uuid         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES identity.users(uuid),
  expires_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  ip_address        INET,
  user_agent        TEXT,
  device_fingerprint TEXT,
  revoked_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
  revoked_reason    TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX sessions_user_idx ON identity.sessions (user_uuid) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX sessions_expires_idx ON identity.sessions (expires_at) WHERE revoked_at IS NULL;

-- Permission grants (sparse overrides on top of role-based defaults)
CREATE TABLE identity.permission_grants (
  -- universal columns
  user_uuid         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES identity.users(uuid),
  permission_action TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- e.g. 'driver_finance.escrow.forfeit'
  granted           BOOLEAN NOT NULL,               -- true=grant, false=explicit revoke
  reason            TEXT NOT NULL,
  expires_at        TIMESTAMPTZ                     -- nullable; permanent if null
);

CREATE INDEX permission_grants_user_idx ON identity.permission_grants (user_uuid) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
```

### 4.1.2 Constraints and rules

**MUST 4.1.2.1** — A user with `role = 'driver'` MUST have `linked_driver_uuid IS NOT NULL`. Enforced via CHECK constraint or trigger.

**MUST 4.1.2.2** — A user with `role != 'driver'` MUST have `linked_driver_uuid IS NULL`. (Office staff are not also drivers in this model; a person who drives AND does office work has two user accounts.)

**MUST 4.1.2.3** — `email_normalized` is generated automatically (lowercase + trim) on INSERT/UPDATE via trigger.

**MUST 4.1.2.4** — Role permissions are defined statically in code (per Part 3.5 role matrices); `permission_grants` is for sparse overrides only (e.g. temporarily granting a Manager `accounting.account.merge` for a one-time migration). Granted permissions MUST have `expires_at` set if granted to a non-default role; permanent grants require Owner approval.

**MUST 4.1.2.5** — Failed login lockout: after 5 failed attempts within 15 minutes, `locked_until` is set to `now() + 30min`. Successful login resets `failed_login_count`.

### 4.1.3 RLS policies

**MUST 4.1.3.1** — Owner and Admin can SELECT all users. Manager can SELECT users in their team scope (TBD in Part 7). Other roles SELECT only their own row. Drivers SELECT their own row only.

**MUST 4.1.3.2** — User UPDATE: own row (limited fields: phone, timezone, locale, password); Owner/Admin can update all fields except role escalations to Owner (which require Owner action).

**MUST 4.1.3.3** — User INSERT: Owner/Admin only. Driver onboarding flow MUST create user row in same transaction as `master_data.drivers` row (3.13.1.2 invariant).

**MUST 4.1.3.4** — User DELETE: forbidden. Use `status = 'deactivated'` instead.

---

## 4.2 — MASTER_DATA SCHEMA

### 4.2.1 Tables

```sql
-- Drivers
CREATE TABLE master_data.drivers (
  -- universal columns
  display_id        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,           -- e.g. 'D-0042'
  full_name         TEXT NOT NULL,
  cdl_number        TEXT,
  cdl_state         TEXT,
  cdl_expires_at    DATE,
  date_of_birth     DATE,
  hire_date         DATE NOT NULL,
  separation_date   DATE,
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active','on_leave','separated','blocked')),
  
  -- Vendor linkage (per 3.13.1.1: every driver MUST have a vendor record)
  vendor_uuid       UUID NOT NULL,                  -- FK to master_data.vendors (within-schema, OK)
  
  -- Escrow config
  escrow_target              NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 1500.00,
  escrow_deduction_per_settlement NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 50.00,
  escrow_balance             NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
  pre_clause_escrow_balance  NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,  -- per 3.13.6.3.D
  post_clause_escrow_balance NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,  -- per 3.13.6.3.D
  
  -- Denormalized debt snapshot columns (maintained by driver_finance triggers per 4.2.2.7;
  -- enables canonical-tables-only view per 4.11.1.3 and the 4.0.7 reference-without-FK contract)
  total_active_debt          NUMERIC(12,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
  pending_ack_liability_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  debt_last_recomputed_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
  
  -- Forfeiture clause status (per 3.13.6.3.A)
  forfeiture_clause_active   BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  forfeiture_clause_acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  forfeiture_clause_evidence_uuid UUID,             -- ref to documents.evidence_records (canonical)
  
  -- Samsara mapping
  samsara_driver_id TEXT UNIQUE,
  
  -- QBO mapping (driver-as-vendor flows through vendor_uuid; drivers themselves not in QBO directly)
  
  -- Phone, address, emergency contact
  phone             TEXT,
  address_line1     TEXT,
  address_line2     TEXT,
  city              TEXT,
  state             TEXT,
  postal_code       TEXT,
  emergency_contact_name TEXT,
  emergency_contact_phone TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX drivers_status_idx ON master_data.drivers (status) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX drivers_separation_idx ON master_data.drivers (separation_date) WHERE separation_date IS NOT NULL;

-- Units (trucks, trailers, company vehicles)
CREATE TABLE master_data.units (
  -- universal columns
  display_id        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,           -- e.g. 'T032', 'TR104'
  unit_class        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (unit_class IN (
    'truck','refrigerated_trailer','dry_van_trailer','flatbed_trailer','company_vehicle'
  )),
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active','retired','sold','out_of_service')),
  
  vin               TEXT UNIQUE,
  make              TEXT,
  model             TEXT,
  year              INT,
  color             TEXT,
  license_plate     TEXT,
  license_state     TEXT,
  license_expires_at DATE,
  
  acquired_date     DATE NOT NULL,
  retired_date      DATE,
  
  -- Samsara
  samsara_vehicle_id TEXT UNIQUE,
  
  -- QBO class linkage (per WF-033)
  qbo_class_uuid    UUID NOT NULL,                  -- FK to catalogs.classes
  
  -- Maintenance baselines
  current_odometer  INT,
  next_pm_due_at_miles INT,
  next_pm_due_at_date  DATE
);

CREATE INDEX units_class_idx ON master_data.units (unit_class) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX units_status_idx ON master_data.units (status) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX units_active_during_period_idx ON master_data.units (acquired_date, retired_date)
  WHERE status = 'active';                          -- supports 3.14 allocation queries

-- Customers (shippers / receivers / brokers)
CREATE TABLE master_data.customers (
  -- universal columns
  display_id        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  legal_name        TEXT NOT NULL,
  display_name      TEXT,
  customer_type     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (customer_type IN ('shipper','receiver','broker','direct')),
  
  -- Contact
  primary_contact_name TEXT,
  primary_email     TEXT,
  primary_phone     TEXT,
  billing_email     TEXT,
  
  -- Billing
  payment_terms_uuid UUID NOT NULL,                 -- FK to catalogs.payment_terms
  factoring_eligible BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true, -- per Q9
  
  -- Detention
  free_time_pickup_minutes   INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 120,    -- per 3.16.4.1
  free_time_delivery_minutes INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 120,
  detention_rate_per_hour    NUMERIC(8,2),
  
  -- QBO
  qbo_customer_id   TEXT UNIQUE,
  qbo_sync_token    TEXT,
  qbo_synced_at     TIMESTAMPTZ
);

CREATE INDEX customers_qbo_idx ON master_data.customers (qbo_customer_id) WHERE qbo_customer_id IS NOT NULL;

-- Vendors (suppliers + drivers-as-vendors)
CREATE TABLE master_data.vendors (
  -- universal columns
  display_id        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  legal_name        TEXT NOT NULL,
  display_name      TEXT,
  vendor_type       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (vendor_type IN (
    'fuel','maintenance','insurance','legal','government',
    'driver','factoring','utility','professional_services','other'
  )),
  
  -- Contact + billing
  primary_email     TEXT,
  primary_phone     TEXT,
  payment_method_default TEXT CHECK (payment_method_default IN ('check','ach','wire','cc','cash')),
  payment_terms_uuid UUID,                          -- FK to catalogs.payment_terms
  
  -- 1099 tracking
  is_1099_eligible  BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  tax_id            TEXT,                           -- encrypted at rest per Part 5
  
  -- Driver linkage (when vendor_type='driver'; per 3.13.1.1)
  driver_uuid       UUID UNIQUE,                    -- FK to master_data.drivers (within-schema)
  
  -- QBO
  qbo_vendor_id     TEXT UNIQUE,
  qbo_sync_token    TEXT,
  qbo_synced_at     TIMESTAMPTZ
);

CREATE INDEX vendors_type_idx ON master_data.vendors (vendor_type) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX vendors_qbo_idx ON master_data.vendors (qbo_vendor_id) WHERE qbo_vendor_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX vendors_driver_uuid_unique ON master_data.vendors (driver_uuid)
  WHERE driver_uuid IS NOT NULL AND deleted_at IS NULL;

-- Locations (customer facilities, ports, border crossings, fuel stops)
CREATE TABLE master_data.locations (
  -- universal columns
  display_id        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  legal_name        TEXT NOT NULL,
  location_type     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (location_type IN (
    'customer_facility','port','border_crossing','fuel_stop','yard','rest_area','office','other'
  )),
  
  -- Address
  address_line1     TEXT,
  address_line2     TEXT,
  city              TEXT NOT NULL,
  state             TEXT NOT NULL,
  postal_code       TEXT,
  country           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'US' CHECK (country IN ('US','MX','CA')),
  
  -- Geocoded coordinates
  latitude          DECIMAL(10,7),
  longitude         DECIMAL(10,7),
  geocoded_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
  geocoded_source   TEXT,                           -- 'google_maps' | 'mapbox' | 'manual'
  
  -- Geofence configuration per 3.16.2
  geofence_radius_meters INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 200,
  arrival_dwell_threshold_seconds   INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 60,
  departure_dwell_threshold_seconds INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 120,
  is_border_crossing BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  
  -- Customer linkage (when location_type='customer_facility')
  customer_uuid     UUID                            -- FK to master_data.customers (within-schema)
);

CREATE INDEX locations_type_idx ON master_data.locations (location_type) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX locations_customer_idx ON master_data.locations (customer_uuid) WHERE customer_uuid IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX locations_border_idx ON master_data.locations (is_border_crossing) WHERE is_border_crossing = true;
-- PostGIS would be ideal but adds operational complexity; v1 uses regular columns

-- Equipment (per WF-026, WF-047 — assignable physical items)
CREATE TABLE master_data.equipment (
  -- universal columns
  display_id        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  equipment_type    TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (equipment_type IN (
    'gps_device','samsara_tablet','locking_jack','straps','tire_chains','toll_tag','fuel_card','other'
  )),
  description       TEXT NOT NULL,
  serial_number     TEXT,
  
  -- Acquisition
  acquired_date     DATE NOT NULL,
  acquired_cost     NUMERIC(10,2),
  
  -- Current assignment
  assigned_to_driver_uuid UUID,                     -- FK to master_data.drivers
  assigned_to_unit_uuid   UUID,                     -- FK to master_data.units
  assigned_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
  assigned_via_evidence_uuid UUID,                  -- FK to documents.evidence_records (signed transfer)
  
  -- Status
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active','assigned','lost','damaged','retired')),
  book_value        NUMERIC(10,2),                  -- per 3.13.3.7
  
  CHECK (
    (assigned_to_driver_uuid IS NULL OR assigned_to_unit_uuid IS NULL) OR
    (assigned_to_driver_uuid IS NOT NULL AND assigned_to_unit_uuid IS NOT NULL)
  )                                                 -- can be assigned to driver, unit, or both, or neither
);

CREATE INDEX equipment_assigned_driver_idx ON master_data.equipment (assigned_to_driver_uuid) WHERE assigned_to_driver_uuid IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX equipment_assigned_unit_idx ON master_data.equipment (assigned_to_unit_uuid) WHERE assigned_to_unit_uuid IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX equipment_status_idx ON master_data.equipment (status);

-- Equipment transfer log (per WF-047 dual confirmation)
CREATE TABLE master_data.equipment_log (
  uuid              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  
  equipment_uuid    UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.equipment(uuid),
  event_type        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (event_type IN ('assigned','transferred','returned','lost','damaged','retired')),
  
  -- From → to
  from_driver_uuid  UUID,
  from_unit_uuid    UUID,
  to_driver_uuid    UUID,
  to_unit_uuid      UUID,
  
  -- Dual confirmation per WF-047
  initiated_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL,
  initiated_at      TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  confirmed_by_user_id UUID,
  confirmed_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  
  -- Evidence chain
  evidence_uuid     UUID,                           -- FK to documents.evidence_records
  
  -- Loss/damage details
  loss_reason       TEXT,
  damage_description TEXT,
  driver_liability_amount NUMERIC(10,2),            -- per 3.13.3.7
  driver_liability_uuid UUID                        -- ref to driver_finance.driver_liabilities (NOT FK; cross-schema-leaf)
);

CREATE INDEX equipment_log_equipment_idx ON master_data.equipment_log (equipment_uuid);
CREATE INDEX equipment_log_pending_confirm_idx ON master_data.equipment_log (confirmed_at) WHERE confirmed_at IS NULL;
```

### 4.2.2 Constraints and rules

**MUST 4.2.2.1** — Per 3.13.1.2: a driver INSERT MUST trigger a vendor INSERT in the same transaction. Enforced by stored procedure `master_data.create_driver_with_vendor()`. Direct INSERT to `drivers` without vendor is forbidden by trigger.

**MUST 4.2.2.2** — A driver-vendor (vendor with `vendor_type = 'driver'`) MUST have `driver_uuid IS NOT NULL` matching exactly one drivers row. Constraint enforced via FK + unique index.

**MUST 4.2.2.3** — Unit's `qbo_class_uuid` MUST resolve to an active class in `catalogs.classes` (per WF-033). Constraint enforced by FK and by RLS check on `catalogs.classes.deleted_at IS NULL`.

**MUST 4.2.2.4** — A unit's `acquired_date` MUST be ≤ `retired_date` when both are set. Constraint via CHECK.

**MUST 4.2.2.5** — Equipment transfer (`equipment_log` row of `event_type = 'transferred'`) MUST have BOTH `initiated_by_user_id` AND `confirmed_by_user_id` populated AND different from each other. The dual-confirmation invariant per WF-047. Enforced by trigger.

**MUST 4.2.2.6** — `forfeiture_clause_active` flips from false→true only via the agreement upload flow (3.13.6.3.C); once true, MAY NOT flip back to false except by Owner action with audit reason.

**MUST 4.2.2.7** — The denormalized columns `total_active_debt`, `pending_ack_liability_count`, `debt_last_recomputed_at`, `escrow_balance`, `pre_clause_escrow_balance`, `post_clause_escrow_balance` on `master_data.drivers` are MAINTAINED by `driver_finance` triggers via the `driver_finance.recompute_driver_debt(driver_uuid)` service function. This function is called whenever a `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` or `driver_finance.escrow_ledger` row is INSERTed or UPDATEd. The denormalization keeps `views.driver_debt_summary` (4.11.1) free of leaf-schema joins per 4.11.1.3. Application code MUST NOT write to these columns directly; writes go through the service function. Direct UPDATE blocked by trigger that rejects the change unless `current_calling_module = 'driver_finance'`. The reconciliation job per 4.0.7.4 detects drift between the cache and the underlying source-of-truth.

### 4.2.3 RLS policies

**MUST 4.2.3.1** — Driver-self access: driver SELECTs only own row from `drivers`; only own assigned `equipment`; cannot SELECT other drivers, units, customers, vendors, or locations.

**MUST 4.2.3.2** — Office roles (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant, Dispatcher, Safety, Mechanic) SELECT all rows in master_data.

**MUST 4.2.3.3** — Driver INSERT/UPDATE: Owner, Admin only (driver onboarding is sensitive). Driver UPDATE of own phone/address/emergency_contact: allowed via dedicated `master_data.driver_update_self_contact` function.

**MUST 4.2.3.4** — Vendor/customer INSERT: Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant. Vendor type='driver' creation only via the driver-creation flow (4.2.2.1).

---

## 4.3 — CATALOGS SCHEMA

### 4.3.1 Tables

These tables mirror QBO master data per Part 2.5 (Option C+). Bidirectional sync per WF-063.

```sql
-- Chart of accounts (per 3.18)
CREATE TABLE catalogs.accounts (
  -- universal columns
  code              TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,           -- e.g. '1010', '5210'; immutable after first posting
  display_name      TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- mutable; renamable
  account_type      TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (account_type IN (
    'bank','accounts_receivable','other_current_asset','fixed_asset','other_asset',
    'accounts_payable','credit_card','other_current_liability','long_term_liability',
    'equity','income','cost_of_goods_sold','expense','other_income','other_expense'
  )),
  
  -- Governance per 3.18.2
  owning_module     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (owning_module IN (
    'identity','master_data','catalogs','dispatch','driver_finance','maintenance','safety',
    'fuel','accounting','banking','documents','notifications','master_data','unmapped'
  )),
  
  -- Lifecycle per 3.18.3
  lifecycle_state   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' CHECK (lifecycle_state IN ('draft','active','frozen','archived')),
  lifecycle_state_changed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  lifecycle_state_reason TEXT,
  
  -- Posting tracking (for code immutability rule per 3.18.4.2)
  first_posting_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,
  
  -- Allocation default per 3.14.2.3
  default_allocation_policy TEXT CHECK (default_allocation_policy IN (
    'single_unit','equal_split_all_active','equal_split_subset','equal_split_class',
    'weighted_by_mileage','weighted_by_revenue'
  )),
  
  -- QBO
  qbo_account_id    TEXT UNIQUE,
  qbo_sync_token    TEXT,
  qbo_synced_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
  qbo_active        BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  
  -- Hierarchy (sub-accounts)
  parent_account_uuid UUID REFERENCES catalogs.accounts(uuid)
);

CREATE INDEX accounts_lifecycle_idx ON catalogs.accounts (lifecycle_state);
CREATE INDEX accounts_owning_module_idx ON catalogs.accounts (owning_module);
CREATE INDEX accounts_qbo_idx ON catalogs.accounts (qbo_account_id) WHERE qbo_account_id IS NOT NULL;

-- Account merge records per 3.18.4.3
CREATE TABLE catalogs.account_merge_records (
  uuid              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  from_account_uuid UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES catalogs.accounts(uuid),
  into_account_uuid UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES catalogs.accounts(uuid),
  merged_by_user_id UUID NOT NULL,
  reason            TEXT NOT NULL,
  CHECK (from_account_uuid <> into_account_uuid)
);

-- Classes (= units in QBO; mirrors master_data.units per WF-033)
CREATE TABLE catalogs.classes (
  -- universal columns
  display_name      TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- usually unit display_id, e.g. 'T032'
  unit_uuid         UUID UNIQUE,                    -- FK to master_data.units (one-to-one when class represents a unit)
  
  -- QBO
  qbo_class_id      TEXT UNIQUE,
  qbo_sync_token    TEXT,
  qbo_synced_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
  qbo_active        BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true
);

-- Items (line items on invoices)
CREATE TABLE catalogs.items (
  -- universal columns
  code              TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  display_name      TEXT NOT NULL,
  description       TEXT,
  item_type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (item_type IN ('service','non_inventory','inventory','category')),
  
  -- Default income account (when item is sold)
  default_income_account_uuid UUID REFERENCES catalogs.accounts(uuid),
  default_unit_price NUMERIC(12,4),
  
  -- QBO
  qbo_item_id       TEXT UNIQUE,
  qbo_sync_token    TEXT,
  qbo_synced_at     TIMESTAMPTZ
);

-- Payment terms (Net 30, etc.)
CREATE TABLE catalogs.payment_terms (
  -- universal columns
  display_name      TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,           -- 'Net 30', 'Due on receipt'
  due_days          INT,
  discount_percent  NUMERIC(5,2),
  discount_days     INT,
  
  -- QBO
  qbo_term_id       TEXT UNIQUE,
  qbo_sync_token    TEXT
);

-- States, counties (for IFTA, IRP, plates)
CREATE TABLE catalogs.us_states (
  code              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,               -- 'TX', 'CA'
  name              TEXT NOT NULL,
  ifta_jurisdiction BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true
);

CREATE TABLE catalogs.us_counties (
  uuid              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  state_code        TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES catalogs.us_states(code),
  county_name       TEXT NOT NULL,
  UNIQUE (state_code, county_name)
);

-- Posting templates (per 3.18.5 — authoritative DB mirror of code-defined templates)
CREATE TABLE catalogs.posting_templates (
  -- universal columns
  template_code     TEXT NOT NULL,
  version           INT NOT NULL,
  owning_module     TEXT NOT NULL,
  description       TEXT NOT NULL,
  effective_from    DATE NOT NULL,
  effective_to      DATE,
  lines_json        JSONB NOT NULL,                 -- the line definitions
  validation_rules_json JSONB NOT NULL,
  wf_ids            TEXT[],                         -- workflows this template implements
  
  UNIQUE (template_code, version)
);

CREATE INDEX posting_templates_effective_idx ON catalogs.posting_templates (template_code, effective_from, effective_to);

-- Account role bindings (per 3.18.5.3)
CREATE TABLE catalogs.account_role_bindings (
  -- universal columns
  role_code         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,           -- e.g. 'driver_receivable', 'bank_operating'
  account_uuid      UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES catalogs.accounts(uuid),
  effective_from    DATE NOT NULL,
  effective_to      DATE,
  bound_by_user_id  UUID NOT NULL,
  reason            TEXT NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX account_role_bindings_role_idx ON catalogs.account_role_bindings (role_code, effective_from);
```

### 4.3.2 Constraints and rules

**MUST 4.3.2.1** — `accounts.code` is immutable after `first_posting_at` is set (per 3.18.4.2). Enforced by trigger that rejects UPDATE of `code` when `first_posting_at IS NOT NULL`.

**MUST 4.3.2.2** — `accounts.first_posting_at` is set automatically by a trigger on first INSERT into `accounting.journal_entry_lines` referencing this account.

**MUST 4.3.2.3** — Lifecycle transitions (`lifecycle_state` column) MUST follow 3.18.3.5 rules. Enforced by trigger validating transitions against the lifecycle DAG.

**MUST 4.3.2.4** — `classes.unit_uuid` MUST be unique (one class per unit). A unit MUST have one class; the class MUST be created in the same transaction as the unit (handled by `master_data.create_unit_with_class()`).

**MUST 4.3.2.5** — `posting_templates` rows MUST be idempotent against code constants. A deploy-time check (per 3.18.5.4) validates that every code-defined template exists as an active row, and no DB row exists without a code definition. Mismatch fails the build.

**MUST 4.3.2.6** — `account_role_bindings`: at any given date, exactly one binding MUST be active per `role_code` (effective range covers the date). Overlap is forbidden; gap is forbidden once a role is in use. Enforced by exclusion constraint or trigger.

### 4.3.3 RLS policies

**MUST 4.3.3.1** — All catalog tables: SELECT permitted to all office roles + driver-self for items/payment_terms only. Drivers MUST NOT SELECT chart of accounts, posting templates, role bindings, or account merge records.

**MUST 4.3.3.2** — Catalog INSERT: per 3.18.2.2, only the owning module of an account can create accounts of types it owns. Enforced via session-set `current_calling_module` checked against `owning_module`.

**MUST 4.3.3.3** — `posting_templates` and `account_role_bindings` UPDATE/INSERT: Owner, Admin only via dedicated admin endpoints.

---

## 4.4 — DISPATCH SCHEMA

### 4.4.1 Tables

```sql
-- Loads (the central operational entity)
CREATE TABLE dispatch.loads (
  -- universal columns
  display_id        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,           -- e.g. 'LD-2026-05-3344'
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft' CHECK (status IN (
    'draft','assigned','dispatched','in_transit','delivered','closed','canceled'
  )),
  
  -- Customer + billing
  customer_uuid     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.customers(uuid),
  load_rate         NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL,
  billing_method    TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (billing_method IN ('per_load','per_mile','per_hour','flat')),
  
  -- Direction (per WF-052 NB/SB)
  direction_class   TEXT CHECK (direction_class IN ('northbound','southbound','round_trip','local')),
  
  -- Assignment
  assigned_unit_uuid    UUID REFERENCES master_data.units(uuid),
  assigned_trailer_uuid UUID REFERENCES master_data.units(uuid),
  assigned_driver_uuid  UUID REFERENCES master_data.drivers(uuid),
  assigned_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
  assigned_by_user_id UUID,
  
  -- Border crossing routing (per 3.13.9 / WF-014)
  has_border_crossing BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  border_routing    TEXT CHECK (border_routing IN ('charge_to_customer','deduct_from_driver','pay_to_driver','pending')),
  border_routing_set_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  border_routing_set_by_user_id UUID,
  
  -- Settlement linkage
  presettlement_uuid UUID,                          -- FK to dispatch.presettlements (within-schema)
  settlement_uuid    UUID,                          -- FK to dispatch.settlements (within-schema)
  
  -- Invoice linkage
  invoice_uuid      UUID,                           -- ref to accounting.invoices (canonical)
  
  -- Driver bill linkage (per WF-039)
  driver_bill_uuid  UUID,                           -- ref to accounting.bills (canonical)
  
  -- Notes
  internal_notes    TEXT,
  customer_reference TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX loads_status_idx ON dispatch.loads (status);
CREATE INDEX loads_assigned_unit_idx ON dispatch.loads (assigned_unit_uuid);
CREATE INDEX loads_assigned_driver_idx ON dispatch.loads (assigned_driver_uuid);
CREATE INDEX loads_customer_idx ON dispatch.loads (customer_uuid);

-- Load stops (pickup, delivery, intermediate)
CREATE TABLE dispatch.load_stops (
  -- universal columns
  load_uuid         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES dispatch.loads(uuid),
  stop_sequence     INT NOT NULL,
  stop_type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (stop_type IN ('pickup','delivery','intermediate','border_crossing','fuel','rest')),
  
  location_uuid     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.locations(uuid),
  
  scheduled_arrival_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  scheduled_departure_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  
  -- State machine per 3.16.1
  state             TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'scheduled' CHECK (state IN (
    'scheduled','en_route','arrived','checked_in','loading','loaded','unloading','unloaded','departed','skipped'
  )),
  
  -- Timestamps for KPIs
  arrived_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
  arrived_at_source TEXT CHECK (arrived_at_source IN ('geofence','driver_pwa','dispatcher_manual')),
  checked_in_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
  loading_started_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  loaded_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
  departed_at       TIMESTAMPTZ,
  departed_at_source TEXT CHECK (departed_at_source IN ('geofence','driver_pwa','dispatcher_manual')),
  
  -- Charges (extra stop fee, etc., per WF-053)
  extra_rate        NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
  
  UNIQUE (load_uuid, stop_sequence)
);

CREATE INDEX load_stops_state_idx ON dispatch.load_stops (state);
CREATE INDEX load_stops_location_idx ON dispatch.load_stops (location_uuid);

-- Stop event log (every state transition)
CREATE TABLE dispatch.load_stop_events (
  uuid              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  
  load_stop_uuid    UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES dispatch.load_stops(uuid),
  from_state        TEXT NOT NULL,
  to_state          TEXT NOT NULL,
  transitioned_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  transitioned_by_source TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (transitioned_by_source IN ('geofence','driver_pwa','dispatcher_manual','correction')),
  transitioned_by_user_id UUID,
  
  -- Geofence correlation (when source=geofence OR pwa+geofence both fired)
  geofence_correlation_json JSONB,
  
  -- Correction details (when this is a backward-correction event)
  correction_reason TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX load_stop_events_stop_idx ON dispatch.load_stop_events (load_stop_uuid, transitioned_at);

-- Load legs (mileage attribution per state for IFTA per 3.14.4.4)
CREATE TABLE dispatch.load_legs (
  -- universal columns
  load_uuid         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES dispatch.loads(uuid),
  leg_sequence      INT NOT NULL,
  unit_uuid         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.units(uuid),
  
  origin_state      TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES catalogs.us_states(code),
  destination_state TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES catalogs.us_states(code),
  
  miles_planned     NUMERIC(8,2),
  miles_actual      NUMERIC(8,2),
  
  started_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ended_at          TIMESTAMPTZ,
  
  UNIQUE (load_uuid, leg_sequence)
);

CREATE INDEX load_legs_unit_period_idx ON dispatch.load_legs (unit_uuid, ended_at)
  WHERE miles_actual IS NOT NULL;                   -- supports 3.14 mileage-weighted allocation

-- Presettlements (per WF-041)
CREATE TABLE dispatch.presettlements (
  -- universal columns
  display_id        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  driver_uuid       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.drivers(uuid),
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open' CHECK (status IN ('open','consolidated_to_settlement','canceled')),
  consolidated_to_settlement_uuid UUID REFERENCES dispatch.settlements(uuid)
);

-- Settlements (per WF-041)
CREATE TABLE dispatch.settlements (
  -- universal columns
  display_id        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  driver_uuid       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.drivers(uuid),
  
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'presettlement' CHECK (status IN ('presettlement','final','locked','voided')),
  
  -- Period
  period_start      DATE NOT NULL,
  period_end        DATE NOT NULL,
  
  -- Totals (computed; cached here for performance)
  total_pay         NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
  total_deductions  NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
  net_payable       NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
  
  -- Acknowledgment per 3.13.8.4
  debt_acknowledged_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  debt_acknowledged_by_user_id UUID,
  
  -- Bill linkage (settlement → driver bill → bill payment)
  driver_bill_uuid  UUID,                           -- ref to accounting.bills
  bill_payment_uuid UUID,                           -- ref to accounting.bill_payments
  
  -- Locking
  locked_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
  locked_by_user_id UUID,
  voided_at         TIMESTAMPTZ,
  voided_by_user_id UUID,
  voided_reason     TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX settlements_driver_status_idx ON dispatch.settlements (driver_uuid, status);
CREATE INDEX settlements_period_idx ON dispatch.settlements (period_start, period_end);

-- In-transit issues (per WF-005, WF-048)
CREATE TABLE dispatch.intransit_issues (
  -- universal columns
  load_uuid         UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES dispatch.loads(uuid),
  reported_by_driver_uuid UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.drivers(uuid),
  
  issue_category    TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (issue_category IN (
    'mechanical','tire','accident','dvir_defect','customer_issue','weather','traffic','other'
  )),
  description       TEXT NOT NULL,
  
  -- Auto-attached telematics context
  samsara_dtc_codes_json JSONB,
  capture_lat       DECIMAL(10,7),
  capture_lng       DECIMAL(10,7),
  
  -- Triage
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'reported' CHECK (status IN ('reported','triaged','converted_to_wo','resolved','dismissed')),
  triaged_at        TIMESTAMPTZ,
  triaged_by_user_id UUID,
  
  -- Conversion to WO per WF-049
  converted_to_wo_uuid UUID,                        -- ref to maintenance.work_orders (NOT FK; leaf)
  converted_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
  
  -- Evidence
  evidence_uuid     UUID                            -- ref to documents.evidence_records
);

CREATE INDEX intransit_issues_load_idx ON dispatch.intransit_issues (load_uuid);
CREATE INDEX intransit_issues_status_idx ON dispatch.intransit_issues (status);
```

### 4.4.2 Constraints and rules

**MUST 4.4.2.1** — A load's `status` MUST advance through valid transitions only: `draft → assigned → dispatched → in_transit → delivered → closed`. Cancellation can occur from any pre-`closed` state. Enforced via trigger.

**MUST 4.4.2.2** — A load with `has_border_crossing = true` MUST have `border_routing` set to a non-pending value before `status` advances to `closed` (per 3.13.9). Enforced via trigger.

**MUST 4.4.2.3** — A settlement of `status = 'final'` MUST have `debt_acknowledged_at IS NOT NULL` per 3.13.8.4. Enforced via trigger.

**MUST 4.4.2.4** — `load_stop_events` is append-only — UPDATE and DELETE forbidden via RLS.

**MUST 4.4.2.5** — A load_stop's state machine transitions per 3.16.1 — backward transitions only via `transitioned_by_source = 'correction'` with required `correction_reason`. Enforced by trigger and by the API layer rejecting non-correction backward calls.

### 4.4.3 RLS policies

**MUST 4.4.3.1** — Drivers SELECT only loads where `assigned_driver_uuid = self`; load_stops, load_stop_events, intransit_issues filtered to their own loads.

**MUST 4.4.3.2** — Office roles (Owner, Admin, Manager, Dispatcher) SELECT all loads. Safety SELECTs loads with associated incidents. Accountant SELECTs loads with billing implications (settled, invoiced).

**MUST 4.4.3.3** — Settlement INSERT: Dispatcher, Accountant, Manager, Admin, Owner. Settlement lock/void: Owner, Admin (lock); Owner, Admin only (void per 3.13.14).

---

## 4.5 — DRIVER_FINANCE SCHEMA

### 4.5.1 Tables

```sql
-- Driver liabilities (fines, accident damage, equipment loss, etc., per 3.13.3)
CREATE TABLE driver_finance.driver_liabilities (
  -- universal columns
  driver_uuid       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.drivers(uuid),
  
  liability_type    TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (liability_type IN (
    'civil_fine','internal_fine','accident_full_fault','accident_deductible',
    'equipment_loss','antidoping_recovery','border_crossing','cash_advance','other'
  )),
  amount_original   NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL,
  current_balance   NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL,         -- decremented by deductions
  
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending_acknowledgment' CHECK (status IN (
    'pending_acknowledgment','active','paid_off','voided','written_off','sent_to_collections'
  )),
  
  -- Source linkage (one is required, depending on type)
  linked_expense_uuid UUID,                         -- ref to accounting.expenses
  linked_bill_uuid    UUID,                         -- ref to accounting.bills
  linked_accident_uuid UUID,                        -- ref to safety.accidents (NOT FK; leaf)
  linked_equipment_log_uuid UUID,                   -- ref to master_data.equipment_log
  
  -- Acknowledgment per 3.13.10
  requires_acknowledgment BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
  acknowledgment_evidence_uuid UUID,                -- FK to documents.evidence_records
  acknowledged_at   TIMESTAMPTZ,
  
  -- Posting linkage
  initial_journal_entry_uuid UUID,                  -- FK to accounting.journal_entries
  
  memo              TEXT NOT NULL,
  
  -- Aging (per 3.13.5.4 for receivables of separated drivers)
  aging_state       TEXT CHECK (aging_state IN ('open','escalated','disposition_required')),
  aging_next_review_at DATE,
  collection_extension_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  
  -- Disposition (per 3.13.5.4.C)
  disposition       TEXT CHECK (disposition IN ('write_off','continue_collections','sent_to_collections')),
  disposition_at    TIMESTAMPTZ,
  disposition_by_user_id UUID,
  disposition_journal_entry_uuid UUID
);

CREATE INDEX driver_liabilities_driver_status_idx ON driver_finance.driver_liabilities (driver_uuid, status) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX driver_liabilities_aging_idx ON driver_finance.driver_liabilities (aging_state, aging_next_review_at) WHERE aging_state IS NOT NULL;

-- Deduction schedules per 3.13.7
CREATE TABLE driver_finance.deduction_schedules (
  -- universal columns
  liability_uuid    UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES driver_finance.driver_liabilities(uuid),
  schedule_type     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (schedule_type IN ('weekly','per_load','custom_quantity')),
  quantity_per_period NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL,
  total_periods     INT,
  start_period      TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- ISO week or date
  current_balance   NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL,
  
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN (
    'pending_acknowledgment','active','held','completed','voided'
  )),
  
  -- Hold per WF-012
  hold_until_period TEXT,
  hold_reason       TEXT,
  held_at           TIMESTAMPTZ,
  held_by_user_id   UUID,
  
  -- Acknowledgment dependency
  requires_acknowledgment BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true
);

CREATE INDEX deduction_schedules_liability_idx ON driver_finance.deduction_schedules (liability_uuid);
CREATE INDEX deduction_schedules_status_idx ON driver_finance.deduction_schedules (status);

-- Settlement lines (per-line deductions on a settlement; per WF-041)
CREATE TABLE driver_finance.settlement_lines (
  -- universal columns
  settlement_uuid   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES dispatch.settlements(uuid),
  line_type         TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (line_type IN (
    'load_pay','per_mile_pay','per_load_pay','layover_pay','border_crossing_pay','extra_stop_pay',
    'deduction','escrow_accumulation','escrow_payout','advance_recovery','other'
  )),
  
  -- For pay lines
  load_uuid         UUID REFERENCES dispatch.loads(uuid),
  
  -- For deduction lines
  deduction_schedule_uuid UUID REFERENCES driver_finance.deduction_schedules(uuid),
  liability_uuid    UUID REFERENCES driver_finance.driver_liabilities(uuid),
  
  amount            NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL,         -- positive=pay, negative=deduction
  description       TEXT NOT NULL,
  
  -- Hold marker (when this line was held per WF-012)
  was_held          BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
  held_reason       TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX settlement_lines_settlement_idx ON driver_finance.settlement_lines (settlement_uuid);

-- Cash advances per 3.13.3.1
CREATE TABLE driver_finance.advances (
  -- universal columns
  driver_uuid       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.drivers(uuid),
  amount            NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL,
  recovered_amount  NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
  status            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active','recovered','partial_recovered','voided')),
  
  -- Linked posting
  bill_payment_uuid UUID,                           -- ref to accounting.bill_payments (advance is paid as bill payment)
  liability_uuid    UUID,                           -- ref to driver_finance.driver_liabilities (within-schema; advance creates a liability for tracking)
  
  -- Reason / context
  reason            TEXT NOT NULL,
  related_load_uuid UUID REFERENCES dispatch.loads(uuid)
);

CREATE INDEX advances_driver_status_idx ON driver_finance.advances (driver_uuid, status);

-- Escrow ledger (sub-ledger over Driver Escrow Liability account per 3.13.4)
CREATE TABLE driver_finance.escrow_ledger (
  uuid              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  
  driver_uuid       UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES master_data.drivers(uuid),
  movement_type     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (movement_type IN (
    'accumulation','payout','forfeiture','antidoping_recovery','correction'
  )),
  
  amount            NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL,         -- positive=add to escrow, negative=remove
  balance_after     NUMERIC(10,2) NOT NULL,
  
  -- Pre/post-clause attribution per 3.13.6.3.D
  clause_attribution TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (clause_attribution IN ('pre_clause','post_clause','n_a')),
  
  -- Linkage
  settlement_uuid   UUID REFERENCES dispatch.settlements(uuid),
  journal_entry_uuid UUID,                          -- FK to accounting.journal_entries
  
  reason            TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX escrow_ledger_driver_idx ON driver_finance.escrow_ledger (driver_uuid, created_at);
```

### 4.5.2 Constraints and rules

**MUST 4.5.2.1** — A liability with `requires_acknowledgment = true` MUST have `acknowledgment_evidence_uuid IS NULL` until the driver acknowledges; status MUST be `pending_acknowledgment` until then. Enforced by trigger.

**MUST 4.5.2.2** — Liability `current_balance` MUST be ≥ 0 unless `status IN ('voided','written_off','sent_to_collections')`. Enforced by CHECK.

**MUST 4.5.2.3** — Settlement_lines on a settlement MUST sum to `settlement.net_payable`. Enforced by trigger that recomputes and validates on every line INSERT/UPDATE.

**MUST 4.5.2.4** — Escrow_ledger entries MUST have monotonic-by-driver `balance_after` matching the rolling sum of `amount` for that driver. Enforced by trigger.

**MUST 4.5.2.5** — A liability transition to `disposition` (`write_off`, `continue_collections`, `sent_to_collections`) MUST be performed only by Owner per 3.13.5.4.D. Enforced by RLS policy on UPDATE.

**MUST 4.5.2.6** — Per 3.13.5.4 Path 2: `collection_extension_count` MAX is 4. The 4th attempt to extend MUST be rejected with `E_RECEIVABLE_EXTENSION_LIMIT_EXCEEDED`. Enforced by trigger.

### 4.5.3 RLS policies

**MUST 4.5.3.1** — Drivers SELECT only own driver_liabilities, deduction_schedules, settlement_lines (via settlement.driver_uuid), advances, escrow_ledger. Drivers cannot SELECT other drivers' financial records.

**MUST 4.5.3.2** — Liability INSERT: Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant, Safety (per 3.13.14).

**MUST 4.5.3.3** — Disposition operations: Owner only.

### 4.5.4 Transactional debt authority (hard rule for cache vs source-of-truth)

The denormalized debt cache on `master_data.drivers` (`total_active_debt`, `pending_ack_liability_count`, `debt_last_recomputed_at`, `escrow_balance`, etc., per 4.2.2.7) exists to keep cross-schema views (per 4.11.1.3) free of leaf joins. The cache is maintained by `driver_finance` triggers and reconciled per 4.0.7. **It is not authoritative for any transactional decision.**

This subsection establishes the hard rule that prevents stale-cache-driven errors in financial workflows.

**MUST 4.5.4.1 — Authority of cached debt columns:**
The denormalized debt columns on `master_data.drivers` are AUTHORITATIVE for **reporting and list-view contexts only**. Specifically, the cache MAY be used by:
- Driver list pages (Drivers section §7) — showing debt totals across drivers in a table
- Reports (§10) — aged AR, debt summaries, weekly digests
- Home page attention list (§1) — high-debt drivers surfaced as routine alerts
- Any read-only screen that does not gate a financial operation on the value

**MUST 4.5.4.2 — Forbidden uses of cached debt columns:**
The denormalized debt columns MUST NOT be used to gate, compute, or display values for any of the following transactional decisions:
- Settlement screen rendering (debt-alert invariant per 3.13.8) — the red banner amount and the acknowledgment-checkbox lock
- Cash advance screen rendering — the debt-before-payable display
- Company-pays-driver-expense screen — the debt context
- PWA driver pay screen rendering
- Settlement lock operation (status `presettlement → final`)
- Settlement payable computation
- Border crossing routing decision when "deduct from driver" is selected
