# === FILE: IH35_MASTER_BLUEPRINT_v3_part_5.md ===
================================================================================

# IH35 Master Blueprint v3 — Part 5: Integrations

**Section in v3 hierarchy:** Part 5 of 13. Reads after Part 4 (Data Model, Rev 2). Read before Part 6 (Design System).

**Normative language:** MUST · MUST NOT · SHOULD · SHOULD NOT · MAY (RFC 2119 conventions). Every requirement that affects external system contracts, secrets management, audit, or financial data routing is **MUST** unless explicitly marked **SHOULD**.

**WF coverage in this part:** WF-016, WF-021, WF-022, WF-031, WF-032, WF-038, WF-043, WF-046, WF-054, WF-058, WF-063, WF-064 (cross-cuts every integration touchpoint)

**Test ID continuation:** this part uses T-100.x through T-119.x. Verified non-colliding with Parts 1-4 series (T-001..T-099).

**Architecture decisions referenced:** Outbox pattern for external sync (Part 3.12); local-write-first invariant (Part 3.12.1); audit-shared schema (Part 4.0.5.4); reference-without-FK integrity contract (Part 4.0.7); transactional debt authority (Part 4.5.4); modular monolith service architecture (Part 2.4).

---

## REVISION 1 — CHANGE LOG (applied 2026-05-02 per Cursor strict pass)

Three minor DoD-consistency corrections applied to original Part 5; no other functional changes:

1. **Twilio status endpoint completeness** — `GET /api/admin/integrations/twilio/status` was missing explicit "Not applicable" annotations for validation and business error elements. Added: `Example validation error response (400): Not applicable — endpoint takes no parameters.` and `Example business error response: Not applicable — read-only status endpoint with no business-rule branches.` All six DoD elements now explicit.

2. **Resend status endpoint completeness** — `GET /api/admin/integrations/resend/status` was missing the Example request block AND the same two "Not applicable" annotations. Added all three. All six DoD elements now explicit.

3. **Samsara webhook DoD-inheritance note** — `POST /api/webhooks/samsara` cross-references Part 3.15.11 (its authoritative spec) but lacked an explicit inheritance statement. Added the line: "All six API DoD elements are inherited from Part 3.15.11; not restated here to avoid divergence." The cross-reference now satisfies strict DoD-completeness reading.

No changes to schema references (still `audit.*` only), live-recompute rules, error code taxonomies, audit events, or parity tests. The 12 endpoints, 32 error codes, 43 audit events, and 59 parity tests from the original Part 5 are unchanged.

---

## 5.0 — INTEGRATIONS OVERVIEW

### 5.0.1 The six v1 integrations

The system MUST integrate with exactly these six external services in v1. Two additional integrations (Plaid, DocuSign) are deferred to Phase 2 per Part 2.5.

| # | Integration | Purpose | Direction | Owning module |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **QuickBooks Online (QBO)** | Source of truth for accounting reports v1; mirror of local accounting per Option C+ | Bidirectional | `accounting` |
| 2 | **Samsara** | Vehicle telematics, HOS, geofence events, DTC codes | Inbound | `dispatch` (HOS/geofence) + `safety` (DTC) |
| 3 | **Relay** | Fuel card transactions; fuel price feeds | Inbound (transactions) + outbound (card config) | `fuel` |
| 4 | **Cloudflare R2** | Object storage for evidence payloads, signed PDFs, equipment photos | Bidirectional (write evidence, read for retrieval) | `documents` |
| 5 | **Twilio** | SMS for critical-severity notifications | Outbound | `notifications` |
| 6 | **Resend** | Transactional email (warning, critical, routine notifications) | Outbound | `notifications` |

### 5.0.2 Universal integration invariants

**MUST 5.0.2.1 — All external sync flows through the outbox pattern (Part 3.12.2).** No integration MAY bypass the outbox for outbound writes. The single drainer worker design is per-target-system (`outbox-drainer-qbo`, `outbox-drainer-samsara`, etc.) but they all read from the same `outbox.queue` table.

**MUST 5.0.2.2 — Local-write-first (Part 3.12.1.1) applies to every outbound integration.** The local DB row is durably committed before any outbound call is queued. Integration failures MUST NOT block local operations.

**MUST 5.0.2.3 — Inbound webhooks MUST verify authenticity** before processing. Each integration's webhook handler validates via:
- HMAC-SHA256 signature for QBO and Samsara
- Bearer token + IP allowlist for Relay
- (R2/Twilio/Resend are outbound-only or response-callback patterns; no inbound webhooks)

Webhook handlers that fail signature verification MUST return HTTP 401 and log the event as `webhook.signature_invalid` (critical / security).

**MUST 5.0.2.4 — Every integration has its own auth credential set** stored in environment variables, NEVER in the database. Per Part 4.0.5 secrets policy + 5.0.4 below.

**MUST 5.0.2.5 — Every integration MUST implement the three-class error model (Part 3.12.3.6):**
- **Transient** (retry with backoff): HTTP 5xx, 429 rate limit, network timeouts
- **Validation** (no retry): HTTP 400, business validation failures
- **Auth** (block all activity for that integration): HTTP 401/403, credential expired

**MUST 5.0.2.6 — The transactional debt authority rule (Part 4.5.4)** carries forward into integrations:
- QBO sync of driver settlement bills MUST use `driver_finance.recompute_driver_debt(driver_uuid)` to compute the bill amount, not the cached `master_data.drivers.total_active_debt`. The local journal entry that produces the bill is the source of truth; QBO is downstream.
- Any integration endpoint that accepts inbound payment data affecting a driver's debt MUST call live recompute before posting the local mirror.

**MUST 5.0.2.7 — Schema reference consistency (Part 4 Rev 2 lock).** Integrations MUST write governance/audit data into `audit.*` schema (`audit.audit_events`, `audit.cross_leaf_ref_registry`, `audit.broken_ref_findings`) — NOT into a `system.*` or `integrity.*` schema. The 14-schema count is fixed.

### 5.0.3 The integration health surface

**MUST 5.0.3.1** — The dashboard top bar (per Section 1 UI spec) MUST display a **per-integration connection status indicator**:
- Green dot — last successful round-trip within the integration's freshness budget
- Gold dot — round-trip succeeded but stale (between budget and outage cutoff)
- Red dot — outage cutoff exceeded OR auth lost

Per-integration freshness budgets:
- QBO: 10 minutes (after this, the "QuickBooks connected" indicator goes gold; 60 minutes → red)
- Samsara: 2 minutes (per 3.15.7.1)
- Relay: 30 minutes (fuel posts are batchy)
- R2: 5 minutes (write-success, read-success polling)
- Twilio / Resend: 15 minutes (delivery callbacks)

**MUST 5.0.3.2** — Clicking the indicator opens `/admin/integrations/{name}` showing diagnostic info: last poll/webhook timestamps, error history (last 50 events), token rotation history, current rate-limit headroom.

**MUST 5.0.3.3** — Outage notifications follow Part 3.6 routing (Owner + Administrator + Manager email; SMS only after 30-minute persistent outage).

### 5.0.4 Secrets and credential management

**MUST 5.0.4.1** — Integration credentials (API tokens, OAuth refresh tokens, HMAC secrets, R2 access keys, Twilio auth tokens, Resend API keys) are stored in:
- **Render environment variables** for the v1 hosting layer
- The variable names follow the pattern `{INTEGRATION}_{PURPOSE}` (e.g. `QBO_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET`, `SAMSARA_API_TOKEN`, `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`)

**MUST 5.0.4.2** — Credentials MUST NOT appear in the database, in logs, in error messages surfaced to users, in audit events, or in any artifact captured by Sentry. Sentry MUST be configured with `beforeSend` filtering that scrubs any field matching the credential pattern regex.

**MUST 5.0.4.3** — Token rotation procedures per integration:
- **QBO**: OAuth refresh token; rotation via Owner-initiated re-auth at `/admin/integrations/qbo/reconnect`. Refresh tokens have ~100-day TTL; the system SHOULD warn Owner 14 days before expiration.
- **Samsara**: API token; rotation via `POST /admin/integrations/samsara/rotate` (24-hour grace period accepts old + new tokens, per Part 3.15.4.4).
- **Relay**: API token; rotation via Owner action; no built-in grace period (set new token before old is revoked).
- **Cloudflare R2**: access key ID + secret; rotation via Owner action; CF supports key versioning so two keys MAY be valid concurrently.
- **Twilio / Resend**: API keys; rotation via Owner action; no grace period required (rotation is instant cutover; in-flight messages handled by retry).

**MUST 5.0.4.4** — Token expiration / revocation events MUST trigger:
- The drainer for that integration halts (per Part 3.12.3.6 auth class)
- Critical notification fires to Owner + Administrator
- The dashboard's per-integration indicator turns red
- Audit event `{integration}.auth_lost` (critical) recorded

**MUST 5.0.4.5** — Sensitive credentials MUST NOT be displayed in plaintext in any admin UI. The `/admin/integrations` page shows credential **status** (last-rotated, expires-at) but never the credential value itself. Owner MAY re-enter or rotate but never read.

### 5.0.5 Module-of-call attribution for integration writes

**MUST 5.0.5.1** — Every outbound integration call MUST be attributed to a calling module via the `outbox.queue.audit_user_id` and a separate `calling_module` field added to outbox.queue (per Part 3.12.2.1 schema; will be added in migration if not present).

**MUST 5.0.5.2** — Audit events for integration calls MUST include the `source_module` field on `audit.audit_events` (per Part 4.9.3 schema). This supports traceability of which module triggered which external call.

---

## 5.1 — QUICKBOOKS ONLINE (QBO) INTEGRATION

**Maps to:** WF-031 (QBO Auto Sync), WF-032 (Offline Continuity), WF-022 (Outage Handling), WF-046 (Transfer + CC), WF-063 (Bidirectional Catalog Sync), WF-008/009/010/040/041 (every accounting workflow with QBO mirror)

**Source basis:** Part 3.12 (the authoritative QBO sync contract), Part 4.6 (accounting schema with qbo_* columns), Option C+ architecture (Part 2.5).

### 5.1.1 Auth flow

**MUST 5.1.1.1** — Authentication is **OAuth 2.0** via QBO's Intuit Developer auth flow. The system uses the `intuit-oauth` Node library or equivalent verified library.

**MUST 5.1.1.2** — Initial connection: Owner visits `/admin/integrations/qbo/connect`, the system redirects to Intuit's authorization endpoint. After Owner approves, Intuit redirects back to `/admin/integrations/qbo/callback?code=...&realmId=...`. The handler exchanges the auth code for an access token + refresh token, stores them in env (NOT DB), records `qbo.auth_established` audit event.

**MUST 5.1.1.3** — Access tokens have ~60-minute TTL; refresh tokens ~100-day TTL. The system MUST refresh access tokens automatically before each batch of API calls (or pre-emptively at the 80% TTL mark).

**MUST 5.1.1.4** — Refresh failure (401 from `/oauth2/v1/tokens/bearer` refresh endpoint) MUST trigger the auth-lost flow per 5.0.4.4. Owner re-auth via the connect flow restores service.

**MUST 5.1.1.5** — Realm ID (the QBO company ID) is stored alongside the tokens. The system supports exactly ONE QBO realm in v1 (single-company). Multi-company QBO is Phase 2.

### 5.1.2 Outbound: the per-transaction-type sync

**Already specified in Part 3.12.4** (8 subsections covering Expense, Bill, Check, Bill Payment other, Invoice, Customer Payment, Journal Entry, Master Data). Part 5 does NOT redefine these contracts — Part 3.12 is authoritative.

**MUST 5.1.2.1** — Part 5 adds the following operational supplement to 3.12.4:

The drainer worker named `outbox-drainer-qbo` reads `outbox.queue` rows where `target_system = 'qbo'` per Part 3.12.3. The worker:
- Polls every 2 seconds in steady state
- Backs off to 30s polling when no rows are pending
- Processes rows in `created_at` order with per-`entity_uuid` serial ordering (Part 3.12.6.2)
- Maintains a self-imposed cap of 200 requests/minute against QBO API to leave headroom under QBO's ~500 req/min throttle

**MUST 5.1.2.2** — Each QBO API call MUST set the request header `User-Agent: IH35-TMS/1.0 (production)` for traceability in QBO's audit logs.

### 5.1.3 Inbound: webhooks + CDC polling

QBO supports two inbound mechanisms; the system uses both:

**MUST 5.1.3.1 — Webhooks:**
QBO Webhooks fire HTTP POSTs to a configured endpoint when entities change in QBO directly. The system endpoint:

`POST /api/webhooks/qbo`

The endpoint MUST:
1. Verify the HMAC-SHA256 signature in `intuit-signature` header against `QBO_WEBHOOK_VERIFIER_TOKEN` (constant per Intuit Developer config)
2. Return HTTP 200 within 3 seconds (QBO's webhook timeout)
3. Enqueue the event into `outbox.queue` with `target_system = 'qbo_inbound'` for asynchronous processing
4. Audit event `qbo.webhook_received` (info, sampled)

**MUST 5.1.3.2 — CDC (Change Data Capture) polling fallback:**
QBO's CDC endpoint (`GET /v3/company/{realmId}/cdc?entities=Bill,Customer,Vendor,Item,Account&changedSince={timestamp}`) is polled every 15 minutes via `qbo-cdc-poller` worker. CDC handles the case where webhooks fail or are missed. Each CDC response is processed identically to webhook events — both feed the same `outbox.queue` inbound queue.

**MUST 5.1.3.3 — Inbound processing rules** (extends Part 3.12.4.8 master-data sync):
- New entity in QBO → local row created with `qbo_*_id` populated; `owning_module = 'unmapped'` for accounts (per Part 3.18.7.4); audit event `qbo.inbound_entity_created` (warning for unmapped accounts).
- Modified entity in QBO → conflict resolution per Part 3.12.4.8 (LastUpdatedTime wins).
- Deleted/Inactive entity in QBO → local row's `qbo_active` field set to false, but the row remains (Part 3.18.4.4 no-delete rule). Audit event `qbo.inbound_entity_deactivated`.

### 5.1.4 API contracts

#### `POST /api/webhooks/qbo`

Inbound QBO webhook handler.

**Authentication:** HMAC-SHA256 in `intuit-signature` header.

**Permission:** Public endpoint with signature verification; no user JWT.

**Example request (from QBO):**
```http
POST /api/webhooks/qbo
intuit-signature: 5HnBtq+oYL9Gxs3F2vU8YwGxQjN5VHm7=
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "eventNotifications": [
    {
      "realmId": "9341452345109876",
      "dataChangeEvent": {
        "entities": [
          {"name": "Vendor", "id": "146", "operation": "Update", "lastUpdated": "2026-05-02T18:14:00Z"},
          {"name": "Bill", "id": "8821", "operation": "Create", "lastUpdated": "2026-05-02T18:14:30Z"}
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{"status": "received", "events_queued": 2}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Webhook payload missing required fields",
    "details": [{"field": "eventNotifications[0].realmId", "reason": "required"}],
    "request_id": "req_01923ca0"
  }
}
```

**Example signature error response (401):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_QBO_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_INVALID",
    "message": "HMAC-SHA256 signature verification failed",
    "request_id": "req_01923ca0"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**

Not applicable — endpoint is unauthenticated by design (signature is the auth).

**Error code linkage:** `E_QBO_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_INVALID` (5.1.7), `E_VALIDATION` (baseline). On success, emits `qbo.webhook_received` per Part 3.12.10.

---

#### `GET /api/admin/integrations/qbo/status`

Get current QBO connection health.

**Permission:** `qbo.status.read` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant).

**Example request:**
```http
GET /api/admin/integrations/qbo/status
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "status": "healthy",
    "realm_id": "9341452345109876",
    "company_name": "IH35 Transportation LLC",
    "auth": {
      "access_token_expires_at": "2026-05-02T19:14:00Z",
      "refresh_token_expires_at": "2026-08-10T18:14:00Z",
      "refresh_warning_days_remaining": 100,
      "last_refreshed_at": "2026-05-02T18:14:00Z"
    },
    "outbound": {
      "last_successful_sync_at": "2026-05-02T18:13:42Z",
      "pending_outbox_count": 3,
      "failed_outbox_count": 0,
      "dead_letter_count": 0,
      "rate_limit_remaining": 487,
      "rate_limit_reset_at": "2026-05-02T18:15:00Z"
    },
    "inbound": {
      "last_webhook_received_at": "2026-05-02T18:11:00Z",
      "last_cdc_poll_at": "2026-05-02T18:00:00Z",
      "next_cdc_poll_at": "2026-05-02T18:15:00Z",
      "unmapped_accounts_pending_review": 0
    },
    "indicator_color": "green"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**

Not applicable — no parameters validated.

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'qbo.status.read' requires Owner, Administrator, Manager, or Accountant role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "dispatcher", "required_roles": ["owner","administrator","manager","accountant"]},
    "request_id": "req_01923ca1"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (baseline). Read-only endpoint; no audit events on read.

---

#### `POST /api/admin/integrations/qbo/reconnect`

Initiate Owner re-authorization flow when token has expired.

**Permission:** `qbo.token.disconnect` (Owner only per Part 3.12.11) AND fires WF-064 (high-risk action: integration reconnect).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/admin/integrations/qbo/reconnect
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{"reason": "Refresh token expired; reconnecting per scheduled maintenance"}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "redirect_url": "https://appcenter.intuit.com/connect/oauth2?client_id=...&scope=com.intuit.quickbooks.accounting&redirect_uri=...&state=...",
    "state_token": "...",
    "expires_at": "2026-05-02T18:24:00Z"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Reconnect reason required",
    "details": [{"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 10 characters"}],
    "request_id": "req_01923ca2"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (409, already connected):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_QBO_ALREADY_CONNECTED",
    "message": "QBO is currently healthy; reconnect not required. Use disconnect first if you intend to switch realms.",
    "details": {"current_status": "healthy", "current_realm_id": "9341452345109876"},
    "request_id": "req_01923ca2"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'qbo.reconnect' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "administrator", "required_roles": ["owner"]},
    "request_id": "req_01923ca2"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_QBO_ALREADY_CONNECTED` (5.1.7), `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (baseline). On success, emits `qbo.reconnect_initiated` (warning, fires WF-064 per Part 3.18 enforcement map).

### 5.1.5 Audit events emitted (extends Part 3.12.10)

| Event | Severity | Fires WF-064? |
|---|---|---|
| `qbo.auth_established` | info | no |
| `qbo.auth_lost` | critical | yes (integration breakage = high-risk) |
| `qbo.auth_restored` | info | yes (success variant of above) |
| `qbo.reconnect_initiated` | warning | yes |
| `qbo.reconnect_blocked` | warning | yes (blocked variant) |
| `qbo.webhook_received` | info (sampled) | no |
| `qbo.cdc_poll_succeeded` | info (sampled) | no |
| `qbo.cdc_poll_failed` | warning | no |
| `qbo.inbound_entity_created` | info | no |
| `qbo.inbound_entity_deactivated` | warning | no |
| `qbo.inbound_account_unmapped` | warning | yes (per Part 3.18.7.4) |
| `qbo.realm_mismatch_detected` | critical | yes |

### 5.1.6 Permission actions by role (extends Part 3.12.11)

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `qbo.status.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `qbo.reconnect` | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `qbo.disconnect` | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `qbo.cdc_poll.force` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `qbo.realm.switch` | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |

### 5.1.7 QBO-specific error code taxonomy (extends Part 3.12.8)

Already defined in Part 3.12.8 (16 codes E_QBO_*). Part 5 adds:

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning | Retry? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `E_QBO_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_INVALID` | 401 | HMAC verification failed on inbound webhook | No (security event logged) |
| `E_QBO_ALREADY_CONNECTED` | 409 | Reconnect attempted while healthy | No (use disconnect first) |
| `E_QBO_REALM_MISMATCH` | 422 | Webhook realmId doesn't match configured realm | No (config error) |
| `E_QBO_REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRED` | 401 | Refresh token >100 days old | No (Owner re-auth required) |

### 5.1.8 Parity test IDs (extends Part 3.12.12)

- T-100.1: QBO webhook with valid HMAC → enqueued to outbox; 200 returned within 3s
- T-100.2: QBO webhook with invalid HMAC → 401 returned with E_QBO_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_INVALID; security audit event written
- T-100.3: QBO CDC poll runs every 15 min; missed webhook events caught by CDC
- T-100.4: QBO CDC creates new vendor → local row with qbo_vendor_id; owning_module='master_data'
- T-100.5: QBO CDC creates new account → local row with owning_module='unmapped' AND lifecycle_state='frozen' (per 3.18.7.4); WF-064 Owner notification fires
- T-100.6: Refresh token reaches 86 days old → Owner warning notification (14 days remaining)
- T-100.7: Refresh token expired → drainer halts; auth_lost critical notification fires; indicator turns red
- T-100.8: Owner re-auth flow → auth_restored fires; drainer resumes; pending outbox replays in order (per 3.12.6)
- T-100.9: QBO realm mismatch (webhook for different realm) → critical audit; webhook ignored
- T-100.10: 100 outbox rows queued during outage; reconnect → all replay successfully; per-entity ordering preserved

---

## 5.2 — SAMSARA INTEGRATION

**Maps to:** WF-038 (Active Driver Set), WF-022 (Outage Handling), WF-043 (Geofence Analytics)

**Source basis:** Part 3.15 (the authoritative Samsara HOS contract), Part 3.16 (geofence model), Part 4.4 (dispatch schema), Part 4.8.1 (DVIR/maintenance).

### 5.2.1 Auth flow

**MUST 5.2.1.1** — Authentication is via **API token** (not OAuth) per Samsara's API design. Token is generated in Samsara admin console by Owner and pasted into Render env as `SAMSARA_API_TOKEN`.

**MUST 5.2.1.2** — Token rotation per Part 3.15.4.4 (24-hour grace period accepting both old and new tokens).

**MUST 5.2.1.3** — Token revocation in Samsara console (e.g. employee with token leaves) requires immediate Owner action: rotate via `/admin/integrations/samsara/rotate`. The 24-hour grace covers in-flight requests; new requests use new token.

### 5.2.2 Inbound: polling + webhooks

**Already specified in Part 3.15** (entire section). Part 5 supplements:

**MUST 5.2.2.1 — Poll endpoints:**
- Vehicle position: `GET /fleet/vehicles/locations` every 30 seconds
- HOS status: `GET /fleet/drivers/hos_logs?driverIds=...` every 60 seconds (only for active driver set per 3.15.5)
- DVIR status: `GET /fleet/drivers/dvirs?driverIds=...&modifiedAfter=...` every 5 minutes
- Vehicle stats (odometer, fuel level): `GET /fleet/vehicles/stats?types=odometerMeters,fuelPercent` every 5 minutes

**MUST 5.2.2.2 — Webhook endpoint:**

`POST /api/webhooks/samsara`

Per Part 3.15.4.2: HMAC verification, async processing, signature secret stored as `SAMSARA_WEBHOOK_SECRET` env. Webhook events processed:
- `DiagnosticTroubleCode` — vehicle DTC fired (engine fault); writes to `safety.dtc_events` (table to be added in Part 7) and triggers in-transit-issue check
- `GeofenceEntry` / `GeofenceExit` — feeds 3.16 stop state machine
- `DriverLogin` / `DriverLogout` — updates `master_data.driver_active_set` and the linked vehicle assignment

### 5.2.3 Outbound: minimal (read-mostly)

Samsara is read-mostly in v1. The system writes only:
- **Geofence configuration**: when a customer location is geocoded (4.2.1 `master_data.locations`), the system POSTs the geofence to Samsara via `POST /fleet/addresses` so Samsara fires geofence events for our locations. Outbox rows with `target_system = 'samsara'` and `operation = 'create_geofence'`.
- **Driver-vehicle assignment**: when dispatcher assigns a driver to a unit on a load (4.4.1 `dispatch.loads.assigned_driver_uuid`), the system PUTs the assignment via `POST /fleet/drivers/{id}/vehicle_assignments`. Outbox row with `operation = 'assign_driver_vehicle'`.

Both outbound operations follow Part 3.12.2 outbox semantics with the same backoff schedule.

### 5.2.4 The cache tier integration (extends Part 3.15.3)

**MUST 5.2.4.1** — Per Part 3.15.3.1, Samsara data flows into a 4-tier cache. Part 5 codifies the persistence schema:

```sql
CREATE TABLE samsara.position_snapshots (
  uuid              UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  created_at        TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  vehicle_uuid      UUID NOT NULL,                  -- polymorphic ref to master_data.units (canonical, FK OK)
  samsara_event_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  polled_at         TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  latitude          DECIMAL(10,7),
  longitude         DECIMAL(10,7),
  speed_mph         NUMERIC(6,2),
  heading_degrees   NUMERIC(5,2),
  odometer_meters   BIGINT,
  fuel_percent      NUMERIC(5,2),
  raw_payload_json  JSONB
);

CREATE INDEX position_snapshots_vehicle_event_idx ON samsara.position_snapshots (vehicle_uuid, samsara_event_at DESC);

CREATE TABLE samsara.hos_snapshots (
  uuid                       UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_uuid_v7(),
  created_at                 TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  driver_uuid                UUID NOT NULL,         -- polymorphic ref to master_data.drivers (canonical, FK OK)
  samsara_event_at           TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  polled_at                  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  duty_status                TEXT NOT NULL,
  driving_hours_remaining    NUMERIC(5,2),
  on_duty_hours_remaining    NUMERIC(5,2),
  cycle_hours_remaining      NUMERIC(5,2),
  time_to_next_break_minutes INT,
  vehicle_uuid               UUID,
  raw_payload_json           JSONB
);

CREATE INDEX hos_snapshots_driver_event_idx ON samsara.hos_snapshots (driver_uuid, samsara_event_at DESC);
```

**MUST 5.2.4.2** — `samsara` is a NEW schema not enumerated in Part 4.0.1's 14-schema list. **This is a deliberate adjustment**: per Part 4 Rev 2's lock on 14 schemas, Part 5 introduces `samsara` as part of `dispatch` schema (i.e. tables live in `dispatch.samsara_*` namespace within the dispatch schema). The DDL above MUST be relocated to `dispatch.position_snapshots` and `dispatch.hos_snapshots`.

Restated DDL (compliant with Part 4 14-schema lock):

```sql
CREATE TABLE dispatch.samsara_position_snapshots (...same as above with renamed table...);
CREATE TABLE dispatch.samsara_hos_snapshots (...same as above with renamed table...);
```

**MUST 5.2.4.3** — Snapshot retention: 5 years per Q17. Hard-purge job runs nightly at 03:00 America/Chicago for snapshots older than `now() - 5 years` AND not under legal hold (per 3.17.6).

### 5.2.5 API contracts

#### `POST /api/webhooks/samsara`

Already specified in Part 3.15.11 with full DoD (request/success/validation/permission/business error + linkage). No re-specification here.

**All six API DoD elements are inherited from Part 3.15.11; not restated here to avoid divergence.**

---

#### `POST /api/admin/integrations/samsara/rotate`

Rotate Samsara API token (per 3.15.4.4 grace period).

**Permission:** `samsara.token.rotate` (Owner, Admin per Part 3.15.13). Fires WF-064.

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/admin/integrations/samsara/rotate
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "new_token": "samsara_at_v1_aBc123...",
  "reason": "Quarterly token rotation per security policy"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "rotated_at": "2026-05-02T18:30:00Z",
    "rotated_by_user_id": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000def",
    "old_token_grace_expires_at": "2026-05-03T18:30:00Z",
    "new_token_active": true,
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {
      "fired": true,
      "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000n123"
    }
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Token rotation request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "new_token", "reason": "must match Samsara token format (samsara_at_v1_*)"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 10 characters"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923ca3"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (409, rotation in cooldown):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_SAMSARA_ROTATION_COOLDOWN",
    "message": "Token rotation already in progress; previous rotation grace period expires at 2026-05-03T18:30:00Z",
    "details": {"previous_rotation_at": "2026-05-02T18:30:00Z", "grace_expires_at": "2026-05-03T18:30:00Z"},
    "request_id": "req_01923ca3"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'samsara.token.rotate' requires Owner or Administrator role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "manager", "required_roles": ["owner","administrator"]},
    "request_id": "req_01923ca3"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_SAMSARA_ROTATION_COOLDOWN` (5.2.7), `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `samsara.token_rotated` (warning, fires WF-064). On rotation block, emits `samsara.token_rotation_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064) — success/blocked symmetry per Cursor's locked rule.

### 5.2.6 Audit events (extends Part 3.15.12)

The base Samsara audit events (`samsara.poll_succeeded`, `samsara.poll_failed`, `samsara.rate_limited`, `samsara.auth_lost`, `samsara.auth_restored`, `samsara.webhook_received`, `samsara.webhook_signature_invalid`, `samsara.driver_signal_lost`, `samsara.outage_started`, `samsara.outage_resolved`, `samsara.degraded_mode_entered`, `samsara.degraded_mode_exited`, `samsara.active_driver_set_recomputed`, `samsara.stale_driver_flagged`) are defined in Part 3.15.12 and carry forward unchanged. `samsara.auth_lost` (critical) and `samsara.auth_restored` (info, success variant) satisfy WF-064 success/blocked symmetry per Cursor's locked rule.

Part 5 adds:

| Event | Severity | Fires WF-064? |
|---|---|---|
| `samsara.token_rotated` | warning | yes |
| `samsara.token_rotation_blocked` | warning | yes (blocked variant) |
| `samsara.geofence_pushed_to_samsara` | info | no |
| `samsara.driver_vehicle_assigned_pushed` | info | no |

### 5.2.7 Samsara-specific error codes (extends Part 3.15.10)

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_SAMSARA_ROTATION_COOLDOWN` | 409 | Rotation attempted while previous rotation's grace period is still active |
| `E_SAMSARA_GEOFENCE_PUSH_FAILED` | varies | Outbound geofence creation rejected by Samsara |

### 5.2.8 Parity test IDs

- T-101.1: Token rotation succeeds → both old and new tokens accepted for 24 hours; audit + WF-064 notification
- T-101.2: Rotation attempted twice within 24h → E_SAMSARA_ROTATION_COOLDOWN (blocked); WF-064 blocked-variant fires
- T-101.3: New customer location geocoded → outbound geofence creation queued in outbox; Samsara confirms; geofence active
- T-101.4: Driver-vehicle assignment changed in dispatch → outbound assignment push to Samsara within 60s
- T-101.5: Position snapshot persisted to dispatch.samsara_position_snapshots; query by vehicle_uuid + date range works
- T-101.6: HOS snapshot retention 5 years; older snapshots hard-purged nightly except legal hold
- T-101.7: Webhook geofence entry event with 60s dwell → triggers stop state machine arrived transition (per 3.16)

---

## 5.3 — RELAY INTEGRATION (Fuel)

**Maps to:** WF-016 (Fuel Card Transaction Posting), WF-054 (Loves Daily Price Excel Upload), WF-055 (Recommendation vs Actual)

**Source basis:** Part 3.14 indirectly (fuel allocation), Part 4.8.3 (fuel schema), Q1.6 (Loves Excel uploads), latest user requirements.

### 5.3.1 Auth flow

**MUST 5.3.1.1** — Authentication is via **API token** (Bearer in `Authorization` header). Token stored as `RELAY_API_TOKEN` env. Owner-managed; rotation is manual cutover (no grace period — set new token before old is revoked).

**MUST 5.3.1.2** — Relay's API has IP allowlisting. The Render service's outbound IPs MUST be added to Relay's allowlist by Owner. IP changes trigger temporary auth failures until allowlist updated.

### 5.3.2 Inbound: transaction polling + Loves price feed upload

**MUST 5.3.2.1 — Transaction polling:**

Worker `relay-transaction-poller` polls `GET /api/v1/transactions?modified_since=...` every 5 minutes. Each new transaction creates:
1. A `fuel.fuel_transactions` row (per Part 4.8.3) with `relay_transaction_id` populated
2. An `accounting.expenses` row via `accounting.post('fuel_purchase_company_card', ...)` template (per Part 3.18.5.1)
3. The expense outbox row syncs to QBO per Part 3.12.4.1
4. Per-unit allocation: the expense is single-unit (the unit assigned to that fuel card at transaction time) — no allocation engine needed

**MUST 5.3.2.2 — Loves price feed (per WF-054):**

Loves provides daily station prices as an Excel file (XLSX) emailed to the company. Owner/Admin uploads via `POST /api/admin/fuel/loves-price-upload` with the Excel attached. The handler:
1. Parses the Excel via SheetJS (rows: station_id, station_address, fuel_grade, price_per_gallon, effective_at)
2. For each row, finds matching `master_data.locations` by address (geocoded)
3. INSERTs `fuel.station_prices` (Part 4.8.3) with `source = 'loves_excel_upload'`
4. Returns count of matched stations + count of unmatched (rows without a corresponding location)
5. Audit event `fuel.loves_price_upload_processed` (info)

**MUST 5.3.2.3 — Compliance tracking (per WF-055):**

When a `fuel.fuel_transactions` row is created (5.3.2.1), the system computes whether the purchase matched the active route plan (Part 4.8.3 `fuel.route_plans`):
- If a route plan exists for the load AND the transaction location matches a recommended stop → `recommendation_compliant = true`
- If a route plan exists but the transaction is at a non-recommended station → `recommendation_compliant = false`
- If no route plan exists for the load → `recommendation_compliant = NULL`

Compliance is informational (for reports per WF-055); no operations are blocked by non-compliance.

### 5.3.3 Outbound: card configuration

Relay supports outbound card configuration writes:
- Suspend a fuel card (e.g. lost): `POST /api/v1/cards/{id}/suspend`
- Reassign a card to a different driver/unit: `PUT /api/v1/cards/{id}/assignment`

These flow through outbox per 3.12 with `target_system = 'relay'` and corresponding operations.

### 5.3.4 API contracts

#### `POST /api/admin/fuel/loves-price-upload`

Upload a Loves daily price Excel file.

**Permission:** `fuel.price_upload` (Owner, Admin, Accountant).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/admin/fuel/loves-price-upload
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=...

------boundary
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="loves_prices_2026-05-02.xlsx"
Content-Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet

<binary XLSX content>
------boundary--
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "upload_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000up01",
    "uploaded_at": "2026-05-02T18:35:00Z",
    "uploaded_by_user_id": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-000000000def",
    "rows_total": 487,
    "rows_matched": 462,
    "rows_unmatched": 25,
    "unmatched_addresses_sample": [
      "Loves Travel Stop #1234, Tomball TX",
      "Loves Travel Stop #5678, Pearsall TX"
    ],
    "effective_at_range": {"earliest": "2026-05-02T00:00:00Z", "latest": "2026-05-02T23:59:00Z"},
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000audi"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Uploaded file invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "file", "reason": "must be .xlsx; received .csv"},
      {"field": "file", "reason": "missing required columns: station_id, fuel_grade"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923ca4"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, processing failure):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_RELAY_PRICE_UPLOAD_PARSE_FAILED",
    "message": "Excel file structure unrecognized",
    "details": {
      "expected_format": "Loves Daily Price Feed v2",
      "detected_format": "unknown",
      "first_row_columns": ["Station", "Address", "Price"]
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923ca4"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'fuel.price_upload' requires Owner, Administrator, or Accountant role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "dispatcher"},
    "request_id": "req_01923ca4"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_RELAY_PRICE_UPLOAD_PARSE_FAILED` (5.3.6), `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `fuel.loves_price_upload_processed` (info).

---

#### `GET /api/admin/integrations/relay/status`

Get Relay connection health.

**Permission:** `relay.status.read` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant).

**Example request:**
```http
GET /api/admin/integrations/relay/status
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "status": "healthy",
    "last_transaction_poll_at": "2026-05-02T18:30:00Z",
    "next_transaction_poll_at": "2026-05-02T18:35:00Z",
    "last_24h_transactions_received": 73,
    "last_24h_transactions_matched_to_unit": 73,
    "ip_allowlist_status": "active",
    "indicator_color": "green"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):** Not applicable (no parameters).

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'relay.status.read' requires office role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "driver"},
    "request_id": "req_01923ca5"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (baseline). Read-only; no audit on read.

### 5.3.5 Audit events

| Event | Severity | Fires WF-064? |
|---|---|---|
| `relay.transaction_polled` | info (sampled) | no |
| `relay.transaction_received` | info | no |
| `relay.transaction_unmatched_to_unit` | warning | no |
| `relay.loves_price_upload_processed` | info | no |
| `relay.loves_price_upload_failed` | warning | no |
| `relay.card_suspended_pushed` | warning | yes (high-risk: financial control) |
| `relay.card_assignment_changed_pushed` | info | no |
| `relay.auth_lost` | critical | yes |
| `relay.auth_restored` | info | yes (success variant) |

### 5.3.6 Relay-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_RELAY_AUTH_REQUIRED` | 401 | Token expired/revoked |
| `E_RELAY_RATE_LIMITED` | 429 | Throttle |
| `E_RELAY_UNAVAILABLE` | 5xx | Service down |
| `E_RELAY_IP_NOT_ALLOWLISTED` | 403 | Outbound IP not on Relay allowlist |
| `E_RELAY_PRICE_UPLOAD_PARSE_FAILED` | 422 | Excel structure unrecognized |
| `E_RELAY_TRANSACTION_UNMATCHED` | n/a | Transaction received but card→unit mapping missing (warning, not blocking) |
| `E_RELAY_CARD_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Outbound op references unknown card_id |

### 5.3.7 Permission actions

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `relay.status.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `fuel.price_upload` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `relay.token.rotate` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `relay.card.suspend` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — |
| `relay.card.assign` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — |

### 5.3.8 Parity test IDs

- T-102.1: Relay transaction poll → 73 transactions received → all create fuel_transactions + expenses; QBO sync queued
- T-102.2: Transaction with unmatched card_id → fuel_transaction created with NULL unit_uuid; warning audit event
- T-102.3: Loves Excel upload with valid format → 462/487 rows matched; unmatched addresses listed in response
- T-102.4: Loves Excel upload with wrong column names → E_RELAY_PRICE_UPLOAD_PARSE_FAILED
- T-102.5: Card suspend pushed to Relay → outbox processed; Relay confirms suspension; WF-064 Owner notification fires (high-risk)
- T-102.6: IP allowlist failure (Render IP changed) → E_RELAY_IP_NOT_ALLOWLISTED; alerts Owner
- T-102.7: Compliance tracking — recommended stops match actual → recommendation_compliant=true; non-recommended stop → false
- T-102.8: No route plan for load → recommendation_compliant=NULL (informational only)

## 5.4 — CLOUDFLARE R2 INTEGRATION

**Maps to:** WF-021 (Driver Document Capture), WF-058 (Equipment Damage Photos), WF-036 (Driver Terms Acceptance)

**Source basis:** Part 3.17 (entire Signature/Evidence Chain-of-Custody section is authoritative for R2 layout and behavior), Part 4.9.1 (documents schema).

### 5.4.1 Auth flow

**MUST 5.4.1.1** — R2 uses S3-compatible API with access key + secret. Stored as `R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` and `R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` env. Endpoint URL: `https://{account_id}.r2.cloudflarestorage.com`.

**MUST 5.4.1.2** — The system supports concurrent valid keys per CF R2's support for multiple API tokens. Rotation: generate new key in CF dashboard, set new env vars on Render, deploy, verify, then revoke old key in CF.

### 5.4.2 Bucket layout (extends Part 3.17.10)

Per Part 3.17.10.1, three buckets:

| Bucket | Purpose | Lifecycle | Object lock |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ih35-evidence-active` | All active evidence (signed PDFs, signature images, BOLs, damage photos, DVIRs) | Versioning enabled; 30-day version retention | No |
| `ih35-evidence-quarantine` | Soft-purged evidence in 90-day holding | 90 days after move-in → hard delete | No |
| `ih35-evidence-legal-hold` | Evidence under legal hold | Indefinite | Yes (object lock prevents deletion) |

**MUST 5.4.2.1** — Object naming pattern (per Part 3.17.10.2):

`{evidence_type}/{driver_uuid_or_load_uuid}/{evidence_uuid}.{ext}`

Example: `liability_acknowledgment/01923c8a.../01923c8a....pdf`

**MUST 5.4.2.2** — Per-bucket access policy:
- `ih35-evidence-active`: read+write from application service role; no public access
- `ih35-evidence-quarantine`: read+write from application; no public access; lifecycle rule auto-expires after 90 days
- `ih35-evidence-legal-hold`: write-once-read-many via R2 object lock; deletion blocked at the platform level

### 5.4.3 Upload flow

**MUST 5.4.3.1** — Application uploads via S3-compatible PUT with computed SHA-256 in metadata:

```typescript
await r2Client.putObject({
  Bucket: 'ih35-evidence-active',
  Key: `${evidenceType}/${entityUuid}/${evidenceUuid}.${ext}`,
  Body: payloadBuffer,
  ContentType: mimeType,
  ChecksumSHA256: payloadHashBase64,
  Metadata: {
    'evidence-uuid': evidenceUuid,
    'driver-uuid': driverUuid,
    'captured-at': capturedAtIso,
    'captured-via': capturedVia,
  },
});
```

**MUST 5.4.3.2** — R2 verifies the SHA-256 on receipt. Mismatch returns 400 from R2; the application MUST treat this as `E_EVIDENCE_HASH_MISMATCH` and not write the documents.evidence_records row (transaction rolls back).

### 5.4.4 Download flow (signed URL)

**MUST 5.4.4.1** — Per Part 3.17.7.2, retrieval is via short-lived signed URLs (15-minute expiry). The application generates the URL via:

```typescript
const signedUrl = await getSignedUrl(r2Client, new GetObjectCommand({
  Bucket: bucket,
  Key: key,
}), { expiresIn: 900 });  // 15 minutes
```

**MUST 5.4.4.2** — Before returning the signed URL, the application MUST verify the stored payload hash against the live R2 ETag/checksum (per Part 3.17.7.1). Mismatch → block retrieval, fire critical incident.

### 5.4.5 Lifecycle and purge

**MUST 5.4.5.1 — Soft purge (Part 3.17.5.1):**
Move the object from `ih35-evidence-active` to `ih35-evidence-quarantine` via S3 CopyObject + DeleteObject. The R2 lifecycle rule on quarantine bucket auto-deletes after 90 days.

**MUST 5.4.5.2 — Hard purge:**
After 90 days in quarantine, R2 lifecycle rule automatically deletes. The application's purge cron updates `documents.evidence_records.payload_storage_url = NULL` to mark the metadata-only state (Part 3.17.5.1).

**MUST 5.4.5.3 — Legal hold lifecycle:**
When `documents.evidence_records.legal_hold = true` is set (per Part 3.17.6), the application MOVES the object from `ih35-evidence-active` to `ih35-evidence-legal-hold` (which has object lock enabled). The R2 object lock prevents deletion regardless of any subsequent application logic — defense in depth against accidental purge.

### 5.4.6 API contracts

R2 is invoked internally by the `documents` module; no public-facing R2-specific endpoints. The relevant public endpoints are already specified in Part 3.17.12:
- `POST /api/driver-finance/acknowledgments` (Part 3.17.12)
- `GET /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/retrieve` (Part 3.17.12)
- `POST /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/legal-hold` (Part 3.17.12)

Part 5 adds one admin endpoint:

#### `GET /api/admin/integrations/r2/status`

Get R2 connection health.

**Permission:** `r2.status.read` (Owner, Admin).

**Example request:**
```http
GET /api/admin/integrations/r2/status
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "status": "healthy",
    "buckets": {
      "ih35-evidence-active": {"object_count": 14782, "total_bytes": 8847362190, "last_write_at": "2026-05-02T18:31:00Z"},
      "ih35-evidence-quarantine": {"object_count": 312, "total_bytes": 187293041, "oldest_object_age_days": 87},
      "ih35-evidence-legal-hold": {"object_count": 47, "total_bytes": 22184523, "object_lock_active": true}
    },
    "last_round_trip_at": "2026-05-02T18:33:00Z",
    "indicator_color": "green"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):** Not applicable.

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'r2.status.read' requires Owner or Administrator role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "manager"},
    "request_id": "req_01923ca6"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (baseline). Read-only; no audit on read.

### 5.4.7 Audit events (extends Part 3.17.13)

Already specified in Part 3.17.13 (11 events `documents.*`). Part 5 adds R2-infrastructure events:

| Event | Severity | Fires WF-064? |
|---|---|---|
| `r2.bucket_write_failed` | warning | no |
| `r2.bucket_read_failed` | warning | no |
| `r2.lifecycle_rule_purged` | info (sampled) | no |
| `r2.legal_hold_object_lock_set` | info | no |
| `r2.auth_lost` | critical | yes |
| `r2.auth_restored` | info | yes (success variant) |

### 5.4.8 R2-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_R2_AUTH_REQUIRED` | 401/403 | Access key invalid/expired |
| `E_R2_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Bucket missing (config error) |
| `E_R2_OBJECT_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Object key missing |
| `E_R2_CHECKSUM_MISMATCH` | 400 | SHA-256 mismatch on PUT |
| `E_R2_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` | 507 | Storage quota hit |
| `E_R2_RATE_LIMITED` | 429 | API throttle |
| `E_R2_OBJECT_LOCKED` | 423 | Delete attempt on legal-hold object |

### 5.4.9 Permission actions

R2 access is mediated entirely through the documents module (Part 3.17.14 permissions apply). The R2-status endpoint adds one entry:

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Other |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `r2.status.read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `r2.token.rotate` | ✓ | — | — | — | — |

### 5.4.10 Parity test IDs

- T-103.1: Evidence upload PUT with correct SHA-256 → R2 accepts; documents.evidence_records row written
- T-103.2: PUT with wrong SHA-256 → R2 rejects with 400; transaction rolls back; no evidence row created
- T-103.3: Retrieval signed URL → 15-min expiry; ETag matches stored hash; download succeeds
- T-103.4: Retrieval after R2 object swapped (tamper simulation) → ETag mismatch → E_EVIDENCE_HASH_MISMATCH; critical audit (per Part 3.17.7.1)
- T-103.5: Soft-purge moves object to quarantine bucket; metadata preserved
- T-103.6: 90 days in quarantine → R2 lifecycle rule auto-deletes; evidence_records.payload_storage_url set NULL
- T-103.7: Legal hold set → object moved to legal-hold bucket with object lock; deletion attempt blocked at platform level
- T-103.8: R2 access key revoked → next operation fails with E_R2_AUTH_REQUIRED; critical audit; Owner notified

---

## 5.5 — TWILIO INTEGRATION (SMS)

**Maps to:** Part 3.6 (notification routing); used by every WF that triggers critical-severity SMS

**Source basis:** Part 3.6 + Part 4.9.2 (`notifications.notification_log`).

### 5.5.1 Auth and configuration

**MUST 5.5.1.1** — Twilio uses Account SID + Auth Token for auth. Stored as `TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID` and `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN` env. Sending number stored as `TWILIO_FROM_NUMBER`.

**MUST 5.5.1.2** — SMS to US numbers MUST use a Twilio toll-free or 10DLC-registered number for compliance (per US carrier rules). Mexican numbers use international SMS via the same number.

### 5.5.2 Outbound flow

**MUST 5.5.2.1** — SMS dispatch is triggered by `notifications.dispatch_pending()` worker, which reads `notifications.notification_log` rows where `delivery_status = 'pending'` AND `channel = 'sms'`. Per row:
1. Format message body from event class + payload (template per event class, defined in code)
2. Call Twilio `POST /2010-04-01/Accounts/{SID}/Messages.json` with `To`, `From`, `Body`
3. On 201, store `delivery_provider_id = response.sid`, set `delivery_status = 'dispatched'`
4. On 4xx, set `delivery_status = 'failed'` (no retry for validation errors like invalid number)
5. On 5xx/network, retry per outbox backoff (this is the integration outbox, separate row from notification_log)

**MUST 5.5.2.2** — Twilio sends delivery status callbacks to `POST /api/webhooks/twilio/status`. The handler updates `notification_log.delivery_status` to `delivered`, `bounced`, or `failed` based on Twilio's status code.

### 5.5.3 Suppression integration (per WF-064.3)

**MUST 5.5.3.1** — Before dispatching, the worker MUST check `notifications.suppression_rules` for an active suppression matching `(user_uuid, event_class)`. If active:
- Set `delivery_status = 'suppressed'`
- Do NOT call Twilio
- Audit event `notifications.dispatch_suppressed` (info)

This honors the WF-064.3 Owner suppression preferences (max 7-day window).

### 5.5.4 Critical-only SMS rule (per Part 3.6)

**MUST 5.5.4.1** — SMS dispatch is reserved for **critical-severity** notifications by default. The notification log row's `event_class` is looked up against the per-class severity table (defined in code per Part 3.6); only critical events trigger SMS.

**MUST 5.5.4.2** — Exceptions exist for **operationally-time-sensitive warning** events that have been marked SMS-eligible (e.g. settlement ready for driver review, where SMS to driver is a routine workflow, not a critical alert). The exception list is defined in code; routine SMS goes to driver only, not to office staff.

### 5.5.5 API contracts

#### `POST /api/webhooks/twilio/status`

Inbound Twilio delivery status callback.

**Authentication:** Twilio signature verification (X-Twilio-Signature header validated against `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`).

**Permission:** Public endpoint with signature verification.

**Example request (from Twilio):**
```http
POST /api/webhooks/twilio/status
X-Twilio-Signature: bSGc7vMqUqTRJ5tQA9V...

MessageSid=SMabc123&MessageStatus=delivered&To=%2B19561234567&From=%2B18005551234
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{"status": "received"}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Twilio status callback missing required field",
    "details": [{"field": "MessageSid", "reason": "required"}],
    "request_id": "req_01923ca7"
  }
}
```

**Example signature error response (401):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_TWILIO_SIGNATURE_INVALID",
    "message": "Twilio signature verification failed",
    "request_id": "req_01923ca7"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):** Not applicable (signature is the auth).

**Error code linkage:** `E_TWILIO_SIGNATURE_INVALID` (5.5.7), `E_VALIDATION`. On success, emits `notifications.delivery_status_received` (info, sampled).

---

#### `GET /api/admin/integrations/twilio/status`

Get Twilio integration health.

**Permission:** `twilio.status.read` (Owner, Admin).

**Example request:**
```http
GET /api/admin/integrations/twilio/status
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "status": "healthy",
    "from_number": "+18005551234",
    "from_number_type": "toll_free",
    "last_24h_dispatched": 47,
    "last_24h_delivered": 45,
    "last_24h_bounced": 1,
    "last_24h_failed": 1,
    "last_24h_suppressed": 8,
    "indicator_color": "green"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):** Not applicable — endpoint takes no parameters.

**Example business error response:** Not applicable — read-only status endpoint with no business-rule branches.

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'twilio.status.read' requires Owner or Administrator role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"},
    "request_id": "req_01923ca8"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. Read-only; no audit on read.

### 5.5.6 Audit events

| Event | Severity | Fires WF-064? |
|---|---|---|
| `notifications.sms_dispatched` | info (sampled) | no |
| `notifications.sms_delivered` | info (sampled) | no |
| `notifications.sms_bounced` | warning | no |
| `notifications.sms_failed` | warning | no |
| `notifications.dispatch_suppressed` | info | no |
| `notifications.delivery_status_received` | info (sampled) | no |
| `twilio.auth_lost` | critical | yes |
| `twilio.auth_restored` | info | yes (success variant) |

### 5.5.7 Twilio-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_TWILIO_AUTH_REQUIRED` | 401 | Auth token invalid |
| `E_TWILIO_INVALID_NUMBER` | 400 | Recipient number malformed or unreachable |
| `E_TWILIO_RATE_LIMITED` | 429 | Throttle |
| `E_TWILIO_UNAVAILABLE` | 5xx | Service down |
| `E_TWILIO_SIGNATURE_INVALID` | 401 | Webhook signature failed |

### 5.5.8 Permission actions

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Other |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `twilio.status.read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `twilio.token.rotate` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — |

### 5.5.9 Parity test IDs

- T-104.1: Critical event → SMS dispatched within 60 seconds; delivery callback updates status to delivered
- T-104.2: User has active suppression for event class → notification logged with `delivery_status='suppressed'`; no Twilio call
- T-104.3: Invalid recipient number → E_TWILIO_INVALID_NUMBER; delivery_status='failed'; no retry
- T-104.4: Twilio 5xx for 1 min → backoff retry; eventual success
- T-104.5: Twilio status callback with valid signature → notification_log updated; sampled audit event
- T-104.6: Twilio status callback with invalid signature → 401; security audit event
- T-104.7: Bounce callback → delivery_status='bounced'; warning audit; Owner notified after 3 bounces in 24h to same number

---

## 5.6 — RESEND INTEGRATION (Email)

**Maps to:** Part 3.6 (notification routing); used by routine, warning, and critical email notifications

**Source basis:** Part 3.6 + Part 4.9.2.

### 5.6.1 Auth and configuration

**MUST 5.6.1.1** — Resend uses API key for auth. Stored as `RESEND_API_KEY` env. Sender domain configured in Resend dashboard with SPF/DKIM/DMARC records on the company's DNS.

**MUST 5.6.1.2** — All outbound emails use a verified sender like `notifications@ih35transportation.com`. Reply-to is configurable per event class (e.g. settlement notifications reply-to `payroll@`, accounting reply-to `accounting@`).

### 5.6.2 Outbound flow

**MUST 5.6.2.1** — Email dispatch is triggered by `notifications.dispatch_pending()` worker reading `notifications.notification_log` rows where `delivery_status = 'pending'` AND `channel = 'email'`. Per row:
1. Render HTML + plaintext bodies from per-event-class templates (stored in code; rendered server-side)
2. Call Resend `POST /emails` with from, to, subject, html, text, reply_to
3. On 200, store `delivery_provider_id = response.id`, set `delivery_status = 'dispatched'`
4. On 4xx, set `delivery_status = 'failed'`
5. On 5xx/network, retry per outbox backoff

**MUST 5.6.2.2** — Resend webhook to `POST /api/webhooks/resend` updates delivery_status when email is delivered/bounced/complained. Suppress users on hard bounces (record on `notifications.suppression_rules` automatically with reason `auto_bounce_suppression`).

### 5.6.3 Suppression integration

**MUST 5.6.3.1** — Same WF-064.3 suppression check as Twilio (5.5.3). Plus auto-suppression on hard bounce per 5.6.2.2.

**MUST 5.6.3.2** — Auto-suppression from hard bounce is a **temporary** suppression (30-day default), not the WF-064.3 user-driven preference. Auto-suppressions stored with distinguishing flag in `notifications.suppression_rules.auto_suppressed = true`. Owner MAY manually clear an auto-suppression via `/admin/notifications/suppressions`.

### 5.6.4 API contracts

#### `POST /api/webhooks/resend`

Inbound Resend delivery status webhook.

**Authentication:** HMAC-SHA256 signature in `svix-signature` header (Resend uses Svix for webhooks).

**Permission:** Public endpoint with signature verification.

**Example request (from Resend):**
```http
POST /api/webhooks/resend
svix-id: msg_2N5...
svix-timestamp: 1714670400
svix-signature: v1,abc123...
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "type": "email.delivered",
  "data": {
    "email_id": "98a13f3b-...",
    "to": ["jorge@ih35transportation.com"],
    "delivered_at": "2026-05-02T18:35:00Z"
  }
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{"status": "received"}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Resend webhook missing required field",
    "details": [{"field": "data.email_id", "reason": "required"}],
    "request_id": "req_01923ca9"
  }
}
```

**Example signature error response (401):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_RESEND_SIGNATURE_INVALID",
    "message": "Svix signature verification failed",
    "request_id": "req_01923ca9"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):** Not applicable (signature is the auth).

**Error code linkage:** `E_RESEND_SIGNATURE_INVALID` (5.6.6), `E_VALIDATION`. On success, emits `notifications.delivery_status_received` (info, sampled).

---

#### `GET /api/admin/integrations/resend/status`

Get Resend integration health.

**Permission:** `resend.status.read` (Owner, Admin).

**Example request:**
```http
GET /api/admin/integrations/resend/status
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "status": "healthy",
    "sender_domain": "ih35transportation.com",
    "domain_verified": true,
    "spf_status": "pass",
    "dkim_status": "pass",
    "dmarc_status": "pass",
    "last_24h_dispatched": 312,
    "last_24h_delivered": 308,
    "last_24h_bounced": 2,
    "last_24h_complained": 0,
    "last_24h_suppressed": 12,
    "indicator_color": "green"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):** Not applicable — endpoint takes no parameters.

**Example business error response:** Not applicable — read-only status endpoint with no business-rule branches.

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'resend.status.read' requires Owner or Administrator role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "manager"},
    "request_id": "req_01923caa"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. Read-only.

### 5.6.5 Audit events

| Event | Severity | Fires WF-064? |
|---|---|---|
| `notifications.email_dispatched` | info (sampled) | no |
| `notifications.email_delivered` | info (sampled) | no |
| `notifications.email_bounced` | warning | no |
| `notifications.email_complained` | warning | no |
| `notifications.email_failed` | warning | no |
| `notifications.email_auto_suppressed` | warning | no |
| `notifications.delivery_status_received` | info (sampled) | no |
| `resend.auth_lost` | critical | yes |
| `resend.auth_restored` | info | yes (success variant) |
| `resend.domain_verification_failed` | critical | yes |

### 5.6.6 Resend-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_RESEND_AUTH_REQUIRED` | 401 | API key invalid |
| `E_RESEND_INVALID_RECIPIENT` | 400 | Email address malformed |
| `E_RESEND_DOMAIN_NOT_VERIFIED` | 403 | Sender domain DNS records broken |
| `E_RESEND_RATE_LIMITED` | 429 | Throttle |
| `E_RESEND_UNAVAILABLE` | 5xx | Service down |
| `E_RESEND_SIGNATURE_INVALID` | 401 | Svix webhook signature failed |
| `E_RESEND_AUTO_SUPPRESSED` | n/a | Recipient on auto-suppression list |

### 5.6.7 Permission actions

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Other |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `resend.status.read` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `resend.token.rotate` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `notifications.suppression.clear` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — |

### 5.6.8 Parity test IDs

- T-105.1: Routine email → Resend dispatch within 60 seconds; delivery callback updates status
- T-105.2: Hard bounce → auto-suppression created with auto_suppressed=true, 30-day expiry
- T-105.3: Recipient on auto-suppression → delivery_status='suppressed'; warning audit
- T-105.4: User-driven suppression (WF-064.3) takes precedence over auto-suppression
- T-105.5: Owner clears auto-suppression manually → resumes delivery on next dispatch
- T-105.6: Resend webhook with valid Svix signature → status update applied
- T-105.7: Resend webhook with invalid signature → 401; security audit
- T-105.8: Domain verification fails (DNS broken) → critical audit; Owner notified; emails to all recipients fail until restored

---

## 5.7 — CROSS-INTEGRATION CONCERNS

### 5.7.1 The integration health dashboard (extends 5.0.3)

**MUST 5.7.1.1** — A consolidated view at `/admin/integrations` MUST display all six integrations in one screen with:
- Per-integration status (green/gold/red)
- Last successful round-trip timestamp
- Pending outbox count per integration
- Token expiry warnings (especially QBO refresh token countdown)
- Recent error counts (last 1h, last 24h)
- One-click links to per-integration deep-dive pages

### 5.7.2 The dispatch board fan-out for rotation

**MUST 5.7.2.1** — Token rotation for any integration is a **scheduled maintenance event**. The recommended Owner workflow:
1. Review the rotation calendar at `/admin/integrations/rotation-calendar`
2. Schedule rotations during low-activity windows
3. Test in staging environment if available (Phase 2)
4. Execute rotation; verify health indicator returns green
5. Monitor for 24 hours

Token rotation is a WF-064 high-risk action (success/blocked symmetric) per Cursor's locked rule.

### 5.7.3 Cross-integration failure correlation

**MUST 5.7.3.1** — If two or more integrations enter `auth_lost` state within 5 minutes, the system MUST emit a `integrations.cross_failure_correlation` (critical) audit event AND notify Owner with the correlation. This catches patterns like "CF infrastructure outage takes down R2 + Resend simultaneously."

**MUST 5.7.3.2** — If an outbound integration's outbox queue exceeds 1,000 pending rows, the system MUST notify Administrator (warning); 5,000 → critical to Owner. These thresholds detect long-running outages before they cascade.

### 5.7.4 Phase 2 deferred integrations

**Plaid** (banking direct) — replaces QBO bank transaction mirroring with direct Plaid Link to banks. Deferred because QBO mirroring is sufficient for v1 and Plaid adds significant onboarding friction. Same outbox + audit + permission patterns apply when added.

**DocuSign** (alternate evidence path) — alternative to PWA-in-app and printed-PDF acknowledgment paths (Part 3.17.9). Deferred because PWA covers all current flows; DocuSign adds value only for non-PWA-using stakeholders (e.g. corporate insurance forms). Same chain-of-custody patterns apply when added.

### 5.7.5 Integration testing strategy

**MUST 5.7.5.1** — Each integration MUST have a **mock service** for development and CI tests:
- QBO: mock OAuth + entity API server (running locally on different port)
- Samsara: mock REST API + webhook simulator
- Relay: mock transaction API + Excel upload fixtures
- R2: MinIO local instance (S3-compatible)
- Twilio: mock SMS API + signature simulator
- Resend: mock email API + Svix webhook simulator

**MUST 5.7.5.2** — Production deploys MUST use real integrations; staging deploys MAY use mocks for cost reasons. The integration's auth credentials are environment-specific.

**MUST 5.7.5.3** — Smoke test on every deploy: each integration MUST round-trip a no-op call (ping endpoint where available, health check otherwise) within 60 seconds of deploy. Failure aborts the deploy.

---

## 5.8 — CROSS-CUTTING PARITY TESTS

- T-110.1: All 6 integrations have green dot in `/admin/integrations` after fresh deploy + smoke test
- T-110.2: Token rotation for QBO → drainer halts during reconnect → re-auth restores → outbox replays preserving order (per 3.12.6)
- T-110.3: Two integrations enter `auth_lost` within 5 min → cross_failure_correlation critical audit
- T-110.4: Outbox count for Samsara crosses 1,000 → Administrator warning notification
- T-110.5: Outbox count for QBO crosses 5,000 → Owner critical notification
- T-110.6: Sentry payload for any integration error has credentials scrubbed (never leaks token, key, or secret)
- T-110.7: Integration smoke tests pass on staging deploy with mocks; pass on production deploy with real services
- T-110.8: WF-064 success variant fires for token_rotated audit events (e.g. samsara.token_rotated)
- T-110.9: WF-064 blocked variant fires for token_rotation_blocked events (cooldown violations)
- T-110.10: Schema reference consistency check — no production query reads from `system.*` or `integrity.*` schemas (only `audit.*` for governance tooling per Part 4 Rev 2)
- T-110.11: Transactional debt authority — settlement bill creation calls `driver_finance.recompute_driver_debt()` before posting QBO outbox row (per 5.0.2.6)

---

# END OF PART 5 — CHECKPOINT

**Coverage check against locked constraints (per Cursor's go-ahead):**

| Locked constraint | How Part 5 honors it |
|---|---|
| `audit.*` for governance tooling, no `system.*`/`integrity.*` tables | 5.0.2.7 explicit; 5.2.4.2 `samsara` schema collapsed into `dispatch.*`; T-110.10 verifies; 14-schema count preserved |
| Transactional debt authority (live recompute, not cache) | 5.0.2.6 explicit; T-110.11 verifies driver settlement bill creation calls `recompute_driver_debt()` before QBO sync |
| API DoD strict (request + success + 400 + permission + business + linkage) | Every API contract in Part 5 has all 6 elements; verified by inspection (3 in 5.1, 2 in 5.2, 2 in 5.3, 1 in 5.4, 2 in 5.5, 2 in 5.6 = 12 endpoints) |
| WF-064 success/blocked symmetry | `qbo.auth_lost`/`qbo.auth_restored`; `samsara.token_rotated`/`samsara.token_rotation_blocked`; `relay.auth_lost`/`relay.auth_restored`; `r2.auth_lost`/`r2.auth_restored`; `twilio.auth_lost`/`twilio.auth_restored`; `resend.auth_lost`/`resend.auth_restored` |

**WF coverage in this part:**

| WF | Coverage section |
|---|---|
| WF-016 Fuel Card Transaction Posting | 5.3.2.1 |
| WF-021 Driver Document Capture | 5.4 (R2 storage + retrieval) |
| WF-022 Integration Outage Handling | 5.0.3, 5.1, 5.2, 5.3 (per-integration outage flow) |
| WF-031 QBO Auto Sync | 5.1 (entire) |
| WF-032 Offline Continuity | 5.1.2 + outbox model |
| WF-038 Active Driver Set | 5.2.2 (HOS polling restricted to active set) |
| WF-043 Geofence Analytics | 5.2.2 (webhook events) |
| WF-046 Transfer + CC Payment | 5.1.2 (Part 3.12.4.4 carries forward) |
| WF-054 Loves Excel Upload | 5.3.2.2 |
| WF-055 Recommendation vs Actual | 5.3.2.3 |
| WF-058 Equipment Damage Photos | 5.4 (R2 evidence storage) |
| WF-063 Bidirectional Catalog Sync | 5.1.3 (webhooks + CDC) |
| WF-064 Manager High-Risk Action | All 6 integrations have token-rotation/reconnect events firing WF-064 |

**New error codes introduced in Part 5: 21 total**
- 4 in 5.1 QBO (E_QBO_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_INVALID, E_QBO_ALREADY_CONNECTED, E_QBO_REALM_MISMATCH, E_QBO_REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRED)
- 2 in 5.2 Samsara (E_SAMSARA_ROTATION_COOLDOWN, E_SAMSARA_GEOFENCE_PUSH_FAILED)
- 7 in 5.3 Relay (E_RELAY_AUTH_REQUIRED, E_RELAY_RATE_LIMITED, E_RELAY_UNAVAILABLE, E_RELAY_IP_NOT_ALLOWLISTED, E_RELAY_PRICE_UPLOAD_PARSE_FAILED, E_RELAY_TRANSACTION_UNMATCHED, E_RELAY_CARD_NOT_FOUND)
- 7 in 5.4 R2 (E_R2_AUTH_REQUIRED, E_R2_BUCKET_NOT_FOUND, E_R2_OBJECT_NOT_FOUND, E_R2_CHECKSUM_MISMATCH, E_R2_QUOTA_EXCEEDED, E_R2_RATE_LIMITED, E_R2_OBJECT_LOCKED)
- 5 in 5.5 Twilio (E_TWILIO_AUTH_REQUIRED, E_TWILIO_INVALID_NUMBER, E_TWILIO_RATE_LIMITED, E_TWILIO_UNAVAILABLE, E_TWILIO_SIGNATURE_INVALID)
- 7 in 5.6 Resend (E_RESEND_AUTH_REQUIRED, E_RESEND_INVALID_RECIPIENT, E_RESEND_DOMAIN_NOT_VERIFIED, E_RESEND_RATE_LIMITED, E_RESEND_UNAVAILABLE, E_RESEND_SIGNATURE_INVALID, E_RESEND_AUTO_SUPPRESSED)

(Total 4+2+7+7+5+7 = **32 codes** — corrected count.)

**New audit events introduced: 28 across 6 integrations**
- 6 in 5.1 (qbo.* added to Part 3.12.10's existing set)
- 4 in 5.2 (samsara.token_rotated, .token_rotation_blocked, .geofence_pushed_to_samsara, .driver_vehicle_assigned_pushed)

(Recount: 6+4+9+6+8+10 = **43 events**.)

- T-102.1 through T-102.8 (Relay) = 8

(Recount: 10+7+8+8+7+8+11 = **59 tests**.)

**Test ID continuity:** T-100.x through T-110.x verified non-colliding with Parts 1-4 (T-001..T-099).
