# === FILE: IH35_MASTER_BLUEPRINT_v3_part_6.md ===
================================================================================

# IH35 Master Blueprint v3 — Part 6: Design System

**Section in v3 hierarchy:** Part 6 of 13. Reads after Part 5 (Integrations, Rev 1). Read before Parts 7-13 (per-module specs); Parts 7-13 reference design tokens and component primitives defined here rather than redefining them.

**Normative language:** MUST · MUST NOT · SHOULD · SHOULD NOT · MAY (RFC 2119 conventions). Visual specifications that affect product feel + accessibility are MUST. Component variations and styling polish are SHOULD.

**WF coverage in this part:** WF-064 (high-risk action visual treatment), WF-022 (outage banner), WF-012 (held deduction marker), WF-021/WF-058 (acknowledgment receipt visual confirmation), WF-041 (settlement debt-alert banner), WF-046 (transfer banner). Cross-cuts every screen via tokens.

**Test ID continuation:** this part uses T-120.x through T-129.x. Verified non-colliding with Parts 1-5 series (T-001..T-110.x).

**Source basis:** All 12 UI sections approved screen-by-screen via visualize:show_widget renders; this Part codifies what those approvals locked in.

---

## 6.0 — DESIGN SYSTEM OVERVIEW

### 6.0.1 Purpose and authority

**MUST 6.0.1.1** — Part 6 is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for visual design decisions across the product. Parts 7-13 (per-module UI specs) MUST reference tokens and primitives defined here rather than redefining them. A per-module spec MAY say "uses the standard table primitive (6.4.1)" but MUST NOT redefine table padding, border, hover states, or row-height tokens. Divergence between Part 6 and a per-module spec is ALWAYS resolved by amending Part 6 first; per-module overrides are permitted only via explicit named exception.

**MUST 6.0.1.2** — Components defined here are for the **operations product** (desktop browser primary) AND the **PWA** (mobile, dark theme). Where mobile and desktop diverge, a single token name has two values (light + dark, or desktop + mobile) and Part 6 specifies both.

**MUST 6.0.1.3** — Every design token MUST appear in the codebase as a named CSS custom property (or equivalent constant in TypeScript) with the EXACT name from this Part. Token names are kebab-case (e.g. `--color-bg-page`, `--space-4`).

**MUST 6.0.1.4** — Hardcoded magic values (colors, sizes, spacing) in component code outside `/design-tokens/` are forbidden. CI lint rule `enforce-design-tokens` MUST scan component source and FAIL the build if any color hex, pixel value, or font-size literal is found outside the token files.

### 6.0.2 Density philosophy (the core operating-system feel)

**MUST 6.0.2.1** — The product is **information-dense, professional, and operational**. It is NOT consumer/marketing in feel. The visual reference points are: Bloomberg Terminal (data density), QuickBooks Online (familiar accounting interaction), Linear (modern operational polish). It is NOT: Stripe Dashboard (too consumer), Notion (too whitespace-heavy), Figma (too tool-creative).

**MUST 6.0.2.2** — Numbers stay petite. Maximum size for inline numeric values is 13px; KPI cards use 16-18px for hero numbers. The product MUST NOT use 24px+ display numbers anywhere outside loading/onboarding states. This was approved screen-by-screen during the UI walkthrough (Sections 1-12) and is locked.

**MUST 6.0.2.3** — Whitespace is calculated, not generous. Default row heights are 28-32px (not 48px). Default padding is 8-12px (not 16-24px). The exception is the PWA driver app, which uses 44-56px touch targets per accessibility floor (6.6.4).

### 6.0.3 What changed from the existing maintenance.html

The existing 37,019-line `maintenance.html` has organic styling that grew over 25 days of patches. Part 6 codifies a clean reset:
- Single navy top bar (#1A1F36) replaces multi-color brand experiments
- Section header bands (`#F1EFE8`, 9px uppercase) replace ad-hoc colored section dividers
- Color bands (gold/red/blue/green) for category coding replace inconsistent badge styles
- Hover dropdowns sized to longest line (no max-width truncation)
- 0.5px row dividers (not 1px) for the dense feel

Existing screens MUST be migrated to the new system in v1; no per-screen variance permitted.

---

## 6.1 — DESIGN TOKENS

### 6.1.1 Color tokens

#### 6.1.1.1 Brand and chrome colors

**MUST 6.1.1.1.1** — Top navigation bar uses navy `#1A1F36` (the `--color-chrome-topbar` token). Sidebar uses slightly lighter navy `#212747` (`--color-chrome-sidebar`). Border between chrome and content is `#0F1320` (`--color-chrome-border`).

```css
--color-chrome-topbar: #1A1F36;       /* top bar background, 48px height */
--color-chrome-sidebar: #212747;       /* left sidebar, 72px width */
--color-chrome-border: #0F1320;        /* divider between chrome and content */
--color-chrome-text-primary: #FFFFFF;  /* logo, primary navigation links */
--color-chrome-text-secondary: #A8B0C7;/* secondary nav, breadcrumbs */
--color-chrome-text-muted: #6B7290;    /* tertiary navigation hints */
```

**MUST 6.1.1.1.2** — The chrome navy MUST NOT appear anywhere in the content area. It is exclusively for top bar, sidebar, and modal-overlay backdrops.

#### 6.1.1.2 Page background and surfaces

```css
--color-bg-page: #FAFAF7;          /* main content background, slight warm cream */
--color-bg-surface: #FFFFFF;       /* card, panel, table backgrounds */
--color-bg-surface-alt: #F8F8F4;   /* alternating row, hover row, subtle nesting */
--color-bg-section-header: #F1EFE8;/* section header band background */
--color-bg-overlay: rgba(15, 19, 32, 0.6); /* modal backdrop */
```

**MUST 6.1.1.2.1** — Page background `#FAFAF7` is the warm cream that distinguishes the product from typical SaaS gray. Approved screen-by-screen during UI walkthrough.

#### 6.1.1.3 Text colors

```css
--color-text-primary: #1A1F36;     /* default body text, headings */
--color-text-secondary: #4A5170;   /* labels, secondary copy */
--color-text-muted: #8A92AB;       /* helper text, captions, timestamps */
--color-text-disabled: #C5C9D6;    /* disabled state */
--color-text-inverse: #FFFFFF;     /* on dark backgrounds (chrome, modals) */
--color-text-link: #2563EB;        /* inline links */
--color-text-link-hover: #1D4ED8;  /* link hover */
```

#### 6.1.1.4 Border and divider colors

```css
--color-border-default: #E5E7EB;    /* default cell borders, panel borders */
--color-border-subtle: #F0F1F4;     /* 0.5px row dividers */
--color-border-strong: #C5C9D6;     /* emphasis borders, focus rings */
--color-border-focus: #2563EB;      /* focus ring color */
```

#### 6.1.1.5 Color band tokens (the operational coding system)

The product uses four color bands for category coding. These bands are the **only** non-chrome colors permitted at large surface scale (badges, banners, highlight bars).

```css
/* Roadside / Field operations — gold */
--color-band-roadside-bg: #FEF3C7;       /* background fill */
--color-band-roadside-border: #FCD34D;   /* border accent */
--color-band-roadside-text: #78350F;     /* text on band */
--color-band-roadside-icon: #D97706;     /* icon color */

/* Severe / Critical / Blocked — red */
--color-band-severe-bg: #FEE2E2;
--color-band-severe-border: #FCA5A5;
--color-band-severe-text: #7F1D1D;
--color-band-severe-icon: #DC2626;

/* Factoring / Financial routing — blue */
--color-band-factoring-bg: #DBEAFE;
--color-band-factoring-border: #93C5FD;
--color-band-factoring-text: #1E3A8A;
--color-band-factoring-icon: #2563EB;

/* Escrow / Reserved / Held — green */
--color-band-escrow-bg: #D1FAE5;
--color-band-escrow-border: #6EE7B7;
--color-band-escrow-text: #064E3B;
--color-band-escrow-icon: #059669;
```

**MUST 6.1.1.5.1** — These four bands are EXHAUSTIVE for category coding. Per-module specs (Parts 7-13) MUST NOT introduce a fifth band (e.g. "purple for safety"). New categories reuse existing bands by semantic mapping (e.g. safety violations → severe red because they are blocked actions).

**MUST 6.1.1.5.2** — Band selection is by *operational meaning*, not aesthetic preference:
- Roadside (gold) — field operations, in-progress/intransit, weather/traffic-class warnings
- Severe (red) — blocked actions, critical alerts, voided/canceled, debt above tolerance, overdue
- Factoring (blue) — financial routing decisions (factor vs hold, advance vs reserve), informational financial state
- Escrow (green) — held/reserved/accumulating positively, escrow movement, paid-off, completed

#### 6.1.1.6 Status semantic tokens (the small-badge system)

For inline status badges (smaller than color bands):

```css
--color-status-success-bg: #DCFCE7;
--color-status-success-text: #166534;
--color-status-warning-bg: #FEF3C7;
--color-status-warning-text: #854D0E;
--color-status-danger-bg: #FEE2E2;
--color-status-danger-text: #991B1B;
--color-status-info-bg: #DBEAFE;
--color-status-info-text: #1E40AF;
--color-status-neutral-bg: #F3F4F6;
--color-status-neutral-text: #4A5170;
```

**MUST 6.1.1.6.1** — Badges MUST use ONLY these five semantic colors. No custom badge colors.

#### 6.1.1.7 PWA dark theme tokens

```css
--color-pwa-bg-page: #0F1219;
--color-pwa-bg-surface: #1A1F36;
--color-pwa-bg-surface-alt: #212747;
--color-pwa-text-primary: #F7F8FB;
--color-pwa-text-secondary: #A8B0C7;
--color-pwa-text-muted: #6B7290;
--color-pwa-border-default: #2A3055;
--color-pwa-border-subtle: #1F2540;
--color-pwa-border-strong: #4A5170;
--color-pwa-band-roadside-bg: #4C2E0C;
--color-pwa-band-severe-bg: #4C1D1B;
--color-pwa-band-factoring-bg: #1E2D5C;
--color-pwa-band-escrow-bg: #14352A;
```

**MUST 6.1.1.7.1** — The PWA MUST use the dark theme tokens exclusively. Light theme is not offered in the PWA in v1 (driver visibility is better at night with dark UI per ergonomics decision).

**MUST 6.1.1.7.2** — The PWA bg `#0F1219` is approved per UI walkthrough §12 and is locked. It is intentionally darker than the chrome navy `#1A1F36` so chrome elements stand out from page background even in dark theme.

### 6.1.2 Typography tokens

#### 6.1.2.1 Font families

```css
--font-family-base: 'Inter', system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif;
--font-family-mono: 'JetBrains Mono', 'SF Mono', Consolas, monospace;
--font-family-display: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; /* same as base; no separate display font */
```

**MUST 6.1.2.1.1** — Inter is the sole primary typeface. Self-hosted (per Render deployment) at `/fonts/Inter-*.woff2`. Subset to Latin + extended Latin (covers EN + ES + MX special characters).

**MUST 6.1.2.1.2** — Numeric data in tables, KPI cards, and currency displays MUST use the `tabular-nums` font feature so digits align vertically. Set via `font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums`.

#### 6.1.2.2 Font size scale (the petite scale per 6.0.2.2)

```css
--font-size-xs: 9px;      /* section header bands, small uppercase labels */
--font-size-sm: 11px;     /* table caption, helper text, secondary metadata */
--font-size-base: 12px;   /* body text, table cells */
--font-size-md: 13px;     /* emphasized body, labels in forms */
--font-size-lg: 14px;     /* section headings within content */
--font-size-xl: 16px;     /* KPI card numbers, page titles in chrome */
--font-size-2xl: 18px;    /* hero KPI values (rare; Home page §1 only) */
```

**MUST 6.1.2.2.1** — Font sizes >18px are FORBIDDEN in production code outside loading screens, error states, and onboarding. CI lint rule `enforce-font-size-cap` MUST flag any size >18px outside the allowed paths.

#### 6.1.2.3 Font weight scale

```css
--font-weight-regular: 400;
--font-weight-medium: 500;   /* labels, table headers */
--font-weight-semibold: 600; /* page titles, emphasized values */
--font-weight-bold: 700;     /* hero KPIs, critical alerts */
```

#### 6.1.2.4 Line height scale

```css
--line-height-tight: 1.25;   /* numbers, single-line labels */
--line-height-normal: 1.4;   /* body text, table cells */
--line-height-relaxed: 1.6;  /* paragraphs, helper text blocks */
```

#### 6.1.2.5 Letter spacing

```css
--letter-spacing-tight: -0.01em;  /* large numbers (xl/2xl) */
--letter-spacing-normal: 0;       /* body */
--letter-spacing-wide: 0.05em;    /* xs uppercase labels */
--letter-spacing-extra-wide: 0.08em; /* section header bands */
```

### 6.1.3 Spacing tokens

```css
--space-0: 0;
--space-1: 2px;
--space-2: 4px;
--space-3: 6px;
--space-4: 8px;
--space-5: 12px;
--space-6: 16px;
--space-7: 24px;
--space-8: 32px;
--space-9: 48px;
--space-10: 64px;
```

**MUST 6.1.3.1** — Spacing tokens are the ONLY permitted padding/margin values. Components MUST NOT use arbitrary pixel values like `padding: 14px`. Use `var(--space-5)` (12px) or `var(--space-6)` (16px) — not `14px`.

### 6.1.4 Border radius tokens

```css
--radius-none: 0;
--radius-sm: 2px;        /* default for inputs, badges */
--radius-md: 4px;        /* cards, panels */
--radius-lg: 6px;        /* modals */
--radius-xl: 8px;        /* extra-emphasized surfaces, rare */
--radius-full: 9999px;   /* pills, circular avatars */
```

**MUST 6.1.4.1** — Radius >8px is forbidden outside pill/avatar contexts. The product feel is operational, not playful.

### 6.1.5 Shadow tokens

```css
--shadow-none: none;
--shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px 0 rgba(15, 19, 32, 0.05);
--shadow-md: 0 2px 4px -1px rgba(15, 19, 32, 0.06), 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(15, 19, 32, 0.05);
--shadow-lg: 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(15, 19, 32, 0.08), 0 4px 6px -2px rgba(15, 19, 32, 0.04);
--shadow-modal: 0 25px 50px -12px rgba(15, 19, 32, 0.25);
--shadow-focus: 0 0 0 2px var(--color-border-focus);
```

### 6.1.6 Z-index tokens

```css
--z-base: 0;
--z-raised: 10;        /* hover row */
--z-dropdown: 50;      /* hover dropdowns, action menus */
--z-sticky: 100;       /* sticky table headers */
--z-modal-backdrop: 900;
--z-modal: 1000;
--z-toast: 1100;       /* toast notifications */
--z-critical-banner: 1200; /* WF-064 critical alert banner */
```

**MUST 6.1.6.1** — All z-index values MUST come from these tokens. No arbitrary z-index values like `z-index: 9999`.

### 6.1.7 Animation and transition tokens

```css
--duration-instant: 0ms;
--duration-fast: 100ms;     /* hover, focus changes */
--duration-base: 200ms;     /* standard transitions */
--duration-slow: 300ms;     /* modal in/out */
--duration-deliberate: 500ms; /* deliberate emphasis (rare) */

--easing-default: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
--easing-in: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 1, 1);
--easing-out: cubic-bezier(0, 0, 0.2, 1);
--easing-spring: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1); /* rare; for reward animations */
```

**MUST 6.1.7.1** — Duration >500ms is forbidden in non-recreational animations. Operations users MUST NOT wait for animations.

**MUST 6.1.7.2** — `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` MUST disable all transitions except critical state changes (e.g. modal appearance is instant rather than animated). Implemented globally via media query.

---

## 6.2 — LAYOUT GRID AND CHROME

### 6.2.1 Page chrome dimensions

**MUST 6.2.1.1** — Top bar: 48px tall, full-width, navy `#1A1F36`. Contains: logo (left), primary navigation links (center-left), search (center), integration health indicators (right), user menu (far right). Sticky to viewport top.

**MUST 6.2.1.2** — Sidebar: 72px wide, navy `#212747`, sticky to viewport left below top bar. Contains: 12 navigation icons (one per section per UI walkthrough), each 44x44px tappable. The sidebar is icon-only at this width; expanded tooltip on hover shows section name.

**MUST 6.2.1.3** — Content area: `calc(100vw - 72px)` wide, `calc(100vh - 48px)` tall. Default page padding is 24px (`--space-7`).

### 6.2.2 The 12-column responsive grid

**MUST 6.2.2.1** — Content area uses a 12-column grid with 16px (`--space-6`) gutters. Breakpoints:
- `desktop` (≥1280px): full 12 columns; default office workflow target
- `tablet` (768-1279px): 8 columns; sidebar collapses to icons-only (already 72px); some KPI cards reflow
- `mobile` (<768px): single-column; PWA territory; office desktop product is NOT optimized for <768px

**MUST 6.2.2.2** — The PWA (driver app) is mobile-first single-column at 100% viewport width. PWA does NOT use the 12-column desktop grid.

### 6.2.3 Section header band

**MUST 6.2.3.1** — Every page MUST have a section header band as the first element below the breadcrumb. Specification:
- Background: `--color-bg-section-header` (#F1EFE8)
- Height: 28px
- Padding: 0 12px (`--space-5`)
- Text: 9px (`--font-size-xs`), uppercase, letter-spacing extra-wide, weight semibold, color `--color-text-secondary`
- Right-aligned: optional contextual actions (export, refresh, settings icon)

This band is the section identifier that the user sees on every page. Approved per UI walkthrough Sections 1-12.

### 6.2.4 KPI cards row

**MUST 6.2.4.1** — KPI cards are 30px tall single-row by default. Specification:
- Background: `--color-bg-surface` (white)
- Border: 1px `--color-border-default`
- Border radius: `--radius-md`
- Padding: `--space-3` (6px) horizontal, `--space-2` (4px) vertical
- Internal layout: label left (11px regular), value right (16-18px semibold tabular-nums)

**MUST 6.2.4.2** — KPI cards MUST be in a row of 4-6 cards typically; cards reflow on tablet to 2-3 per row.

### 6.2.5 Sticky table headers

**MUST 6.2.5.1** — Tables with >10 rows MUST have sticky headers (`position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: var(--z-sticky)`). Header background MUST be opaque so content beneath doesn't show through.

---

## 6.3 — INTERACTION PRIMITIVES

### 6.3.1 Hover dropdowns

**MUST 6.3.1.1** — Hover dropdowns MUST be sized to the longest line of content; no fixed-width or max-width truncation. If the longest action label is "Convert to Work Order", the dropdown is wide enough to show that without truncation. This was approved during UI walkthrough.

**MUST 6.3.1.2** — Hover dropdown delay-in: 150ms. Delay-out: 300ms (so users have time to traverse to the menu).

**MUST 6.3.1.3** — Hover dropdowns MUST be keyboard-accessible: Tab to the trigger, Enter or Space opens, Arrow keys navigate, Esc closes. Implements WAI-ARIA menu pattern.

### 6.3.2 Click feedback

**MUST 6.3.2.1** — All clickable elements MUST have visible feedback within 100ms of click. Either: visual depression (active state), color change, ripple, or loading indicator (for async actions taking >200ms).

**MUST 6.3.2.2** — Async actions (network, mutation) MUST show a loading state on the triggering element (button → spinner inside button) within 200ms, OR a global loading bar at the top of the page. Never silent network calls without user feedback.

### 6.3.3 Form input focus

**MUST 6.3.3.1** — All focusable elements (inputs, buttons, links) MUST have a visible focus ring. Default: `--shadow-focus` (2px solid `--color-border-focus`).

**MUST 6.3.3.2** — Focus ring MUST be visible regardless of input method (mouse, keyboard, touch) — `:focus-visible` is preferred but the design accepts `:focus` also showing the ring; we do not hide focus on mouse use.

### 6.3.4 Right-click + context menus

**MUST 6.3.4.1** — Right-click context menus MUST be implemented for table rows in operational contexts (drivers list, units list, loads list). Menu MUST mirror the actions available in the row's hover dropdown, plus optionally additional context-specific actions.

**MUST 6.3.4.2** — Right-click on non-table elements (chrome, page background) MUST defer to browser default (no custom context menu).

---

## 6.4 — COMPONENT PRIMITIVES

### 6.4.1 Tables

The dominant primitive in this product. Used in Drivers list, Units list, Loads list, Settlements list, Bills list, Invoices list, Maintenance WO list, Safety incidents, Fuel transactions, every report.

**MUST 6.4.1.1 — Default table row spec:**
- Row height: 28px (`--space-7` row + 4px padding)
- Cell padding: `--space-3` (6px) horizontal, `--space-2` (4px) vertical
- Row divider: 0.5px solid `--color-border-subtle` (the dense feel)
- Header row: weight medium (500), 11px, uppercase, color `--color-text-secondary`, sticky per 6.2.5
- Hover row: background `--color-bg-surface-alt`
- Selected row: background `--color-band-factoring-bg`, left border 3px `--color-band-factoring-border`

**MUST 6.4.1.2 — Numeric columns** MUST be right-aligned with `tabular-nums`. Currency columns include `$` prefix (or appropriate currency symbol per locale; v1 USD only). Negative amounts in red `--color-status-danger-text`.

**MUST 6.4.1.3 — Table action columns:**
- Left-most column: optional row checkbox (24px wide)
- Right-most column: actions (icons or hover dropdown; 80-100px wide)
- Actions column MUST have a hover-dropdown trigger (3-dot icon `MoreHorizontal` from Lucide) for >2 actions

**MUST 6.4.1.4 — Empty state:** when a table has 0 rows, MUST show an empty-state message with: icon (32px), short title (14px semibold), description (12px regular), optional CTA button.

**MUST 6.4.1.5 — Loading state:** while loading, table MUST show 5-10 skeleton rows with shimmering placeholders. NEVER show a blank table or generic spinner alone.

**MUST 6.4.1.6 — Error state:** if data fetch fails, table MUST show error message with retry button. Error MUST quote the error code (e.g. "E_QBO_AUTH_REQUIRED") for support diagnostics.

### 6.4.2 Buttons

**MUST 6.4.2.1 — Button variants** (the only permitted variants):

| Variant | Use | Background | Text | Border | Hover |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `primary` | Confirm transactional actions, Save, Submit | `--color-text-link` (#2563EB) | white | none | darker blue |
| `secondary` | Cancel, secondary actions | `--color-bg-surface` | `--color-text-primary` | `--color-border-default` | `--color-bg-surface-alt` |
| `ghost` | Tertiary, low-emphasis | transparent | `--color-text-secondary` | none | `--color-bg-surface-alt` |
| `danger` | Destructive, void, delete | `--color-status-danger-text` (#991B1B) | white | none | darker red |
| `link` | Inline actions in dense lists | transparent | `--color-text-link` | none | underline |

**MUST 6.4.2.2 — Button sizes:**

| Size | Height | Padding | Font size | Use |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `xs` | 24px | `--space-3` (6px) | 11px | inline table actions |
| `sm` | 28px | `--space-4` (8px) | 12px | default in dense screens |
| `md` | 32px | `--space-5` (12px) | 13px | modals, forms |
| `lg` | 36px | `--space-6` (16px) | 14px | landing CTAs (rare) |

**MUST 6.4.2.3** — A button performing an async action MUST show a spinner inside the button (replaces the icon if any) AND become disabled during the operation. Button label MAY change to "Saving…" / "Posting…" but it is OPTIONAL.

**MUST 6.4.2.4** — `danger` buttons MUST have a confirmation step (modal or inline confirm) before executing — never one-click destruction.

**MUST 6.4.2.5** — `primary` buttons that fire **WF-064** (high-risk actions) MUST display a small lightning-bolt icon (`Zap` from Lucide) BEFORE the label, AND open a 2-step confirmation modal (per 6.4.5.4). Examples: void settlement, write-off receivable, merge accounts, rotate integration token, switch QBO realm. The icon visually flags WF-064 actions for the user before they click.

### 6.4.3 Form inputs

**MUST 6.4.3.1 — Text input spec:**
- Height: 32px (`md` size); 28px (`sm` size)
- Border: 1px `--color-border-default`
- Border radius: `--radius-sm`
- Padding: `--space-4` (8px) horizontal
- Font size: 13px (`--font-size-md`)
- Focus: border `--color-border-focus`, focus shadow per `--shadow-focus`
- Disabled: bg `--color-bg-surface-alt`, text `--color-text-disabled`
- Error: border `--color-status-danger-text`

**MUST 6.4.3.2 — Label:**
- Above input, weight medium, 11px (`--font-size-sm`)
- Required marker: `*` after label, color `--color-status-danger-text`

**MUST 6.4.3.3 — Helper text:**
- Below input, 11px, `--color-text-muted`
- Error message replaces helper text on error: 11px, `--color-status-danger-text`

**MUST 6.4.3.4 — Validation:**
- Inline validation on blur (not on every keystroke; that's annoying)
- Submit-time validation in addition to blur validation
- Server-side errors display inline beneath the failing field
- Error focus: first error field auto-focused on validation failure

**MUST 6.4.3.5 — Number input** MUST disable browser's increment/decrement spinners (they introduce accidental changes in dense workflows) and rely on numeric keyboard input. Currency inputs MAY have explicit increment buttons if explicitly designed.

**MUST 6.4.3.6 — Date input** MUST use a native picker on mobile and a custom keyboard-accessible picker on desktop (default browser pickers are inconsistent across browsers).

**MUST 6.4.3.7 — Select / Dropdown** MUST support type-ahead filtering when there are >10 options. For >100 options (e.g. customer selection), MUST use an async-search combobox.

### 6.4.4 Banners

Banners are persistent in-content alerts. Distinct from toasts (which are dismissable, time-limited).

**MUST 6.4.4.1 — Banner spec:**
- Min height: 40px
- Padding: `--space-5` (12px)
- Border radius: `--radius-md`
- Left border accent: 3px solid (color matches band)
- Icon: 16px, left of content, color matches band
- Content: 12px (`--font-size-base`)
- Optional right action: text link or `xs` button

**MUST 6.4.4.2 — Banner variants:**

| Variant | When | Background | Border accent | Icon |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `info` | Informational; user can ignore | `--color-status-info-bg` | `--color-band-factoring-border` | `Info` |
| `success` | Action succeeded | `--color-status-success-bg` | `--color-band-escrow-border` | `CheckCircle` |
| `warning` | Caution; user should review | `--color-status-warning-bg` | `--color-band-roadside-border` | `AlertTriangle` |
| `danger` | Blocked; user must act | `--color-status-danger-bg` | `--color-band-severe-border` | `AlertOctagon` |
| `factoring` | Factoring routing decision | `--color-band-factoring-bg` | `--color-band-factoring-border` | `Banknote` |

**MUST 6.4.4.3 — The Settlement Debt Alert Banner** (per WF-041 + Part 4.5.4) is a `danger` variant that:
- MUST be rendered at the top of the settlement screen when `total_active_debt > 0` (computed live per 4.5.4.3)
- MUST display the LIVE debt amount with `computed_at` timestamp
- MUST include a "Recompute" link that re-fires `recompute_driver_debt()`
- MUST NOT be dismissable; persists until debt is acknowledged or resolved

**MUST 6.4.4.4 — The Outage Banner** (per WF-022) is a `warning` variant displayed at the top of any screen affected by integration outage:
- Appears in chrome-content boundary (below top bar, above section header)
- Text format: "QuickBooks unavailable since 14:32 — local operations continue; sync will replay when restored"
- Persists for the duration of the outage; auto-removes when integration returns to healthy

**MUST 6.4.4.5 — The Held Deduction Marker** (per WF-012) is a `factoring` variant inline in settlement preview screens, indicating a deduction is held until acknowledgment.

### 6.4.5 Modals

**MUST 6.4.5.1 — Modal spec:**
- Backdrop: `--color-bg-overlay`, z-index `--z-modal-backdrop`
- Container: white surface, `--radius-lg`, max-width 600px (sm), 800px (md), 1000px (lg)
- Padding: `--space-7` (24px)
- Header: 16px semibold; close icon top-right
- Body: 13px, scrollable if content exceeds 60% viewport height
- Footer: right-aligned actions; `secondary` for Cancel, `primary` for Confirm

**MUST 6.4.5.2** — Modals MUST trap keyboard focus (Tab cycles within modal; Esc closes). Implements WAI-ARIA dialog pattern.

**MUST 6.4.5.3** — Closing a modal during an in-flight async action MUST show a confirmation ("Cancel this operation?"). Closing CANNOT abort an outbox-queued operation (those are fire-and-forget per Part 3.12); the modal close just dismisses the UI, the operation continues.

**MUST 6.4.5.4 — The WF-064 high-risk confirmation modal:**
- 2-step pattern: Step 1 explains the action and consequences; Step 2 requires typing a confirmation phrase
- Step 1: Modal title prefixed with lightning-bolt icon and "Confirm high-risk action"; description includes specific consequences (e.g. "This will void Settlement S-2026-05-3344 and all 7 lines, totaling $3,847.50")
- Step 2: User types a phrase like "VOID SETTLEMENT" (case-sensitive); Confirm button stays disabled until exact match
- Both steps include a `reason` text field (required, 10-character minimum) that is recorded in the audit event

**MUST 6.4.5.5** — WF-064 confirmation modals MUST NOT have a "Skip confirmation" preference. Owner can disable WF-064 NOTIFICATIONS per WF-064.3 suppression but cannot disable the confirmation step itself.

### 6.4.6 Toasts

**MUST 6.4.6.1 — Toast spec:**
- Position: bottom-right of viewport, fixed
- Width: 320px
- Padding: `--space-5` (12px)
- Border radius: `--radius-md`
- Shadow: `--shadow-lg`
- Auto-dismiss: 5 seconds for `success`/`info`; 10 seconds for `warning`; manual-dismiss for `danger`
- Variants: same five color variants as banners (6.4.4.2)
- Stack: max 3 visible; older toasts stack below newer ones

**MUST 6.4.6.2** — Toasts MUST NEVER carry critical-severity information that requires user action (those go in banners or modals). Toasts are for transient confirmations only.

### 6.4.7 Cards / Panels

**MUST 6.4.7.1 — Card spec:**
- Background: `--color-bg-surface`
- Border: 1px `--color-border-default`
- Border radius: `--radius-md`
- Padding: `--space-6` (16px) for default, `--space-7` (24px) for emphasized
- Optional header: 13px semibold title + optional action area right-aligned
- Optional footer: 11px secondary text, top border `--color-border-subtle`

### 6.4.8 Status badges (small inline pills)

**MUST 6.4.8.1 — Badge spec:**
- Inline-flex, min-height 20px
- Padding: `--space-1` (2px) vertical, `--space-3` (6px) horizontal
- Border radius: `--radius-full`
- Font size: 11px (`--font-size-sm`)
- Font weight: 500
- Background + text per 6.1.1.6 semantic tokens

**MUST 6.4.8.2** — Badges are for **status** (Active, Inactive, Pending, Failed, Voided, Held, Acknowledged). They are NOT for category coding (use color bands for that).

---

## 6.5 — TYPOGRAPHY APPLIED

### 6.5.1 Page hierarchy

**MUST 6.5.1.1** — Page title (in section header band area): 13px (`--font-size-md`) semibold uppercase, `--color-text-primary`. NOT 16-24px like consumer apps.

**MUST 6.5.1.2** — H1 in content (page-level heading below breadcrumb): 14px (`--font-size-lg`) semibold, `--color-text-primary`.

**MUST 6.5.1.3** — H2 (section heading within page): 13px (`--font-size-md`) semibold.

**MUST 6.5.1.4** — H3 (subsection heading): 12px (`--font-size-base`) semibold uppercase.

### 6.5.2 Numeric formatting

**MUST 6.5.2.1 — Currency:**
- Format: `$1,234.56` (USD, 2 decimal places, comma thousands separator)
- Negative: `-$1,234.56` in red `--color-status-danger-text`
- Zero: `$0.00` not "—" or blank (explicit zero shows the field is computed, not missing)

**MUST 6.5.2.2 — Percentages:** `12.5%`, one decimal place, no space before `%`.

**MUST 6.5.2.3 — Dates:** ISO format `YYYY-MM-DD` for data tables; localized format `May 2, 2026` for narrative text and headers.

**MUST 6.5.2.4 — Times:** `14:32 CT` (24-hour, with TZ abbreviation for clarity in trucking ops where timezones matter).

**MUST 6.5.2.5 — Mileage / weight:** `1,234 mi` / `12,345 lb` (comma-separated thousands; explicit unit suffix).

---

## 6.6 — ACCESSIBILITY FLOOR

### 6.6.1 Color contrast

**MUST 6.6.1.1** — All text MUST meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large 18px+). Verified for every color token combination via automated check.

**MUST 6.6.1.2** — Color is NEVER the sole carrier of meaning. Status indicators (red/green) MUST also have icons or text labels. Color bands MUST also have icons.

### 6.6.2 Keyboard navigation

**MUST 6.6.2.1** — Every interactive element MUST be reachable via Tab. Tab order MUST follow visual reading order (top-to-bottom, left-to-right).

**MUST 6.6.2.2** — Skip-to-content link MUST be the first focusable element on every page (visually hidden until focused, then visible).

**MUST 6.6.2.3** — Modal focus trap per 6.4.5.2. Dropdown keyboard nav per 6.3.1.3.

### 6.6.3 Screen reader support

**MUST 6.6.3.1** — All `<button>`, `<a>`, `<input>` MUST have accessible names (visible text or `aria-label`).

**MUST 6.6.3.2** — Tables MUST use proper `<thead>`/`<tbody>` and `<th scope="col">` markup. Decorative tables (used for layout) are FORBIDDEN.

**MUST 6.6.3.3** — Live regions: toast notifications MUST be `aria-live="polite"`; critical banners MUST be `aria-live="assertive"`.

### 6.6.4 Touch targets (PWA + tablet)

**MUST 6.6.4.1** — On mobile/PWA, all interactive elements MUST be minimum 44x44px tap target (per WCAG 2.5.5). Buttons MAY visually be smaller IF they have invisible padding extending the tap target.

**MUST 6.6.4.2** — Form inputs in PWA MUST be minimum 48px tall (allows comfortable single-tap focus on phones with moderate fat-finger tolerance).

### 6.6.5 Reduced motion

**MUST 6.6.5.1** — `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` media query MUST disable all transitions and animations, except critical state changes that would otherwise be invisible (modal in/out becomes instant; toast slide becomes instant fade).

---

## 6.7 — PWA-SPECIFIC DESIGN

### 6.7.1 PWA chrome

**MUST 6.7.1.1** — PWA top bar: 56px tall (taller than desktop's 48 to accommodate touch targets and status bar safe area). Background `--color-pwa-bg-surface` (#1A1F36).

**MUST 6.7.1.2** — PWA bottom navigation: 64px tall (with 8px safe-area padding at bottom for iPhones with home indicator). Contains: Today, Loads, Earnings, Profile (4 sections).

**MUST 6.7.1.3** — PWA does NOT have a desktop-style sidebar.

### 6.7.2 PWA card-list patterns

**MUST 6.7.2.1** — PWA replaces tables with **stacked cards**. Each "row" is a full-width card with multi-line content. Card has 16px padding, 12px gap between cards.

**MUST 6.7.2.2** — Pull-to-refresh MUST be implemented for all data-list screens.

### 6.7.3 PWA acknowledgment screens (per WF-021, WF-058, 3.17)

**MUST 6.7.3.1** — Acknowledgment screens (driver acknowledging a liability, accepting forfeiture clause, signing equipment transfer) MUST:
- Display the full document text in a scrollable area (>50% of viewport)
- Require user to scroll to bottom before "Acknowledge" button is enabled
- Capture geofenced location at acknowledgment time (if available)
- Open device camera for signature capture (drawn signature) OR signed name entry
- Show real-time confirmation: spinner during R2 upload, then green checkmark + "Acknowledgment recorded" with audit_event_uuid timestamp

**MUST 6.7.3.2** — Acknowledgment screens MUST NOT be dismissable until completed or explicitly canceled. Cancellation requires confirmation.

### 6.7.4 PWA dark theme contrast

**MUST 6.7.4.1** — Dark theme MUST maintain WCAG AA contrast (verified per 6.6.1.1). The `--color-pwa-text-primary` (#F7F8FB) on `--color-pwa-bg-surface` (#1A1F36) has contrast ratio 12.4:1 — exceeds AA.

---

## 6.8 — VISUAL TREATMENT FOR HIGH-RISK ACTIONS (WF-064)

This section codifies how WF-064 high-risk actions are visually distinguished throughout the product.

### 6.8.1 Visual markers

**MUST 6.8.1.1** — Buttons firing WF-064 actions MUST display the lightning-bolt icon (`Zap` from Lucide) BEFORE the label per 6.4.2.5. The icon color matches button variant (white on primary, red on danger).

**MUST 6.8.1.2** — Hover dropdown items that trigger WF-064 MUST display the lightning-bolt icon next to the action label, plus a subtle right-border accent in `--color-band-severe-border`.

**MUST 6.8.1.3** — The 2-step confirmation modal per 6.4.5.4 is REQUIRED for all WF-064 actions. No exceptions per 6.4.5.5.

### 6.8.2 The 17 WF-064 actions (core 7 + integration/data-model extensions)

Per Part 3.18.5 Cursor-locked enforcement map:
1. Manual JE creation
2. Account merge
3. Account lifecycle transition (e.g. active → frozen)
4. Account role binding change
5. Posting template version pin
6. Settlement void
7. Receivable disposition (write-off, sent-to-collections)

PLUS Part 5 integration WF-064 actions (8 more):
8. QBO reconnect
9. QBO disconnect
10. QBO realm switch
11. Samsara token rotation
12. Relay token rotation
13. R2 token rotation
14. Twilio token rotation
15. Resend token rotation

PLUS additional WF-064 actions identified in the data model (Part 4):
16. Forfeiture clause flip false → true (master_data.drivers per 4.2.2.6)
17. Driver liability disposition (per 4.5.2.5, owner-only)

**Total: 17 WF-064 actions across the product.** All MUST display the lightning-bolt icon and trigger the 2-step modal.

### 6.8.3 Success and blocked variants

Per Cursor's locked rule (Part 5 confirmation):
- **Success variant** of any WF-064 action MUST emit a `success` toast on completion (e.g. "Settlement voided successfully")
- **Blocked variant** of any WF-064 action MUST emit a `danger` banner explaining why the action was blocked and what the user can do

The audit event symmetry (`{action}.executed` + `{action}.blocked`) is mirrored in the UI feedback symmetry.

---

## 6.9 — TRACEABILITY: WF → DESIGN ELEMENT

| WF | Design element |
|---|---|
| WF-012 (Held Deduction) | Held Deduction Marker per 6.4.4.5 (factoring banner variant) |
| WF-021 (Driver Document Capture) | PWA acknowledgment screens per 6.7.3 |
| WF-022 (Outage Handling) | Outage banner per 6.4.4.4 |
| WF-041 (Settlement Pre-final) | Settlement Debt Alert Banner per 6.4.4.3 |
| WF-046 (Transfer/CC) | Banner factoring variant per 6.4.4.2 |
| WF-058 (Equipment Damage Photos) | PWA acknowledgment screens per 6.7.3 |
| WF-064 (High-Risk Action) | Lightning-bolt button icon per 6.4.2.5 + 2-step modal per 6.4.5.4 + symmetric success/blocked feedback per 6.8.3 |

---

## 6.10 — PARITY TEST IDS (Part 6 series)

- T-120.1: Every component in the codebase uses CSS custom properties from `/design-tokens/`; CI lint `enforce-design-tokens` blocks hardcoded color/spacing values
- T-120.2: Font size >18px in production code outside loading/onboarding paths → CI lint `enforce-font-size-cap` fails
- T-120.3: Every page renders the section header band per 6.2.3 with the correct color, height, and font specs
- T-120.4: KPI cards measure 30px tall; values use tabular-nums; 4-6 cards reflow correctly on tablet breakpoint
- T-121.1: Hover dropdown sized to longest line; "Convert to Work Order" displays without truncation
- T-121.2: Hover dropdown keyboard-accessible: Tab + Enter opens, arrow navigates, Esc closes
- T-121.3: Focus ring visible on all interactive elements regardless of input method
- T-122.1: Table row height 28px; 0.5px border between rows; sticky header on >10 rows
- T-122.2: Numeric columns right-aligned with tabular-nums; negative values in red
- T-122.3: Empty state shows icon + title + description + optional CTA
- T-122.4: Loading state shows 5-10 skeleton rows, never a blank table
- T-122.5: Error state shows error code (e.g. "E_QBO_AUTH_REQUIRED") for support diagnostics
- T-123.1: Primary button performs async action → spinner inside button + disabled state within 200ms
- T-123.2: Danger button click → confirmation modal before execution; no one-click destruction
- T-123.3: WF-064 button displays lightning-bolt icon before label
- T-123.4: WF-064 click opens 2-step confirmation modal: step 1 explains, step 2 requires typed confirmation phrase
- T-123.5: WF-064 modal `Confirm` stays disabled until typed phrase exactly matches
- T-123.6: WF-064 modal `reason` field required, minimum 10 characters
- T-124.1: All 5 banner variants (info/success/warning/danger/factoring) render with correct colors, icons, and accent borders
- T-124.2: Settlement debt alert banner: live recompute fires; banner shows live amount + computed_at timestamp; recompute link works (per Part 4.5.4)
- T-124.3: Outage banner appears below top bar when integration enters outage; auto-removes on resolved
- T-124.4: Held deduction marker appears in settlement preview when WF-012 has held deductions
- T-125.1: Modal traps keyboard focus; Tab cycles within modal; Esc closes
- T-125.2: Modal close during in-flight async → confirmation prompt
- T-126.1: Toasts auto-dismiss per variant timing (5s success, 10s warning, manual danger)
- T-126.2: Toasts NEVER carry critical-action info; those route to banners or modals
- T-127.1: WCAG AA contrast verified for every token combination via automated check
- T-127.2: All interactive elements reachable via Tab in visual reading order
- T-127.3: Skip-to-content link is first focusable element
- T-127.4: Tables use proper thead/tbody/th-scope markup; no decorative tables
- T-127.5: Live regions: toast `aria-live="polite"`, critical banner `aria-live="assertive"`
- T-127.6: Touch targets minimum 44x44px on PWA; form inputs minimum 48px tall
- T-127.7: prefers-reduced-motion disables transitions; modal/toast become instant
- T-128.1: PWA top bar 56px with safe-area padding; bottom nav 64px with safe-area padding
- T-128.2: PWA acknowledgment screen requires scroll-to-bottom before button enables
- T-128.3: PWA dark theme contrast: F7F8FB on 1A1F36 = 12.4:1, exceeds WCAG AA
- T-129.1: All 17 WF-064 actions enumerated in 6.8.2 display lightning-bolt icon and trigger 2-step modal
- T-129.2: WF-064 success variant fires success toast on completion
- T-129.3: WF-064 blocked variant fires danger banner with reason and remediation
- T-129.4: Lightning-bolt icon appears in hover-dropdown items that trigger WF-064 with subtle right-border accent

---

# === FILE: IH35_MASTER_BLUEPRINT_v3_part_7.md ===
