| `master_data.location.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — |
| `master_data.location.create` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `master_data.location.update` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `master_data.equipment.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | self assigned | ✓ |
| `master_data.equipment.create` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — |
| `master_data.equipment.transfer.initiate` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — |
| `master_data.equipment.transfer.confirm` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | ✓ | self assigned | ✓ |
| `master_data.equipment.report_loss` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | self | — |
| `master_data.equipment.report_damage` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | ✓ | self | ✓ |

**WF-064 actions involving this module (3 originated here + 2 inherited from cross-module flows = 5 total participation paths):**

The Master Data module ORIGINATES three WF-064 actions where the high-risk decision and audit event source from a master_data API call. Two additional WF-064 actions INHERIT through master_data because a master_data API call invokes a cross-module service function that itself originates a WF-064 audit. The two categories are mapped explicitly below to eliminate trace ambiguity.

**A. Originated in Master Data (3 actions):**

| # | Action | Originating Module | Why Master Data participates | API endpoint | Audit event(s) (success / blocked) | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `master_data.driver.forfeiture_clause.activate` | master_data | Originates here — the flag flip happens in `master_data.drivers` per Part 4.2.2.6 | `POST /api/drivers/{uuid}/forfeiture-clause/activate` | `master_data.forfeiture_clause_activated` / `master_data.forfeiture_clause_activation_blocked` | T-131.10, T-131.11, T-131.12 |
| 2 | `master_data.unit.retire` | master_data | Originates here — `master_data.units.status = 'retired'` mutation; affects historical reporting + IFTA per 7.2.7 | `POST /api/units/{uuid}/retire` | `master_data.unit_retired` / `master_data.unit_retirement_blocked` | T-132.6, T-132.7 |
| 3 | `master_data.equipment.report_loss` | master_data | Originates here — sets `master_data.equipment.status = 'lost'`; via service function downstream INHERITS to driver_finance liability creation (see row B-1 below) | `POST /api/equipment/{uuid}/report-loss` | `master_data.equipment_loss_reported` / `master_data.equipment_loss_blocked` | T-135.6 |

**B. Inherited through Master Data (2 actions originated in OTHER modules but reachable via master_data flows):**

These actions are NOT originated by master_data API endpoints — they originate in their owning module per Part 6.8.2 enumeration. They appear here only because a master_data API call may trigger them as a downstream side effect (via cross-module service function invocation). The originating module owns the audit event, the WF-064 notification, and the test coverage.

| # | Action | Originating Module | How Master Data triggers it | Originating module API | Originating audit event(s) | Test IDs (originating module) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `driver_finance.driver_liability.disposition` (Part 6.8.2 item 17) | driver_finance | When `master_data.equipment.report_loss` fires, the equipment-loss service function downstream calls `driver_finance.create_liability_from_equipment_loss()` per Part 3.13.3.7; if the driver's separation triggers disposition, that's a downstream WF-064 | `POST /api/driver-finance/liabilities/{uuid}/disposition` | `driver_finance.liability_dispositioned` / `driver_finance.liability_disposition_blocked` | (defined in Part 8 Driver Finance — TBD; tracked by T-150.x range) |
| 2 | `identity.user_deactivated` (analogous to identity.user.deactivate) | identity | Driver deactivation is performed via the identity user.deactivate flow (per 7.1.7) when the linked user is deactivated. Master data participates via the driver record's `status = 'separated'` cascade. The originating WF-064 is identity's. | `POST /api/admin/users/{uuid}/deactivate` | `identity.user_deactivated` / `identity.user_deactivation_blocked` | T-130.14, T-130.15 |

**Note on Part 6.8.2 enumeration items NOT inherited here:** prior drafts speculatively listed `accounting.account.lifecycle_change` (item 3), `qbo.reconnect` (item 8), and `samsara.token.rotate` (item 11) as "inherited." Investigation determined these do NOT actually trigger from master_data flows: classes (auto-created with units) don't have the lifecycle_change WF-064; QBO reconnect and Samsara token rotation are Owner-initiated admin operations independent of master_data writes. They remain in Part 6.8.2's overall 17-action enumeration but are NOT counted as master_data participation.

**Net result of the explicit mapping:**
- **3 WF-064 actions ORIGINATED by master_data API endpoints.** These have full success/blocked event pairs in §7.2.9, dedicated parity tests in §7.2.10, and ⚡ markers in §7.2.7 permission matrix.
- **2 WF-064 actions INHERITED through master_data downstream service calls** (the two rows in Table B above). These are tracked by their originating module's tests; master_data carries no separate test coverage for them.

**Total: master_data participates in 5 WF-064 paths — 3 originated + 2 inherited.** This is the canonical count throughout Part 7 Rev 3 and downstream parts.

**MUST 7.2.7.1 — Driver self-access scope** (per Part 4.2.3.1): drivers SELECT only own row from `drivers` table; only own assigned `equipment` rows; cannot SELECT other drivers, customers, vendors, locations, or non-self equipment.

**MUST 7.2.7.2 — Driver self-update limited fields** (per Part 4.2.3.3): driver MAY update own phone, address_line1, address_line2, city, state, postal_code, emergency_contact_name, emergency_contact_phone via `master_data.driver_update_self_contact` service function. CANNOT update CDL, hire/separation dates, vendor_uuid, escrow config, forfeiture clause, or any debt cache columns.

### 7.2.8 Element 8: Notifications

| Audit event | Channel | Recipient | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `master_data.driver_created` | email | Owner + new driver (welcome) | info |
| `master_data.driver_status_changed` | email | Owner + the driver | warning |
| `master_data.forfeiture_clause_activated` | email | Owner + the driver | warning + WF-064 |
| `master_data.cdl_expiring_30days` | email | Owner + Manager + the driver | warning |
| `master_data.cdl_expiring_7days` | email + SMS | Owner + Manager + the driver | warning + SMS allowed (operationally time-sensitive per Part 5.5.4.2 exception) |
| `master_data.cdl_expired` | email + SMS | Owner + Manager + the driver | critical + SMS |
| `master_data.unit_created` | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `master_data.unit_retired` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `master_data.equipment_transfer_initiated` | email | the receiving driver/user (action required) | warning |
| `master_data.equipment_transferred` | email | Owner + Manager (audit notice) | info |
| `master_data.equipment_loss_reported` | email | Owner + Manager + Safety + the driver | warning + WF-064 |
| `master_data.equipment_damage_reported` | email | Manager + Safety + Mechanic | warning |
| `master_data.customer_created` | email | Accountant + Manager | info |
| `master_data.vendor_created` | email | Accountant | info |

**MUST 7.2.8.1** — CDL expiration cascade (warning at 30d, warning+SMS at 7d, critical+SMS at expired) is the ONE place SMS is operationally appropriate for Master Data per Part 5.5.4.2 exception list. All other notifications go via Resend per Part 5.6.

### 7.2.9 Element 9: Audit events

| Event class | Severity | Subject | Fires WF-064? | Symmetric pair |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `master_data.driver_created` | info | the driver + linked vendor | no | none |
| `master_data.driver_updated` | info | the driver | no | none |
| `master_data.driver_status_changed` | warning | the driver | no | none (own state machine, not WF-064) |
| `master_data.driver_separated` | warning | the driver | no | none |
| `master_data.forfeiture_clause_activated` | warning | the driver + evidence | yes | `master_data.forfeiture_clause_activation_blocked` |
| `master_data.forfeiture_clause_activation_blocked` | warning | the driver | yes | success: `master_data.forfeiture_clause_activated` |
| `master_data.unit_created` | info | the unit + linked class | no | none |
| `master_data.unit_updated` | info | the unit | no | none |
| `master_data.unit_retired` | warning | the unit | yes | `master_data.unit_retirement_blocked` |
| `master_data.unit_retirement_blocked` | warning | the unit | yes | success: `master_data.unit_retired` |
| `master_data.customer_created` | info | the customer | no | none |
| `master_data.customer_updated` | info | the customer | no | none |
| `master_data.customer_detention_config_updated` | info | the customer | no | none |
| `master_data.vendor_created` | info | the vendor | no | none |
| `master_data.vendor_updated` | info | the vendor | no | none |
| `master_data.vendor_tax_id_accessed` | warning | the vendor + accessor | no | none (audit-only; not blocking) |
| `master_data.location_created` | info | the location | no | none |
| `master_data.location_updated` | info | the location | no | none |
| `master_data.location_geofence_pushed_to_samsara` | info (sampled) | the location | no | none |
| `master_data.equipment_created` | info | the equipment | no | none |
| `master_data.equipment_transfer_initiated` | info | the equipment_log | no | none |
| `master_data.equipment_transferred` | info | the equipment_log + equipment | no | none |
| `master_data.equipment_transfer_dual_confirmation_violation` | warning | the equipment_log | no | none (security/integrity audit) |
| `master_data.equipment_loss_reported` | warning | the equipment + the driver | yes | `master_data.equipment_loss_blocked` |
| `master_data.equipment_loss_blocked` | warning | the equipment | yes | success: `master_data.equipment_loss_reported` |
| `master_data.equipment_damage_reported` | warning | the equipment | no | none |
| `master_data.driver_debt_cache_drift_detected` | warning | the driver | no | none (per Part 4.5.4.6) |

**MUST 7.2.9.1** — All event INSERTs go through `audit.append_event()` (per Part 4.9.3.2). Direct INSERT blocked by RLS.

**MUST 7.2.9.2** — `master_data.vendor_tax_id_accessed` MUST be emitted EVERY time the encrypted tax_id column is decrypted and read (no sampling). This is an SOX-style audit requirement for sensitive financial data access.

**MUST 7.2.9.3** — `master_data.driver_debt_cache_drift_detected` is emitted by the daily reconciliation job (per Part 4.5.4.6); 3 occurrences for the same driver in 7 days escalates to a critical Owner notification (trigger bug suspected).

#### 7.2.10.1 Master Data-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_DRIVER_CDL_DUPLICATE` | 409 | A driver with this CDL already exists (active or separated) |
| `E_UNIT_DISPLAY_ID_DUPLICATE` | 409 | A unit with this display_id already exists (active or retired) |
| `E_FORFEITURE_CLAUSE_ALREADY_ACTIVE` | 409 | Attempted to flip already-active clause |
| `E_TRANSFER_DUAL_CONFIRMATION_VIOLATION` | 422 | Confirmer must differ from initiator |
| `E_EQUIPMENT_TRANSFER_PENDING` | 409 | Pending transfer blocks new transfer attempt |
| `E_EQUIPMENT_NOT_ASSIGNABLE` | 422 | Attempted to assign retired/lost equipment |
| `E_UNIT_HAS_OPEN_WO` | 422 | Cannot retire unit with open work orders |
| `E_DRIVER_HAS_ACTIVE_LOAD` | 422 | Cannot separate driver with active dispatched load |
| `E_VIN_CHECKSUM_INVALID` | 400 | VIN check digit failed |
| `E_DEBT_CACHE_DIRECT_UPDATE_FORBIDDEN` | 403 | Cache columns can only be updated via driver_finance.recompute_driver_debt() |

---

## 7.3 — CATALOGS MODULE

### 7.3.1 Element 1: Module overview

**Purpose:** Catalogs owns the system's lookup tables and configuration data — chart of accounts, classes (units-as-classes for QBO), items, payment terms, US states/counties, posting templates, and account role bindings. It is governance-heavy: chart-of-accounts changes are tightly controlled per Part 3.18, posting templates have version-pin semantics, and account role bindings mediate which physical account fulfills which logical role in the system.

**Owning schema:** `catalogs` (per Part 4.3).

**Key invariants:**
- `accounts.code` is immutable after `first_posting_at` is set (per Part 3.18.4.2).
- `accounts.lifecycle_state` transitions follow the lifecycle DAG: draft → active → frozen → archived (per Part 3.18.3.5); backward transitions blocked.
- `classes.unit_uuid` is unique 1:1 with master_data.units (per Part 4.3.2.4); created in same transaction as the unit.
- `posting_templates` rows MUST be idempotent against code constants (per Part 3.18.5.4); deploy-time validation blocks divergence.
- `account_role_bindings`: at any given date, exactly ONE binding active per role_code (per Part 4.3.2.6); overlap or gap forbidden.
- Owning module ownership is enforced (per Part 3.18.2.2): only the owning module can create accounts of its types.

**Cross-module dependencies:**
- READ FROM: `master_data.units` (for class creation; one-to-one mapping)
- WRITES to: `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()`
- WRITES to: `outbox.queue` (QBO sync of accounts, classes, items, payment terms — per Part 3.12.4.8)

**WFs implemented:** WF-028 (Domain-Grouped Catalog Management), WF-061 (Hover-Dropdown Report Library — partial; reports module completes), WF-031 (QBO Auto Sync — catalog mirror per Part 3.12.4.8). Cross-cuts every accounting WF via account/class references.

### 7.3.2 Element 2: UI behaviors

#### 7.3.2.1 Chart of accounts (`/catalogs/accounts`)

**MUST 7.3.2.1.1 — Section header:** "CHART OF ACCOUNTS" per Part 6.2.3.

**MUST 7.3.2.1.2 — Tree view default:** displays accounts in hierarchical tree (parent_account_uuid relationships); collapsible nodes; type icons per account_type.

**MUST 7.3.2.1.3 — Filters:** account_type multi-select, lifecycle_state multi-select, owning_module multi-select, search by code/name.

**MUST 7.3.2.1.4 — Per-account row:** code (immutable badge if first_posting_at set), display_name, account_type badge, lifecycle_state badge per Part 6.4.8 (draft=neutral, active=success, frozen=warning, archived=danger), owning_module badge, qbo_active indicator, balance YTD (live computed from journal_entry_lines), actions hover dropdown.

**MUST 7.3.2.1.5 — Actions:** view detail, edit display_name, change lifecycle_state (lightning-bolt ⚡ WF-064 if active→frozen or any backward attempt), merge into other account (lightning-bolt ⚡ WF-064 per Part 6.8.2), view posting history.

#### 7.3.2.2 Account detail (`/catalogs/accounts/{uuid}`)

**MUST 7.3.2.2.1 — Tabs:** Profile, Postings (links to journal_entry_lines), Lifecycle History, Audit.

**MUST 7.3.2.2.2 — Profile tab fields:** code (read-only after first_posting_at set; banner if locked), display_name (editable), account_type (read-only), owning_module (read-only after creation), lifecycle_state (read-only; change via dedicated button), parent_account_uuid (editable for hierarchy), default_allocation_policy, qbo_account_id + qbo_active.

#### 7.3.2.3 Account merge (`/catalogs/accounts/{uuid}/merge`)

**MUST 7.3.2.3.1 — WF-064 modal per Part 6.4.5.4:** 2-step confirmation. Step 1 explains: "Merging account [from_code] [from_name] INTO [into_code] [into_name] will move all postings from the source to the target. Source account will be archived. This is irreversible." Step 2 requires typed phrase "MERGE ACCOUNT" + reason ≥20 chars.

**MUST 7.3.2.3.2 — Pre-merge validation:** target account must be `lifecycle_state IN ('active','frozen')`. Source account must not be in any active `account_role_bindings`. Both accounts must be the same `account_type`.

#### 7.3.2.4 Posting templates (`/catalogs/posting-templates`)

**MUST 7.3.2.4.1 — List view:** table grouped by template_code; rows show version, owning_module, effective_from, effective_to, wf_ids tags, status (active/superseded).

**MUST 7.3.2.4.2 — Detail view:** read-only display of `lines_json` and `validation_rules_json`. Templates are NOT editable in UI — they are code-managed per Part 3.18.5.4. UI is for inspection and audit only.

**MUST 7.3.2.4.3 — Version pin action:** lightning-bolt ⚡ WF-064. Per Part 6.8.2 enumeration item 5. Allows pinning a specific template version for retroactive corrections; rare Owner-only action.

#### 7.3.2.5 Account role bindings (`/catalogs/account-role-bindings`)

**MUST 7.3.2.5.1 — List view:** table of role_code → account_uuid bindings with effective dates.

**MUST 7.3.2.5.2 — Add/Edit binding:** lightning-bolt ⚡ WF-064 per Part 6.8.2 enumeration item 4. 2-step modal explains the binding change and requires reason. Ensures no overlap/gap on the role_code's effective range (per Part 4.3.2.6 invariant).

#### 7.3.2.6 Items (`/catalogs/items`)

**MUST 7.3.2.6.1** — Standard list/detail/edit pattern. Items are simpler than accounts; no governance state machine. Owner+Admin+Accountant CRUD.

#### 7.3.2.7 Payment terms (`/catalogs/payment-terms`)

**MUST 7.3.2.7.1** — Standard list/detail/edit pattern. Owner+Admin+Accountant CRUD.

### 7.3.3 Element 3: Data entities

The Catalogs module owns these tables (DDL in Part 4.3.1, NOT redefined here):

- `catalogs.accounts` — chart of accounts; canonical reference for journal_entry_lines, bills, expenses, invoices, etc.
- `catalogs.account_merge_records` — append-only merge audit
- `catalogs.classes` — units-as-classes for QBO; canonical reference; one-to-one with master_data.units
- `catalogs.items` — line items for invoices; canonical reference for invoice_lines
- `catalogs.payment_terms` — Net 30 etc.; canonical reference for customers + vendors
- `catalogs.us_states` + `catalogs.us_counties` — geographic lookup
- `catalogs.posting_templates` — template definitions mirrored from code constants
- `catalogs.account_role_bindings` — logical role → physical account mappings

The Catalogs module reads from these canonical reference tables in other schemas:
- `master_data.units` — for class creation linkage

The Catalogs module writes to these shared schemas:
- `audit.audit_events` (via `audit.append_event()`)
- `outbox.queue` (for QBO sync of accounts, classes, items, payment terms — per Part 3.12.4.8)

**No new tables introduced beyond Part 4.3 DDL.** Schema lock per Part 4 Rev 2 honored.

### 7.3.4 Element 4: API contracts

#### `POST /api/catalogs/accounts`

Create a new account in the chart of accounts.

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `catalogs.account.create` (Owner, Admin, Accountant) — but additionally subject to `owning_module` ownership rule per Part 3.18.2.2 (the calling module determines which account types it can create).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/catalogs/accounts
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "code": "5215",
  "display_name": "Tire & Wheel Expense",
  "account_type": "expense",
  "owning_module": "maintenance",
  "parent_account_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct010",
  "default_allocation_policy": "single_unit",
  "lifecycle_state": "draft"
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct215",
    "code": "5215",
    "display_name": "Tire & Wheel Expense",
    "account_type": "expense",
    "owning_module": "maintenance",
    "lifecycle_state": "draft",
    "first_posting_at": null,
    "qbo_account_id": null,
    "qbo_sync_pending": true,
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T19:35:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi10"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Account creation request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "code", "reason": "must match pattern ^[0-9]{4,6}$"},
      {"field": "owning_module", "reason": "must be one of the 12 module names + 'unmapped'"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923cd0"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (409, code conflict):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_ACCOUNT_CODE_DUPLICATE",
    "message": "An account with code 5215 already exists",
    "details": {"existing_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct214", "existing_lifecycle_state": "archived"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cd0"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'catalogs.account.create' for account_type=expense + owning_module=maintenance requires that the calling module match owning_module",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant", "caller_module": "accounting", "required_calling_module": "maintenance"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cd0"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_ACCOUNT_CODE_DUPLICATE` (7.3.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `catalogs.account_created` (info). Account is queued for QBO sync per Part 3.12.4.8.

---

#### `POST /api/catalogs/accounts/{uuid}/lifecycle-transition`

Transition an account's lifecycle_state (draft → active → frozen → archived). **Fires WF-064 — high-risk (per Part 6.8.2 enumeration item 3).**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `catalogs.account.lifecycle_change` (Owner, Admin per role matrix 7.3.7).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/catalogs/accounts/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct215/lifecycle-transition
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "to_lifecycle_state": "active",
  "reason": "Account 5215 reviewed and approved for posting; promoting from draft to active"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct215",
    "from_lifecycle_state": "draft",
    "to_lifecycle_state": "active",
    "lifecycle_state_changed_at": "2026-05-02T19:40:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi11",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif05"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Lifecycle transition request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "to_lifecycle_state", "reason": "must be one of: draft, active, frozen, archived"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 20 characters"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923cd1"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, invalid transition):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION_INVALID",
    "message": "Cannot transition from active to draft (backward transitions blocked per Part 3.18.3.5)",
    "details": {"current_state": "active", "requested_state": "draft", "allowed_next_states": ["frozen", "archived"]},
    "request_id": "req_01923cd1"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'catalogs.account.lifecycle_change' requires Owner or Administrator role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cd1"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION_INVALID` (7.3.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `catalogs.account_lifecycle_changed` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `catalogs.account_lifecycle_change_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/catalogs/accounts/{uuid}/merge`

Merge this account INTO another account. **Fires WF-064 — high-risk (per Part 6.8.2 enumeration item 2).**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `catalogs.account.merge` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/catalogs/accounts/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct099/merge
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "into_account_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct100",
  "reason": "Merging legacy account 5099 (Misc Fuel Expense) into account 5100 (Diesel Fuel Expense) as part of CoA cleanup project; both are expense accounts; source has 47 historical postings that will move to target"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "merge_record_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000merg001",
    "from_account_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct099",
    "into_account_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct100",
    "from_account_postings_moved": 47,
    "from_account_lifecycle_state_after": "archived",
    "merged_at": "2026-05-02T19:45:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi12",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif06"}
  }
}
```

Note: per Part 3.18.7 chart-of-accounts changes do NOT migrate historical postings as a rule (account merges leave historical postings on the original account). The "47 historical postings moved" above is when the user explicitly requests historical reattribution; the default is to LEAVE them on source (mark source archived; reports across boundary surface footnote). The request body would include `migrate_historical_postings: true` (default false) to opt into reattribution.

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Merge request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "into_account_uuid", "reason": "target account not found or in archived/draft state"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 20 characters"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923cd2"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, type mismatch):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_MERGE_ACCOUNT_TYPE_MISMATCH",
    "message": "Cannot merge accounts of different account_types (source=expense, target=income)",
    "details": {"source_type": "expense", "target_type": "income"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cd2"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, role-bound):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_MERGE_SOURCE_ROLE_BOUND",
    "message": "Source account is currently bound to role_code 'fuel_expense_default'; remove role binding first",
    "details": {"role_code": "fuel_expense_default", "binding_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bind001"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cd2"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'catalogs.account.merge' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "administrator"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cd2"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_MERGE_ACCOUNT_TYPE_MISMATCH`, `E_MERGE_SOURCE_ROLE_BOUND` (7.3.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `catalogs.account_merged` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `catalogs.account_merge_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

#### 7.3.4.1 Contract Profile Mapping (DoD closure for permissions not given full endpoint specs)

The Catalogs module has more permission actions in §7.3.7 than full endpoint contracts in §7.3.4. The three high-risk endpoints above (`account.create`, `account.lifecycle-transition`, `account.merge`) are specced in full because they carry novel validation, multi-step workflows, or WF-064 confirmation surfaces. The remaining permission actions are STANDARD CRUD operations covered by reusable **Contract Profiles** defined in this subsection. Each profile encapsulates the strict 6-element DoD (request example + success response + validation error + business error + permission error + error code linkage) once; per-action mapping below specifies which profile applies + the action-specific error taxonomy + the test IDs that exercise it.

This subsection is the formal DoD closure: every permission action in §7.3.7 has either an inline endpoint contract (§7.3.4) OR a row in this mapping table. There is NO permission action without an API mapping artifact.

##### Contract Profile A — Standard CRUD list/read

**Applies to:** any read-only action on a single entity type with optional filters (no mutation, no WF-064, no high-risk).

**Endpoints follow this shape:**
- `GET /api/catalogs/{entity-plural}` — list with filters
- `GET /api/catalogs/{entity-plural}/{uuid}` — single read

**Request example (representative):**
```http
GET /api/catalogs/items?status=active&limit=50&offset=0
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
```

**Success response (200):** `{"data": [{...entity...}], "pagination": {"total": N, "limit": L, "offset": O}}`

**Validation error (400):** filter parameter type mismatches → `E_VALIDATION` with field name + expected type.

**Business error:** Not applicable — read-only endpoint with no business-rule branches.

**Permission error (403):** caller role lacks the required action → `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`.

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (baseline). No audit event on read by default; sampled `catalogs.{entity}_read` (info, sampled 1%) for accounts and posting_templates only (sensitive read tracking).

##### Contract Profile B — Standard CRUD create/update (non-WF-064)

**Applies to:** mutation actions on entities that are NOT high-risk; create/update returns the resulting entity, emits an info-severity audit, and queues QBO outbox where applicable.

**Endpoints follow this shape:**
- `POST /api/catalogs/{entity-plural}`
- `PATCH /api/catalogs/{entity-plural}/{uuid}`

**Request example (representative):**
```http
POST /api/catalogs/items
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{ "code": "FUEL-DEF", "display_name": "Diesel Exhaust Fluid", "item_type": "non_inventory", "default_unit_price": 4.85 }
```

**Success response (201 create / 200 update):** the resulting entity with audit_event_uuid + qbo_sync_pending boolean.

**Validation error (400):** required fields missing, format errors, type mismatches → `E_VALIDATION` with field-specific reasons.

**Business error (409):** uniqueness conflicts (e.g. duplicate code) → entity-specific `E_*_DUPLICATE`. Version conflict on UPDATE → `E_VERSION_CONFLICT` (per Part 4.0.2.2 optimistic concurrency).

**Permission error (403):** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` — calling role lacks the action.

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, entity-specific `E_*_DUPLICATE`, `E_VERSION_CONFLICT`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `catalogs.{entity}_created` or `catalogs.{entity}_updated` (info, sampled 10%). Queues QBO outbox row (per Part 3.12.4.8) for Item, Class, PaymentTerm entities.

##### Contract Profile C — WF-064 mutation with 2-step confirmation

**Applies to:** high-risk mutation actions. Carries the lightning-bolt UI (per Part 6.4.2.5), the 2-step confirmation modal (per Part 6.4.5.4), success/blocked audit symmetry (per Part 6.8.3), and Owner notification.

**Endpoints follow this shape:**
- `POST /api/catalogs/{entity}/{uuid}/{action}` — verb-style for state changes (e.g. `/version-pin`, `/role-bindings/{uuid}`, etc.)

**Request example (representative — see §7.3.4 for `lifecycle-transition` and `merge` as fully-specified instances):**
```http
POST /api/catalogs/posting-templates/{template_code}/version-pin
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{ "to_version": 3, "reason": "Pinning v3 of fuel_purchase_company_card template for retroactive Q4 2025 corrections per Owner directive" }
```

**Success response (200):** the resulting entity state with `audit_event_uuid` + `wf_064_owner_notification: {fired: true, notification_uuid: "..."}`.

**Validation error (400):** missing required fields including `reason` (≥20 chars) → `E_VALIDATION` field-specific.

**Business error (422):** action not allowed in current state → action-specific `E_*` (e.g. `E_LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION_INVALID`, `E_MERGE_ACCOUNT_TYPE_MISMATCH`, `E_ROLE_BINDING_OVERLAP`).

**Permission error (403):** `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` — typically Owner-only for WF-064 actions; refusal includes `caller_role` + `required_roles` array.

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, action-specific `E_*` from §7.3.10.1, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `catalogs.{action}_executed` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `catalogs.{action}_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064) — success/blocked symmetry per Cursor's locked rule.

##### Per-permission Contract Profile assignment

Every permission action in §7.3.7 maps to either a fully-specified endpoint in §7.3.4 (rows marked "FULL") or to a Contract Profile (A / B / C):

| Permission action | Contract artifact | Endpoint(s) | Profile-specific error set | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `catalogs.account.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/catalogs/accounts` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-136.1 (list rendering), trace via §7.3.7 |
| `catalogs.account.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/catalogs/accounts/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-136.6 (first_posting_at populated visible) |
| `catalogs.account.create` | FULL | `POST /api/catalogs/accounts` (§7.3.4) | `E_ACCOUNT_CODE_DUPLICATE`, `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_OWNING_MODULE_MISMATCH` | T-136.1, T-136.2, T-136.4, T-136.5 |
| `catalogs.account.update_display_name` | Profile B | `PATCH /api/catalogs/accounts/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_VERSION_CONFLICT`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_ACCOUNT_CODE_LOCKED` (when attempting code change) | T-136.3 |
| `catalogs.account.lifecycle_change` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `POST /api/catalogs/accounts/{uuid}/lifecycle-transition` (§7.3.4) | `E_LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION_INVALID`, `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-137.1..6 |
| `catalogs.account.merge` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `POST /api/catalogs/accounts/{uuid}/merge` (§7.3.4) | `E_MERGE_ACCOUNT_TYPE_MISMATCH`, `E_MERGE_SOURCE_ROLE_BOUND`, `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-138.1..6 |
| `catalogs.posting_template.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/catalogs/posting-templates` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-139.1 |
| `catalogs.posting_template.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/catalogs/posting-templates/{template_code}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-139.1 |
| `catalogs.posting_template.version_pin` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `POST /api/catalogs/posting-templates/{code}/version-pin` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_TEMPLATE_VERSION_NOT_FOUND` (action-specific; add to §7.3.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-139.3, T-139.4 |
| `catalogs.account_role_binding.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/catalogs/account-role-bindings` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-140.1 (overlap test reads list) |
| `catalogs.account_role_binding.create` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `POST /api/catalogs/account-role-bindings` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_ROLE_BINDING_OVERLAP`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-140.1, T-140.3 |
| `catalogs.account_role_binding.update` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `PATCH /api/catalogs/account-role-bindings/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_ROLE_BINDING_OVERLAP`, `E_ROLE_BINDING_GAP_PROHIBITED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-140.2, T-140.4, T-140.5 |
| `catalogs.class.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/catalogs/classes` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-140.6 (uniqueness verification reads list) |
| `catalogs.class.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/catalogs/classes/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-140.7 |
| `catalogs.item.crud` | Profile A (list/read) + Profile B (create/update) | `GET /api/catalogs/items`, `POST /api/catalogs/items`, `PATCH /api/catalogs/items/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_ITEM_CODE_DUPLICATE` (add to §7.3.10.1), `E_VERSION_CONFLICT`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-140.8 |
| `catalogs.payment_terms.crud` | Profile A (list/read) + Profile B (create/update) | `GET /api/catalogs/payment-terms`, `POST /api/catalogs/payment-terms`, `PATCH /api/catalogs/payment-terms/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PAYMENT_TERMS_NAME_DUPLICATE` (add to §7.3.10.1), `E_VERSION_CONFLICT`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-140.9 |
| `catalogs.us_states.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/catalogs/us-states` | `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (rare; this is read-by-all) | T-140.11 |

**Coverage summary:**
- 3 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §7.3.4 (account.create, account.lifecycle_change, account.merge) — covering all WF-064 actions that needed dedicated treatment
- 14 additional permission actions are mapped via Contract Profiles A/B/C above
- **17 of 17 permission actions in §7.3.7 now have an explicit API mapping artifact.** No permission action is left without DoD closure.

**Three additional error codes referenced above are added to §7.3.10.1 below:** `E_TEMPLATE_VERSION_NOT_FOUND`, `E_ITEM_CODE_DUPLICATE`, `E_PAYMENT_TERMS_NAME_DUPLICATE`. `E_NOT_FOUND` is a baseline error code defined in Part 3 (entity not found).

### 7.3.5 Element 5: Validation rules

**MUST 7.3.5.1 — Account code format:** 4-6 digit numeric string per industry CoA conventions. Validated via regex `^[0-9]{4,6}$`. Letters and special chars rejected.

**MUST 7.3.5.2 — Account code immutability:** UPDATE of `code` blocked by trigger when `first_posting_at IS NOT NULL` (per Part 3.18.4.2). Returns `E_ACCOUNT_CODE_LOCKED`.

**MUST 7.3.5.3 — Lifecycle transition DAG (per Part 3.18.3.5):**
- `draft → active` allowed
- `active → frozen` allowed
- `frozen → archived` allowed
- `frozen → active` allowed (un-freeze)
- All backward transitions otherwise FORBIDDEN (return `E_LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION_INVALID`)
- `archived → *` FORBIDDEN (archived is terminal)

**MUST 7.3.5.4 — Owning module enforcement (per Part 3.18.2.2):** account creation MUST be initiated by the module that owns the account_type per the static ownership map (defined in code; e.g. `expense + maintenance` accounts can only be created by maintenance module API calls). Cross-module creation rejected with `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` referencing the required calling module.

**MUST 7.3.5.5 — Posting template idempotency (per Part 3.18.5.4):** at deploy time, code-defined posting template constants are compared against `catalogs.posting_templates` rows. Mismatches FAIL the deploy. Adding a new template requires both a code change AND a migration creating the row.

**MUST 7.3.5.6 — Role binding non-overlap (per Part 4.3.2.6):** at any given date, exactly one binding active per role_code. INSERT/UPDATE that creates overlap blocked by exclusion constraint or trigger; returns `E_ROLE_BINDING_OVERLAP`. Gap detection (no binding active for an in-use role on a date) blocks bind-removal; returns `E_ROLE_BINDING_GAP_PROHIBITED`.

**MUST 7.3.5.7 — Class one-to-one with unit (per Part 4.3.2.4):** classes.unit_uuid is unique. Create class without matching unit blocked. Delete class while unit references it blocked.

**MUST 7.3.5.8 — Account merge rules:**
- Source and target MUST have same account_type (per 7.3.4 example)
- Source MUST NOT be bound to any active role_code (per 7.3.4 example)
- Target MUST be `lifecycle_state IN ('active', 'frozen')`
- Source becomes `lifecycle_state = 'archived'` after merge
- Default behavior: historical postings stay on source (per Part 3.18.7); opt-in via `migrate_historical_postings: true` in request body to move them

### 7.3.6 Element 6: Accounting effects

**Account created:** queued for QBO sync per Part 3.12.4.8 Account entity sync. `qbo_account_id` populated on confirmation.

**Account merged:** if `migrate_historical_postings: true`, an outbox row is queued for QBO to update all journal entries referencing the source account (this is a complex multi-call operation; deferred behavior in v1 may simply mark the QBO source account inactive without rewriting history). Default behavior: source account marked inactive in QBO (`qbo_active = false` outbox push); journal entries unchanged.

**Account lifecycle transitioned:** sync to QBO happens for `frozen → archived` (sets `qbo_active = false` in QBO). `draft → active` triggers initial QBO Account creation (this is when the account first becomes postable; see Part 3.18.3.4).

**Class created:** queued for QBO sync per Part 3.12.4.8 Class entity sync. Atomic with master_data unit creation.

**Item / payment_terms created:** queued for QBO sync.

**No direct journal entries are emitted from Catalogs.** All accounting postings happen through the accounting module's `accounting.post()` service function which references catalog entries.

¹ Account creation additionally subject to `owning_module` enforcement per 7.3.5.4 — the calling module MUST match `owning_module` parameter.

**WF-064 actions in this module (5 total — included in Part 6.8.2 enumeration as items 2, 3, 4, 5):**
1. `catalogs.account.merge` — irreversible aggregation; affects historical reporting
2. `catalogs.account.lifecycle_change` — frozen accounts cannot accept new postings; archive is terminal
3. `catalogs.account_role_binding.create` — changes which physical account fulfills a logical role across the system
4. `catalogs.account_role_binding.update` — same risk as above
5. `catalogs.posting_template.version_pin` — retroactive correction; affects historical postings

#### 7.3.10.1 Catalogs-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_ACCOUNT_CODE_DUPLICATE` | 409 | Code already in use (any lifecycle_state) |
| `E_ACCOUNT_CODE_LOCKED` | 422 | Cannot update code after first posting |
| `E_LIFECYCLE_TRANSITION_INVALID` | 422 | Backward or terminal-state transition forbidden |
| `E_POSTING_ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVE` | 422 | Posting to draft/archived account forbidden (per Part 4.6.2.3; emitted from accounting module but originates from catalogs lifecycle state) |
| `E_MERGE_ACCOUNT_TYPE_MISMATCH` | 422 | Source and target account_types differ |
| `E_MERGE_SOURCE_ROLE_BOUND` | 422 | Source bound to active role; remove binding first |
| `E_ROLE_BINDING_OVERLAP` | 409 | Date-range overlap with existing binding for same role_code |
| `E_ROLE_BINDING_GAP_PROHIBITED` | 422 | Removing only active binding for in-use role |
| `E_OWNING_MODULE_MISMATCH` | 403 | Calling module doesn't match account's owning_module |
| `E_TEMPLATE_DEPLOY_VALIDATION_FAILED` | n/a | Deploy-time check; no HTTP equivalent |
| `E_TEMPLATE_VERSION_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Posting template version_pin requested for non-existent version (introduced via §7.3.4.1 Profile C mapping) |
| `E_ITEM_CODE_DUPLICATE` | 409 | Item code conflict on create/update (introduced via §7.3.4.1 Profile B mapping) |
| `E_PAYMENT_TERMS_NAME_DUPLICATE` | 409 | Payment terms display_name conflict (introduced via §7.3.4.1 Profile B mapping) |
