#### 9.1.10.1 Maintenance-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_WO_STATUS_TRANSITION_INVALID` | 422 | Backward or terminal-state transition forbidden |
| `E_ACCIDENT_WO_REQUIRES_ACCIDENT_RECORD` | 422 | wo_type=accident insert without linked accident_record blocked |
| `E_UNIT_RETIRED_NO_NEW_WO` | 422 | Cannot create WO on retired unit |
| `E_VENDOR_INACTIVE_FOR_WO` | 422 | Vendor must be active for WO creation |
| `E_WO_PHOTO_ATTACH_BLOCKED_TERMINAL` | 422 | Cannot attach photos to closed WO |
| `E_WO_TERMINAL` | 422 | WO in terminal state; mutation blocked |
| `E_WO_CANCEL_REQUIRES_OWNER_HIGH_COST` | 403 | Cancellation of WO with cost > $5000 requires Owner |
| `E_NOT_ACCIDENT_WO` | 422 | Severity escalate only valid on accident WOs |
| `E_INTRANSIT_ALREADY_PROMOTED` | 409 | Issue already promoted to WO |
| `E_INTRANSIT_ALREADY_TERMINAL` | 409 | Issue already in terminal state |

---

## 9.2 — SAFETY MODULE

### 9.2.1 Element 1: Module overview

**Purpose:** Safety owns DOT compliance records (roadside inspections), accident records (created from maintenance accident WOs), insurance claim tracking with multi-policy separation (WF-027), and the Driver Vehicle Inspection Report (DVIR) workflow from PWA (WF-050).

**Owning schema:** `safety` (per Part 4.8.1 — shares Part 4 section with Maintenance + Fuel but separate schema namespace).

**Key invariants:**
- A `safety.accident_records` row is created ONLY via `safety.create_accident_from_wo()` service function from a `wo_type='accident'` work order (per Part 4.8.2.1 + 4.8.6.1 + WF-004 + WF-007). Direct INSERT blocked.
- An `safety.accident_records` row MUST link to exactly one `maintenance.work_orders` row via `source_wo_uuid` (per 4.8.6.2).
- `safety.insurance_claims` rows MAY link to multiple `safety.accident_records` rows (one accident → potentially multiple claims if multiple policies involved per WF-027).
- Each claim is bound to exactly one `master_data.vendors` row (the insurance carrier) at creation; rebinding requires Owner action (per 4.8.6.3).
- DVIR records are append-only; corrections require new DVIR + reason link (per 4.8.6.4 + WF-050).
- DVIR with `defect_severity = 'major'` MUST set the linked unit's `is_dispatch_blocked = true` until the linked WO is closed (per 4.8.6.5 + WF-050).
- Roadside inspection records are append-only; corrections require new record with `corrects_inspection_uuid` linkage (per 4.8.6.6).

**Cross-module dependencies:**
- READS from canonical reference: `master_data.units`, `master_data.drivers`, `master_data.vendors` (insurance carriers), `master_data.locations` (inspection locations), `catalogs.us_states` (DOT jurisdictions).
- READS from `maintenance.work_orders` (when accident_record sources from accident WO).
- WRITES to (via service functions): `master_data.units.is_dispatch_blocked` (for major DVIR defects per WF-050; via `safety.set_unit_dispatch_block()`); `accounting.journal_entry_lines` (for claim financial impacts via `accounting.post()`); `accounting.bills` (for legal fees etc. via `accounting.create_bill_for_claim()`).
- WRITES to `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()`.
- WRITES to `outbox.queue` (transitively via accounting flows for QBO sync).

**WFs implemented:** WF-006, WF-007, WF-027, WF-050. Cross-cuts WF-024 + WF-064.

### 9.2.2 Element 2: UI behaviors

#### 9.2.2.1 Roadside inspections list (`/safety/roadside-inspections`)

**MUST 9.2.2.1.1 — Section header:** "ROADSIDE INSPECTIONS" per Part 6.2.3.

**MUST 9.2.2.1.2 — KPI cards:** total inspections this period, clean count, OOS (out-of-service) count, with violations count, fines pending payment.

**MUST 9.2.2.1.3 — Filters:** outcome (clean / warning / violation / oos), unit, driver, date range, US state.

**MUST 9.2.2.1.4 — Table columns:** inspection_id, inspection_at, inspection_state, unit, driver, outcome badge, violation_count, fine_amount_total, has_followup_wo badge.

#### 9.2.2.2 Accident records list (`/safety/accidents`)

**MUST 9.2.2.2.1 — List columns:** accident_id, accident_at, location_description, unit, involved_driver, severity badge per Part 6.4.4 (red severe for major), source_wo (linked back to maintenance), claim_count, status.

**MUST 9.2.2.2.2 — Detail tabs:** Overview, Source Work Order (linked), Insurance Claims (1-N), Photos, Police Report, Audit.

**MUST 9.2.2.2.3 — Multi-claim creation per WF-027:** "Add Claim" button on accident detail. Form requires: insurance_vendor_uuid (the carrier), policy_number, claim_type (collision / liability / medical / cargo / other), estimated_amount. Each claim is independent; multiple claims per accident allowed.

#### 9.2.2.3 Insurance claims (`/safety/claims`)

**MUST 9.2.2.3.1 — List:** standard table; filters by status, carrier, accident_record link.

**MUST 9.2.2.3.2 — Status workflow:** open → submitted → adjusting → approved/denied → paid → closed. Lightning-bolt ⚡ WF-064 on transitions to `denied`, `closed_with_settlement`, `closed_without_payment` — these are high-risk financial decisions (Owner only).

#### 9.2.2.4 DVIR queue (`/safety/dvir`)

**MUST 9.2.2.4.1 — Purpose:** office-side queue of recent DVIR submissions from PWA (WF-050).

**MUST 9.2.2.4.2 — List columns:** dvir_id, submitted_at, driver, unit, dvir_type (pre_trip / post_trip), defect_count, defect_severity badge (none / minor / major), unit_blocked badge, follow-up WO link.

**MUST 9.2.2.4.3 — Major-defect resolution:** when DVIR has major defect, unit is blocked from dispatch (WF-050). Office must create a follow-up WO, complete it, then `safety.clear_unit_dispatch_block()` is invoked automatically when the follow-up WO transitions to closed.

#### 9.2.2.5 PWA DVIR capture (`/pwa/safety/dvir`)

**MUST 9.2.2.5.1 — Trigger:** driver opens DVIR form at start-shift (pre_trip) or end-shift (post_trip).

**MUST 9.2.2.5.2 — Checklist:** standard DVIR items (brakes, tires, lights, windshield, mirrors, horn, fluids, etc.). Each item: pass / minor_defect / major_defect with optional photo + notes.

**MUST 9.2.2.5.3 — Submission:** writes `safety.dvir_records` row + linked `safety.dvir_defects` rows. If any major defect, sets `master_data.units.is_dispatch_blocked = true` via service function. Cross-module audit event chain.

### 9.2.3 Element 3: Data entities

The Safety module owns these tables (DDL in Part 4.8.1, NOT redefined here):

- `safety.roadside_inspections`
- `safety.accident_records`
- `safety.insurance_claims`
- `safety.claim_status_history` — append-only
- `safety.dvir_records`
- `safety.dvir_defects`

The Safety module reads from canonical references: `master_data.units`, `master_data.drivers`, `master_data.vendors`, `master_data.locations`, `catalogs.us_states`.

The Safety module reads from `maintenance.work_orders` (for accident WO linkage).

The Safety module writes (via service functions only) to:
- `master_data.units.is_dispatch_blocked` (via `safety.set_unit_dispatch_block()` + `safety.clear_unit_dispatch_block()`)
- `accounting.journal_entry_lines` + `accounting.bills` (via standard accounting service functions)

**No new tables introduced beyond Part 4.8.1 DDL.** 6 tables above ARE the safety schema.

### 9.2.4 Element 4: API contracts

#### `POST /api/safety/roadside-inspections`

Record a DOT roadside inspection result (WF-006).

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `safety.roadside_inspection.create` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Safety).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/safety/roadside-inspections
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "inspection_at": "2026-05-02T14:00:00Z",
  "inspection_state": "TX",
  "inspection_location": "I-35 weigh station MM 26 northbound",
  "unit_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000t032",
  "driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000d042",
  "inspector_name": "Trooper J. Garcia",
  "inspection_level": "level_2",
  "outcome": "warning",
  "violations": [
    {"violation_code": "393.9", "description": "Inoperative left rear marker lamp", "severity": "minor"}
  ],
  "fine_amount_cents": 15000,
  "report_evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid060"
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000insp01",
    "display_id": "INSP-25040",
    "outcome": "warning",
    "violation_count": 1,
    "fine_amount_cents": 15000,
    "report_evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid060",
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T22:00:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi50",
    "followup_wo_recommended": true,
    "followup_reason": "Minor violation 393.9 should be repaired and documented"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Roadside inspection request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "inspection_level", "reason": "must be one of: level_1, level_2, level_3, level_4, level_5, level_6"},
      {"field": "outcome", "reason": "must be one of: clean, warning, violation, oos"},
      {"field": "violations", "reason": "required when outcome != 'clean'"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923cf4"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, unit not found):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_UNIT_NOT_FOUND",
    "message": "Unit not found",
    "details": {"unit_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000t999"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf4"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'safety.roadside_inspection.create' requires Owner, Administrator, Manager, or Safety role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "dispatcher"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf4"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_UNIT_NOT_FOUND` (9.2.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success with violations, emits `safety.roadside_inspection_recorded` (warning); clean inspection emits info. OOS outcome additionally sets `master_data.units.is_dispatch_blocked = true` until follow-up WO closes (similar to DVIR major defect path).

---

#### `POST /api/safety/accidents/{uuid}/claims`

Add an insurance claim to an accident record (multi-policy support per WF-027).

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `safety.claim.create` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Safety, Accountant).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/safety/accidents/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acc001/claims
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "carrier_vendor_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000vend020",
  "policy_number": "AUTO-2026-487923",
  "claim_type": "collision",
  "estimated_amount_cents": 850000,
  "deductible_cents": 100000,
  "claim_external_id": "CL-487923-2026-04528",
  "internal_notes": "Collision coverage claim filed with primary auto carrier; expect adjuster contact within 48h"
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000clm001",
    "display_id": "CLM-25040",
    "accident_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acc001",
    "carrier_vendor_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000vend020",
    "policy_number": "AUTO-2026-487923",
    "claim_type": "collision",
    "status": "open",
    "estimated_amount_cents": 850000,
    "deductible_cents": 100000,
    "created_at": "2026-05-02T22:05:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi51"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Claim creation request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "carrier_vendor_uuid", "reason": "must reference an active vendor of type 'insurance'"},
      {"field": "claim_type", "reason": "must be one of: collision, liability, medical, cargo, comprehensive, other"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923cf5"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, accident terminal):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_ACCIDENT_TERMINAL",
    "message": "Cannot add claim to accident in status 'closed'",
    "details": {"accident_status": "closed", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T16:00:00Z"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf5"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'safety.claim.create' requires Owner, Administrator, Manager, Safety, or Accountant role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "dispatcher"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf5"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_ACCIDENT_TERMINAL` (9.2.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `safety.claim_created` (info) with carrier + policy details.

---

#### `POST /api/safety/claims/{uuid}/status-transition`

Transition claim status. Some terminal transitions fire WF-064.

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `safety.claim.transition` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Safety, Accountant for non-WF-064 transitions); `safety.claim.transition.terminal` (Owner only for `denied`, `closed_with_settlement`, `closed_without_payment`).

**Example request (terminal — fires WF-064):**
```http
POST /api/safety/claims/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000clm001/status-transition
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "to_status": "closed_with_settlement",
  "settlement_amount_cents": 720000,
  "reason": "Carrier approved settlement of $7,200 less $1,000 deductible; signing release; closing claim per Owner authorization"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000clm001",
    "from_status": "approved",
    "to_status": "closed_with_settlement",
    "settlement_amount_cents": 720000,
    "transitioned_at": "2026-05-02T22:10:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi52",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif20"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Claim transition request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "to_status", "reason": "must be one of valid claim statuses"},
      {"field": "settlement_amount_cents", "reason": "required when to_status='closed_with_settlement'"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required for terminal transitions; minimum 20 characters"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923cf6"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, invalid transition):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_CLAIM_STATUS_TRANSITION_INVALID",
    "message": "Cannot transition from 'closed_with_settlement' to 'open'; terminal state",
    "details": {"current_status": "closed_with_settlement", "requested_status": "open"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf6"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'safety.claim.transition.terminal' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "manager", "to_status": "closed_with_settlement"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf6"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_CLAIM_STATUS_TRANSITION_INVALID` (9.2.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success (non-terminal), emits `safety.claim_status_changed` (info). On success (terminal), emits `safety.claim_terminal_transitioned` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `safety.claim_terminal_transition_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/pwa/safety/dvir`

PWA endpoint for driver to submit a DVIR (WF-050).

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `safety.dvir.create.pwa` (the assigned driver via PWA only; NOT office staff).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/pwa/safety/dvir
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "dvir_type": "pre_trip",
  "unit_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000t032",
  "checklist": {
    "brakes": {"status": "pass"},
    "tires": {"status": "minor_defect", "notes": "Slight wear on driver-side outside front tire", "evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid070"},
    "lights": {"status": "major_defect", "notes": "Left rear marker lamp inoperative; same issue from yesterday's inspection", "evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid071"},
    "windshield": {"status": "pass"},
    "mirrors": {"status": "pass"},
    "horn": {"status": "pass"},
    "fluids": {"status": "pass"}
  },
  "client_request_id": "dvir-20260502-pretrip-d042-1"
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000dvir01",
    "display_id": "DVIR-25040",
    "dvir_type": "pre_trip",
    "submitted_at": "2026-05-02T22:15:00Z",
    "defect_count_minor": 1,
    "defect_count_major": 1,
    "unit_dispatch_blocked": true,
    "unit_blocked_reason": "Major defect: lights (Left rear marker lamp inoperative)",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi53",
    "followup_required": true,
    "followup_wo_recommended": "Repair WO for lights"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "DVIR submission invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "dvir_type", "reason": "must be one of: pre_trip, post_trip"},
      {"field": "checklist.brakes.status", "reason": "must be one of: pass, minor_defect, major_defect"},
      {"field": "checklist.lights.evidence_uuid", "reason": "required when status=major_defect"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923cf7"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (409, idempotency):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_DVIR_DUPLICATE_REQUEST",
    "message": "DVIR with this client_request_id already submitted; returning original record",
    "details": {"original_dvir_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000dvir01", "submitted_at": "2026-05-02T22:15:00Z"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf7"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'safety.dvir.create.pwa' restricted to the driver assigned to the unit via PWA",
    "details": {"caller_role": "driver", "caller_driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000d055", "unit_assigned_driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000d042"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf7"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_DVIR_DUPLICATE_REQUEST` (9.2.10.1; idempotent return-original behavior), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success with major defect, emits `safety.dvir_submitted` (warning) + `safety.unit_dispatch_blocked_by_dvir` (warning) — both fire to Safety + Manager + Maintenance per 9.2.8.

### 9.2.5 Element 5: Validation rules

**MUST 9.2.5.1 — Roadside inspection violation array consistency:** when outcome != 'clean', violations array MUST be non-empty. Returns `E_VALIDATION` if mismatched.

**MUST 9.2.5.2 — Roadside OOS unit-block:** outcome='oos' MUST set `master_data.units.is_dispatch_blocked = true` via `safety.set_unit_dispatch_block()` service function; also queues critical-severity Owner+Manager email per 9.2.8.

**MUST 9.2.5.3 — Accident record creation atomicity (per Part 4.8.6.1):** `safety.accident_records` rows are ONLY created via `safety.create_accident_from_wo()` service function from a wo_type='accident' work order. Direct INSERT blocked by trigger; returns `E_ACCIDENT_DIRECT_INSERT_FORBIDDEN`.

**MUST 9.2.5.4 — Multi-policy claim independence (per WF-027 + 4.8.6.3):** each `safety.insurance_claims` row links to exactly one `master_data.vendors` (carrier). Cross-claim affecting financial computations are independent — claim A's settlement does NOT auto-cascade to claim B even when both link to the same accident.

**MUST 9.2.5.5 — Claim status DAG:**
- `open → submitted → adjusting → approved | denied`
- `approved → paid → closed`
- `approved → closed_with_settlement` (terminal)
- `denied → closed_without_payment` (terminal)
- All terminal states are one-way; transitions to terminal fire WF-064.

**MUST 9.2.5.6 — DVIR major defect blocks dispatch (per 4.8.6.5 + WF-050):** any DVIR row with at least one defect of severity='major' MUST set `master_data.units.is_dispatch_blocked = true` AND record the dvir_uuid as the blocking source. Dispatch flow checks this flag in WF-044 + load assignment.

**MUST 9.2.5.7 — DVIR major defect → followup WO recommended:** the API response MUST surface `followup_required: true` when major defect present. The followup WO is created via standard `maintenance.work_order.create` flow; once it transitions to `closed`, `safety.clear_unit_dispatch_block()` runs automatically.

**MUST 9.2.5.8 — DVIR PWA idempotency:** PWA endpoint accepts `client_request_id`; duplicate request_id returns 409 with original DVIR record (returns success-equivalent payload, not error). Defensive against retries.

**MUST 9.2.5.9 — DVIR append-only (per 4.8.6.4):** UPDATE/DELETE on `safety.dvir_records` and `safety.dvir_defects` blocked by RLS for all roles. Corrections require new DVIR with `corrects_dvir_uuid` linkage.

**MUST 9.2.5.10 — Roadside inspection append-only (per 4.8.6.6):** same as DVIR — corrections require new record with `corrects_inspection_uuid` linkage.

### 9.2.6 Element 6: Accounting effects

**Roadside inspection with fine_amount_cents > 0:** posts journal entry via `accounting.post()` per posting template `roadside_fine_post`:
- Debit: `regulatory_fines_expense` account (per role binding)
- Credit: `accrued_fines_payable` (until paid via banking flow)

**Accident record creation:** no journal entry yet (the linked WO posting handles maintenance cost; claim flow handles insurance).

**Claim creation:** no journal entry until claim is approved or settled.

**Claim approved → payment received:** posts entry per posting template `claim_payment_received`:
- Debit: cash/bank account
- Credit: `insurance_recovery_revenue` account
- Optional split: deductible portion stays as company expense

**Claim closed_with_settlement (final):** posts entry per posting template `claim_settlement_closed`:
- Debit: cash/bank for settlement received
- Credit: `accident_repair_expense` to offset original maintenance cost
- Net effect: company keeps net amount after deductible

**Claim closed_without_payment (denied/dropped):** no settlement posting; original maintenance cost stays as company expense.

**DVIR submission:** no journal entry (operational record only).

**Posting templates referenced:** `roadside_fine_post`, `claim_payment_received`, `claim_settlement_closed`. All code-managed per Part 3.18.5.

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `safety.roadside_inspection.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | self | ✓ |
| `safety.roadside_inspection.create` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | ✓ | — | — |
| `safety.accident.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | self | ✓ |
| `safety.accident.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | self | ✓ |
| `safety.claim.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — |
| `safety.claim.create` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — |
| `safety.claim.transition` (non-terminal) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — |
| `safety.claim.transition.terminal` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `safety.claim.rebind_carrier` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `safety.dvir.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | ✓ | self | ✓ |
| `safety.dvir.create.pwa` | — | — | — | — | — | — | self | — |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_block.override` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |

#### 9.2.7.1 WF-064 originated vs inherited mapping (Part 7 Tables A/B pattern)

**A. Originated in Safety (3 actions):**

| # | Action | Originating Module | Why Safety is the originator | API endpoint | Audit event(s) (success / blocked) | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `safety.claim.transition.terminal` | safety | Originates here — terminal claim transitions (`denied`, `closed_with_settlement`, `closed_without_payment`) involve final settlement decisions per WF-007 | `POST /api/safety/claims/{uuid}/status-transition` | `safety.claim_terminal_transitioned` / `safety.claim_terminal_transition_blocked` | T-167.5..7 |
| 2 | `safety.claim.rebind_carrier` | safety | Originates here — rebinding a claim from one carrier vendor to another mid-flight; rare but high-impact for accounting per 4.8.6.3 | `POST /api/safety/claims/{uuid}/rebind-carrier` (Profile C — see §9.2.7.3) | `safety.claim_carrier_rebound` / `safety.claim_carrier_rebind_blocked` | T-167.8 |
| 3 | `safety.unit_dispatch_block.override` | safety | Originates here — Owner override of DVIR-major-defect-induced dispatch block; high-risk because it overrides the operational safety check | `POST /api/safety/units/{uuid}/dispatch-block-override` (Profile C) | `safety.unit_dispatch_block_overridden` / `safety.unit_dispatch_block_override_blocked` | T-169.5, T-169.6 |

**B. Inherited through Safety (1 action):**

| # | Action | Originating Module | How Safety is triggered | Originating module API | Originating audit event(s) | Test IDs (originating module) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `maintenance.severity_escalated` (Part 9 §9.1.7.1 Table A row 2) | maintenance | When `maintenance.severity_escalated` fires on an accident WO, the severity-escalate service function downstream calls `safety.update_accident_severity()` to keep accident_records.severity in sync. The WF-064 origination is maintenance's | `POST /api/maintenance/work-orders/{uuid}/escalate-severity` | `maintenance.severity_escalated` / `maintenance.severity_escalation_blocked` | T-162.7, T-162.8 |

**Total: Safety participates in 4 WF-064 paths — 3 originated + 1 inherited.**

#### 9.2.7.2 Driver self-access scope

**MUST 9.2.7.2.1** — Drivers SELECT only own DVIR rows + roadside inspections + accident records where they are listed as `driver_uuid`. RLS enforces.

**MUST 9.2.7.2.2** — Drivers cannot SELECT insurance_claims (financial information out of scope for driver visibility).

#### 9.2.7.3 Contract Profile Mapping (Cursor Part 7.3.4.1 pattern)

| Permission action | Contract artifact | Endpoint(s) | Profile-specific error set | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `safety.roadside_inspection.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/safety/roadside-inspections` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-166.1 |
| `safety.roadside_inspection.create` | FULL | `POST /api/safety/roadside-inspections` (§9.2.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_UNIT_NOT_FOUND`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-166.2..5 |
| `safety.accident.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/safety/accidents` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-167.1 |
| `safety.accident.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/safety/accidents/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-167.2 |
| `safety.claim.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/safety/claims` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-167.3 |
| `safety.claim.create` | FULL | `POST /api/safety/accidents/{uuid}/claims` (§9.2.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_ACCIDENT_TERMINAL`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-167.4 |
| `safety.claim.transition` (non-terminal) | Profile B | `POST /api/safety/claims/{uuid}/status-transition` (with non-terminal target) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_CLAIM_STATUS_TRANSITION_INVALID`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-167.5 |
| `safety.claim.transition.terminal` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | same endpoint with terminal target (§9.2.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_CLAIM_STATUS_TRANSITION_INVALID`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-167.6, T-167.7 |
| `safety.claim.rebind_carrier` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `POST /api/safety/claims/{uuid}/rebind-carrier` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_CLAIM_TERMINAL`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-167.8 |
| `safety.dvir.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/safety/dvir` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-168.1 |
| `safety.dvir.create.pwa` | FULL | `POST /api/pwa/safety/dvir` (§9.2.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_DVIR_DUPLICATE_REQUEST`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-168.2..6 |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_block.override` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `POST /api/safety/units/{uuid}/dispatch-block-override` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_UNIT_NOT_BLOCKED` | T-169.5, T-169.6 |

**Coverage summary:**
- 4 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §9.2.4
- 8 additional permission actions are mapped via Contract Profiles A/B/C
- **12 of 12 permission actions in §9.2.7 now have an explicit API mapping artifact.** No permission action is left without DoD closure.

### 9.2.8 Element 8: Notifications

| Audit event | Channel | Recipient | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `safety.roadside_inspection_recorded` (clean) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `safety.roadside_inspection_recorded` (warning/violation) | email | Owner + Manager + Safety | warning |
| `safety.roadside_inspection_recorded` (oos) | email + SMS | Owner + Manager + Safety + Dispatcher | critical (operationally time-critical per Part 5.5.4.2 exception) |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_blocked_by_oos` | email + SMS | Owner + Manager + Dispatcher | critical |
| `safety.accident_record_created` (severity major+) | email | Owner + Manager + Safety + Accountant | warning |
| `safety.claim_created` | email | Manager + Safety + Accountant | info |
| `safety.claim_status_changed` (non-terminal) | email | Manager + Safety + Accountant | info |
| `safety.claim_terminal_transitioned` | email | Owner + Manager + Safety + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `safety.claim_carrier_rebound` | email | Owner + Manager + Safety + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `safety.dvir_submitted` (no defects) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `safety.dvir_submitted` (minor defects) | email | Manager + Maintenance + Safety | info |
| `safety.dvir_submitted` (major defects) | email + SMS | Owner + Manager + Maintenance + Safety + Dispatcher | critical (operationally time-critical) |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_blocked_by_dvir` | email + SMS | Owner + Manager + Dispatcher | critical |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_block_cleared` | email | Owner + Manager + Dispatcher | info |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_block_overridden` | email + SMS | Owner + Manager + Dispatcher + Safety | warning + WF-064 + SMS (operationally significant) |

**MUST 9.2.8.1 — Operational SMS exceptions:** OOS roadside outcomes, DVIR major defects, and unit dispatch block events are the Safety module's SMS-allowed cases per Part 5.5.4.2. All other Safety events are email-only.

### 9.2.9 Element 9: Audit events

Following the success/blocked symmetry pattern for the 3 originated WF-064 actions, plus routine events:

| Event class | Severity | Subject | Fires WF-064? | Symmetric pair |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `safety.roadside_inspection_recorded` | info/warning/critical (per outcome) | the inspection | no | none |
| `safety.accident_record_created` | warning | the accident + linked WO | no | none |
| `safety.claim_created` | info | the claim | no | none |
| `safety.claim_status_changed` (non-terminal) | info | the claim | no | none |
| `safety.claim_terminal_transitioned` | warning | the claim + status_history row | yes | `safety.claim_terminal_transition_blocked` |
| `safety.claim_terminal_transition_blocked` | warning | the claim | yes | success: `safety.claim_terminal_transitioned` |
| `safety.claim_carrier_rebound` | warning | the claim + before/after carrier | yes | `safety.claim_carrier_rebind_blocked` |
| `safety.claim_carrier_rebind_blocked` | warning | the claim | yes | success: `safety.claim_carrier_rebound` |
| `safety.dvir_submitted` | info/warning/critical (per defect_severity) | the DVIR + defects | no | none |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_blocked_by_oos` | critical | the unit + roadside_inspection_uuid | no | none |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_blocked_by_dvir` | critical | the unit + dvir_uuid | no | none |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_block_cleared` | info | the unit + clearing_wo_uuid | no | none |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_block_overridden` | warning | the unit + override reason | yes | `safety.unit_dispatch_block_override_blocked` |
| `safety.unit_dispatch_block_override_blocked` | warning | the unit | yes | success: `safety.unit_dispatch_block_overridden` |

**MUST 9.2.9.1** — All event INSERTs through `audit.append_event()` per Part 4.9.3.2.

**MUST 9.2.9.2** — 3 originated WF-064 actions × 2 = 6 paired audit events specific to Safety.

### 9.2.10 Element 10: Parity tests

| Test ID | Description | WF traced | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| T-166.1 | GET roadside-inspections RLS-scoped; driver sees only own | WF-024 | 9.2.7 |
| T-166.2 | Create roadside inspection with violations records correctly; emits warning audit | WF-006 | 9.2.4 |
| T-166.3 | Roadside outcome=oos sets unit.is_dispatch_blocked=true via service function; emits critical audit + SMS | WF-006 | 9.2.5.2 + 9.2.8 |
| T-166.4 | Roadside without violations array when outcome != clean returns E_VALIDATION | WF-006 | 9.2.5.1 |
| T-166.5 | Inspection records append-only; UPDATE/DELETE blocked by RLS | WF-006 | 9.2.5.10 |
| T-167.1 | GET accidents RLS-scoped | WF-024 | 9.2.7 |
| T-167.2 | GET accident/{uuid} returns linked WO + claims; RLS for driver self-only | WF-024 | 9.2.7 |
| T-167.3 | GET claims by driver returns empty (drivers cannot SELECT claims) | WF-024 | 9.2.7.2.2 |
| T-167.4 | Create claim on accident; multiple claims per accident allowed (WF-027); each independent | WF-007 + WF-027 | 9.2.5.4 + 9.2.4 |
| T-167.5 | Claim non-terminal transition (open→submitted→adjusting→approved) succeeds for office roles | WF-007 | 9.2.5.5 |
| T-167.6 | Claim terminal transition (closed_with_settlement) by Owner succeeds; emits claim_terminal_transitioned warning + WF-064 | WF-064 + WF-007 | 9.2.5.5 + 9.2.7.1 Table A row 1 |
| T-167.7 | Claim terminal transition by Manager returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED + claim_terminal_transition_blocked | WF-064 | 9.2.7.1 |
| T-167.8 | Claim carrier rebind by Owner emits claim_carrier_rebound + WF-064; by Admin blocked | WF-064 + WF-027 | 9.2.7.1 Table A row 2 |
| T-167.9 | Direct accident INSERT without service function blocked by trigger | WF-004 + WF-007 | 9.2.5.3 |
| T-167.10 | Multi-policy claims on same accident track independently — settlement of claim A does NOT auto-cascade to claim B | WF-027 | 9.2.5.4 |
| T-168.1 | DVIR list shows recent submissions; office roles see all; driver self-only via RLS | WF-024 + WF-050 | 9.2.7 |
| T-168.2 | DVIR submission via PWA records correctly with checklist details | WF-050 | 9.2.4 |
| T-168.3 | DVIR with major defect sets unit.is_dispatch_blocked=true via service function; emits critical SMS | WF-050 | 9.2.5.6 + 9.2.8 |
| T-168.4 | DVIR with major defect requires evidence_uuid for each major defect; missing returns E_VALIDATION | WF-050 | 9.2.5.6 |
| T-168.5 | DVIR with no defects emits sampled audit; no notification | WF-050 | 9.2.8 |
| T-168.6 | DVIR PWA idempotency: duplicate client_request_id returns 409 with original record | WF-050 | 9.2.5.8 |
| T-168.7 | DVIR records append-only; UPDATE/DELETE blocked by RLS | WF-050 | 9.2.5.9 |
| T-168.8 | Office staff cannot create DVIR via PWA endpoint (driver-only) | WF-050 | 9.2.7 |
| T-169.1 | Unit dispatch block by OOS auto-clears when followup WO closed | WF-006 | 9.2.5.2 |
| T-169.2 | Unit dispatch block by DVIR major defect auto-clears when followup WO closed | WF-050 | 9.2.5.7 |
| T-169.3 | Dispatch flow checks is_dispatch_blocked in WF-044 + load assignment; refuses to assign blocked unit | WF-044 + WF-050 | 9.2.5.6 + Part 8a |
| T-169.4 | Multiple block sources on same unit (e.g. OOS + DVIR major) tracked separately; all must clear before unblock | WF-006 + WF-050 | 9.2.5.6 |
| T-169.5 | Unit dispatch block override by Owner succeeds; emits unit_dispatch_block_overridden warning + WF-064 | WF-064 | 9.2.7.1 Table A row 3 |
| T-169.6 | Unit dispatch block override by Manager returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED + WF-064 blocked variant | WF-064 | 9.2.7.1 |
| T-170.1 | Inherited WF-064: maintenance.severity_escalated triggers safety.update_accident_severity() service call | WF-027 + WF-064 | 9.2.7.1 Table B row 1 |
| T-170.2 | Severity update from maintenance side keeps accident_records.severity in sync; verified end-to-end | WF-027 | 9.2.7.1 Table B |
| T-171.1 | Accident with severity_escalation creates audit chain: maintenance.severity_escalated → safety.update_accident_severity → no separate WF-064 from safety | WF-027 | 9.2.7.1 |
| T-171.2 | All 3 originated Safety WF-064 actions display lightning-bolt icon per Part 6.4.2.5 + 2-step modal per Part 6.4.5.4 | WF-064 | 9.2.7 |
| T-172.1 | Profile A (read-only) endpoints: 5 mapped actions all RLS-scoped correctly | WF-024 | 9.2.7.3 |
| T-172.2 | Profile B (CRUD non-WF-064) endpoints: claim non-terminal transitions covered | WF-007 | 9.2.7.3 |
| T-172.3 | Profile C (WF-064 mutation) endpoints: claim_carrier_rebind + unit_dispatch_block_override covered | WF-064 | 9.2.7.3 |

#### 9.2.10.1 Safety-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_UNIT_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Unit referenced in request not found |
| `E_ACCIDENT_DIRECT_INSERT_FORBIDDEN` | 403 | accident_records can only be created via service function from accident WO |
| `E_ACCIDENT_TERMINAL` | 422 | Accident in terminal state; no new claims |
| `E_CLAIM_STATUS_TRANSITION_INVALID` | 422 | Backward or terminal-state transition forbidden |
| `E_CLAIM_TERMINAL` | 422 | Claim in terminal state; no rebind allowed |
| `E_DVIR_DUPLICATE_REQUEST` | 409 | Idempotent return of original DVIR by client_request_id |
| `E_UNIT_NOT_BLOCKED` | 422 | Cannot override dispatch block when unit is not blocked |

---

## 9.3 — FUEL MODULE

### 9.3.1 Element 1: Module overview

**Purpose:** Fuel owns fuel transaction records (from Relay fuel-card webhooks + manual entry), per-state IFTA mileage and fuel data aggregation (WF-019), and the Form 425C monthly compliance report data feed (WF-020). Fuel transactions feed driver_finance for fuel-card overage liabilities (WF-022 — inherited at driver_finance side).

**Owning schema:** `fuel` (per Part 4.8.1).

**Key invariants:**
- A `fuel.fuel_transactions` row is created either via Relay webhook ingest (with `source='relay_webhook'`) or manual entry (with `source='manual'`); manual entries require ≥10-char reason and Owner+Admin+Manager+Accountant role per 4.8.7.1.
- Relay webhook ingestion is idempotent per `relay_external_uuid`; duplicate webhooks MUST NOT create duplicate transactions (per 4.8.7.2 + WF-022 ingestion path).
- Per-state IFTA aggregations (`fuel.ifta_state_quarterly`) are derived rows; UPDATE/DELETE blocked by RLS — recompute via `fuel.recompute_ifta_quarterly()` service function (per 4.8.7.3 + WF-019).
- IFTA filing is one-way Owner action (`fuel.ifta_quarterly_filings.status = 'filed'`); reopen requires Owner reversal flow (per 4.8.7.4).
- Form 425C monthly run is one-way Owner+Accountant action; corrections require Owner reversal flow (per 4.8.7.5 + WF-020).
- Fuel-card overage detection (transaction amount > driver's fuel-card daily/weekly limit per master_data.drivers config) auto-creates a `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` row of type `fuel_card_overage` via service function (per 4.8.7.6 + WF-022).

**Cross-module dependencies:**
- READS from canonical reference: `master_data.units`, `master_data.drivers`, `master_data.locations` (fuel stops), `master_data.vendors` (fuel card issuers like WEX), `catalogs.us_states` (IFTA jurisdictions).
- WRITES to (via service functions): `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` (for fuel-card overage via `driver_finance.create_liability_from_fuel_overage()` per WF-022); `accounting.bills` (for fuel-card vendor invoicing via `accounting.create_bill_for_fuel_period()`).
- WRITES to `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()`.
- WRITES to `outbox.queue` (for QBO bill sync).

**WFs implemented:** WF-019 (IFTA), WF-020 (Form 425C), WF-022 (Fuel card overage — partial; Driver Finance side completes the liability creation). Cross-cuts WF-024 + WF-064.

### 9.3.2 Element 2: UI behaviors

#### 9.3.2.1 Fuel transactions list (`/fuel/transactions`)

**MUST 9.3.2.1.1 — KPI cards:** transactions this period count, total gallons, total amount, avg price-per-gallon, overage transactions count.

**MUST 9.3.2.1.2 — Filters:** date range, driver, unit, vendor (fuel card), state, has-overage flag, source (relay_webhook | manual).

**MUST 9.3.2.1.3 — Table columns:** transaction_id, transaction_at, driver, unit, location (city/state), vendor, gallons, amount_cents, price_per_gallon, is_overage badge, source badge.

#### 9.3.2.2 IFTA quarterly (`/fuel/ifta`)

**MUST 9.3.2.2.1 — Quarter selector:** dropdown with current/past quarters.

**MUST 9.3.2.2.2 — Per-state breakdown:** rows per US state showing miles_in_state, gallons_purchased_in_state, mpg, tax_owed, tax_paid_at_pump, net_owed_or_refund.

**MUST 9.3.2.2.3 — File button** (lightning-bolt ⚡ WF-064): finalizes the quarter's IFTA filing — Owner only per 9.3.7. 2-step confirmation modal.

#### 9.3.2.3 Form 425C monthly (`/fuel/form-425c`)

**MUST 9.3.2.3.1 — Month selector + run button:** generates the monthly 425C export for the selected month.

**MUST 9.3.2.3.2 — Lightning-bolt ⚡ WF-064 on finalization:** Owner+Accountant; 2-step confirmation; emits success/blocked symmetric audit pair.

### 9.3.3 Element 3: Data entities

The Fuel module owns these tables (DDL in Part 4.8.1, NOT redefined here):

- `fuel.fuel_transactions` — canonical fuel-card transactions (Relay webhook + manual)
- `fuel.ifta_state_quarterly` — derived per-state quarterly aggregates
- `fuel.ifta_quarterly_filings` — filing records (one per quarter)
- `fuel.form_425c_runs` — monthly 425C generation records

The Fuel module reads from canonical references: `master_data.units`, `master_data.drivers`, `master_data.locations`, `master_data.vendors`, `catalogs.us_states`.

The Fuel module writes (via service functions only) to:
- `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` (via `driver_finance.create_liability_from_fuel_overage()`)
- `accounting.bills` (via `accounting.create_bill_for_fuel_period()`)

**No new tables introduced beyond Part 4.8.1 DDL.** 4 tables above ARE the fuel schema.

### 9.3.4 Element 4: API contracts

#### `POST /api/fuel/transactions`

Manual fuel transaction entry (used when Relay webhook unavailable or for retroactive corrections).

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `fuel.transaction.manual_create` (Owner, Admin, Manager, Accountant).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/fuel/transactions
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "transaction_at": "2026-05-02T13:45:00Z",
  "driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000d042",
  "unit_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000t032",
  "location_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000locn030",
  "vendor_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000vend030",
  "gallons": 85.5,
  "amount_cents": 32490,
  "fuel_grade": "diesel_clear",
  "reason": "Manual entry — Relay webhook missed this transaction; reconstructed from receipt photo evidence_uuid attached"
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000fuel01",
    "display_id": "FT-25040",
    "source": "manual",
    "transaction_at": "2026-05-02T13:45:00Z",
    "gallons": 85.5,
    "amount_cents": 32490,
    "price_per_gallon_cents": 380,
    "is_overage": false,
    "overage_amount_cents": 0,
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi60"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Fuel transaction request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "gallons", "reason": "must be > 0 and <= 250"},
      {"field": "fuel_grade", "reason": "must be one of: diesel_clear, diesel_dyed, gasoline_regular, gasoline_premium, def"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required for manual entries; minimum 10 characters"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923cf8"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, driver-unit mismatch):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_FUEL_DRIVER_UNIT_NOT_PAIRED",
    "message": "Driver and unit not associated with active load on transaction date",
    "details": {"driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000d042", "unit_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000t032", "transaction_at": "2026-05-02T13:45:00Z"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf8"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'fuel.transaction.manual_create' requires Owner, Administrator, Manager, or Accountant role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "dispatcher"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf8"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_FUEL_DRIVER_UNIT_NOT_PAIRED` (9.3.10.1; warning, not blocking — record allowed but flagged), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `fuel.transaction_recorded` (info) with source=manual; if overage detected, additionally calls `driver_finance.create_liability_from_fuel_overage()` and emits cascading audit per WF-022.

---

#### `POST /api/fuel/ifta/{quarter}/file`

Finalize an IFTA quarterly filing. **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `fuel.ifta.file` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/fuel/ifta/2026-Q1/file
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "filed_with_jurisdiction": "TX",
  "filed_external_reference": "IFTA-TX-2026Q1-487923",
  "total_tax_owed_cents": 1250000,
  "evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid080",
  "reason": "Filed 2026 Q1 IFTA return with Texas as base jurisdiction; payment scheduled for $12,500.00; confirmation reference attached"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "quarter": "2026-Q1",
    "filing_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000filg01",
    "status": "filed",
    "filed_at": "2026-05-02T22:30:00Z",
    "total_tax_owed_cents": 1250000,
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi61",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif30"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "IFTA filing request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "filed_with_jurisdiction", "reason": "must be a valid 2-letter US state code"},
      {"field": "evidence_uuid", "reason": "required; must reference an existing evidence record"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 20 characters"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923cf9"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (409, already filed):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_IFTA_ALREADY_FILED",
    "message": "Quarter 2026-Q1 already filed at 2026-04-25T18:00:00Z",
    "details": {"filed_at": "2026-04-25T18:00:00Z", "filing_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000filg00"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf9"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'fuel.ifta.file' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"},
    "request_id": "req_01923cf9"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_IFTA_ALREADY_FILED` (9.3.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `fuel.ifta_quarterly_filed` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `fuel.ifta_filing_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

### 9.3.5 Element 5: Validation rules

**MUST 9.3.5.1 — Manual entry reason length:** ≥10 chars for source='manual' transactions per 4.8.7.1. Webhook entries don't have reason field (auto-source from Relay).

**MUST 9.3.5.2 — Webhook idempotency (per 4.8.7.2):** `relay_external_uuid` UNIQUE constraint enforces; duplicate webhooks return 200 with original transaction (idempotent reply).

**MUST 9.3.5.3 — Gallons range:** must be > 0 and ≤ 250 (typical truck tank cap; manual entries beyond this require Owner override flag).

**MUST 9.3.5.4 — Driver-unit pairing on transaction date:** if driver and unit are not associated via active dispatch.loads on `transaction_at`, returns warning `E_FUEL_DRIVER_UNIT_NOT_PAIRED` — the transaction is recorded but flagged for review.

**MUST 9.3.5.5 — Overage detection (per WF-022 + 4.8.7.6):** transaction `amount_cents > driver.fuel_card_daily_limit_cents` (cumulative same day) OR `amount_cents > driver.fuel_card_per_transaction_limit_cents` triggers `is_overage = true` and creates `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` row of type `fuel_card_overage` via service function.

**MUST 9.3.5.6 — IFTA quarterly recompute trigger (per 4.8.7.3):** any new fuel_transactions row with transaction_at in the current open quarter triggers `fuel.recompute_ifta_quarterly()` async (queued via outbox — recompute is expensive; not synchronous).

**MUST 9.3.5.7 — IFTA filing one-way (per 4.8.7.4):** `fuel.ifta_quarterly_filings.status = 'filed'` is terminal. UPDATE attempting to revert blocked by trigger; reopen requires Owner reversal flow with new filing record marked `corrects_filing_uuid`.

**MUST 9.3.5.8 — 425C monthly run one-way (per 4.8.7.5):** same pattern as IFTA filing — corrections require Owner reversal flow.

### 9.3.6 Element 6: Accounting effects

**Fuel transaction (Relay webhook or manual):** typically posts AP bill line entry via `accounting.create_bill_for_fuel_period()` (batched at end of period, not per-transaction):
- Debit: `fuel_expense` account (per role binding `fuel_expense_default`)
- Credit: AP control account (vendor liability — fuel card issuer)
- Class: the unit's `qbo_class_uuid` (per master_data → catalogs link)

**Per-state IFTA tax allocation:** at filing time, posts entry via posting template `ifta_tax_post`:
- Debit: `ifta_tax_expense` account
- Credit: cash/bank when payment occurs (separate banking flow)

**Fuel card overage liability creation (cross-module via WF-022):** posts entry via `driver_finance` standard liability creation flow:
- Debit: receivable-from-driver
- Credit: `fuel_card_overage_recovery` account

**Form 425C monthly run:** report-only; no journal entries (425C is regulatory report, not financial transaction).

**Posting templates referenced:** `fuel_card_bill_post`, `ifta_tax_post`. All code-managed per Part 3.18.5.

### 9.3.7 Element 7: Permissions

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `fuel.transaction.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | self | — |
| `fuel.transaction.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | self | — |
| `fuel.transaction.manual_create` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `fuel.transaction.update` (manual entries only) | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `fuel.transaction.delete` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `fuel.ifta.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `fuel.ifta.file` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `fuel.ifta.reopen` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `fuel.form_425c.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `fuel.form_425c.run` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | ✓ ¹ | — | — | — | — |

¹ Accountant allowed for routine monthly runs; only Owner can rerun a previously-completed month (correction flow).

#### 9.3.7.1 WF-064 originated vs inherited mapping (Part 7 Tables A/B pattern)

**A. Originated in Fuel (4 actions):**

| # | Action | Originating Module | Why Fuel is the originator | API endpoint | Audit event(s) (success / blocked) | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `fuel.ifta.file` | fuel | Originates here — IFTA quarterly filing finalization is a regulatory action with one-way semantics per 4.8.7.4; Owner only | `POST /api/fuel/ifta/{quarter}/file` (§9.3.4) | `fuel.ifta_quarterly_filed` / `fuel.ifta_filing_blocked` | T-176.1, T-176.2 |
| 2 | `fuel.ifta.reopen` | fuel | Originates here — reversing a filed IFTA filing is rare Owner-only action with audit-heavy reason | `POST /api/fuel/ifta/{quarter}/reopen` (Profile C — see §9.3.7.3) | `fuel.ifta_filing_reopened` / `fuel.ifta_filing_reopen_blocked` | T-176.3, T-176.4 |
| 3 | `fuel.form_425c.run` | fuel | Originates here — Form 425C monthly finalization is regulatory; one-way per 4.8.7.5 | `POST /api/fuel/form-425c/{month}/run` (Profile C) | `fuel.form_425c_run_completed` / `fuel.form_425c_run_blocked` | T-177.1, T-177.2 |
| 4 | `fuel.transaction.delete` | fuel | Originates here — deletion of fuel transactions affects historical IFTA + 425C aggregations; Owner only | `DELETE /api/fuel/transactions/{uuid}` (Profile C) | `fuel.transaction_deleted` / `fuel.transaction_deletion_blocked` | T-175.6, T-175.7 |

**B. Inherited through Fuel (1 action):**

| # | Action | Originating Module | How Fuel is triggered | Originating module API | Originating audit event(s) | Test IDs (originating module) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `driver_finance.liability.create` (fuel_card_overage variant; cross-cuts WF-022) | driver_finance | When fuel_transaction is recorded with is_overage=true, the fuel module's transaction-create service function downstream calls `driver_finance.create_liability_from_fuel_overage()`. The liability creation is in driver_finance's domain; fuel emits `fuel.transaction_overage_detected` info but the WF-064-style liability handling (acknowledgment + deduction schedule) is owned by driver_finance per Part 8b §8b.1.4. Note: liability CREATION itself is NOT a WF-064 action (per Part 8b §8b.1.7.1) — disposition is. So this row documents the cross-module write but does not represent a WF-064 inheritance. | (cross-module service call from `POST /api/fuel/transactions`) | `driver_finance.liability_created` (info, NOT WF-064) | T-151.4 (Part 8b — liability create in general; specific to fuel-overage tested by T-178.1..3 here) |

**Total: Fuel originates 4 WF-064 actions; 0 actual WF-064 inheritances. Row B-1 is informational documentation of the cross-module write, not a WF-064 inheritance per Cursor's accuracy standard from Part 7 Master Data Tables A/B refinement.**

#### 9.3.7.2 Driver self-access scope

**MUST 9.3.7.2.1** — Drivers SELECT only own fuel_transactions via RLS (driver_uuid match). Cannot SELECT IFTA aggregates or Form 425C records (those are office-only).

#### 9.3.7.3 Contract Profile Mapping (Cursor Part 7.3.4.1 pattern)

| Permission action | Contract artifact | Endpoint(s) | Profile-specific error set | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `fuel.transaction.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/fuel/transactions` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-173.1 |
| `fuel.transaction.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/fuel/transactions/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-173.2 |
| `fuel.transaction.manual_create` | FULL | `POST /api/fuel/transactions` (§9.3.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_FUEL_DRIVER_UNIT_NOT_PAIRED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-173.3..6 |
| `fuel.transaction.update` (manual entries) | Profile B | `PATCH /api/fuel/transactions/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_VERSION_CONFLICT`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_FUEL_TRANSACTION_NOT_MANUAL` | T-175.1 |
| `fuel.transaction.delete` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `DELETE /api/fuel/transactions/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_FUEL_TRANSACTION_AGGREGATED_FILED` | T-175.6, T-175.7 |
| `fuel.ifta.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/fuel/ifta/{quarter}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-174.1 |
| `fuel.ifta.file` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `POST /api/fuel/ifta/{quarter}/file` (§9.3.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_IFTA_ALREADY_FILED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-176.1, T-176.2 |
| `fuel.ifta.reopen` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `POST /api/fuel/ifta/{quarter}/reopen` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_IFTA_NOT_FILED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-176.3, T-176.4 |
| `fuel.form_425c.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/fuel/form-425c/{month}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-177.3 |
| `fuel.form_425c.run` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `POST /api/fuel/form-425c/{month}/run` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_FORM_425C_ALREADY_RUN` | T-177.1, T-177.2 |

**Coverage summary:**
- 2 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §9.3.4
- 8 additional permission actions are mapped via Contract Profiles A/B/C
- **10 of 10 permission actions in §9.3.7 now have an explicit API mapping artifact.** No permission action is left without DoD closure.

### 9.3.8 Element 8: Notifications

| Audit event | Channel | Recipient | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fuel.transaction_recorded` (Relay webhook, no overage) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `fuel.transaction_recorded` (manual entry) | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `fuel.transaction_overage_detected` | email | Manager + Accountant + the driver | warning |
| `fuel.ifta_quarterly_recomputed` | none | n/a | info — sampled (async) |
| `fuel.ifta_quarterly_filed` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `fuel.ifta_filing_reopened` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `fuel.form_425c_run_completed` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `fuel.transaction_deleted` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |

**MUST 9.3.8.1 — No SMS for Fuel events.** Per Part 5.5.4 critical-only-SMS rule, Fuel events are operational/financial but not time-critical to the SMS bar. Email + PWA push only.

### 9.3.9 Element 9: Audit events

| Event class | Severity | Subject | Fires WF-064? | Symmetric pair |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `fuel.transaction_recorded` | info (sampled for webhook; full for manual) | the transaction | no | none |
| `fuel.transaction_overage_detected` | warning | the transaction + linked liability | no | none |
| `fuel.transaction_updated` | info | the transaction | no | none |
| `fuel.transaction_deleted` | warning | the transaction | yes | `fuel.transaction_deletion_blocked` |
| `fuel.transaction_deletion_blocked` | warning | the transaction | yes | success: `fuel.transaction_deleted` |
| `fuel.ifta_quarterly_recomputed` | info (sampled) | the quarter | no | none |
| `fuel.ifta_quarterly_filed` | warning | the filing | yes | `fuel.ifta_filing_blocked` |
| `fuel.ifta_filing_blocked` | warning | the filing | yes | success: `fuel.ifta_quarterly_filed` |
| `fuel.ifta_filing_reopened` | warning | the filing | yes | `fuel.ifta_filing_reopen_blocked` |
| `fuel.ifta_filing_reopen_blocked` | warning | the filing | yes | success: `fuel.ifta_filing_reopened` |
| `fuel.form_425c_run_completed` | warning | the run | yes | `fuel.form_425c_run_blocked` |
| `fuel.form_425c_run_blocked` | warning | the run | yes | success: `fuel.form_425c_run_completed` |

**MUST 9.3.9.1** — All event INSERTs through `audit.append_event()`.

**MUST 9.3.9.2** — 4 originated WF-064 actions × 2 = 8 paired audit events specific to Fuel.

#### 9.3.10.1 Fuel-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_FUEL_DRIVER_UNIT_NOT_PAIRED` | 422 (warning) | Driver and unit not paired on transaction_at; record allowed but flagged |
| `E_FUEL_TRANSACTION_NOT_MANUAL` | 422 | Cannot update/delete webhook-sourced transaction except via Owner deletion flow |
| `E_FUEL_TRANSACTION_AGGREGATED_FILED` | 422 | Transaction is in a filed IFTA quarter; cannot delete |
| `E_IFTA_ALREADY_FILED` | 409 | Quarter already filed; reopen first |
| `E_IFTA_NOT_FILED` | 422 | Cannot reopen quarter that hasn't been filed |
| `E_FORM_425C_ALREADY_RUN` | 409 | Month already has finalized 425C run |
