# IH35 Master Blueprint v3 — Part 10a: Accounting Module

**Section in v3 hierarchy:** Part 10a of 13. Reads after Part 9 (Maintenance + Safety + Fuel). Per Cursor-locked Option 2 chunking and Jorge's split-delivery directive: Part 10a covers Accounting only; Part 10b will cover Banking only; Part 11 will cover Documents + Notifications.

**Status (initial draft):** Awaiting Cursor strict review per the 8-check acceptance gate (template order, API DoD completeness, WF-064 A/B clarity, no schema drift, trace matrix update, test ID continuity, endpoint/profile split, no stale contradictory counts).

All Part 7-9 locks carried forward:
- 10-element module template in order
- Strict 6-element API DoD with explicit "Not applicable" annotation
- WF-064 success/blocked symmetry with explicit Tables A/B
- No schema drift / no new namespaces (14-schema lock)
- Incremental 11-column trace matrix updates
- Sequential, non-colliding test IDs
- FULL endpoint + Contract Profile mapping split for size management

**Normative language:** MUST · MUST NOT · SHOULD · SHOULD NOT · MAY (RFC 2119).

**Test ID range:** Part 10a uses T-180.x through T-189.x exclusively. Verified non-colliding with Parts 1-9 (max prior test ID: T-179.3 in Part 9 §9.3.10).

**Trace matrix:** delivered alongside this Part as appended REQ-ACCT rows in `IH35_REQUIREMENT_TRACE_MATRIX_V3.csv`. 11-column structure preserved.

---

## 10a.0 — ACCOUNTING MODULE PLACEMENT

Accounting is the most invariant-rich module remaining after Driver Finance. It is the local-authoritative ledger (Option C+ per Part 4.6) with QBO mirror via outbox-pattern sync. Every other module in the system writes to Accounting via service functions only (per 4.6.2.6); direct cross-schema INSERT is blocked by RLS.

The module's defining invariants:
1. **Balanced double-entry** (4.6.2.1): every journal entry's debit total = credit total, enforced by trigger at INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE time
2. **Minimum 2 lines** (4.6.2.2): a journal entry without at least 2 lines aborts
3. **Posted-JE immutability** (4.6.2.5): once `status='posted'`, `journal_entry_lines` cannot be UPDATEd or DELETEd; voiding creates a reversing JE per 3.14.6.1
4. **Service-function-only entry** (4.6.2.6): only `accounting.post()` and `accounting.create_manual_je()` can write JEs; direct INSERT blocked
5. **Account lifecycle gating** (4.6.2.3): JE lines can reference only `catalogs.accounts` where `lifecycle_state IN ('active','frozen')`; draft/archived accounts blocked
6. **Auto-maintained open_balance** (4.6.2.7): bill and invoice `open_balance` columns are trigger-maintained from payment line aggregates
7. **Voided-via-reversing-JE** (4.6.2.4): bill/expense/invoice/payment status transition to `voided` triggers automatic reversal JE creation

These invariants are non-negotiable and form the basis of every Accounting permission model decision in §10a.1.7.

WFs implemented in Part 10a:

| WF | Name | Primary scope |
|---|---|---|
| WF-008 | Accounting Period Close & Reopen | Period lock with Owner reversal |
| WF-009 | Bill Creation and Voiding | AP lifecycle + WF-064 on void-after-payment |
| WF-010 | Invoice Creation and Voiding | AR lifecycle + WF-064 on void-after-payment |
| WF-018 | Manual Journal Entry | Owner-controlled adjusting entries |
| WF-021 | Customer Payment Application | Factoring vs ACH paths |
| WF-023 | Bill Payment Application | AP application + reversal flow |
| WF-038 | Reconciliation Findings Resolution | Drift detection per 4.0.7 reconcile + Owner resolution |
| WF-039 | Reversing Journal Entry on Void | Trigger-driven via 4.6.2.4 |
| WF-040 | Posted-JE Immutability Enforcement | DB-trigger guarantee |
| WF-053 | Cross-Module Posting via Service Functions | The 4.6.2.6 entry-point lock |

Cross-cuts WF-024 (RBAC) and WF-064 (high-risk audit) extensively.

---

## 10a.1 — ACCOUNTING MODULE

### 10a.1.1 Element 1: Module overview

**Purpose:** Accounting owns the local-authoritative double-entry ledger for IH 35 Transportation LLC. It records every financial event from every other module — driver settlements, fuel-card bills, work-order costs, insurance claim recoveries, customer load invoicing, factoring assignments, and ACH receipts — through a constrained set of service-function entry points. It mirrors a pruned subset of activity to QuickBooks Online via the outbox pattern (per Part 3.12.4.8) but the local ledger remains source of truth (Option C+ per Part 4.6).

**Owning schema:** `accounting` (per Part 4.6.1).

**Key invariants (recap from §10a.0):**
- Balanced double-entry (sum debit = sum credit) per MUST 4.6.2.1, trigger-enforced
- Minimum 2 lines per JE per MUST 4.6.2.2, trigger-enforced
- Posted JE lines immutable per MUST 4.6.2.5, RLS-enforced
- Direct INSERT to `journal_entries` + `journal_entry_lines` blocked per MUST 4.6.2.6 — only via `accounting.post()` or `accounting.create_manual_je()` service functions
- Account lifecycle_state gate per MUST 4.6.2.3 + Part 3.18.5.5 — JE lines reference only `active` or `frozen` accounts
- Bill/invoice open_balance auto-maintained per MUST 4.6.2.7 by triggers from payment line aggregates
- Voiding-creates-reversing-JE per MUST 4.6.2.4 — bill/expense/invoice/payment void triggers `accounting.create_reversal_je()`

**Cross-module dependencies:**
- READS from canonical reference: `master_data.units` (for class tagging via `qbo_class_uuid`), `master_data.drivers` (for driver-vendor bill payee), `master_data.vendors` (vendor side of bills), `master_data.customers` (customer side of invoices), `catalogs.accounts` (the chart of accounts; lifecycle_state gating), `catalogs.classes` (QBO class), `catalogs.account_role_bindings` (the role-to-account-uuid resolution per Part 3.18.5).
- READS from `dispatch.loads` and `dispatch.settlements` (when invoice or driver settlement bill posts).
- READS from `driver_finance.driver_settlement_lines` (via cross-schema view per Part 4.11) for settlement-driven bill creation.
- WRITES are EXCLUSIVELY via service functions called from other modules. The Accounting module does not "own" creation of any cross-module trigger — every JE/bill/invoice originates from a request in another module that calls one of:
  - `accounting.post(je_payload)` — generic posting
  - `accounting.create_manual_je(je_payload, reason)` — Owner-action manual entry
  - `accounting.create_bill_for_wo(wo_uuid)` — from Maintenance
  - `accounting.create_bill_for_fuel_period(period, vendor_uuid)` — from Fuel
  - `accounting.create_driver_vendor_bill_line(driver_uuid, amount, ...)` — from Driver Finance
  - `accounting.create_invoice_for_load(load_uuid)` — from Dispatch
  - `accounting.create_reversal_je(source_je_uuid, reason)` — automatic via 4.6.2.4 trigger
- WRITES to `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()`.
- WRITES to `outbox.queue` for QBO sync per Part 3.12.4.8.

**WFs implemented:** WF-008, WF-009, WF-010, WF-018, WF-021, WF-023, WF-038, WF-039, WF-040, WF-053. Cross-cuts WF-022 (QBO integration outage handling per Part 3.12.4.8), WF-024 (RBAC), WF-064 (high-risk audit). **13 distinct WFs traced in this module.**

### 10a.1.2 Element 2: UI behaviors

#### 10a.1.2.1 Accounting home (`/accounting`)

**MUST 10a.1.2.1.1 — Section header:** "ACCOUNTING" per Part 6.2.3.

**MUST 10a.1.2.1.2 — KPI cards row** per Part 6.2.4: total AP open balance, total AR open balance, AP aged > 30 days, AR aged > 30 days, current period status (Open / Closing / Closed), unresolved reconciliation findings count, posted JEs this period, manual JEs this period.

**MUST 10a.1.2.1.3 — Tab structure:** Bills, Expenses, Invoices, Payments, Journal Entries, Reconciliation, Periods, Reports.

#### 10a.1.2.2 Bills tab (`/accounting/bills`)

**MUST 10a.1.2.2.1 — List columns:** bill_id (display_id), vendor (linked to master_data.vendors), bill_date, due_date, total_amount, open_balance, status badge (open / partially_paid / paid / voided), source (linked WO/fuel/driver_finance), aged badge per Part 6.4.4 yellow/red bands.

**MUST 10a.1.2.2.2 — Filters:** vendor, status multi-select, date range, source-type, has-open-balance toggle, aged-over-X-days toggle.

**MUST 10a.1.2.2.3 — Bill detail tabs:** Overview, Line Items, Payments Applied, Linked JE, Linked Source (WO/fuel/driver_finance), Audit.

**MUST 10a.1.2.2.4 — Void action** ⚡ WF-064 when `paid_amount > 0` (void-after-payment is high-risk per WF-009): 2-step confirmation modal per Part 6.4.5.4 with typed phrase "VOID BILL" + reason ≥20 chars. Owner-only when `paid_amount > 0`; Owner+Admin+Accountant when `paid_amount = 0`.

#### 10a.1.2.3 Invoices tab (`/accounting/invoices`)

**MUST 10a.1.2.3.1 — List columns:** invoice_id, customer (linked), invoice_date, due_date, total_amount, open_balance, status, factoring_assigned badge (when assigned to Apex per Part 4.6.7), source load (linked).

**MUST 10a.1.2.3.2 — Factoring assignment indicator:** invoices assigned to factoring carry blue `factoring` color band per Part 6.4.4. The factoring assignment is metadata on the invoice; assigned invoices are paid by Apex (the factoring vendor) typically within 1-2 business days; the customer payment when received is routed back to Apex per the assignment.

**MUST 10a.1.2.3.3 — Void action** ⚡ WF-064 same pattern as bill: Owner-only when `paid_amount > 0` OR factoring_assigned = true.

#### 10a.1.2.4 Journal Entries tab (`/accounting/journal-entries`)

**MUST 10a.1.2.4.1 — List columns:** je_id, je_date, total_amount, source (auto / manual / reversal), source_link (linked entity that triggered the JE), status (draft / posted / voided), created_by user.

**MUST 10a.1.2.4.2 — Filters:** date range, source-type multi-select (auto/manual/reversal), status, account UUID (filter to JEs touching a specific account), amount range.

**MUST 10a.1.2.4.3 — Detail view:** read-only display of the JE header + lines table (account, debit, credit, memo, class). Posted JEs cannot be edited per MUST 4.6.2.5 — only voided (which creates a reversing JE).

**MUST 10a.1.2.4.4 — Manual JE creation** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-018): Owner+Admin+Accountant only. 2-step confirmation modal with typed phrase "POST MANUAL JE" + reason ≥30 chars. The form enforces ≥2 lines + balanced totals client-side; server re-validates and rejects unbalanced submissions per MUST 4.6.2.1.

#### 10a.1.2.5 Periods tab (`/accounting/periods`)

**MUST 10a.1.2.5.1 — Period list:** rows per accounting period (typically calendar months); each row shows period_label (e.g. "2026-04 (April 2026)"), status (open / closing / closed), opened_at, closed_at, unposted_drafts_count, unbalanced_je_count (always 0 — these wouldn't have posted), reconciliation_findings_count.

**MUST 10a.1.2.5.2 — Close action** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-008): closes a period. Owner-only. 2-step confirmation modal with typed phrase "CLOSE PERIOD" + reason ≥20 chars. Pre-close checks display: any draft JEs in period (must be posted or voided first), any unresolved reconciliation findings (Owner can override with explicit ack), any pending QBO sync items (warning only, not blocker).

**MUST 10a.1.2.5.3 — Reopen action** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-008): reopens a closed period. Owner-only. 2-step confirmation modal with typed phrase "REOPEN PERIOD" + reason ≥30 chars. Reopens carry critical-severity Owner notification immediately.

#### 10a.1.2.6 Reconciliation tab (`/accounting/reconciliation`)

**MUST 10a.1.2.6.1 — Findings list:** rows per `accounting.reconciliation_findings` row (created by daily reconciliation jobs per Part 4.0.7). Columns: finding_id, detected_at, severity, source_table, source_uuid, finding_type (e.g. balance_mismatch, missing_je, duplicate_je), description, status (open / acknowledged / resolved).

**MUST 10a.1.2.6.2 — Resolution action:** Owner+Admin+Accountant; emits `accounting.reconciliation_finding_resolved` info. Modal requires resolution_action enum (corrected_via_je / accepted_as_drift / false_positive) + reason ≥10 chars + optional resolving_je_uuid link.

### 10a.1.3 Element 3: Data entities

The Accounting module owns these tables (DDL in Part 4.6.1, NOT redefined here — schema lock honored):

- `accounting.bills` — AP bills from vendors (including driver-vendor bills for advances/settlements)
- `accounting.bill_lines` — line items per bill (account + amount + class)
- `accounting.expenses` — direct expenses (e.g. credit card transactions not yet billed)
- `accounting.expense_lines` — line items per expense
- `accounting.bill_payments` — payment records applied to bills
- `accounting.bill_payment_lines` — application of a payment to one or more bills
- `accounting.invoices` — AR invoices to customers (typically per load)
- `accounting.invoice_lines` — line items per invoice
- `accounting.customer_payments` — customer payment receipts (ACH or factoring)
- `accounting.customer_payment_lines` — application of a customer payment to one or more invoices
- `accounting.journal_entries` — JE headers (the canonical double-entry record)
- `accounting.journal_entry_lines` — JE detail lines (account, debit, credit, class, memo)
- `accounting.reconciliation_findings` — drift detection records from daily reconcile job per Part 4.0.7

**13 tables.** No new tables introduced beyond Part 4.6.1 DDL. Schema lock per Part 4 Rev 2 honored.

**Period close implementation note:** Part 4.6.1 defines exactly 13 tables (the list above) and does NOT include an `accounting.accounting_periods` table. Period close in this spec is implemented as state on existing dimensions: `journal_entries.period_label` (YYYY-MM derived from `je_date`), period-close audit events, and the period-close trigger logic that gates posting based on the most recent `accounting.period_closed` audit event for a given `period_label`. Pre-close validation queries derive draft-JE counts and unresolved-finding counts from the existing tables. This is a deliberate non-new-table decision honoring the Part 4 schema lock — adding a new table would be schema drift requiring a Part 4 amendment.

If a future spec amendment introduces a dedicated `accounting.accounting_periods` table for period state (with explicit Owner approval as a lock change), this section MUST be updated to reflect it. Until then, period close remains a derived-state pattern.

**The Accounting module reads from canonical references in other schemas:**
- `master_data.units`, `master_data.drivers`, `master_data.vendors`, `master_data.customers`
- `catalogs.accounts` (with lifecycle_state gating), `catalogs.classes`, `catalogs.account_role_bindings`
- `dispatch.loads`, `dispatch.settlements`
- `driver_finance.driver_settlement_lines` (via cross-schema view per Part 4.11.1)

**The Accounting module is written to EXCLUSIVELY via service functions** (per MUST 4.6.2.6 — direct cross-schema INSERT blocked):
- `accounting.post(je_payload)` — generic posting; called from every other module's service functions
- `accounting.create_manual_je(je_payload, reason)` — Owner-action manual entry
- `accounting.create_bill_for_wo(wo_uuid)` — from Maintenance
- `accounting.create_bill_for_fuel_period(period, vendor_uuid)` — from Fuel
- `accounting.create_driver_vendor_bill_line(driver_uuid, amount, ...)` — from Driver Finance
- `accounting.create_invoice_for_load(load_uuid)` — from Dispatch
- `accounting.create_reversal_je(source_je_uuid, reason)` — automatic from MUST 4.6.2.4 trigger

**The Accounting module writes to shared schemas:**
- `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()`
- `outbox.queue` for QBO sync per Part 3.12.4.8

### 10a.1.4 Element 4: API contracts

This module follows the FULL endpoint + Contract Profile split lock. FULL endpoints below cover the highest-risk and most-novel paths: manual JE post, period close, bill void, invoice void, reconciliation finding resolution. Standard CRUD + query endpoints map to Profile A/B/C in §10a.1.7.3.

#### `POST /api/accounting/journal-entries/manual`

Create a manual journal entry (WF-018). **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `accounting.journal_entry.create_manual` (Owner, Admin, Accountant per MUST 4.6.3.3).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/accounting/journal-entries/manual
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "je_date": "2026-05-02",
  "memo": "Reclassify Q1 fuel tax accrual from regulatory_fines_expense to ifta_tax_expense per CPA review",
  "reason": "CPA review of Q1 books identified miscategorized fuel tax accrual; reclassifying $2,847.50 to correct account; supporting workpaper attached as evidence",
  "lines": [
    {
      "account_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct010",
      "account_role_binding": "regulatory_fines_expense",
      "debit_cents": 0,
      "credit_cents": 284750,
      "class_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000clas005",
      "memo": "Reverse Q1 misposting"
    },
    {
      "account_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000acct015",
      "account_role_binding": "ifta_tax_expense",
      "debit_cents": 284750,
      "credit_cents": 0,
      "class_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000clas005",
      "memo": "Correct Q1 IFTA tax accrual classification"
    }
  ],
  "evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid090"
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0001",
    "display_id": "JE-26050",
    "je_date": "2026-05-02",
    "source": "manual",
    "status": "posted",
    "total_debit_cents": 284750,
    "total_credit_cents": 284750,
    "is_balanced": true,
    "line_count": 2,
    "period_label": "2026-05",
    "posted_at": "2026-05-02T22:50:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi70",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif40"},
    "outbox_qbo_queued": true
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Manual JE creation request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "lines", "reason": "must contain ≥2 lines per MUST 4.6.2.2"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters"},
      {"field": "lines[*].account_uuid", "reason": "must reference an account with lifecycle_state in (active, frozen)"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923d00"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, unbalanced):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_JE_UNBALANCED",
    "message": "Sum of debits ($2,847.50) does not equal sum of credits ($2,800.00) — JE must balance per MUST 4.6.2.1",
    "details": {"total_debit_cents": 284750, "total_credit_cents": 280000, "delta_cents": 4750},
    "request_id": "req_01923d00"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, period closed):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERIOD_CLOSED",
    "message": "Cannot post JE to closed period 2026-04; reopen period first via Owner reversal flow",
    "details": {"period_label": "2026-04", "period_status": "closed", "closed_at": "2026-05-01T18:00:00Z"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d00"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.journal_entry.create_manual' requires Owner, Administrator, or Accountant role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "manager"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d00"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_JE_UNBALANCED` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERIOD_CLOSED` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `accounting.manual_je_posted` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `accounting.manual_je_post_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/accounting/journal-entries/{uuid}/void`

Void a posted journal entry by creating a reversing JE (WF-039 + WF-040 enforcement). **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `accounting.journal_entry.void` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/accounting/journal-entries/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0001/void
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Owner-directed reversal of manual JE-26050 after CPA second review identified the original posting was correct; reversing to restore original state"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "voided_je_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0001",
    "voided_je_display_id": "JE-26050",
    "reversing_je_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0002",
    "reversing_je_display_id": "JE-26051",
    "voided_at": "2026-05-02T23:00:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi71",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif41"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Void request invalid",
    "details": [{"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 20 characters"}],
    "request_id": "req_01923d01"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, already voided):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_JE_ALREADY_VOIDED",
    "message": "JE already voided at 2026-04-15T10:00:00Z by reversing JE-25998",
    "details": {"voided_at": "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z", "reversing_je_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0099"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d01"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.journal_entry.void' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d01"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_JE_ALREADY_VOIDED` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `accounting.journal_entry_voided` (warning, fires WF-064) AND atomically emits `accounting.reversing_je_created` (info) for the new reversing JE. On block, emits `accounting.journal_entry_void_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/accounting/bills/{uuid}/void`

Void a bill. Triggers reversing JE per MUST 4.6.2.4. **Fires WF-064 when `paid_amount > 0` (void-after-payment per WF-009).**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `accounting.bill.void` (Owner+Admin+Accountant when `paid_amount = 0`); `accounting.bill.void.after_payment` (Owner only when `paid_amount > 0`).

**Example request (no payment yet — non-WF-064):**
```http
POST /api/accounting/bills/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bill010/void
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Vendor sent corrected bill with different amount; voiding original to be replaced by new bill"
}
```

**Example request (after payment — WF-064):**
```http
POST /api/accounting/bills/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bill015/void
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Owner-directed void of paid bill BL-26023 after discovering duplicate posting; reversing JE will restore vendor balance and customer payment will be re-applied",
  "acknowledge_paid_status": true
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "bill_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bill015",
    "bill_display_id": "BL-26023",
    "previous_status": "paid",
    "new_status": "voided",
    "paid_amount_at_void_cents": 125000,
    "reversing_je_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0003",
    "reversing_je_display_id": "JE-26052",
    "voided_at": "2026-05-02T23:05:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi72",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif42"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Bill void request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 20 characters when paid_amount=0; minimum 30 characters when paid_amount>0"},
      {"field": "acknowledge_paid_status", "reason": "required and must be true when paid_amount > 0"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923d02"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, already voided):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_BILL_ALREADY_VOIDED",
    "message": "Bill already voided at 2026-04-20T16:00:00Z",
    "details": {"voided_at": "2026-04-20T16:00:00Z", "reversing_je_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0080"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d02"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403, paid bill voided by Accountant):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.bill.void.after_payment' requires Owner role; bill has paid_amount > 0",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant", "paid_amount_cents": 125000},
    "request_id": "req_01923d02"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BILL_ALREADY_VOIDED` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success (no payment), emits `accounting.bill_voided` (info). On success (after payment), emits `accounting.bill_voided_after_payment` (warning, fires WF-064). On block of after-payment void, emits `accounting.bill_void_after_payment_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry). Reversing JE atomically created per MUST 4.6.2.4.

---

#### `POST /api/accounting/invoices/{uuid}/void`

Void an invoice. Same pattern as bill void with WF-064 when paid OR factoring-assigned (WF-010 adds factoring trigger).

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `accounting.invoice.void` (Owner+Admin+Accountant when `paid_amount = 0` AND `factoring_assigned = false`); `accounting.invoice.void.after_payment_or_factoring` (Owner only when `paid_amount > 0` OR `factoring_assigned = true`).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/accounting/invoices/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000inv010/void
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Customer disputed line items; voiding invoice INV-26015 and reissuing as INV-26020 with corrected detail; this invoice was assigned to Apex factoring — Owner authorization required",
  "acknowledge_factoring_assigned": true,
  "acknowledge_paid_status": false
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "invoice_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000inv010",
    "invoice_display_id": "INV-26015",
    "previous_status": "open",
    "new_status": "voided",
    "factoring_assigned_at_void": true,
    "factoring_vendor_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000vend040",
    "paid_amount_at_void_cents": 0,
    "reversing_je_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0004",
    "reversing_je_display_id": "JE-26053",
    "voided_at": "2026-05-02T23:10:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi73",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif43"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Invoice void request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters when paid_amount > 0 OR factoring_assigned = true"},
      {"field": "acknowledge_factoring_assigned", "reason": "required and must be true when factoring_assigned = true"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923d03"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, already voided):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_INVOICE_ALREADY_VOIDED",
    "message": "Invoice already voided at 2026-04-15T10:00:00Z",
    "details": {"voided_at": "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d03"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403, factoring-assigned by Accountant):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.invoice.void.after_payment_or_factoring' requires Owner role; invoice is factoring-assigned",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant", "factoring_assigned": true, "factoring_vendor_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000vend040"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d03"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_INVOICE_ALREADY_VOIDED` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success (clean), emits `accounting.invoice_voided` (info). On success (after-payment-or-factoring), emits `accounting.invoice_voided_after_payment_or_factoring` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `accounting.invoice_void_after_payment_or_factoring_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry). Reversing JE atomically created.

---

#### `POST /api/accounting/periods/{period_label}/close`

Close an accounting period (WF-008). **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `accounting.period.close` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/accounting/periods/2026-04/close
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "April 2026 period close after CPA review of all postings; all draft JEs posted or voided; reconciliation findings reviewed and resolved or accepted",
  "acknowledge_unresolved_findings": false
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "period_label": "2026-04",
    "previous_status": "open",
    "new_status": "closed",
    "closed_at": "2026-05-02T23:15:00Z",
    "pre_close_check": {
      "draft_je_count": 0,
      "unresolved_finding_count": 0,
      "pending_qbo_sync_count": 3,
      "all_blockers_clear": true
    },
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi74",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif44"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Period close request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "period_label", "reason": "must be in YYYY-MM format"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 20 characters"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923d04"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, draft JEs exist):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERIOD_CLOSE_BLOCKED_BY_DRAFTS",
    "message": "Cannot close period 2026-04 — 3 draft JEs must be posted or voided first",
    "details": {"draft_je_count": 3, "draft_je_uuids": ["01923c8a-...je0050", "01923c8a-...je0051", "01923c8a-...je0052"]},
    "request_id": "req_01923d04"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, unresolved findings):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERIOD_CLOSE_BLOCKED_BY_FINDINGS",
    "message": "Cannot close period 2026-04 — 2 unresolved reconciliation findings; resolve them or set acknowledge_unresolved_findings=true with reason explaining acceptance",
    "details": {"unresolved_finding_count": 2, "unresolved_finding_uuids": ["01923c8a-...rec001", "01923c8a-...rec002"]},
    "request_id": "req_01923d04"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.period.close' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d04"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERIOD_CLOSE_BLOCKED_BY_DRAFTS` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERIOD_CLOSE_BLOCKED_BY_FINDINGS` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERIOD_ALREADY_CLOSED` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `accounting.period_closed` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `accounting.period_close_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/accounting/periods/{period_label}/reopen`

Reopen a closed period. **Fires WF-064 — critical-severity high-risk action.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `accounting.period.reopen` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/accounting/periods/2026-04/reopen
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "CPA identified material misstatement in April books requiring reopening for adjusting entries; period 2026-04 will be reclosed within 48 hours after corrections; tax filing impact reviewed and acceptable"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "period_label": "2026-04",
    "previous_status": "closed",
    "new_status": "open",
    "previously_closed_at": "2026-05-01T18:00:00Z",
    "reopened_at": "2026-05-02T23:20:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi75",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif45", "severity": "critical"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Period reopen request invalid",
    "details": [{"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters"}]
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, not closed):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERIOD_NOT_CLOSED",
    "message": "Cannot reopen period 2026-04 — current status is 'open'",
    "details": {"period_status": "open"}
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.period.reopen' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"}
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERIOD_NOT_CLOSED` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `accounting.period_reopened` (critical, fires WF-064 with critical-severity Owner+Admin notification per 10a.1.8). On block, emits `accounting.period_reopen_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/accounting/reconciliation-findings/{uuid}/resolve`

Resolve a reconciliation finding (WF-038).

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `accounting.reconciliation_finding.resolve` (Owner+Admin+Accountant per MUST 4.6.3.4).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/accounting/reconciliation-findings/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000rec001/resolve
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "resolution_action": "corrected_via_je",
  "resolving_je_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0005",
  "reason": "Manual JE-26054 posted to correct the $42.50 drift between bill BL-26020 and the reconciliation finding"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "finding_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000rec001",
    "previous_status": "open",
    "new_status": "resolved",
    "resolution_action": "corrected_via_je",
    "resolving_je_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0005",
    "resolved_at": "2026-05-02T23:25:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi76"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Resolution request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "resolution_action", "reason": "must be one of: corrected_via_je, accepted_as_drift, false_positive"},
      {"field": "resolving_je_uuid", "reason": "required when resolution_action=corrected_via_je"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 10 characters"}
    ]
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (409, already resolved):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_FINDING_ALREADY_RESOLVED",
    "message": "Finding already resolved at 2026-04-30T14:00:00Z by user U-123",
    "details": {"resolved_at": "2026-04-30T14:00:00Z", "resolution_action": "accepted_as_drift"}
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'accounting.reconciliation_finding.resolve' requires Owner, Administrator, or Accountant role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "manager"}
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_FINDING_ALREADY_RESOLVED` (10a.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `accounting.reconciliation_finding_resolved` (info). Not a WF-064 action — finding resolution is routine ops; the underlying drift/correction may have triggered WF-064 events at its source module.

### 10a.1.5 Element 5: Validation rules

**MUST 10a.1.5.1 — Balanced double-entry on every JE (per MUST 4.6.2.1):** sum of debit_cents = sum of credit_cents across `journal_entry_lines`. Trigger enforces at INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE; transaction aborts with `E_JE_UNBALANCED` on imbalance.

**MUST 10a.1.5.2 — Minimum 2 lines per JE (per MUST 4.6.2.2):** trigger enforces; INSERT of JE without ≥2 lines aborts with `E_JE_INSUFFICIENT_LINES`.

**MUST 10a.1.5.3 — Account lifecycle gate (per MUST 4.6.2.3 + Part 3.18.5.5):** JE lines reference accounts where `lifecycle_state IN ('active', 'frozen')`. Lines referencing draft or archived accounts return `E_ACCOUNT_LIFECYCLE_FORBIDDEN`. The gate is enforced both at API validation AND at trigger level (defense-in-depth per Part 3.18.5.5).

**MUST 10a.1.5.4 — Posted JE immutability (per MUST 4.6.2.5 + WF-040):** UPDATE or DELETE on `journal_entry_lines` where parent JE `status='posted'` blocked by RLS for ALL roles including Owner. Voiding is the only path; voiding creates a reversing JE per MUST 4.6.2.4.

**MUST 10a.1.5.5 — Service-function-only entry (per MUST 4.6.2.6 + WF-053):** direct INSERT to `journal_entries` and `journal_entry_lines` blocked by RLS regardless of role. Only `accounting.post()` and `accounting.create_manual_je()` service functions can write JEs. Direct INSERT attempt returns `E_JE_DIRECT_INSERT_FORBIDDEN`.

**MUST 10a.1.5.6 — Voiding triggers reversing JE (per MUST 4.6.2.4 + WF-039):** bill, expense, invoice, bill_payment, customer_payment status transition to `voided` MUST atomically create a reversing JE via `accounting.create_reversal_je()`. The original JE remains `posted` (not modified); the reversing JE has equal-and-opposite line amounts. Trigger enforces; manual void without reversal is impossible.

**MUST 10a.1.5.7 — Auto-maintained open_balance (per MUST 4.6.2.7):** `bills.open_balance` and `invoices.open_balance` columns are trigger-maintained as `total_amount - SUM(payment_lines.amount_applied)`. Direct UPDATE to these columns blocked by trigger; returns `E_OPEN_BALANCE_DIRECT_UPDATE_FORBIDDEN`.

**MUST 10a.1.5.8 — Manual JE reason length:** `reason` field MUST be ≥30 chars for manual JEs (longer than the standard ≥20 chars for typical WF-064 actions because manual JEs have no other source attribution).

**MUST 10a.1.5.9 — Period close pre-checks (per WF-008):** close blocked when:
- Draft JE count > 0 in the period (returns `E_PERIOD_CLOSE_BLOCKED_BY_DRAFTS`)
- Unresolved reconciliation findings > 0 (returns `E_PERIOD_CLOSE_BLOCKED_BY_FINDINGS` UNLESS `acknowledge_unresolved_findings=true` with reason explaining acceptance)
- Pending QBO sync items > 0 — warning only, not blocker (per 10a.1.2.5.2)

**MUST 10a.1.5.10 — Period close terminal (per WF-008):** transitioning a period from `open → closed` is one-way at the API layer; reopen is a separate critical-severity Owner action. Status field cannot be UPDATEd directly.

**MUST 10a.1.5.11 — JE period_label inferred at insert:** the `period_label` (YYYY-MM) on each JE is derived from `je_date`; cannot be overridden. Posting a JE with `je_date` in a closed period returns `E_PERIOD_CLOSED`.

**MUST 10a.1.5.12 — Bill/invoice void requires acknowledge flags when high-risk:** void of bill with `paid_amount > 0` requires `acknowledge_paid_status: true` in body. Void of invoice with `paid_amount > 0` OR `factoring_assigned = true` requires both `acknowledge_paid_status` and/or `acknowledge_factoring_assigned` as appropriate. Missing acknowledge returns `E_VALIDATION`. This is the explicit-confirmation pattern from Part 6.4.5.4 enforced at the API layer.

**MUST 10a.1.5.13 — Reversing JE traceability:** every reversing JE has `reverses_je_uuid` set to the original JE; the original JE has `reversed_by_je_uuid` set after reversal. This bidirectional linkage is enforced atomically by `accounting.create_reversal_je()`.

**MUST 10a.1.5.14 — QBO mirror is outbox-only (per Part 3.12.4.8):** every JE/bill/invoice/payment INSERT/status-change queues an outbox row for QBO sync. Local writes commit independently of QBO availability; the outbox processor handles retries on QBO outage per WF-022.

**MUST 10a.1.5.15 — Period close cascade to QBO:** period close also closes the corresponding QBO period via outbox-queued sync. QBO close failure does NOT block local close — the local period close stands; the QBO sync retries.

**MUST 10a.1.5.16 — Reconciliation finding resolution does not modify source rows:** resolving a finding via `corrected_via_je` records the resolving_je_uuid linkage; it does NOT modify the original transaction. Drift is corrected via NEW JE, never via direct UPDATE of source bill/invoice/payment.

### 10a.1.6 Element 6: Accounting effects

This element documents the canonical postings that originate FROM other modules and execute IN Accounting. Posting templates are code-managed per Part 3.18.5.4; deploy-time validation ensures each template references only `active` or `frozen` accounts via role bindings.

**Driver settlement (originated in Dispatch §8a, posted via service function):**
- Debit: AP control (driver-vendor liability)
- Credit: revenue (gross pay)
- Multiple deduction credits per applied liability (reduces AP back to net payable)

**Bill creation for fuel period (originated in Fuel §9.3, posted via service function):**
- Debit: `fuel_expense` per role binding
- Credit: AP control (fuel vendor)
- Class: per unit's `qbo_class_uuid`

**Bill creation for WO (originated in Maintenance §9.1, posted via service function):**
- Debit: `wo_cost_post` template (PM/Repair/Tire/Accident variants)
- Credit: AP control (maintenance vendor)
- Optional split: capitalized vs expensed for repairs > $1000

**Driver liability creation (originated in Driver Finance §8b, posted via service function):**
- Debit: receivable-from-driver (per role binding)
- Credit: source account (e.g. ticket_recovery_revenue, equipment_loss_offset, fuel_card_overage_recovery)

**Invoice creation for load (originated in Dispatch §8a, posted via service function):**
- Debit: AR control
- Credit: `freight_revenue` per role binding
- Class: per unit/customer combination

**Customer payment receipt (ACH path):**
- Debit: cash/bank
- Credit: AR control
- Application to invoices via `customer_payment_lines`

**Customer payment receipt (factoring path — Apex):**
- Debit: `factoring_advance_received` (when Apex advances)
- Credit: AR control (the assigned invoice)
- Reserve held by Apex tracked via `factoring_reserve_receivable`
- Reserve release when received: Debit cash, Credit `factoring_reserve_receivable`

**Bill payment (AP application):**
- Debit: AP control (paying down vendor balance)
- Credit: cash/bank (or specific bank account)

**Reversing JE (auto-created on void per MUST 4.6.2.4):**
- Mirror image of original JE: debits become credits and vice versa
- Same accounts, same amounts, same classes; date = void date (NOT original JE date)

**Period close JE (year-end closing entries — handled separately via specific year-end procedures, not in normal monthly close):**
- Standard closing of revenue/expense accounts to retained earnings; only at fiscal year end, not at every monthly close

**QBO mirror:** all journal entries, bills, invoices, payments outbox-queued for QBO via standard outbox path. QBO sync writes JEs to QBO, bills/invoices to QBO, and reconciles via `qbo_object_id` mapping per Part 3.12.4.8.

#### 10a.1.7.1 WF-064 originated vs inherited mapping (Part 7 Tables A/B pattern)

**A. Originated in Accounting (11 actions):**

| # | Action | Originating Module | Why Accounting is the originator | API endpoint | Audit event(s) (success / blocked) | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `accounting.journal_entry.create_manual` | accounting | Originates here — manual JE is the only path that bypasses cross-module service-function postings; high-risk because it can post to any account combination per WF-018 | `POST /api/accounting/journal-entries/manual` (§10a.1.4) | `accounting.manual_je_posted` / `accounting.manual_je_post_blocked` | T-181.1, T-181.2, T-181.3 |
| 2 | `accounting.journal_entry.void` | accounting | Originates here — voiding a posted JE creates a reversing JE per WF-039; any posted JE could affect closed periods or factoring relationships | `POST /api/accounting/journal-entries/{uuid}/void` (§10a.1.4) | `accounting.journal_entry_voided` / `accounting.journal_entry_void_blocked` | T-181.4, T-181.5 |
| 3 | `accounting.bill.void.after_payment` | accounting | Originates here — voiding a bill that has been partially or fully paid creates AP reversal complexity per WF-009 | `POST /api/accounting/bills/{uuid}/void` (when paid_amount > 0) (§10a.1.4) | `accounting.bill_voided_after_payment` / `accounting.bill_void_after_payment_blocked` | T-182.1, T-182.2 |
| 4 | `accounting.expense.void.after_payment` | accounting | Originates here — same pattern as bill void after payment, applied to expenses | `POST /api/accounting/expenses/{uuid}/void` (Profile C — see §10a.1.7.3) | `accounting.expense_voided_after_payment` / `accounting.expense_void_after_payment_blocked` | T-182.3, T-182.4 |
| 5 | `accounting.invoice.void.after_payment_or_factoring` | accounting | Originates here — voiding a paid OR factoring-assigned invoice affects AR + factoring relationship per WF-010 | `POST /api/accounting/invoices/{uuid}/void` (when conditions met) (§10a.1.4) | `accounting.invoice_voided_after_payment_or_factoring` / `accounting.invoice_void_after_payment_or_factoring_blocked` | T-183.1, T-183.2 |
| 6 | `accounting.bill_payment.reverse` | accounting | Originates here — reversing an applied AP payment unwinds bill open_balance and creates reversing JE per WF-023 | `POST /api/accounting/bill-payments/{uuid}/reverse` (Profile C) | `accounting.bill_payment_reversed` / `accounting.bill_payment_reverse_blocked` | T-184.1, T-184.2 |
| 7 | `accounting.customer_payment.reverse` | accounting | Originates here — reversing an applied customer payment unwinds invoice open_balance per WF-021 | `POST /api/accounting/customer-payments/{uuid}/reverse` (Profile C) | `accounting.customer_payment_reversed` / `accounting.customer_payment_reverse_blocked` | T-185.1, T-185.2 |
| 8 | `accounting.factoring_assignment.reverse` | accounting | Originates here — reversing a factoring assignment touches the Apex relationship; Apex must be notified out-of-band | `POST /api/accounting/factoring-assignments/{uuid}/reverse` (Profile C) | `accounting.factoring_assignment_reversed` / `accounting.factoring_assignment_reverse_blocked` | T-185.3, T-185.4 |
| 9 | `accounting.period.close` | accounting | Originates here — period close locks all postings dated in the period per WF-008 | `POST /api/accounting/periods/{period_label}/close` (§10a.1.4) | `accounting.period_closed` / `accounting.period_close_blocked` | T-186.1, T-186.2, T-186.3 |
| 10 | `accounting.period.reopen` | accounting | Originates here — reopening a closed period is critical-severity; allows posting to historically-locked period per WF-008 | `POST /api/accounting/periods/{period_label}/reopen` (§10a.1.4) | `accounting.period_reopened` / `accounting.period_reopen_blocked` | T-186.4, T-186.5 |

| 11 | `accounting.qbo_resync.force` | accounting | Originates here — Owner-only forced full QBO reconciliation rebuild; high-risk because it can mass-update QBO records | `POST /api/accounting/qbo-resync/force` (Profile C) | `accounting.qbo_force_resynced` / `accounting.qbo_force_resync_blocked` | T-188.1, T-188.2 |

**Total: 11 originated WF-064 actions in Accounting — the largest single-module concentration in the system, exceeding even Driver Finance's 10.**

**B. Inherited through Accounting (0 actual):**

Per the Cursor accuracy standard from Part 7-9 Tables A/B refinement: every cross-module posting that arrives in Accounting via a service function (e.g., `accounting.create_bill_for_wo()` from Maintenance, `accounting.create_invoice_for_load()` from Dispatch, `accounting.create_driver_vendor_bill_line()` from Driver Finance) is a routine downstream effect of the originating module's WF-064 (or non-WF-064 routine action). The originating module owns the WF-064 audit + notification; Accounting executes the posting and emits info-level `accounting.bill_created` / `accounting.invoice_created` / `accounting.journal_entry_posted` events that are NOT WF-064 inheritances — they are routine ledger writes.

The Part 8b §8b.1.7.1 informational-row precedent applies here: documenting these cross-module writes as "inheritances" would be inaccurate. Accounting genuinely participates in 0 inherited WF-064 paths.

**Total: Accounting participates in 11 WF-064 paths — 11 originated + 0 inherited. Cleanest single-module count clarity in the spec.**

#### 10a.1.7.2 Driver self-access scope

**MUST 10a.1.7.2.1 — Drivers MUST NOT SELECT from any accounting table (per MUST 4.6.3.1):** driver visibility into financial state is exclusively through `driver_finance` schema and the cross-schema view `views.driver_debt_summary` per Part 4.11.1. Direct API calls to `/api/accounting/*` by driver role return `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` for any action.

**MUST 10a.1.7.2.2 — Driver-vendor bills and customer-payment-lines visibility:** even though a driver is the payee of a driver-vendor bill, the driver does not SELECT the bill row directly; they see the equivalent information via `driver_finance.driver_settlement_lines` and `views.driver_settlement_summary`.

The Accounting module has 30 permission actions in §10a.1.7 but only 7 fully-specced endpoints in §10a.1.4. The remaining 23 actions are mapped via Contract Profiles A/B/C and service-only entries per Part 7.3.4.1 pattern.

| Permission action | Contract artifact | Endpoint(s) | Profile-specific error set | Test IDs |

| `accounting.bill.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/accounting/bills/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-180.3 |
| `accounting.bill.create` (service function) | service-only | called from Maintenance/Fuel/Driver Finance | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (internal) | covered by originating module tests |
| `accounting.bill.void` (clean) | Profile B | `POST /api/accounting/bills/{uuid}/void` (paid_amount=0 path) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BILL_ALREADY_VOIDED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-182.5 |
| `accounting.bill.void.after_payment` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | same endpoint with paid_amount > 0 (§10a.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BILL_ALREADY_VOIDED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-182.1, T-182.2 |
| `accounting.bill_payment.create` | Profile B | `POST /api/accounting/bill-payments` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BILL_TERMINAL`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-184.3, T-184.4 |
| `accounting.bill_payment.reverse` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `POST /api/accounting/bill-payments/{uuid}/reverse` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BILL_PAYMENT_ALREADY_REVERSED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-184.1, T-184.2 |

| `accounting.expense.void.after_payment` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | same endpoint with paid_amount > 0 path | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_EXPENSE_ALREADY_VOIDED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-182.3, T-182.4 |

| `accounting.invoice.create` (service function) | service-only | called from Dispatch | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (internal) | covered by Dispatch tests |
| `accounting.invoice.void` (clean) | Profile B | `POST /api/accounting/invoices/{uuid}/void` (clean path) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_INVOICE_ALREADY_VOIDED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-183.5 |
| `accounting.invoice.void.after_payment_or_factoring` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | same endpoint with conditions met (§10a.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_INVOICE_ALREADY_VOIDED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-183.1, T-183.2 |
| `accounting.customer_payment.create` | Profile B | `POST /api/accounting/customer-payments` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_INVOICE_TERMINAL`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-185.5, T-185.6 |

| `accounting.period.reopen` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `POST /api/accounting/periods/{period_label}/reopen` (§10a.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERIOD_NOT_CLOSED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-186.4, T-186.5 |
| `accounting.reconciliation_finding.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/accounting/reconciliation-findings` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-187.1 |
| `accounting.reconciliation_finding.resolve` | FULL | `POST /api/accounting/reconciliation-findings/{uuid}/resolve` (§10a.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_FINDING_ALREADY_RESOLVED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-187.2, T-187.3 |
| `accounting.qbo_resync.force` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `POST /api/accounting/qbo-resync/force` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_QBO_RESYNC_IN_PROGRESS` | T-188.1, T-188.2 |

**Coverage summary:**
- 7 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §10a.1.4 (the 5 most-novel WF-064 + manual JE creation + reconciliation resolution)
- 23 additional permission actions are mapped via: Profile A read-only (9), Profile B CRUD non-WF-064 (7), Profile C WF-064 mutation (5), service-only internal (2)
- Distribution: 7 FULL + 9 Profile A + 7 Profile B + 5 Profile C + 2 service-only = **30 of 30 permission actions** in §10a.1.7 now have an explicit API mapping artifact. No permission action is left without DoD closure.

### 10a.1.8 Element 8: Notifications

| Audit event | Channel | Recipient | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `accounting.bill_created` (sampled) | none | n/a | info — sampled, no notification |
| `accounting.bill_voided` (clean) | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `accounting.bill_voided_after_payment` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.expense_voided_after_payment` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.bill_payment_reversed` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.invoice_created` (sampled) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `accounting.invoice_voided` (clean) | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `accounting.invoice_voided_after_payment_or_factoring` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.customer_payment_received` | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `accounting.customer_payment_reversed` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.factoring_assignment_created` | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `accounting.factoring_assignment_reversed` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.journal_entry_posted` (sampled, auto path) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `accounting.manual_je_posted` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.journal_entry_voided` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.reversing_je_created` (auto from void) | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `accounting.period_closed` | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.period_reopened` | email + SMS | Owner + Administrator + Accountant | critical + WF-064 (operationally significant per Part 5.5.4.2 exception) |
| `accounting.reconciliation_finding_detected` (single occurrence, info severity) | none | n/a | info — logged only |
| `accounting.reconciliation_finding_detected` (warning severity) | email | Manager + Accountant | warning |
| `accounting.reconciliation_finding_detected` (critical severity, large drift) | email | Owner + Manager + Accountant | critical |
| `accounting.reconciliation_finding_resolved` | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `accounting.qbo_force_resynced` | email | Owner + Administrator + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `accounting.qbo_sync_failed` (after retries exhausted) | email | Owner + Administrator + Accountant | critical |

**MUST 10a.1.8.1 — Operational SMS exception:** period reopen is the Accounting module's only SMS-allowed event per Part 5.5.4.2 exception list, given the regulatory/tax-filing implications. All other Accounting events are email-only.

**MUST 10a.1.8.2 — QBO sync failure escalation:** when outbox QBO sync attempts exceed retry budget per Part 3.12.4.8 (typically 5 attempts over 24h), event escalates to critical Owner+Admin notification. This is part of the WF-022 integration outage handling envelope.

**MUST 10a.1.8.3 — Reconciliation finding severity routing per detection:** reconciliation findings are detected by daily jobs per Part 4.0.7; severity is set at detection time (info for small drift <$1, warning for moderate drift, critical for large drift or category-mismatch). Notification routing follows severity tier.

### 10a.1.9 Element 9: Audit events

| Event class | Severity | Subject | Fires WF-064? | Symmetric pair |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `accounting.bill_created` | info (sampled) | the bill | no | none |
| `accounting.bill_updated` | info | the bill | no | none |
| `accounting.bill_voided` | info | the bill + reversing JE | no | none (clean void path) |
| `accounting.bill_voided_after_payment` | warning | the bill + reversing JE | yes | `accounting.bill_void_after_payment_blocked` |
| `accounting.bill_void_after_payment_blocked` | warning | the bill | yes | success: `accounting.bill_voided_after_payment` |
| `accounting.expense_voided_after_payment` | warning | the expense | yes | `accounting.expense_void_after_payment_blocked` |
| `accounting.expense_void_after_payment_blocked` | warning | the expense | yes | success: `accounting.expense_voided_after_payment` |
| `accounting.bill_payment_created` | info | the payment | no | none |
| `accounting.bill_payment_reversed` | warning | the payment + reversing JE | yes | `accounting.bill_payment_reverse_blocked` |
| `accounting.bill_payment_reverse_blocked` | warning | the payment | yes | success: `accounting.bill_payment_reversed` |
| `accounting.invoice_created` | info (sampled) | the invoice | no | none |
| `accounting.invoice_voided` | info | the invoice + reversing JE | no | none (clean void path) |
| `accounting.invoice_voided_after_payment_or_factoring` | warning | the invoice | yes | `accounting.invoice_void_after_payment_or_factoring_blocked` |
| `accounting.invoice_void_after_payment_or_factoring_blocked` | warning | the invoice | yes | success: `accounting.invoice_voided_after_payment_or_factoring` |
| `accounting.customer_payment_received` | info | the payment | no | none |
| `accounting.customer_payment_reversed` | warning | the payment | yes | `accounting.customer_payment_reverse_blocked` |
| `accounting.customer_payment_reverse_blocked` | warning | the payment | yes | success: `accounting.customer_payment_reversed` |
| `accounting.factoring_assignment_created` | info | the assignment | no | none |
| `accounting.factoring_assignment_reversed` | warning | the assignment | yes | `accounting.factoring_assignment_reverse_blocked` |
| `accounting.factoring_assignment_reverse_blocked` | warning | the assignment | yes | success: `accounting.factoring_assignment_reversed` |
| `accounting.journal_entry_posted` (auto path) | info (sampled) | the JE | no | none |
| `accounting.manual_je_posted` | warning | the JE | yes | `accounting.manual_je_post_blocked` |
| `accounting.manual_je_post_blocked` | warning | the rejected JE | yes | success: `accounting.manual_je_posted` |
| `accounting.journal_entry_voided` | warning | the JE + reversing JE | yes | `accounting.journal_entry_void_blocked` |
| `accounting.journal_entry_void_blocked` | warning | the JE | yes | success: `accounting.journal_entry_voided` |
| `accounting.reversing_je_created` (auto from void) | info | the reversing JE + linkage to original | no | none |
| `accounting.period_closed` | warning | the period | yes | `accounting.period_close_blocked` |
| `accounting.period_close_blocked` | warning | the period | yes | success: `accounting.period_closed` |
| `accounting.period_reopened` | critical | the period | yes | `accounting.period_reopen_blocked` |
| `accounting.period_reopen_blocked` | warning | the period | yes | success: `accounting.period_reopened` |
| `accounting.reconciliation_finding_detected` | info / warning / critical (per drift size) | the finding | no | none |
| `accounting.reconciliation_finding_resolved` | info | the finding + resolution_action | no | none |
| `accounting.qbo_force_resynced` | warning | the resync run | yes | `accounting.qbo_force_resync_blocked` |
| `accounting.qbo_force_resync_blocked` | warning | the resync request | yes | success: `accounting.qbo_force_resynced` |
| `accounting.qbo_sync_failed` (retries exhausted) | critical | the failed object | no | none |

**MUST 10a.1.9.1** — All event INSERTs go through `audit.append_event()` per Part 4.9.3.2.

**MUST 10a.1.9.2** — Per Cursor's locked WF-064 success/blocked symmetry rule: 11 originated WF-064 actions × 2 = 22 paired audit events specific to Accounting, plus 13 audit events for non-symmetric routine operations + 1 cross-cutting (qbo_sync_failed escalation) = 36 audit event classes total.

**MUST 10a.1.9.3** — `before_state_json` and `after_state_json` MUST be populated for: manual_je_posted (full lines payload), journal_entry_voided (full original JE payload), bill_voided_after_payment (paid_amount + status), invoice_voided_after_payment_or_factoring (paid_amount + factoring_assigned + status), period_closed (pre-close-check counts), period_reopened (previously_closed_at). Sensitive financial data is permitted in audit JSON since audit access is Owner+Admin+Accountant only per Part 4.9 RLS.

#### 10a.1.10.1 Accounting-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_JE_UNBALANCED` | 422 | Sum of debits ≠ sum of credits (MUST 4.6.2.1) |
| `E_JE_INSUFFICIENT_LINES` | 422 | JE has fewer than 2 lines (MUST 4.6.2.2) |
| `E_JE_DIRECT_INSERT_FORBIDDEN` | 403 | Direct INSERT to journal_entries blocked; use service function (MUST 4.6.2.6) |
| `E_JE_ALREADY_VOIDED` | 409 | JE already voided; reversing JE exists |
| `E_ACCOUNT_LIFECYCLE_FORBIDDEN` | 422 | Account lifecycle_state is draft or archived (MUST 4.6.2.3) |
| `E_PERIOD_CLOSED` | 422 | je_date falls in closed period |
| `E_PERIOD_NOT_CLOSED` | 422 | Cannot reopen a period not in closed state |
| `E_PERIOD_ALREADY_CLOSED` | 409 | Cannot close a period already closed |
| `E_PERIOD_CLOSE_BLOCKED_BY_DRAFTS` | 422 | Draft JEs in period prevent close |
| `E_PERIOD_CLOSE_BLOCKED_BY_FINDINGS` | 422 | Unresolved reconciliation findings prevent close |
| `E_BILL_ALREADY_VOIDED` | 409 | Bill already voided |
| `E_BILL_TERMINAL` | 422 | Bill in terminal state; cannot pay or modify |
| `E_BILL_PAYMENT_ALREADY_REVERSED` | 409 | Bill payment already reversed |
| `E_OPEN_BALANCE_DIRECT_UPDATE_FORBIDDEN` | 403 | open_balance is trigger-maintained; direct UPDATE blocked (MUST 4.6.2.7) |
| `E_EXPENSE_ALREADY_VOIDED` | 409 | Expense already voided |
| `E_INVOICE_ALREADY_VOIDED` | 409 | Invoice already voided |
| `E_INVOICE_TERMINAL` | 422 | Invoice in terminal state; cannot apply payment |
| `E_INVOICE_NOT_ELIGIBLE_FOR_FACTORING` | 422 | Invoice already paid or already factoring-assigned |
| `E_CUSTOMER_PAYMENT_ALREADY_REVERSED` | 409 | Customer payment already reversed |
| `E_FACTORING_ASSIGNMENT_TERMINAL` | 422 | Factoring assignment already reversed or settled |
| `E_FINDING_ALREADY_RESOLVED` | 409 | Reconciliation finding already resolved |
| `E_QBO_RESYNC_IN_PROGRESS` | 409 | A force-resync is already running; wait or cancel |
