# IH35 Master Blueprint v3 — Part 10b: Banking Module

**Section in v3 hierarchy:** Part 10b of 13. Reads after Part 10a (Accounting). Per Cursor-locked Option 2 chunking and Jorge's split-delivery directive: Part 10a covered Accounting only; Part 10b covers Banking only; Part 11 will cover Documents + Notifications.

**Status (initial draft):** Awaiting Cursor strict review per the same 8-check acceptance gate applied to Part 10a (template order, API DoD completeness, WF-064 A/B clarity, no schema drift, trace matrix update, test ID continuity, endpoint/profile split, no stale contradictory counts).

All Part 7-10a locks carried forward:
- 10-element module template in order
- Strict 6-element API DoD with explicit "Not applicable" annotation
- WF-064 success/blocked symmetry with explicit Tables A/B
- No schema drift / no new namespaces (14-schema lock)
- Incremental 11-column trace matrix updates
- Sequential, non-colliding test IDs
- FULL endpoint + Contract Profile mapping split for size management
- Single canonical count statements per category (no stale contradictions)

**Normative language:** MUST · MUST NOT · SHOULD · SHOULD NOT · MAY (RFC 2119).

**Test ID range:** Part 10b uses T-190.x through T-194.x exclusively. Verified non-colliding with Parts 1-10a (max prior test ID: T-189.7 in Part 10a §10a.1.10).

**Trace matrix:** delivered alongside this Part as appended REQ-BANK rows in `IH35_REQUIREMENT_TRACE_MATRIX_V3.csv`. 11-column structure preserved.

---

## 10b.0 — BANKING MODULE PLACEMENT

Banking is a smaller and more contained module than Accounting. It owns three tables (`banking.bank_accounts`, `banking.bank_transactions`, `banking.factoring_transactions`) and serves three primary purposes:

1. **Bank account registry** — the canonical list of company bank accounts, credit cards, and the Apex factoring virtual account, each linked to a `catalogs.accounts` chart entry.
2. **Bank transaction record** — the operational log of bank-originated transactions (deposits, debits, transfers, fees) that gets matched to `accounting.bill_payments` and `accounting.customer_payments` during reconciliation.
3. **Factoring transaction tracking** — the Apex-specific receivable + reserve mechanics, complementing the `accounting.invoices.factoring_assigned` metadata side per WF-021.

Banking is consumer-side of Accounting: where Accounting owns the double-entry ledger and the AP/AR objects, Banking owns the bank-side operational record that gets reconciled against the ledger. A categorized bank_transaction MUST have exactly one outbound link to an Accounting object (per MUST 4.7.2.2); uncategorized bank_transactions sit in a triage queue until matched.

WFs implemented in Part 10b:

| WF | Name | Primary scope |
|---|---|---|
| WF-012 | Bank Transaction Categorization | The 4.7.2.2 trigger-enforced single-link constraint |
| WF-013 | Reconciliation: Bank vs Ledger | Daily reconciliation matching bank_transactions ↔ accounting.bill_payments / customer_payments |
| WF-031 | Apex Factoring Transaction Lifecycle | Advance + reserve + release flow per Part 4.6.7 |
| WF-032 | Encrypted PII Handling for Account Numbers | App-layer encryption per MUST 4.7.2.3 |
| WF-033 | Bank Account Lifecycle (active/closed) | Status transitions; closing affects transaction acceptance |
| WF-052 | Bank Transaction Manual Entry | Office entry when no statement-import feed |

Cross-cuts WF-021 (customer payment / factoring lifecycle — originated in Accounting per Part 10a §10a.1.7.1; Banking owns the bank-side records), WF-024 (RBAC), WF-064 (high-risk audit).

---

## 10b.1 — BANKING MODULE

### 10b.1.1 Element 1: Module overview

**Purpose:** Banking owns the company's bank-side operational record. It tracks every bank account, credit card, and the Apex factoring virtual account; logs every bank transaction (cleared deposits, debits, transfers, fees); and records the factoring-specific advance and reserve mechanics with Apex. It is the reconciliation counterpart to Accounting — bank-side reality vs ledger-side bookkeeping.

**Owning schema:** `banking` (per Part 4.7.1).

**Key invariants:**
- Each `bank_accounts` row links to exactly one `catalogs.accounts` row of `account_type IN ('bank', 'credit_card', 'other_current_asset')` per MUST 4.7.2.1, enforced via FK + check.
- Bank transaction categorization (`categorization_status = 'categorized'`) sets EXACTLY ONE of the `linked_bill_payment_uuid`, `linked_customer_payment_uuid`, `linked_factoring_transaction_uuid`, or `linked_journal_entry_uuid` columns per MUST 4.7.2.2, trigger-enforced.
- Account numbers and routing numbers are encrypted at the application layer before INSERT per MUST 4.7.2.3; the DB never sees plaintext; `_encrypted` columns store ciphertext.
- Bank transaction `cleared_at` timestamps are the source of truth for cash position; `posted_at` (when entered into the system) may differ.
- Factoring transactions are append-only (status transitions logged via new rows, not UPDATE on existing rows) per Part 4.6.7 + 4.7.2 pattern.
- Reconciliation matching is one-way: a bank_transaction can be matched to AT MOST ONE accounting object; un-matching requires Owner action (touches reconciliation status).

**Cross-module dependencies:**
- READS from canonical reference: `catalogs.accounts` (the linked chart entry per 4.7.2.1), `catalogs.account_role_bindings` (for matching default categorization rules), `master_data.vendors` (for transaction descriptions referencing vendors), `master_data.customers` (for incoming payment descriptions).
- READS from `accounting.bill_payments`, `accounting.customer_payments`, `accounting.journal_entries` (for categorization linking and reconciliation).
- WRITES are EXCLUSIVELY via service functions called from other modules and via the direct API endpoints in §10b.1.4 for office workflows. The Banking module does NOT post to Accounting directly; categorizing a bank transaction does not create a JE — it links to an existing JE/payment record. Posting JEs from Banking events (e.g. bank fees) is via `accounting.post()` called from Banking service functions.
- WRITES to shared schemas: `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()`; `outbox.queue` for QBO sync per Part 3.12.4.8 (bank_account creation + bank_transaction reconciliation status are mirrored to QBO as bank-rule and transaction-match metadata).

**WFs implemented:** WF-012, WF-013, WF-031, WF-032, WF-033, WF-052. Cross-cuts WF-021 (customer payment / factoring lifecycle — originated in Accounting per Part 10a §10a.1.7.1; Banking owns the bank-side records), WF-024 (RBAC), WF-064 (high-risk audit). **9 distinct WFs traced in this module.**

### 10b.1.2 Element 2: UI behaviors

#### 10b.1.2.1 Banking home (`/banking`)

**MUST 10b.1.2.1.1 — Section header:** "BANKING" per Part 6.2.3.

**MUST 10b.1.2.1.2 — KPI cards row** per Part 6.2.4: total bank balance across all active accounts, uncategorized bank transactions count (color band yellow if > 0 per Part 6.4.4), oldest uncategorized transaction age (red severe band if > 7 days), factoring reserve balance held by Apex, last reconciliation completed timestamp.

**MUST 10b.1.2.1.3 — Tab structure:** Accounts, Transactions, Reconciliation, Factoring, Reports.

#### 10b.1.2.2 Accounts tab (`/banking/accounts`)

**MUST 10b.1.2.2.1 — List columns:** account_name, account_type badge (bank/credit_card/factoring_virtual), institution, last_four (display only — full account number masked), linked_chart_account (linked to catalogs.accounts), current_balance, status badge (active/closed), last_imported_at.

**MUST 10b.1.2.2.2 — Account number display:** the full account number is NEVER displayed; only the last four digits are shown. Owner+Admin can request a one-time decrypt-and-display via a dedicated `view-account-number` flow that emits an audit event per Part 5 secrets policy and 4.7.3.2 RLS.

**MUST 10b.1.2.2.3 — Deactivate action** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-033): Owner-only. 2-step confirmation modal per Part 6.4.5.4 with typed phrase "DEACTIVATE BANK ACCOUNT" + reason ≥20 chars. Deactivation blocks new transaction INSERTs against the account but preserves historical transactions for audit.

#### 10b.1.2.3 Transactions tab (`/banking/transactions`)

**MUST 10b.1.2.3.1 — List columns:** transaction_date (cleared_at), bank_account, description, amount (signed; negative = debit), categorization_status badge (uncategorized / categorized / excluded), linked_object link (when categorized — to bill_payment/customer_payment/JE/factoring), reconciliation_status badge.

**MUST 10b.1.2.3.2 — Filters:** bank_account, categorization_status, date range, amount range, has-linked-object toggle, reconciliation_status.

**MUST 10b.1.2.3.3 — Categorize action** (WF-012): Office roles. Modal lets the user select from candidate matches (bill_payments, customer_payments, JEs, factoring_transactions) filtered by amount-and-date proximity. On selection, the link is set; on save, trigger validates exactly-one-linked-uuid invariant per MUST 4.7.2.2.

**MUST 10b.1.2.3.4 — Recategorize action:** allowed for office roles when the linked accounting object is in an open period; **fires WF-064** when target is in a closed period (per WF-013 — recategorizing an already-reconciled transaction in a closed period requires Owner action). Distinction enforced at API layer.

**MUST 10b.1.2.3.5 — Delete transaction action** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-052): Owner-only. 2-step confirmation modal with typed phrase "DELETE TRANSACTION" + reason ≥30 chars. Deletion blocked if the transaction has been included in a closed-period reconciliation; returns `E_BANK_TXN_DELETE_BLOCKED_RECONCILED_CLOSED`.

#### 10b.1.2.4 Reconciliation tab (`/banking/reconciliation`)

**MUST 10b.1.2.4.1 — Reconciliation runs:** list of past reconciliation runs with status, date range, bank account, target balance, matched_count, unmatched_count, drift_amount.

**MUST 10b.1.2.4.2 — Per-run detail view:** matched transactions and unmatched transactions side-by-side; ability to manually link or unlink matches; drift breakdown.

**MUST 10b.1.2.4.3 — Daily reconciliation feed** (per Part 4.0.7): the daily reconcile job runs and emits findings to `accounting.reconciliation_findings`; banking-side display of the same findings filtered to banking-relevant rows (i.e., findings where source_table = `banking.bank_transactions` or `banking.factoring_transactions`).

#### 10b.1.2.5 Factoring tab (`/banking/factoring`)

**MUST 10b.1.2.5.1 — Apex transactions list:** advances + reserve holds + reserve releases per WF-031, displayed chronologically. Each row: factoring_transaction_id, date, type (advance / reserve_hold / reserve_release), invoice_uuid (if advance — linked to accounting.invoices), amount, running_reserve_balance, status.

**MUST 10b.1.2.5.2 — Reverse action** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-031): Owner-only. 2-step confirmation modal with typed phrase "REVERSE FACTORING TRANSACTION" + reason ≥30 chars. Coordinated with `accounting.factoring_assignment.reverse` per Part 10a §10a.1.7.1 Table A row 8 — reversal here represents the BANK-SIDE record of the same business event.

### 10b.1.3 Element 3: Data entities

The Banking module owns these tables (DDL in Part 4.7.1, NOT redefined here — schema lock honored):

- `banking.bank_accounts` — registry of company bank accounts, credit cards, and Apex factoring virtual account
- `banking.bank_transactions` — operational log of cleared bank transactions; categorization links to Accounting objects
- `banking.factoring_transactions` — Apex-specific advance + reserve + release records

**3 tables.** No new tables introduced beyond Part 4.7.1 DDL. Schema lock per Part 4 Rev 2 honored.

**The Banking module reads from these canonical reference tables in other schemas:**
- `catalogs.accounts` (the chart entry FK per MUST 4.7.2.1; lifecycle_state gating not required for banking — banking accounts always link to active or frozen chart accounts)
- `catalogs.account_role_bindings` (for default categorization rules per posting templates)
- `master_data.vendors`, `master_data.customers` (for transaction descriptions and matching)

**The Banking module reads from these other schemas (matching + reconciliation):**
- `accounting.bill_payments`, `accounting.customer_payments`, `accounting.journal_entries` (for categorization candidate matching + linkage validation)
- `accounting.invoices` (for factoring transaction → invoice linkage per WF-031)
- `accounting.reconciliation_findings` (banking-relevant findings displayed in §10b.1.2.4.3 view)

**The Banking module writes (via service functions) to other schemas:**
- `accounting.journal_entries` via `accounting.post()` for bank-fee JEs and other Banking-originated postings (e.g. interest earned, account fees)
- No direct cross-schema INSERT — service-function-only entry per Part 4 lock pattern

**The Banking module writes to shared schemas:**
- `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()`
- `outbox.queue` for QBO sync of bank account metadata + transaction reconciliation status per Part 3.12.4.8

### 10b.1.4 Element 4: API contracts

This module follows the FULL endpoint + Contract Profile split lock. FULL endpoints below cover the highest-risk paths: bank account deactivate, bank transaction categorize/recategorize, bank transaction delete, factoring transaction reverse. Standard CRUD + query endpoints map to Profile A/B/C in §10b.1.7.3.

#### `POST /api/banking/bank-accounts/{uuid}/deactivate`

Deactivate a bank account (WF-033). **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `banking.bank_account.deactivate` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/banking/bank-accounts/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bnka01/deactivate
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Closing checking account ending 4521 — switching to new institution; balance transferred yesterday; no further transactions expected on this account",
  "expected_zero_balance": true
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bnka01",
    "previous_status": "active",
    "new_status": "closed",
    "current_balance_at_deactivation_cents": 0,
    "deactivated_at": "2026-05-02T23:30:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi80",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif50"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Bank account deactivate request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 20 characters"},
      {"field": "expected_zero_balance", "reason": "required; must be true if account is being closed normally"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923d10"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, balance not zero):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_BANK_ACCOUNT_NONZERO_BALANCE",
    "message": "Cannot deactivate bank account with non-zero balance; current balance is $1,247.50; transfer balance first or set expected_zero_balance=false to override",
    "details": {"current_balance_cents": 124750, "expected_zero_balance": true},
    "request_id": "req_01923d10"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'banking.bank_account.deactivate' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d10"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_NONZERO_BALANCE` (10b.1.10.1), `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_ALREADY_CLOSED` (10b.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `banking.bank_account_deactivated` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `banking.bank_account_deactivate_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}/categorize`

Categorize an uncategorized bank transaction by linking to an Accounting object (WF-012). Routine flow — NOT a WF-064 action when the linked target is in an open period. **Fires WF-064** when recategorizing in a closed period (separate endpoint variant via `recategorize-closed-period` below).

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `banking.bank_transaction.categorize` (Owner, Admin, Accountant).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/banking/bank-transactions/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bnkt001/categorize
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "link_target_type": "bill_payment",
  "link_target_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bpay010",
  "categorization_note": "Matched to bill payment BP-26015 for vendor TXP fuel card; amounts and dates align"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bnkt001",
    "previous_categorization_status": "uncategorized",
    "new_categorization_status": "categorized",
    "linked_target_type": "bill_payment",
    "linked_target_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bpay010",
    "categorized_at": "2026-05-02T23:35:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi81",
    "queued_qbo_match_sync": true
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Categorize request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "link_target_type", "reason": "must be one of: bill_payment, customer_payment, journal_entry, factoring_transaction"},
      {"field": "link_target_uuid", "reason": "must reference an existing accounting object of the specified type"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923d11"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, already categorized):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_BANK_TXN_ALREADY_CATEGORIZED",
    "message": "Bank transaction already categorized to bill_payment BP-26012 at 2026-04-30T16:00:00Z; use recategorize endpoint to change",
    "details": {
      "current_link_target_type": "bill_payment",
      "current_link_target_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bpay005",
      "categorized_at": "2026-04-30T16:00:00Z"
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923d11"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, target in closed period):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD",
    "message": "Cannot categorize to target in closed period 2026-04 via this endpoint; use recategorize-closed-period (WF-064) instead",
    "details": {"target_period_label": "2026-04", "target_period_status": "closed"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d11"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'banking.bank_transaction.categorize' requires Owner, Administrator, or Accountant role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "manager"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d11"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_TXN_ALREADY_CATEGORIZED` (10b.1.10.1), `E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD` (10b.1.10.1), `E_LINKED_TARGET_NOT_FOUND` (10b.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `banking.bank_transaction_categorized` (info). NOT a WF-064 action — routine categorization in open periods.

---

#### `POST /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}/recategorize-closed-period`

Recategorize a bank transaction whose linked accounting target is in a closed period. **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action (per WF-013 closed-period sensitivity).**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `banking.bank_transaction.recategorize_after_period_close` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/banking/bank-transactions/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bnkt002/recategorize-closed-period
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "new_link_target_type": "journal_entry",
  "new_link_target_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0010",
  "reason": "Owner-directed recategorization after CPA review identified mis-link to bill_payment BP-26012; correct target is JE-26008 manual adjustment in same closed period 2026-04",
  "acknowledge_closed_period": true
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bnkt002",
    "previous_link_target_type": "bill_payment",
    "previous_link_target_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bpay012",
    "new_link_target_type": "journal_entry",
    "new_link_target_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000je0010",
    "target_period_label": "2026-04",
    "target_period_status": "closed",
    "recategorized_at": "2026-05-02T23:40:00Z",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi82",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif51"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Recategorize request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters"},
      {"field": "acknowledge_closed_period", "reason": "required and must be true"}
    ]
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, target in open period):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_NOT_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD",
    "message": "Target is in open period; use the routine categorize endpoint instead of recategorize-closed-period",
    "details": {"target_period_label": "2026-05", "target_period_status": "open"}
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'banking.bank_transaction.recategorize_after_period_close' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"}
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_NOT_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD` (10b.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `banking.bank_transaction_recategorized_closed_period` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `banking.bank_transaction_recategorize_closed_period_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `DELETE /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}`

Delete a bank transaction (WF-052). **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `banking.bank_transaction.delete` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
DELETE /api/banking/bank-transactions/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bnkt003
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Owner deleting duplicate bank transaction recorded twice via manual entry; original was 0000bnkt001; deleting duplicate 0000bnkt003 to clean up uncategorized queue"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "deleted_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000bnkt003",
    "deleted_at": "2026-05-02T23:45:00Z",
    "soft_deleted": true,
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi83",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif52"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Delete request invalid",
    "details": [{"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters"}]
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, reconciled in closed period):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_BANK_TXN_DELETE_BLOCKED_RECONCILED_CLOSED",
    "message": "Cannot delete bank transaction included in closed-period reconciliation; reopen period first via accounting.period.reopen and Owner approval",
    "details": {"reconciliation_run_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000recon01", "period_label": "2026-04", "period_status": "closed"}
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'banking.bank_transaction.delete' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"}
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_TXN_DELETE_BLOCKED_RECONCILED_CLOSED` (10b.1.10.1), `E_BANK_TXN_ALREADY_DELETED` (10b.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `banking.bank_transaction_deleted` (warning, fires WF-064; soft-delete preserves audit trail). On block, emits `banking.bank_transaction_delete_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/banking/factoring-transactions/{uuid}/reverse`

Reverse a factoring transaction (advance, reserve hold, or reserve release) per WF-031. **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action; coordinates with `accounting.factoring_assignment.reverse` per Part 10a.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `banking.factoring_transaction.reverse` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/banking/factoring-transactions/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000fact01/reverse
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Apex retracted advance on invoice INV-26020 due to customer dispute; reversing both bank-side factoring transaction here and accounting.factoring_assignment in coordinated Owner action; Apex notified out-of-band",
  "linked_accounting_factoring_assignment_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000fass01",
  "acknowledge_apex_coordination": true
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "factoring_transaction_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000fact01",
    "transaction_type": "advance",
    "reversed_at": "2026-05-02T23:50:00Z",
    "reversal_factoring_transaction_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000fact02",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi84",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif53"},
    "coordinated_with_accounting": true,
    "linked_accounting_factoring_assignment_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000fass01"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Factoring transaction reverse request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters"},
      {"field": "acknowledge_apex_coordination", "reason": "required and must be true"}
    ]
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, already reversed):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_FACTORING_TXN_ALREADY_REVERSED",
    "message": "Factoring transaction already reversed at 2026-04-15T10:00:00Z by reversing transaction FT-26050",
    "details": {"reversed_at": "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z", "reversal_factoring_transaction_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000fact99"}
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'banking.factoring_transaction.reverse' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"}
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_FACTORING_TXN_ALREADY_REVERSED` (10b.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `banking.factoring_transaction_reversed` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `banking.factoring_transaction_reverse_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

### 10b.1.5 Element 5: Validation rules

**MUST 10b.1.5.1 — Bank account → catalogs link (per MUST 4.7.2.1):** every `bank_accounts` row MUST have `linked_chart_account_uuid` set to a `catalogs.accounts` row of `account_type IN ('bank', 'credit_card', 'other_current_asset')`. Enforced via FK + check constraint at the DB layer.

**MUST 10b.1.5.2 — Categorization exactly-one-link (per MUST 4.7.2.2):** when `bank_transactions.categorization_status = 'categorized'`, EXACTLY ONE of `linked_bill_payment_uuid`, `linked_customer_payment_uuid`, `linked_journal_entry_uuid`, `linked_factoring_transaction_uuid` is non-NULL. Setting two or more, or zero, returns `E_BANK_TXN_LINK_INVARIANT_VIOLATED`. Trigger-enforced at INSERT/UPDATE.

**MUST 10b.1.5.3 — Account number encryption (per MUST 4.7.2.3):** `account_number_encrypted` and `routing_number_encrypted` columns receive ciphertext only. The API layer encrypts plaintext from the client request body before INSERT; the DB never sees plaintext. Decryption for display is Owner+Admin only per MUST 4.7.3.2.

**MUST 10b.1.5.4 — Categorization target period gate:** routine `categorize` endpoint blocks linking to targets in closed periods; returns `E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD`. Closed-period categorization requires the dedicated `recategorize-closed-period` endpoint (WF-064).

**MUST 10b.1.5.5 — Categorize idempotency:** double-categorize requests for the same bank_transaction return `E_BANK_TXN_ALREADY_CATEGORIZED` 422 with current link details; client must call recategorize variant to change.

**MUST 10b.1.5.6 — Recategorize-closed-period scope:** the `recategorize-closed-period` endpoint accepts only target objects in CLOSED periods. Targets in open periods return `E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_NOT_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD`; the routine categorize endpoint must be used.

**MUST 10b.1.5.7 — Bank transaction soft-delete (per WF-052):** delete endpoint sets `deleted_at` timestamp + actor; row is preserved for audit. Direct DELETE blocked by RLS. Subsequent SELECTs filter by `deleted_at IS NULL` by default; Owner can SELECT WITH soft-deleted rows via explicit query parameter.

**MUST 10b.1.5.8 — Closed-period reconciliation lock:** bank transaction delete is blocked when the transaction is included in a reconciliation run for a closed period (returns `E_BANK_TXN_DELETE_BLOCKED_RECONCILED_CLOSED`). Period reopen via `accounting.period.reopen` is required before deletion.

**MUST 10b.1.5.9 — Factoring transaction append-only:** UPDATE on `factoring_transactions` rows blocked by RLS for all roles; reversals are NEW rows referencing the original via `reverses_factoring_transaction_uuid`. The original `status` field is not modified — query layer infers reversal status from reversal-row presence.

**MUST 10b.1.5.10 — Bank fees post via accounting.post():** Banking-originated fee transactions (e.g. monthly account fee, wire transfer fee) post a JE via `accounting.post()` with debit to `bank_fees_expense` (per role binding) and credit to the bank account's chart entry. Banking does NOT directly write to `accounting.journal_entries`; it calls the service function, consistent with Part 10a §10a.1.5.5 service-function-only pattern.

**MUST 10b.1.5.11 — Bank account current_balance is NOT trigger-maintained from transactions:** unlike `bills.open_balance` and `invoices.open_balance` which ARE auto-maintained, `bank_accounts.current_balance` is updated via explicit reconciliation runs and statement imports. This is because bank reality is the source of truth, not the company ledger; the ledger reconciles TO the bank, not the other way around.

**MUST 10b.1.5.12 — Encrypted PII column SELECT scoping (per MUST 4.7.3.2):** SELECT on `account_number_encrypted` and `routing_number_encrypted` columns is RLS-restricted to Owner+Admin only. Other roles get NULL on these columns even if they can SELECT the rest of the row.

### 10b.1.6 Element 6: Accounting effects

Banking does not directly post to Accounting. Banking service functions call `accounting.post()` for events that have ledger impact. The events are:

**Bank fee transaction (e.g., monthly account fee, wire fee):** posts JE via `accounting.post()`:
- Debit: `bank_fees_expense` (per role binding)
- Credit: bank account's linked chart entry

**Bank interest earned:** posts JE via `accounting.post()`:
- Debit: bank account's linked chart entry
- Credit: `interest_income` (per role binding)

**Bank transaction categorization:** does NOT post a new JE — categorization is a metadata link to an EXISTING JE/payment. The original posting was already done by the originating module (Accounting via `accounting.create_*` service functions); Banking simply links its bank-side row to the existing accounting object.

**Factoring advance received from Apex:** posts JE via `accounting.post()` (originated from Accounting per Part 10a §10a.1.6 factoring path; Banking records the bank-side `factoring_transaction` of type `advance` in the same atomic transaction):
- Debit: `factoring_advance_received` (the Apex virtual account)
- Credit: AR control (the assigned invoice)

**Factoring reserve released:** posts JE via `accounting.post()`:
- Debit: cash/bank
- Credit: `factoring_reserve_receivable` (the Apex reserve held)

**Factoring transaction reverse:** creates a NEW factoring_transaction row of opposite type (e.g., `advance_reversal`) AND posts a reversing JE via `accounting.post()` with mirror-image debits/credits.

**Posting templates referenced from this module:** `bank_fee_post`, `bank_interest_post`, `factoring_advance_received_post`, `factoring_reserve_release_post`, `factoring_transaction_reverse_post`. All code-managed per Part 3.18.5.4; deploy-time validation ensures template references valid role bindings.

**QBO sync:** bank account creation, deactivation, and bank transaction reconciliation status changes are outbox-queued for QBO bank-rule and bank-match sync per Part 3.12.4.8. The factoring virtual account is mirrored to QBO as an `Other Current Asset` chart entry per Part 4.6.7.

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |

| `banking.bank_account.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_account.read` (without account number) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_account.read.decrypt_pii` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_account.create` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_account.update` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_account.deactivate` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_transaction.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_transaction.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_transaction.create_manual` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_transaction.import_statement` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_transaction.categorize` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_transaction.recategorize_open_period` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_transaction.recategorize_after_period_close` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.bank_transaction.delete` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.factoring_transaction.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.factoring_transaction.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.factoring_transaction.create` (via service function) | service | service | — | service | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.factoring_transaction.reverse` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.reconciliation_run.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — |
| `banking.reconciliation_run.start` | ✓ | ✓ | — | ✓ | — | — | — | — |

**Role gating notes:**
- Per MUST 4.7.3.1: Owner+Admin+Accountant SELECT/UPDATE; Manager SELECT only; Dispatcher, Safety, Mechanic, Driver no access. The matrix above implements this exactly.
- Per MUST 4.7.3.2: encrypted PII column access (`bank_account.read.decrypt_pii`) is Owner+Admin only — narrower than the standard Owner+Admin+Accountant row-level access.

#### 10b.1.7.1 WF-064 originated vs inherited mapping (Part 7 Tables A/B pattern)

**A. Originated in Banking (4 actions):**

| # | Action | Originating Module | Why Banking is the originator | API endpoint | Audit event(s) (success / blocked) | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `banking.bank_account.deactivate` | banking | Originates here — deactivating a bank account is high-risk per WF-033; transaction acceptance against the account is blocked thereafter | `POST /api/banking/bank-accounts/{uuid}/deactivate` (§10b.1.4) | `banking.bank_account_deactivated` / `banking.bank_account_deactivate_blocked` | T-191.1, T-191.2, T-191.3 |
| 2 | `banking.bank_transaction.recategorize_after_period_close` | banking | Originates here — recategorizing a bank transaction whose linked target is in a closed period requires Owner action per WF-013; touches closed-period audit chain | `POST /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}/recategorize-closed-period` (§10b.1.4) | `banking.bank_transaction_recategorized_closed_period` / `banking.bank_transaction_recategorize_closed_period_blocked` | T-192.5, T-192.6 |
| 3 | `banking.bank_transaction.delete` | banking | Originates here — deleting a bank transaction is rare and Owner-only per WF-052; soft-delete preserves audit trail | `DELETE /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}` (§10b.1.4) | `banking.bank_transaction_deleted` / `banking.bank_transaction_delete_blocked` | T-193.1, T-193.2 |
| 4 | `banking.factoring_transaction.reverse` | banking | Originates here — reversing a factoring transaction touches Apex relationship per WF-031; coordinated with `accounting.factoring_assignment.reverse` but the bank-side reversal is its own WF-064 origination | `POST /api/banking/factoring-transactions/{uuid}/reverse` (§10b.1.4) | `banking.factoring_transaction_reversed` / `banking.factoring_transaction_reverse_blocked` | T-194.1, T-194.2 |

**Total: 4 originated WF-064 actions in Banking.**

**B. Inherited through Banking (0 actual):**

Per the precedent established in Part 8b §8b.1.7.1 and applied in Parts 9 and 10a: every cross-module write into Banking via service functions (e.g., `banking.create_bank_transaction_from_payment()` from Accounting, `banking.create_factoring_transaction()` from Accounting's factoring assignment flow) is a routine downstream effect. The originating module owns the WF-064 audit + notification; Banking executes the bank-side record and emits info-level `banking.bank_transaction_created` / `banking.factoring_transaction_created` events that are NOT WF-064 inheritances — they are routine bank-side records.

The coordination case worth noting explicitly: when `accounting.factoring_assignment.reverse` fires (a WF-064 in Accounting per Part 10a §10a.1.7.1 Table A row 8), the coordinated bank-side `banking.factoring_transaction.reverse` is a SEPARATE Owner action — not an inherited audit. Both fire WF-064 independently because each touches different schemas with different business meaning (the Accounting side reverses the AR factoring metadata; the Banking side reverses the bank-account-impacting transaction). They are coordinated, not inherited.

**Total: Banking participates in 4 WF-064 paths — 4 originated + 0 inherited.**

#### 10b.1.7.2 Driver self-access scope

**MUST 10b.1.7.2.1 — Drivers MUST NOT SELECT from any banking table (per MUST 4.7.3.1):** drivers have NO read or write access to `banking.*` tables. Driver financial visibility is exclusively through `driver_finance` schema. Direct API calls to `/api/banking/*` by driver role return `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` for any action.

**MUST 10b.1.7.2.2 — Other operational roles also blocked:** Dispatcher, Safety, Mechanic also have no Banking access per MUST 4.7.3.1. The Banking module is exclusively for office finance roles (Owner, Admin, Manager-read-only, Accountant).

#### 10b.1.7.3 Contract Profile Mapping (Cursor Part 7.3.4.1 pattern)

The Banking module has 20 permission actions in §10b.1.7. 5 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §10b.1.4 (covering all originated WF-064 + categorize routine flow). The remaining 15 actions are mapped via Contract Profiles A/B/C and service-only entries per Part 7.3.4.1 pattern.

| Permission action | Contract artifact | Endpoint(s) | Profile-specific error set | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `banking.bank_account.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/banking/bank-accounts` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-190.1, T-190.2 |
| `banking.bank_account.read` (without PII) | Profile A | `GET /api/banking/bank-accounts/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-190.3 |
| `banking.bank_account.read.decrypt_pii` | Profile C (audit-emitting) | `GET /api/banking/bank-accounts/{uuid}/decrypt-pii` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-190.4 |
| `banking.bank_account.create` | Profile B | `POST /api/banking/bank-accounts` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_LINK_TYPE_INVALID`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-190.5, T-190.6 |
| `banking.bank_account.update` | Profile B | `PATCH /api/banking/bank-accounts/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_VERSION_CONFLICT`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_TERMINAL` | T-190.7 |
| `banking.bank_account.deactivate` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `POST /api/banking/bank-accounts/{uuid}/deactivate` (§10b.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_NONZERO_BALANCE`, `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_ALREADY_CLOSED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-191.1..3 |
| `banking.bank_transaction.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/banking/bank-transactions` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-192.1 |
| `banking.bank_transaction.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-192.2 |
| `banking.bank_transaction.create_manual` | Profile B | `POST /api/banking/bank-transactions` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVE`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-192.3 |
| `banking.bank_transaction.import_statement` | Profile B | `POST /api/banking/bank-transactions/import` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVE`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-192.4 |
| `banking.bank_transaction.categorize` | FULL | `POST /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}/categorize` (§10b.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_TXN_ALREADY_CATEGORIZED`, `E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD`, `E_LINKED_TARGET_NOT_FOUND`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-192.7..10 |
| `banking.bank_transaction.recategorize_open_period` | Profile B | `POST /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}/recategorize` (open-period variant) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-192.11 |
| `banking.bank_transaction.recategorize_after_period_close` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `POST /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}/recategorize-closed-period` (§10b.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_NOT_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-192.5, T-192.6 |
| `banking.bank_transaction.delete` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `DELETE /api/banking/bank-transactions/{uuid}` (§10b.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BANK_TXN_DELETE_BLOCKED_RECONCILED_CLOSED`, `E_BANK_TXN_ALREADY_DELETED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-193.1, T-193.2 |
| `banking.factoring_transaction.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/banking/factoring-transactions` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-194.3 |
| `banking.factoring_transaction.read` | Profile A | `GET /api/banking/factoring-transactions/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`, `E_NOT_FOUND` | T-194.4 |
| `banking.factoring_transaction.create` (service function) | service-only | called from Accounting factoring flow | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (internal) | covered by Accounting tests |
| `banking.factoring_transaction.reverse` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `POST /api/banking/factoring-transactions/{uuid}/reverse` (§10b.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_FACTORING_TXN_ALREADY_REVERSED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-194.1, T-194.2 |
| `banking.reconciliation_run.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/banking/reconciliation-runs` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-194.5 |
| `banking.reconciliation_run.start` | Profile B | `POST /api/banking/reconciliation-runs` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_RECONCILIATION_IN_PROGRESS`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-194.6 |

**Coverage summary:**
- 5 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §10b.1.4 (deactivate, categorize routine, recategorize-closed-period, delete, factoring reverse)
- 15 additional permission actions are mapped via: Profile A read-only (7), Profile B CRUD non-WF-064 (6), Profile C audit-emitting non-WF-064 (1 — PII decrypt), service-only internal (1)
- Distribution: 5 FULL + 7 Profile A + 6 Profile B + 1 Profile C + 1 service-only = **20 of 20 permission actions** in §10b.1.7 now have an explicit API mapping artifact. No permission action is left without DoD closure.

### 10b.1.8 Element 8: Notifications

| Audit event | Channel | Recipient | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `banking.bank_account_created` | email | Owner + Admin + Accountant | info |
| `banking.bank_account_updated` (sampled) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `banking.bank_account_deactivated` | email | Owner + Admin + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `banking.bank_account_pii_decrypted` (audit-emitting view) | email | Owner | warning (audit-only; PII access logging) |
| `banking.bank_transaction_created` (sampled, statement import) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `banking.bank_transaction_created` (manual entry) | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `banking.bank_transaction_categorized` (sampled) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `banking.bank_transaction_recategorized_open_period` | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `banking.bank_transaction_recategorized_closed_period` | email | Owner + Admin + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `banking.bank_transaction_deleted` | email | Owner + Admin + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `banking.factoring_transaction_created` | email | Manager + Accountant | info |
| `banking.factoring_transaction_reversed` | email | Owner + Admin + Accountant | warning + WF-064 |
| `banking.reconciliation_run_started` | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `banking.reconciliation_run_completed` (drift = 0) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `banking.reconciliation_run_completed` (drift > 0) | email | Manager + Accountant | warning |
| `banking.reconciliation_run_completed` (drift > $1000) | email | Owner + Admin + Accountant | critical |

**MUST 10b.1.8.1 — No SMS for Banking events:** per Part 5.5.4 critical-only-SMS rule + Part 5.5.4.2 exception list, no Banking event meets the SMS bar in v1. Period reopen (Accounting) is the financial module's only SMS-allowed event.

**MUST 10b.1.8.2 — PII decrypt access audit logging:** every successful `bank_account.read.decrypt_pii` request emits an audit event regardless of routine vs investigative context. The Owner is notified by email of any decryption access, providing visibility into who is viewing sensitive account numbers and when.

**MUST 10b.1.8.3 — Reconciliation drift severity routing:** drift = 0 is sampled-only no-notification; drift > $0 to $1000 is warning to Manager+Accountant; drift > $1000 escalates to critical Owner+Admin+Accountant. This complements the Accounting `reconciliation_finding_detected` severity routing per Part 10a §10a.1.8.3.

### 10b.1.9 Element 9: Audit events

| Event class | Severity | Subject | Fires WF-064? | Symmetric pair |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `banking.bank_account_created` | info | the account | no | none |
| `banking.bank_account_updated` | info (sampled) | the account | no | none |
| `banking.bank_account_deactivated` | warning | the account | yes | `banking.bank_account_deactivate_blocked` |
| `banking.bank_account_deactivate_blocked` | warning | the account | yes | success: `banking.bank_account_deactivated` |
| `banking.bank_account_pii_decrypted` | warning | the account + viewer user_uuid | no | none (audit-only PII access logging) |
| `banking.bank_transaction_created` | info (sampled for import; full for manual) | the transaction | no | none |
| `banking.bank_transaction_categorized` | info (sampled) | the transaction + linked target | no | none |
| `banking.bank_transaction_recategorized_open_period` | info | the transaction + before/after links | no | none |
| `banking.bank_transaction_recategorized_closed_period` | warning | the transaction + before/after links + period | yes | `banking.bank_transaction_recategorize_closed_period_blocked` |
| `banking.bank_transaction_recategorize_closed_period_blocked` | warning | the transaction | yes | success: `banking.bank_transaction_recategorized_closed_period` |
| `banking.bank_transaction_deleted` | warning | the transaction | yes | `banking.bank_transaction_delete_blocked` |
| `banking.bank_transaction_delete_blocked` | warning | the transaction | yes | success: `banking.bank_transaction_deleted` |
| `banking.factoring_transaction_created` | info | the factoring transaction | no | none |
| `banking.factoring_transaction_reversed` | warning | the factoring transaction + reversing record | yes | `banking.factoring_transaction_reverse_blocked` |
| `banking.factoring_transaction_reverse_blocked` | warning | the factoring transaction | yes | success: `banking.factoring_transaction_reversed` |
| `banking.reconciliation_run_started` | info (sampled) | the reconciliation run | no | none |
| `banking.reconciliation_run_completed` | info / warning / critical (per drift) | the reconciliation run | no | none |

**MUST 10b.1.9.1** — All event INSERTs go through `audit.append_event()` per Part 4.9.3.2.

**MUST 10b.1.9.2** — Per Cursor's locked WF-064 success/blocked symmetry rule: 4 originated WF-064 actions × 2 = 8 paired audit events specific to Banking, plus 9 audit events for non-symmetric routine operations = 17 audit event classes total.

**MUST 10b.1.9.3** — `before_state_json` and `after_state_json` MUST be populated for: bank_account_deactivated (current_balance + status), bank_transaction_recategorized_closed_period (full before/after link target details + period), bank_transaction_deleted (full transaction payload), factoring_transaction_reversed (full original + reversing record). Sensitive financial data is permitted in audit JSON since audit access is Owner+Admin+Accountant only.

### 10b.1.10 Element 10: Parity tests

| Test ID | Description | WF traced | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| T-190.1 | GET /api/banking/bank-accounts returns RLS-scoped list; Manager sees read-only; Dispatcher returns 403 | WF-024 | 10b.1.7 + MUST 4.7.3.1 |
| T-190.2 | List filters by status, account_type, last_imported_at | WF-024 | 10b.1.4 |
| T-190.3 | GET bank-account/{uuid} returns row WITHOUT account_number_encrypted; last_four only | WF-032 | 10b.1.5.12 + MUST 4.7.3.2 |
| T-190.4 | GET bank-account/{uuid}/decrypt-pii by Owner returns decrypted account number; emits banking.bank_account_pii_decrypted warning | WF-032 | 10b.1.7 + 10b.1.8.2 |
| T-190.5 | Create bank account links to catalogs.accounts of valid type (bank/credit_card/other_current_asset); INSERT with invalid linked-account-type returns E_BANK_ACCOUNT_LINK_TYPE_INVALID | WF-033 | 10b.1.5.1 + MUST 4.7.2.1 |
| T-190.6 | Create bank account stores encrypted account_number + routing_number; plaintext never reaches DB | WF-032 | 10b.1.5.3 + MUST 4.7.2.3 |
| T-190.7 | PATCH bank-account/{uuid} with stale version returns E_VERSION_CONFLICT | WF-033 | Part 4.0.2.2 |
| T-190.8 | Driver caller to ANY /api/banking/* returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED per MUST 4.7.3.1 | WF-024 | 10b.1.7.2.1 |
| T-191.1 | Bank account deactivate by Owner with zero balance succeeds; emits bank_account_deactivated warning + WF-064 | WF-033 + WF-064 | 10b.1.4 |
| T-191.2 | Bank account deactivate by Accountant returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED + bank_account_deactivate_blocked | WF-064 | 10b.1.7 |
| T-191.3 | Bank account deactivate with non-zero balance returns E_BANK_ACCOUNT_NONZERO_BALANCE unless override flag | WF-033 | 10b.1.4 |
| T-191.4 | Bank account already-deactivated returns E_BANK_ACCOUNT_ALREADY_CLOSED | WF-033 | 10b.1.4 |
| T-191.5 | Deactivated account blocks new INSERT to bank_transactions; returns E_BANK_ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVE | WF-033 | 10b.1.5 |
| T-192.1 | GET bank-transactions list with categorization_status filter | WF-012 | 10b.1.4 |
| T-192.2 | GET bank-transaction/{uuid} returns transaction with linked-target metadata | WF-012 | 10b.1.4 |
| T-192.3 | Bank transaction manual create by Accountant succeeds; emits bank_transaction_created info | WF-052 | 10b.1.7.3 Profile B |
| T-192.4 | Bank transaction statement import (e.g. Plaid feed) creates batch of transactions; emits sampled audits | WF-012 | 10b.1.7.3 Profile B |
| T-192.5 | Bank transaction recategorize-closed-period by Owner emits bank_transaction_recategorized_closed_period warning + WF-064 | WF-013 + WF-064 | 10b.1.4 |
| T-192.6 | Bank transaction recategorize-closed-period by Accountant returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED + recategorize_closed_period_blocked | WF-064 | 10b.1.7 |
| T-192.7 | Bank transaction categorize via routine endpoint with target in OPEN period succeeds; emits sampled audit | WF-012 | 10b.1.4 |
| T-192.8 | Bank transaction categorize with target in CLOSED period returns E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD; routes to recategorize-closed-period instead | WF-012 + WF-013 | 10b.1.5.4 |
| T-192.9 | Bank transaction already-categorized returns E_BANK_TXN_ALREADY_CATEGORIZED with current link details | WF-012 | 10b.1.5.5 |
| T-192.10 | Bank transaction categorize with two linked-uuid columns set returns E_BANK_TXN_LINK_INVARIANT_VIOLATED (trigger-enforced exactly-one constraint) | WF-012 | 10b.1.5.2 + MUST 4.7.2.2 |
| T-192.11 | Bank transaction recategorize-open-period for routine corrections succeeds for office roles | WF-012 | 10b.1.7.3 Profile B |
| T-192.12 | Recategorize-closed-period with target in OPEN period returns E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_NOT_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD | WF-013 | 10b.1.5.6 |
| T-193.1 | Bank transaction delete by Owner soft-deletes (preserves audit trail); emits bank_transaction_deleted warning + WF-064 | WF-052 + WF-064 | 10b.1.4 + 10b.1.5.7 |
| T-193.2 | Bank transaction delete by Accountant returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED + bank_transaction_delete_blocked | WF-064 | 10b.1.7 |
| T-193.3 | Bank transaction delete on transaction included in closed-period reconciliation returns E_BANK_TXN_DELETE_BLOCKED_RECONCILED_CLOSED | WF-052 + WF-013 | 10b.1.5.8 |
| T-193.4 | Soft-deleted bank transactions preserved for audit; SELECT filters by deleted_at IS NULL by default | WF-052 | 10b.1.5.7 |
| T-194.1 | Factoring transaction reverse by Owner succeeds; emits factoring_transaction_reversed warning + WF-064 | WF-031 + WF-064 | 10b.1.4 |
| T-194.2 | Factoring transaction reverse by Accountant returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED + factoring_transaction_reverse_blocked | WF-064 | 10b.1.7 |
| T-194.3 | GET factoring-transactions list shows advances + reserve holds + releases chronologically | WF-031 | 10b.1.7.3 Profile A |
| T-194.4 | GET factoring-transaction/{uuid} returns full record with linked invoice (if advance) | WF-031 | 10b.1.7.3 Profile A |
| T-194.5 | GET reconciliation-runs list with date range filter | WF-013 | 10b.1.7.3 Profile A |
| T-194.6 | Reconciliation run start by Accountant queues async job; subsequent run while previous in-progress returns E_RECONCILIATION_IN_PROGRESS | WF-013 | 10b.1.7.3 Profile B |
| T-194.7 | Reconciliation run completed with drift > $1000 emits critical Owner notification per 10b.1.8.3 | WF-013 | 10b.1.8.3 |
| T-194.8 | Factoring transaction append-only invariant: UPDATE blocked by RLS for all roles; reversals are NEW rows | WF-031 | 10b.1.5.9 + Part 4.7.2 |
| T-194.9 | Coordinated reverse: accounting.factoring_assignment.reverse + banking.factoring_transaction.reverse fire as separate Owner actions; both emit WF-064 independently | WF-064 + WF-031 | 10b.1.7.1 Table B narrative |
| T-194.10 | All 4 originated Banking WF-064 actions display lightning-bolt icon per Part 6.4.2.5 + 2-step modal per Part 6.4.5.4 | WF-064 | 10b.1.7 |
| T-194.11 | All 4 originated Banking WF-064 actions emit success/blocked symmetric pairs (verified by enumerating audit table) | WF-064 | 10b.1.9.2 |
| T-194.12 | Inherited WF-064 count is 0 — no upstream module's WF-064 origination cascades to a separate Banking WF-064 audit | WF-064 | 10b.1.7.1 Table B |
| T-194.13 | Profile A read-only endpoints (7): all RLS-scoped; tested by sampling each | WF-024 | 10b.1.7.3 |
| T-194.14 | Profile B (CRUD non-WF-064) endpoints (6): bank-account create + update + bank-transaction manual-create + statement-import + recategorize-open + reconciliation-start covered | WF-012 + WF-013 + WF-033 + WF-052 | 10b.1.7.3 |
| T-194.15 | Profile C (PII decrypt) endpoint emits warning audit on every successful decrypt access | WF-032 | 10b.1.7.3 + 10b.1.8.2 |

#### 10b.1.10.1 Banking-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_LINK_TYPE_INVALID` | 422 | Linked catalogs.accounts row not of type bank/credit_card/other_current_asset (MUST 4.7.2.1) |
| `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_NONZERO_BALANCE` | 422 | Cannot deactivate account with non-zero balance |
| `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_ALREADY_CLOSED` | 409 | Account already deactivated |
| `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_TERMINAL` | 422 | Account in terminal state; mutation blocked |
| `E_BANK_ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVE` | 422 | Cannot insert transaction against non-active bank account |
| `E_BANK_TXN_LINK_INVARIANT_VIOLATED` | 422 | Categorization sets zero or multiple linked_*_uuid columns (MUST 4.7.2.2) |
| `E_BANK_TXN_ALREADY_CATEGORIZED` | 422 | Bank transaction already categorized; use recategorize endpoint |
| `E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD` | 422 | Routine categorize blocked when target is in closed period |
| `E_BANK_TXN_TARGET_NOT_IN_CLOSED_PERIOD` | 422 | recategorize-closed-period requires target in closed period |
| `E_BANK_TXN_DELETE_BLOCKED_RECONCILED_CLOSED` | 422 | Cannot delete transaction included in closed-period reconciliation |
| `E_BANK_TXN_ALREADY_DELETED` | 409 | Bank transaction already soft-deleted |
| `E_LINKED_TARGET_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Categorize target object does not exist |
| `E_FACTORING_TXN_ALREADY_REVERSED` | 409 | Factoring transaction already reversed |
| `E_RECONCILIATION_IN_PROGRESS` | 409 | A reconciliation run is already in progress |
