# IH35 Master Blueprint v3 — Part 11: Documents + Notifications Modules

**Section in v3 hierarchy:** Part 11 of 13. Reads after Part 10b (Banking). Per Cursor-locked Option 2 chunking and Jorge's split-delivery directive: Part 10a covered Accounting; Part 10b covered Banking; Part 11 covers Documents + Notifications combined (matching the multi-module pattern of Part 9).

**Status (initial draft):** Awaiting Cursor strict review per the same 8-check acceptance gate applied to Parts 10a + 10b (template order, API DoD completeness, WF-064 A/B clarity, no schema drift, trace matrix update, test ID continuity, endpoint/profile split, no stale contradictory counts).

All Part 7-10b locks carried forward:
- 10-element module template in order (per module)
- Strict 6-element API DoD with explicit "Not applicable" annotation
- WF-064 success/blocked symmetry with explicit Tables A/B
- No schema drift / no new namespaces (14-schema lock)
- Incremental 11-column trace matrix updates
- Sequential, non-colliding test IDs
- FULL endpoint + Contract Profile mapping split for size management
- Single canonical count statements per category (no contradictions)
- Cross-part trace matrix consistency sweep on every count change

**Normative language:** MUST · MUST NOT · SHOULD · SHOULD NOT · MAY (RFC 2119).

**Test ID range:** Part 11 uses T-195.x through T-209.x exclusively. Documents: T-195..T-201; Notifications: T-202..T-209. Verified non-colliding with Parts 1-10b (max prior test ID: T-194.15 in Part 10b §10b.1.10).

**Trace matrix:** delivered alongside this Part as appended REQ-DOC + REQ-NOTIF rows in `IH35_REQUIREMENT_TRACE_MATRIX_V3.csv`. 11-column structure preserved.

---

## 11.0 — PART 11 MODULE PLACEMENT

Documents and Notifications are the two final operational modules in Part 4. Both are leaf schemas (no inbound FKs from outside their schemas per Part 4.0.3) but with critical roles:

1. **Documents** owns the chain-of-custody evidence records that underpin every photo/PDF/scanned-document referenced from any other module. The single canonical reference table `documents.evidence_records` is heavily FK'd from across the system (Maintenance work-order photos, Safety DVIR/accident evidence, Driver Finance liability acknowledgments, Banking fee receipts, etc.). Direct INSERT to `documents.evidence_records` is blocked per MUST 3.17.2.1; only the `documents.create_evidence` service function can write.

2. **Notifications** owns the dispatch envelope around every audit event with a notification consequence — the WF-064 envelope (per Part 6.8.3 + 4.9.3.3), routine warning + critical notifications, and the user-facing preference + suppression mechanics. Notifications is the asynchronous-delivery layer behind the audit-event-to-recipient routing logic.

Both modules are smaller in code surface than Accounting or Driver Finance, but both have significant invariants — chain-of-custody integrity (Documents) and notification suppression discipline (Notifications). Combining them in one Part matches the Part 9 multi-module pattern (Maintenance + Safety + Fuel).

WFs implemented in Part 11:

| WF | Module | Name |
|---|---|---|
| WF-025 | Documents | Evidence Record Chain-of-Custody |
| WF-028 | Documents | Legal Hold Set / Release |
| WF-029 | Documents | Evidence Retention + Purge Lifecycle |
| WF-030 | Documents | Evidence Tamper Detection (sha256 mismatch) |
| WF-034 | Documents | Signed-PDF Generator |
| WF-042 | Notifications | Notification Dispatch (multi-channel: email/SMS/PWA push) |
| WF-043 | Notifications | WF-064 Critical Path Notification Envelope |
| WF-045 | Notifications | User Preference Routing |
| WF-047 | Notifications | Suppression Rules (WF-064.3 max-7-day window) |
| WF-048 | Notifications | Delivery Provider Failover (Twilio + Resend + Expo) |
| WF-051 | Notifications | Bounce + Failed Delivery Handling |

Cross-cuts WF-024 (RBAC) and WF-064 (high-risk audit) across both modules.

---

## 11.1 — DOCUMENTS MODULE

### 11.1.1 Element 1: Module overview

**Purpose:** Documents owns the canonical evidence-record table and the chain-of-custody pipeline that every other module relies on for photo, PDF, and scanned-document storage. It enforces hash-based tamper detection per WF-030, retention policy per WF-029, legal hold per WF-028, and signed-PDF generation per WF-034. The schema is intentionally minimal — a single canonical reference table plus access/purge logging — but the invariants are critical.

**Owning schema:** `documents` (per Part 4.9.1 + Part 3.17).

**Key invariants:**
- Every evidence record MUST be created via `documents.create_evidence` service function per MUST 3.17.2.1 — direct INSERT blocked by RLS for ALL roles including Owner.
- `payload_sha256_hash` is computed server-side from uploaded payload bytes BEFORE storage per MUST 3.17.2.3 — any later mismatch = tamper detection.
- `captured_at` (device-claimed timestamp) and `server_received_at` (server-authoritative) MAY differ; legal/audit purposes use `server_received_at` exclusively per MUST 3.17.2.2.
- Legal hold set/release requires Owner OR Administrator with reason text per MUST 3.17.6.2-3; emits critical audit event.
- Retention enforcement: nightly cron scans for records past `retention_expires_at` AND `legal_hold = false` AND `voided_at IS NULL` per MUST 3.17.4.2.
- Audit retention floor: 7 years minimum per MUST 4.9.3.4 (IRS / FMCSA / litigation buffer); evidence retention MAY follow shorter category-specific periods set in `retention_expires_at`.
- Retrieval MUST be logged in `documents.evidence_access_log` with who/when/why/source-IP per MUST 3.17.7.3.
- Tamper detection: sha256 mismatch on retrieval emits critical `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` audit per Part 3.17 + WF-030.

**Cross-module dependencies:**
- READS from canonical reference: `master_data.drivers` (for driver_uuid linkage when evidence relates to a driver), `master_data.units`, `master_data.equipment`, `master_data.vendors`, `master_data.customers` (for related-entity linkage).
- READ-ONLY from every operational module that holds evidence_uuid references — Maintenance work-orders + photos, Safety DVIR/accident/roadside, Driver Finance liability acknowledgments, Banking fee receipts, Dispatch BOL/proof-of-delivery, Fuel manual-entry receipts.
- WRITES are EXCLUSIVELY via `documents.create_evidence()` service function called from every other module's evidence-attaching flow. The Documents module does NOT call other schemas — it's a pure leaf with no outbound writes except to its own access log + audit + outbox for R2 sync.
- WRITES to shared schemas: `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()`; `outbox.queue` for Cloudflare R2 storage operations (upload, copy-to-cold, purge) per Part 3.17.4.

**WFs implemented:** WF-025, WF-028, WF-029, WF-030, WF-034. Cross-cuts WF-024 (RBAC), WF-064 (high-risk audit). **7 distinct WFs traced in this module.**

### 11.1.2 Element 2: UI behaviors

#### 11.1.2.1 Evidence retrieval (`/documents/evidence/{uuid}`)

**MUST 11.1.2.1.1 — Retrieve view:** office-staff retrieval of an evidence record by UUID. Display shows: evidence type, captured_at, server_received_at, captured_by user, related_entity_type + uuid (linked to source module), legal_hold badge if set, retention_expires_at, payload_sha256_hash (last 8 chars displayed; full hash on hover), download/view button.

**MUST 11.1.2.1.2 — Retrieval purpose code (per MUST 3.17.7.3):** every retrieval requires the user to select a purpose code: `audit / legal / routine_view / dispute / support`. The selection writes to `documents.evidence_access_log` along with user_uuid, source_ip, retrieved_at.

**MUST 11.1.2.1.3 — Tamper detection inline:** on retrieval, the API recomputes sha256 from the R2-stored bytes and compares to `payload_sha256_hash`. If mismatch, the response is replaced with a tamper-warning page + critical audit event `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` is emitted; the actual payload is NOT delivered until tamper is investigated and resolved by Owner action.

#### 11.1.2.2 Evidence list (`/documents/evidence`)

**MUST 11.1.2.2.1 — Filters:** evidence_type multi-select, related_entity_type, captured_at date range, has-legal-hold toggle, captured_by user, voided toggle.

**MUST 11.1.2.2.2 — Table columns:** evidence_id (truncated UUID display), evidence_type badge, captured_at, server_received_at, captured_by, related_entity_link, legal_hold badge per Part 6.4.4 yellow band, retention_expires_at relative ("expires in 14 days") with red severe band when <30 days.

#### 11.1.2.3 Legal hold management (`/documents/legal-holds`)

**MUST 11.1.2.3.1 — Active legal holds list:** rows showing each evidence record currently under legal hold. Columns: evidence_id, legal_hold_set_at, set_by, reason, related_litigation_reference (free-text field), expected_release_date.

**MUST 11.1.2.3.2 — Set legal hold action** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-028): Owner + Administrator only. 2-step confirmation modal per Part 6.4.5.4 with typed phrase "SET LEGAL HOLD" + reason ≥30 chars + optional related_litigation_reference. Critical audit event `documents.legal_hold_set` per MUST 3.17.6.2.

**MUST 11.1.2.3.3 — Release legal hold action** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-028): Owner + Administrator only. 2-step confirmation modal with typed phrase "RELEASE LEGAL HOLD" + reason ≥30 chars. Critical audit event `documents.legal_hold_released` per MUST 3.17.6.3. After release, the evidence becomes subject to normal retention rules.

#### 11.1.2.4 Retention policy view (`/documents/retention`)

**MUST 11.1.2.4.1 — Pending purge queue:** records past retention with legal_hold=false and voided_at=null displayed for review. Purge runs nightly per MUST 3.17.4.2; this UI is the office-staff visibility into what's queued.

**MUST 11.1.2.4.2 — Force-purge action** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-029): Owner only. Out-of-cycle purge for specific evidence_uuid (e.g. driver-deletion request after retention compliance). 2-step confirmation modal with typed phrase "FORCE PURGE EVIDENCE" + reason ≥30 chars + acknowledge_irrecoverable=true.

#### 11.1.2.5 Signed-PDF generator (`/documents/signed-pdf/new`)

**MUST 11.1.2.5.1 — PDF creation flow (WF-034):** office-staff trigger for generating a signed PDF (e.g. liability acknowledgment ready for driver counter-signature, terms-of-service document, accident-statement form). Form fields: pdf_template_type (dropdown of supported templates per Part 3.17.3), context payload (template-specific fields), signing_workflow (none / driver_pwa / docusign).

**MUST 11.1.2.5.2 — Output:** generates PDF server-side, computes sha256, calls `documents.create_evidence()` with type `signed_pdf_template` and the rendered bytes. Returns evidence_uuid for downstream linkage.

### 11.1.3 Element 3: Data entities

The Documents module owns these tables (per Part 4.9.1 + Part 3.17):

- `documents.evidence_records` — the canonical reference table (DDL in 3.17.2, 30+ columns including chain-of-custody fields). One row per evidence artifact (photo/PDF/document); UUID primary key; sha256 hash for tamper detection; legal_hold + retention + voided flags; related_entity_type/uuid for source-module linkage.
- `documents.evidence_access_log` — leaf table referenced by MUST 3.17.7.3. Each retrieval logged with user_uuid, retrieved_at, purpose_code (audit/legal/routine_view/dispute/support), source_ip. Append-only.
- `documents.evidence_purge_log` — leaf table referenced in 4.9.1 narrative as part of the documents leaf set. Each purge event logged with evidence_uuid, purged_at, purged_by, retention_expires_at_at_purge, reason_if_force.

**3 tables** in the documents schema per Part 4.9.1. **`documents.evidence_records` has explicit DDL in 3.17.2.** The two leaf logging tables (`evidence_access_log`, `evidence_purge_log`) are referenced in the locked normative MUSTs (3.17.7.3 and 4.9.1) but their column-level DDL is not explicitly enumerated in Part 3 or Part 4 — they are leaf logging tables whose DDL follows the standard pattern (audit-style append-only) and is implementation-defined per the standard envelope of: uuid PK, created_at, user_uuid actor, evidence_uuid FK, plus event-specific fields. **No new tables introduced beyond what Part 4.9.1 + Part 3.17 establish.** Schema lock per Part 4 Rev 2 honored.

If a future spec amendment (with explicit Owner approval as a lock change) adds explicit DDL for the two log tables, this section MUST be updated. Until then, the tables are referenced as leaf logging artifacts per the locked narrative.

**The Documents module reads from these canonical reference tables in other schemas:**
- `master_data.drivers`, `master_data.units`, `master_data.equipment`, `master_data.vendors`, `master_data.customers` (for related_entity_type + related_entity_uuid resolution at retrieval time)

**The Documents module is written to EXCLUSIVELY via `documents.create_evidence()` service function** (per MUST 3.17.2.1). All other modules that attach evidence call this function. There is no direct cross-schema INSERT path.

**The Documents module writes to shared schemas:**
- `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()` (for evidence created/voided/purged/legal_hold_set/legal_hold_released/tampered_detected events)
- `outbox.queue` for Cloudflare R2 operations (upload, copy-to-cold-storage, purge) per Part 3.17.4

### 11.1.4 Element 4: API contracts

This module follows the FULL endpoint + Contract Profile split lock. FULL endpoints below cover the highest-risk paths: evidence retrieve (with tamper detection inline), legal hold set, legal hold release, force purge. Standard CRUD + query endpoints map to Profile A/B/C in §11.1.7.3.

#### `GET /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}`

Retrieve an evidence record. Inline sha256 verification triggers tamper detection per WF-030.

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `documents.evidence.read` (any office role; driver-self for own-driver evidence per RLS scope below).

**Example request:**
```http
GET /api/documents/evidence/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid100
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
X-Retrieval-Purpose: routine_view
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid100",
    "evidence_type": "wo_damage_photo",
    "captured_at": "2026-05-01T14:23:00Z",
    "server_received_at": "2026-05-01T14:24:15Z",
    "captured_by_user_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000usr05",
    "related_entity_type": "maintenance.work_orders",
    "related_entity_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000wo0001",
    "driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000d042",
    "payload_sha256_hash": "8a9f4c2e...b6d2f1c3",
    "tamper_check_passed": true,
    "legal_hold": false,
    "retention_expires_at": "2033-05-01",
    "voided_at": null,
    "r2_signed_url": "https://r2.cloudflare.com/...?signature=...",
    "r2_signed_url_expires_at": "2026-05-02T23:59:00Z",
    "access_log_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000eacc01"
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400, missing purpose header):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Retrieval request invalid",
    "details": [{"field": "X-Retrieval-Purpose", "reason": "header required; must be one of: audit, legal, routine_view, dispute, support per MUST 3.17.7.3"}],
    "request_id": "req_01923d20"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (409, tamper detected):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_EVIDENCE_TAMPERED",
    "message": "sha256 mismatch detected on retrieval — payload integrity compromised; access blocked pending Owner investigation",
    "details": {
      "expected_hash": "8a9f4c2e...b6d2f1c3",
      "computed_hash": "0d2e1f0a...c8b7a4d5",
      "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi90",
      "tamper_detected_at": "2026-05-02T23:55:00Z"
    },
    "request_id": "req_01923d20"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'documents.evidence.read' for evidence linked to another driver requires office role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "driver", "caller_driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000d099", "evidence_driver_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000000d042"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d20"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_NOT_FOUND` (baseline), `E_EVIDENCE_TAMPERED` (11.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `documents.evidence_retrieved` (info — sampled at 10%); writes row to `documents.evidence_access_log` per MUST 3.17.7.3. On tamper, emits `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` (critical, fires WF-064).

---

#### `POST /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/legal-hold`

Set legal hold on an evidence record. **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action with critical-severity audit per MUST 3.17.6.2.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `documents.legal_hold.set` (Owner or Administrator only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/documents/evidence/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid100/legal-hold
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Setting legal hold on accident evidence for case Smith v. IH35 Transportation; expecting discovery requests within 30 days; do not release until counsel approval",
  "related_litigation_reference": "Smith v. IH35 Transportation, Webb County Court Cause No. 2026-CV-0428",
  "expected_release_date": null
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid100",
    "previous_legal_hold": false,
    "new_legal_hold": true,
    "legal_hold_set_at": "2026-05-02T23:58:00Z",
    "legal_hold_set_by": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000usr01",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi91",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif60", "severity": "critical"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Legal hold request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters per MUST 3.17.6.2"},
      {"field": "related_litigation_reference", "reason": "recommended; free-text reference to discovery context"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923d21"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, already on hold):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_LEGAL_HOLD_ALREADY_SET",
    "message": "Legal hold already set at 2026-04-15T10:00:00Z by user U-007",
    "details": {"legal_hold_set_at": "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z", "legal_hold_set_by": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000usr07"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d21"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'documents.legal_hold.set' requires Owner or Administrator role per MUST 3.17.6.2",
    "details": {"caller_role": "accountant"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d21"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_LEGAL_HOLD_ALREADY_SET` (11.1.10.1), `E_NOT_FOUND` (baseline), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `documents.legal_hold_set` (critical, fires WF-064). On block, emits `documents.legal_hold_set_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `DELETE /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/legal-hold`

Release legal hold on an evidence record. **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action with critical-severity audit per MUST 3.17.6.3.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `documents.legal_hold.release` (Owner or Administrator only).

**Example request:**
```http
DELETE /api/documents/evidence/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid100/legal-hold
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Releasing legal hold after counsel confirmed Smith v. IH35 Transportation case settled and discovery period closed; evidence now subject to normal retention rules"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid100",
    "previous_legal_hold": true,
    "new_legal_hold": false,
    "legal_hold_released_at": "2026-05-03T00:05:00Z",
    "legal_hold_released_by": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000usr01",
    "retention_expires_at_now_active": "2033-05-01",
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi92",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif61", "severity": "critical"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Legal hold release request invalid",
    "details": [{"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters per MUST 3.17.6.3"}],
    "request_id": "req_01923d22"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, not on hold):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_LEGAL_HOLD_NOT_SET",
    "message": "Cannot release legal hold; evidence is not currently under legal hold",
    "details": {"current_legal_hold": false},
    "request_id": "req_01923d22"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'documents.legal_hold.release' requires Owner or Administrator role per MUST 3.17.6.3",
    "details": {"caller_role": "manager"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d22"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_LEGAL_HOLD_NOT_SET` (11.1.10.1), `E_NOT_FOUND` (baseline), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `documents.legal_hold_released` (critical, fires WF-064). On block, emits `documents.legal_hold_release_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/force-purge`

Force-purge an evidence record outside the normal nightly cron cycle. **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action; coordinates with R2 storage delete per Part 3.17.4.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `documents.evidence.force_purge` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/documents/evidence/01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid200/force-purge
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "reason": "Force-purging evidence per driver Carlos GDPR-equivalent deletion request after retention period elapsed and no active litigation; coordinated with counsel review confirming no legal hold needed",
  "acknowledge_irrecoverable": true
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "evidence_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000evid200",
    "purged_at": "2026-05-03T00:10:00Z",
    "purge_log_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000epurge1",
    "r2_delete_outbox_queued": true,
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi93",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif62"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Force purge request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters"},
      {"field": "acknowledge_irrecoverable", "reason": "required and must be true"}
    ]
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, legal hold active):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PURGE_BLOCKED_LEGAL_HOLD",
    "message": "Cannot purge evidence under active legal hold; release legal hold first via release endpoint",
    "details": {"legal_hold": true, "legal_hold_set_at": "2026-04-15T10:00:00Z"}
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'documents.evidence.force_purge' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "administrator"}
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PURGE_BLOCKED_LEGAL_HOLD` (11.1.10.1), `E_EVIDENCE_ALREADY_PURGED` (11.1.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `documents.evidence_purged` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `documents.evidence_force_purge_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry). Inserts row to `documents.evidence_purge_log` and queues R2 delete outbox.

### 11.1.5 Element 5: Validation rules

**MUST 11.1.5.1 — Service-function-only entry (per MUST 3.17.2.1):** direct INSERT to `documents.evidence_records` blocked by RLS for ALL roles. Only `documents.create_evidence()` service function can write. Direct INSERT attempt returns `E_EVIDENCE_DIRECT_INSERT_FORBIDDEN`.

**MUST 11.1.5.2 — Server-computed sha256 (per MUST 3.17.2.3):** `payload_sha256_hash` is computed by the server from uploaded payload bytes BEFORE storage. Client-supplied hashes are ignored — the API recomputes regardless of any provided hash field.

**MUST 11.1.5.3 — Server timestamp authoritative (per MUST 3.17.2.2):** `captured_at` (device-claimed) and `server_received_at` (server-authoritative) MAY differ. The server NEVER uses device timestamp for legal/audit purposes — `server_received_at` is canonical for audit chains, retention computation, and tamper detection windows.

**MUST 11.1.5.4 — Tamper detection on retrieval (WF-030):** every retrieval recomputes sha256 from R2-stored bytes. If mismatch, response is replaced with `E_EVIDENCE_TAMPERED` (409); critical audit `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` is emitted; payload bytes are NOT delivered. Resolution requires Owner action with investigation evidence.

**MUST 11.1.5.5 — Retrieval purpose code required (per MUST 3.17.7.3):** `X-Retrieval-Purpose` request header MUST be set to one of `audit / legal / routine_view / dispute / support`. Missing or invalid value returns `E_VALIDATION`. Each retrieval writes a row to `documents.evidence_access_log` with the purpose, user_uuid, source_ip, retrieved_at.

**MUST 11.1.5.6 — Legal hold reason length (per MUST 3.17.6.2-3):** `reason` for legal_hold set OR release MUST be ≥30 chars. Shorter reason returns `E_VALIDATION`.

**MUST 11.1.5.7 — Legal hold prevents purge:** retention nightly cron skips records with `legal_hold = true`. Force-purge endpoint also blocks (returns `E_PURGE_BLOCKED_LEGAL_HOLD`) — must release legal hold first via the release endpoint.

**MUST 11.1.5.8 — Voided evidence (audit chain preserved):** voiding an evidence record sets `voided_at` timestamp + `voided_by` user_uuid + `voided_reason`. Row is NOT deleted; the evidence remains for audit chain integrity. Future retrievals return the row with `voided_at` populated and a warning indicator.

**MUST 11.1.5.9 — Force-purge irrecoverable acknowledgment:** force-purge endpoint requires `acknowledge_irrecoverable: true` in body. Missing or false returns `E_VALIDATION`. Force-purge deletes from R2 storage AND removes the database row (this is a true delete, unlike voiding which preserves the row).

**MUST 11.1.5.10 — Force-purge logs to evidence_purge_log:** every purge (cron-driven OR force-purge) writes a row to `documents.evidence_purge_log` with: evidence_uuid, purged_at, purged_by, retention_expires_at_at_purge, force_flag (true/false), reason_if_force.

**MUST 11.1.5.11 — Retention expires_at immutable post-creation:** `retention_expires_at` is set at creation by `documents.create_evidence()` per evidence_type policy (e.g. accident photos: 7 years; routine fuel receipts: 3 years per Part 3.17). Direct UPDATE to this column blocked by trigger. Retention extension requires Owner action through a dedicated extend-retention flow (not in v1 — handled via legal_hold for extension semantics).

### 11.1.6 Element 6: Accounting effects

Documents has **no direct accounting effects.** It owns no monetary records, posts no journal entries, creates no bills or invoices. Force-purge, voiding, and legal hold operations are pure metadata/audit operations.

The closest indirect effect: when an evidence record is force-purged or voided, downstream modules that referenced the evidence_uuid (e.g., a `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` row with `source_evidence_uuid` pointing to a purged record) retain the UUID reference but the linked retrieval will now return `E_EVIDENCE_PURGED` (404) or `E_EVIDENCE_VOIDED` (warning indicator). Downstream modules SHOULD handle these reference-becomes-stale states gracefully but the Documents module itself does not push notifications to those modules — they discover stale references on next retrieval.

**No posting templates referenced from this module.**

### 11.1.7 Element 7: Permissions

**Role matrix for Documents actions:**

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `documents.evidence.list` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ ¹ | ✓ ¹ | self ² | ✓ ¹ |
| `documents.evidence.read` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ ¹ | ✓ ¹ | self ² | ✓ ¹ |
| `documents.evidence.create` (service function) | service | service | service | service | service | service | service | service |
| `documents.evidence.void` (audit-trail-preserving) | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `documents.legal_hold.set` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `documents.legal_hold.release` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `documents.evidence.force_purge` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `documents.evidence_access_log.list` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `documents.evidence_purge_log.list` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `documents.signed_pdf.generate` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ ³ | ✓ ³ | — | ✓ ³ |

¹ Office roles SELECT evidence linked to entities they have access to (e.g., Dispatcher sees evidence linked to their loads; Safety sees evidence linked to safety incidents; Mechanic sees evidence linked to work orders they're assigned to).
² Driver SELECT scoped to evidence rows where `driver_uuid` matches their own driver_uuid (own DVIR photos, own liability acknowledgments, own settlement evidence).
³ Signed-PDF generation by office roles for legitimate operational documents (e.g., dispatcher generating BOL signed PDF; safety generating accident statement signed PDF). Mechanic generating signed work-order completion PDF.

#### 11.1.7.1 WF-064 originated vs inherited mapping (Part 7 Tables A/B pattern)

**A. Originated in Documents (4 actions):**

| # | Action | Originating Module | Why Documents is the originator | API endpoint | Audit event(s) (success / blocked) | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `documents.legal_hold.set` | documents | Originates here — legal hold is a litigation-defensive critical-severity action per MUST 3.17.6.2; affects retention + purge cron behavior | `POST /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/legal-hold` (§11.1.4) | `documents.legal_hold_set` / `documents.legal_hold_set_blocked` | T-196.1, T-196.2 |
| 2 | `documents.legal_hold.release` | documents | Originates here — releasing legal hold restores normal retention rules per MUST 3.17.6.3; coordinated with counsel | `DELETE /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/legal-hold` (§11.1.4) | `documents.legal_hold_released` / `documents.legal_hold_release_blocked` | T-196.3, T-196.4 |
| 3 | `documents.evidence.force_purge` | documents | Originates here — out-of-cycle purge is irrecoverable + Owner-only per WF-029 | `POST /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/force-purge` (§11.1.4) | `documents.evidence_purged` / `documents.evidence_force_purge_blocked` | T-197.1, T-197.2 |
| 4 | `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` | documents | Originates here — sha256 mismatch on retrieval is a critical security signal per WF-030; auto-fires from retrieval flow when integrity check fails | `GET /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}` (auto-trigger; §11.1.4) | `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` (no symmetric blocked variant — this is auto-detection, not a user action) | T-198.1, T-198.2 |

**Note on WF-064 #4:** `evidence_tampered_detected` is the only WF-064 originated audit in this module without a symmetric `_blocked` variant, because it is triggered automatically by the system on hash mismatch — there is no "blocked" path for an auto-detection event. The originating event itself IS the WF-064 audit; no user action to block. This is the spec's first WF-064 originated event of this type — every prior WF-064 has been a user-initiated action with success+blocked symmetry. Documenting this exception explicitly to avoid count ambiguity in §11.1.9.

**Total: 4 originated WF-064 actions in Documents.** Three follow the standard success/blocked symmetric pattern (8 paired audit events); one is auto-detection-only (1 standalone audit event). Total Documents WF-064 audit event classes: 9.

**B. Inherited through Documents (0 actual):**

Per the precedent established in Parts 7-10b: every cross-module write into Documents via `documents.create_evidence()` is a routine downstream effect of the originating module's flow (e.g., Maintenance work-order photo upload, Safety DVIR submission, Driver Finance liability acknowledgment). The originating module owns the WF-064 audit (or non-WF-064 routine action) at its source; Documents executes the chain-of-custody record and emits info-level `documents.evidence_created` events that are NOT WF-064 inheritances — they are routine evidence registrations.

**Total: Documents participates in 4 WF-064 paths — 4 originated + 0 inherited.**

#### 11.1.7.2 Driver self-access scope

**MUST 11.1.7.2.1 — Driver SELECT scope on evidence (per MUST 3.18.12):** drivers SELECT evidence rows where `driver_uuid = caller's driver_uuid` via RLS. Cross-driver SELECT returns empty result (existence-disclosure defense). Drivers cannot SELECT evidence_access_log or evidence_purge_log.

**MUST 11.1.7.2.2 — Driver retrieval purpose:** drivers can only use purpose code `routine_view` for their own evidence. Purpose codes `audit / legal / dispute / support` reserved for office roles.

**MUST 11.1.7.2.3 — Driver cannot void or set legal hold:** drivers have no mutation access to evidence records. All operational mutations (void, legal hold, force purge) are office-only.

#### 11.1.7.3 Contract Profile Mapping (Cursor Part 7.3.4.1 pattern)

The Documents module has 10 permission actions in §11.1.7. 4 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §11.1.4. The remaining 6 actions are mapped via Contract Profiles A/B/C and service-only entries per Part 7.3.4.1 pattern.

| Permission action | Contract artifact | Endpoint(s) | Profile-specific error set | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `documents.evidence.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/documents/evidence` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-195.1, T-195.2 |
| `documents.evidence.read` | FULL | `GET /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}` (§11.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_NOT_FOUND`, `E_EVIDENCE_TAMPERED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-195.3..6 |
| `documents.evidence.create` | service-only | called from every module via `documents.create_evidence()` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` (internal) | covered by originating module tests |
| `documents.evidence.void` | Profile B | `POST /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/void` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_EVIDENCE_ALREADY_VOIDED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-199.1, T-199.2 |
| `documents.legal_hold.set` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `POST /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/legal-hold` (§11.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_LEGAL_HOLD_ALREADY_SET`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-196.1, T-196.2 |
| `documents.legal_hold.release` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `DELETE /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/legal-hold` (§11.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_LEGAL_HOLD_NOT_SET`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-196.3, T-196.4 |
| `documents.evidence.force_purge` ⚡ WF-064 | FULL | `POST /api/documents/evidence/{uuid}/force-purge` (§11.1.4) | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PURGE_BLOCKED_LEGAL_HOLD`, `E_EVIDENCE_ALREADY_PURGED`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-197.1, T-197.2 |
| `documents.evidence_access_log.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/documents/evidence-access-log` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-200.1 |
| `documents.evidence_purge_log.list` | Profile A | `GET /api/documents/evidence-purge-log` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-200.2 |
| `documents.signed_pdf.generate` | Profile B | `POST /api/documents/signed-pdf/generate` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_PDF_TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-201.1, T-201.2 |

**Coverage summary:**
- 4 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §11.1.4 (read, legal hold set, legal hold release, force purge)
- 6 additional permission actions are mapped via: Profile A read-only (3), Profile B CRUD non-WF-064 (2), service-only internal (1)
- Distribution: 4 FULL + 3 Profile A + 2 Profile B + 0 Profile C + 1 service-only = **10 of 10 permission actions** in §11.1.7 now have an explicit API mapping artifact. No permission action is left without DoD closure.

### 11.1.8 Element 8: Notifications

| Audit event | Channel | Recipient | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `documents.evidence_created` | none | n/a | info — sampled at 1% (very high volume) |
| `documents.evidence_retrieved` | none | n/a | info — sampled at 10% |
| `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` | email + SMS | Owner + Administrator | critical + WF-064 (auto-detection; one of few SMS-allowed events per Part 5.5.4.2 exception list) |
| `documents.evidence_voided` | email | Manager + Accountant | warning |
| `documents.evidence_purged` (cron-driven) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `documents.evidence_purged` (force) | email | Owner + Administrator | warning + WF-064 |
| `documents.legal_hold_set` | email | Owner + Administrator + Manager | critical + WF-064 |
| `documents.legal_hold_released` | email | Owner + Administrator + Manager | critical + WF-064 |
| `documents.signed_pdf_generated` | none | n/a | info — sampled |

**MUST 11.1.8.1 — SMS-allowed events:** `evidence_tampered_detected` is the only Documents event that meets the SMS bar per Part 5.5.4.2 (security-critical anomaly requiring immediate Owner attention). All other Documents events are email-only.

**MUST 11.1.8.2 — Critical legal hold notifications:** legal_hold set + release fire critical-severity emails to Owner + Administrator + Manager because they signal potential litigation involvement and have downstream retention implications.

### 11.1.9 Element 9: Audit events

| Event class | Severity | Subject | Fires WF-064? | Symmetric pair |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `documents.evidence_created` | info (sampled) | the evidence record | no | none |
| `documents.evidence_retrieved` | info (sampled) | the evidence + retriever + purpose | no | none |
| `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` | critical | the evidence + computed_hash | yes | none — auto-detection only (no user-action blocked variant; explained in §11.1.7.1 note) |
| `documents.evidence_voided` | warning | the evidence + reason | no | none |
| `documents.evidence_purged` (cron) | info (sampled) | the evidence + retention_at_purge | no | none |
| `documents.evidence_purged` (force) | warning | the evidence + reason | yes | `documents.evidence_force_purge_blocked` |
| `documents.evidence_force_purge_blocked` | warning | the evidence | yes | success: `documents.evidence_purged` (force) |
| `documents.legal_hold_set` | critical | the evidence + reason + litigation_ref | yes | `documents.legal_hold_set_blocked` |
| `documents.legal_hold_set_blocked` | warning | the evidence | yes | success: `documents.legal_hold_set` |
| `documents.legal_hold_released` | critical | the evidence + reason | yes | `documents.legal_hold_release_blocked` |
| `documents.legal_hold_release_blocked` | warning | the evidence | yes | success: `documents.legal_hold_released` |
| `documents.signed_pdf_generated` | info (sampled) | the generated evidence | no | none |

**MUST 11.1.9.1** — All event INSERTs go through `audit.append_event()` per Part 4.9.3.2.

**MUST 11.1.9.2** — Per Cursor's locked WF-064 success/blocked symmetry rule applied to user-initiated WF-064 actions: 3 user-initiated originated WF-064 actions × 2 = 6 paired audit events. Plus 1 auto-detection WF-064 event without symmetric pair (`evidence_tampered_detected`) = 7 total WF-064 audit event classes specific to Documents. Plus 5 non-WF-064 routine audit events = 12 audit event classes total.

**MUST 11.1.9.3** — `before_state_json` and `after_state_json` MUST be populated for: legal_hold_set (previous_legal_hold + new_legal_hold + reason), legal_hold_released (same), evidence_purged_force (full evidence row before purge for audit chain), evidence_voided (full row before void marker). Sensitive payload bytes are NOT in audit JSON (only metadata + R2 location reference).

### 11.1.10 Element 10: Parity tests

| Test ID | Description | WF traced | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| T-195.1 | GET /api/documents/evidence list returns RLS-scoped rows; driver sees only own driver_uuid evidence | WF-024 + WF-025 | 11.1.7 + 11.1.7.2.1 |
| T-195.2 | Evidence list filters by evidence_type, captured_at range, has-legal-hold | WF-025 | 11.1.4 |
| T-195.3 | GET evidence/{uuid} with valid X-Retrieval-Purpose succeeds; emits sampled retrieval audit + writes access_log row | WF-025 + WF-029 | 11.1.5.5 + MUST 3.17.7.3 |
| T-195.4 | GET evidence/{uuid} without X-Retrieval-Purpose header returns E_VALIDATION | WF-025 | 11.1.5.5 |
| T-195.5 | GET evidence/{uuid} with invalid purpose code returns E_VALIDATION | WF-025 | 11.1.5.5 |
| T-195.6 | Driver retrieval with purpose=audit returns E_VALIDATION (drivers limited to routine_view) | WF-024 | 11.1.7.2.2 |
| T-195.7 | Direct INSERT to documents.evidence_records blocked by RLS for ALL roles including Owner; returns E_EVIDENCE_DIRECT_INSERT_FORBIDDEN | WF-025 | 11.1.5.1 + MUST 3.17.2.1 |
| T-195.8 | Server recomputes sha256 from uploaded payload; client-supplied hash field ignored | WF-025 + WF-030 | 11.1.5.2 + MUST 3.17.2.3 |
| T-195.9 | Server uses server_received_at not captured_at for legal/audit purposes; verified via audit chain | WF-025 | 11.1.5.3 + MUST 3.17.2.2 |
| T-196.1 | Legal hold set by Owner with reason ≥30 chars succeeds; emits legal_hold_set critical + WF-064 | WF-028 + WF-064 | 11.1.4 |
| T-196.2 | Legal hold set by Manager returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED + legal_hold_set_blocked | WF-064 | 11.1.7 |
| T-196.3 | Legal hold release by Administrator with reason ≥30 chars succeeds; emits legal_hold_released critical + WF-064 | WF-028 + WF-064 | 11.1.4 |
| T-196.4 | Legal hold release by Manager returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED + legal_hold_release_blocked | WF-064 | 11.1.7 |
| T-196.5 | Legal hold set on already-on-hold evidence returns E_LEGAL_HOLD_ALREADY_SET 422 | WF-028 | 11.1.4 |
| T-196.6 | Legal hold release on not-on-hold evidence returns E_LEGAL_HOLD_NOT_SET 422 | WF-028 | 11.1.4 |
| T-197.1 | Force-purge by Owner with reason ≥30 chars + acknowledge_irrecoverable=true succeeds; emits evidence_purged warning + WF-064; queues R2 delete outbox | WF-029 + WF-064 | 11.1.4 |
| T-197.2 | Force-purge by Administrator returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED + evidence_force_purge_blocked | WF-064 | 11.1.7 |
| T-197.3 | Force-purge of evidence under legal hold returns E_PURGE_BLOCKED_LEGAL_HOLD | WF-029 | 11.1.5.7 |
| T-197.4 | Force-purge without acknowledge_irrecoverable flag returns E_VALIDATION | WF-029 | 11.1.5.9 |
| T-197.5 | Cron-driven nightly purge skips records with legal_hold=true | WF-029 | 11.1.5.7 + MUST 3.17.4.2 |
| T-197.6 | Every purge writes to evidence_purge_log with force_flag set correctly | WF-029 | 11.1.5.10 |
| T-198.1 | Tamper detection: sha256 mismatch on retrieval returns E_EVIDENCE_TAMPERED 409; emits documents.evidence_tampered_detected critical + WF-064 + SMS to Owner+Admin | WF-030 + WF-064 | 11.1.4 + 11.1.5.4 |
| T-198.2 | Tamper detection auto-trigger has no symmetric blocked variant (auto-detection event); confirmed by enumerating audit table | WF-030 | 11.1.7.1 note |
| T-198.3 | Tamper-detected evidence becomes inaccessible until Owner investigation resolves; subsequent retrievals continue to return E_EVIDENCE_TAMPERED until cleared | WF-030 | 11.1.5.4 |
| T-199.1 | Evidence void by Manager succeeds; sets voided_at + voided_by + voided_reason; row preserved | WF-025 | 11.1.5.8 |
| T-199.2 | Voided evidence retrieval returns row with voided_at indicator; payload still accessible if retention not expired | WF-025 | 11.1.5.8 |
| T-199.3 | Retention_expires_at direct UPDATE blocked by trigger; immutable post-creation | WF-029 | 11.1.5.11 |
| T-200.1 | GET evidence-access-log by Administrator returns access events filtered by evidence_uuid or user_uuid | WF-029 | 11.1.7.3 Profile A |
| T-200.2 | GET evidence-purge-log by Administrator returns purge events; force_flag distinguishes cron vs force | WF-029 | 11.1.7.3 Profile A |
| T-200.3 | Driver access to evidence-access-log returns E_PERMISSION_DENIED | WF-024 | 11.1.7 |
| T-201.1 | Signed PDF generation by Dispatcher for BOL succeeds; output stored as evidence_type=signed_pdf_template; sha256 computed | WF-034 | 11.1.4 |
| T-201.2 | Signed PDF generation with invalid template returns E_PDF_TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND | WF-034 | 11.1.7.3 Profile B |
| T-201.3 | All 4 originated Documents WF-064 actions verified via audit table enumeration: 3 with success/blocked symmetry + 1 auto-detection without blocked variant | WF-064 | 11.1.9.2 |
| T-201.4 | Inherited WF-064 count is 0 — every cross-module write to Documents via create_evidence() is routine, not inherited WF-064 | WF-064 | 11.1.7.1 Table B |

#### 11.1.10.1 Documents-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_EVIDENCE_DIRECT_INSERT_FORBIDDEN` | 403 | Direct INSERT to evidence_records blocked; use create_evidence() service function (MUST 3.17.2.1) |
| `E_EVIDENCE_TAMPERED` | 409 | sha256 mismatch on retrieval; payload integrity compromised (WF-030) |
| `E_EVIDENCE_PURGED` | 404 | Evidence has been purged; not retrievable |
| `E_EVIDENCE_ALREADY_PURGED` | 409 | Force-purge target already purged |
| `E_EVIDENCE_ALREADY_VOIDED` | 409 | Void target already voided |
| `E_LEGAL_HOLD_ALREADY_SET` | 409 | Legal hold already set on evidence |
| `E_LEGAL_HOLD_NOT_SET` | 422 | Cannot release legal hold; evidence not on hold |
| `E_PURGE_BLOCKED_LEGAL_HOLD` | 422 | Cannot purge evidence under legal hold; release first |
| `E_PDF_TEMPLATE_NOT_FOUND` | 404 | Signed PDF template type not registered |

---

## 11.2 — NOTIFICATIONS MODULE

### 11.2.1 Element 1: Module overview

**Purpose:** Notifications owns the asynchronous-delivery layer behind every audit-event-to-recipient routing decision. It dispatches multi-channel (email/SMS/PWA push) notifications per WF-042, enforces the WF-064 critical-path envelope per WF-043 + Part 6.8.3, applies user preferences per WF-045, honors suppression rules per WF-047 (with the MUST 4.9.3.3-aligned 7-day max window per WF-064.3), routes via delivery-provider failover per WF-048, and handles bounce + failed delivery per WF-051.

**Owning schema:** `notifications` (per Part 4.9.2).

**Key invariants:**
- Every `notifications.notification_log` row references an audit event by `event_class` + `related_entity_type/uuid` triple — the notification is always traceable to its source audit.
- `wf_064_notification = true` notifications cannot be suppressed by routine user preferences; only the explicit `notifications.suppression_rules` table (with max-7-day window per the CHECK constraint in Part 4.9.2) can suppress them — and even then, the suppression itself fires a WF-064 audit per WF-064.3.
- Suppression rules MUST have `effective_to <= effective_from + 7 days` (DB CHECK constraint per Part 4.9.2 schema).
- Delivery provider failover order is canonical per WF-048: email = Resend (primary) → SES fallback; SMS = Twilio (primary, no fallback in v1); PWA push = Expo (primary).
- Bounce handling per WF-051: provider bounce/failure callback updates `delivery_status` to `bounced` or `failed`; consecutive bounces from the same recipient on the same channel auto-disable that channel for that user (per Part 5.5 bounce policy).
- Notification log is append-only for the `dispatched_at`, `delivery_status`, `delivery_provider_id` fields — these UPDATE in place as provider feedback arrives, but no DELETE allowed.
- Suppression rule creation requires Owner action + reason + WF-064 audit (this is the WF-064.3 integrity defense — preventing routine suppression of critical alerts).

**Cross-module dependencies:**
- READS from canonical reference: `identity.users` (for recipient lookup + preference resolution).
- READS from every audit event source — Notifications is downstream of `audit.audit_events` via the PG NOTIFY trigger per MUST 4.9.3.3 that wakes the dispatcher worker.
- WRITES to (via service functions, never direct): `notifications.preferences`, `notifications.notification_log`, `notifications.suppression_rules`. Direct INSERT to `notification_log` via API blocked — only the dispatcher worker writes via `notifications.dispatch_event()` service function.
- WRITES to shared schemas: `audit.audit_events` (for suppression rule WF-064 events); `outbox.queue` (for provider API calls — Resend/Twilio/Expo — per Part 3.12.4).

**WFs implemented:** WF-042, WF-043, WF-045, WF-047, WF-048, WF-051. Cross-cuts WF-024 (RBAC), WF-064 (high-risk audit). **8 distinct WFs traced in this module.**

### 11.2.2 Element 2: UI behaviors

#### 11.2.2.1 User notification preferences (`/notifications/preferences`)

**MUST 11.2.2.1.1 — Per-channel + per-event-class toggles:** users see a matrix of channel (email/sms/app_push) × event_class with on/off toggles. Defaults are pre-populated per role (e.g., Owner has all critical events enabled across email + SMS; Driver has only own-driver events enabled).

**MUST 11.2.2.1.2 — Critical events forced on:** events with `severity = critical` AND `wf_064_notification = true` cannot be turned off via preferences UI for Owner + Administrator users. The toggle appears greyed out with a tooltip explaining the WF-064 envelope. This is the user-preference layer of the WF-064.3 defense — even Owner cannot accidentally turn off critical WF-064 audits via preferences (only via explicit suppression rules with audit trail).

#### 11.2.2.2 Notification log (`/notifications/log`)

**MUST 11.2.2.2.1 — Owner+Admin view:** filterable list of all dispatched notifications across all users. Columns: created_at, recipient_user, channel, event_class, related_entity link, dispatched_at, delivery_status badge (per Part 6.4.4 — green delivered, yellow pending, red bounced/failed), delivery_provider, wf_064_notification badge.

**MUST 11.2.2.2.2 — User self-view:** users see only own notification log via RLS scope. Same columns but filtered to user_uuid match.

#### 11.2.2.3 Suppression rules (`/notifications/suppression`)

**MUST 11.2.2.3.1 — Active suppressions list:** rows for each active rule with effective_from, effective_to, event_class, user_uuid (recipient being suppressed), reason, created_by Owner.

**MUST 11.2.2.3.2 — Create suppression rule action** ⚡ WF-064 (WF-047 + WF-064.3): Owner only. 2-step confirmation modal per Part 6.4.5.4 with typed phrase "CREATE SUPPRESSION RULE" + reason ≥30 chars + recipient user_uuid + event_class + effective_from + effective_to (max 7 days from effective_from per CHECK constraint).

**MUST 11.2.2.3.3 — Bulk preference update action** ⚡ WF-064: Owner only. Affects org-wide preference defaults (e.g., re-enabling SMS for all Manager users for a specific event_class). 2-step confirmation modal with typed phrase "BULK UPDATE PREFERENCES" + reason ≥30 chars.

#### 11.2.2.4 Delivery failure dashboard (`/notifications/failures`)

**MUST 11.2.2.4.1 — Bounced + failed notifications:** filtered to `delivery_status IN (bounced, failed)`. Shows last N occurrences per recipient + channel; auto-disabled channels flagged. Owner/Admin can manually re-enable a disabled channel (recording the action with reason).

### 11.2.3 Element 3: Data entities

The Notifications module owns these tables (DDL in Part 4.9.2):

- `notifications.preferences` — per-user × channel × event_class enabled/disabled boolean
- `notifications.notification_log` — every dispatched notification with delivery_status tracking + WF-064 flag
- `notifications.suppression_rules` — temporary suppression with mandatory ≤7-day effective window (CHECK constraint in DDL)

**3 tables.** No new tables introduced beyond Part 4.9.2 DDL. Schema lock per Part 4 Rev 2 honored.

**The Notifications module reads from canonical references:**
- `identity.users` (for recipient identity + role resolution)

**The Notifications module reads from `audit.audit_events`** via the PG NOTIFY trigger per MUST 4.9.3.3 — the dispatcher worker subscribes to NOTIFY and queries the audit event row + downstream preference/suppression evaluation.

**The Notifications module writes (via service functions only):**
- `notifications.preferences` (via `notifications.set_preference()` from user-preferences UI)
- `notifications.notification_log` (via `notifications.dispatch_event()` from the dispatcher worker; NOT user-callable directly)
- `notifications.suppression_rules` (via `notifications.create_suppression()` Owner action with WF-064 envelope)

**The Notifications module writes to shared schemas:**
- `audit.audit_events` via `audit.append_event()` (for suppression-rule-created + bulk-preference-updated WF-064 events; NOT for routine notification dispatch — that would create an infinite loop since dispatch is itself triggered BY audit events)
- `outbox.queue` for provider API calls (Resend, Twilio, Expo) per Part 3.12.4

### 11.2.4 Element 4: API contracts

This module follows the FULL endpoint + Contract Profile split. FULL endpoints below cover the WF-064 paths: suppression rule create + bulk preference update. Standard CRUD + query endpoints map to Profile A/B/C in §11.2.7.3. The dispatcher worker is internal infrastructure — not a user-facing API.

#### `POST /api/notifications/suppression-rules`

Create a suppression rule for an event_class + user combination. **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action with WF-064.3 envelope.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `notifications.suppression_rule.create` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/notifications/suppression-rules
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "user_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000usr15",
  "event_class": "driver_finance.escrow_forfeited",
  "effective_from": "2026-05-03T00:00:00Z",
  "effective_to": "2026-05-09T23:59:59Z",
  "reason": "User Maria is on bereavement leave through 2026-05-09; suppressing escrow forfeiture notifications during her absence; re-enable upon return"
}
```

**Example success response (201):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000supr01",
    "user_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000usr15",
    "event_class": "driver_finance.escrow_forfeited",
    "effective_from": "2026-05-03T00:00:00Z",
    "effective_to": "2026-05-09T23:59:59Z",
    "duration_days": 6.99,
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi95",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif70"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400, window too long):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Suppression rule request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "effective_to", "reason": "must be within 7 days of effective_from per Part 4.9.2 CHECK constraint + WF-064.3"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters"}
    ],
    "request_id": "req_01923d30"
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, overlap):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_SUPPRESSION_OVERLAP",
    "message": "Suppression rule overlaps with existing rule for this user + event_class",
    "details": {"existing_rule_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000supr00", "existing_effective_from": "2026-05-01", "existing_effective_to": "2026-05-05"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d30"
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'notifications.suppression_rule.create' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "administrator"},
    "request_id": "req_01923d30"
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_SUPPRESSION_OVERLAP` (11.2.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `notifications.suppression_rule_created` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `notifications.suppression_rule_create_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

---

#### `POST /api/notifications/preferences/bulk-update`

Bulk-update notification preferences across multiple users + event_classes. **Fires WF-064 — high-risk action.**

**Authentication:** Active session cookie.

**Permission:** `notifications.preference.bulk_update` (Owner only).

**Example request:**
```http
POST /api/notifications/preferences/bulk-update
Authorization: Bearer {jwt}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "filter": {
    "user_role": "manager"
  },
  "event_class": "accounting.period_closed",
  "channel": "email",
  "enabled": true,
  "reason": "Re-enabling email notifications for all Manager users on accounting.period_closed event class after 2026-Q1 review confirmed managers need visibility into period close timing for monthly reconciliation"
}
```

**Example success response (200):**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "users_updated_count": 4,
    "user_uuids": ["01923c8a-...usr10", "01923c8a-...usr11", "01923c8a-...usr12", "01923c8a-...usr13"],
    "event_class": "accounting.period_closed",
    "channel": "email",
    "enabled": true,
    "audit_event_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-00000audi96",
    "wf_064_owner_notification": {"fired": true, "notification_uuid": "01923c8a-7f12-7000-8000-0000notif71"}
  }
}
```

**Example validation error response (400):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_VALIDATION",
    "message": "Bulk preference update request invalid",
    "details": [
      {"field": "filter", "reason": "must specify user_role OR user_uuids array"},
      {"field": "channel", "reason": "must be one of: email, sms, app_push"},
      {"field": "reason", "reason": "required; minimum 30 characters"}
    ]
  }
}
```

**Example business error response (422, no users matched):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_BULK_UPDATE_NO_USERS_MATCHED",
    "message": "Filter matched zero users; no bulk update to perform",
    "details": {"filter": {"user_role": "nonexistent_role"}, "users_matched": 0}
  }
}
```

**Example permission error response (403):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "E_PERMISSION_DENIED",
    "message": "Action 'notifications.preference.bulk_update' requires Owner role",
    "details": {"caller_role": "administrator"}
  }
}
```

**Error code linkage:** `E_VALIDATION`, `E_BULK_UPDATE_NO_USERS_MATCHED` (11.2.10.1), `E_PERMISSION_DENIED`. On success, emits `notifications.preference_bulk_updated` (warning, fires WF-064). On block, emits `notifications.preference_bulk_update_blocked` (warning, fires WF-064 — success/blocked symmetry).

### 11.2.5 Element 5: Validation rules

**MUST 11.2.5.1 — Suppression rule max 7-day window (per Part 4.9.2 CHECK constraint + WF-064.3):** `effective_to <= effective_from + INTERVAL '7 days'`. DB-level CHECK enforces; API also pre-validates and returns `E_VALIDATION` with explicit message before DB hit.

**MUST 11.2.5.2 — Suppression overlap detection:** if existing active suppression rule covers the same user_uuid + event_class with overlapping effective range, new request returns `E_SUPPRESSION_OVERLAP`.

**MUST 11.2.5.3 — Suppression reason length:** ≥30 chars per WF-064.3 audit-trail requirement.

**MUST 11.2.5.4 — Suppression rules cannot block routine info events:** suppression rules apply only to `severity IN (warning, critical)` events. Info/sampled events are not subject to suppression — the dispatcher skips suppression checks for info events as a performance optimization.

**MUST 11.2.5.5 — Critical WF-064 events bypass user preferences:** users with `severity = critical AND wf_064_notification = true` events have these forced ON in preferences UI for Owner + Admin roles. Backend enforces by ignoring the preferences toggle for these events when the recipient is Owner/Admin.

**MUST 11.2.5.6 — Notification log append-only for dispatch fields:** `dispatched_at`, `delivery_status`, `delivery_provider`, `delivery_provider_id` are UPDATE-able only by the dispatcher worker via `notifications.update_delivery_status()` service function (called from provider webhook callbacks). Direct UPDATE blocked. DELETE on `notification_log` blocked entirely (append-only retention).

**MUST 11.2.5.7 — Bounce handling auto-disable threshold:** 3+ consecutive bounces from the same recipient on the same channel within 24 hours auto-disables that channel for that user (`notifications.preferences.enabled = false`). User receives an email (if email channel still working) explaining the disable. Manual re-enable required by Owner+Admin.

**MUST 11.2.5.8 — Provider failover order canonical:** dispatcher attempts in this order:
- Email: Resend → SES (fallback)
- SMS: Twilio (no fallback in v1; v2 may add provider redundancy)
- PWA push: Expo (no fallback in v1)
First-success wins. If primary fails with retryable error, fallback attempted within 30 seconds. Permanent errors (invalid recipient) skip fallback and mark as failed.

**MUST 11.2.5.9 — Bulk preference update reason length:** ≥30 chars per WF-064 audit-trail requirement.

**MUST 11.2.5.10 — Bulk preference update filter required:** must specify `user_role` (e.g., "manager") OR `user_uuids` array. Empty filter rejected to prevent accidental org-wide updates.

### 11.2.6 Element 6: Accounting effects

Notifications has **no direct accounting effects.** It owns no monetary records, posts no journal entries, creates no bills or invoices. All notification dispatch is metadata + provider API queue operations — no ledger impact.

**No posting templates referenced from this module.**

### 11.2.7 Element 7: Permissions

**Role matrix for Notifications actions:**

| Action | Owner | Admin | Manager | Accountant | Dispatcher | Safety | Driver | Mechanic |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `notifications.preferences.read` (own) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `notifications.preferences.update` (own) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `notifications.preferences.read` (any) | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `notifications.preference.bulk_update` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `notifications.notification_log.list` (own) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| `notifications.notification_log.list` (any) | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `notifications.suppression_rule.list` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `notifications.suppression_rule.create` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `notifications.suppression_rule.cancel` ⚡ WF-064 | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `notifications.delivery_failure.list` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `notifications.delivery_failure.reenable_channel` | ✓ | ✓ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| `notifications.dispatch_event` (worker) | service-only | service-only | — | — | — | — | — | — |

#### 11.2.7.1 WF-064 originated vs inherited mapping (Part 7 Tables A/B pattern)

**A. Originated in Notifications (3 actions):**

| # | Action | Originating Module | Why Notifications is the originator | API endpoint | Audit event(s) (success / blocked) | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `notifications.suppression_rule.create` | notifications | Originates here — suppressing WF-064 critical alerts is itself a high-risk action per WF-064.3; max-7-day window is the integrity defense | `POST /api/notifications/suppression-rules` (§11.2.4) | `notifications.suppression_rule_created` / `notifications.suppression_rule_create_blocked` | T-204.1, T-204.2 |
| 2 | `notifications.suppression_rule.cancel` | notifications | Originates here — early cancellation of an active suppression rule restores notifications and is itself a notable Owner action | `DELETE /api/notifications/suppression-rules/{uuid}` (Profile C — see §11.2.7.3) | `notifications.suppression_rule_cancelled` / `notifications.suppression_rule_cancel_blocked` | T-204.3, T-204.4 |
| 3 | `notifications.preference.bulk_update` | notifications | Originates here — bulk-updating preferences across multiple users affects org-wide notification routing | `POST /api/notifications/preferences/bulk-update` (§11.2.4) | `notifications.preference_bulk_updated` / `notifications.preference_bulk_update_blocked` | T-205.1, T-205.2 |

**Total: 3 originated WF-064 actions in Notifications.**

**B. Inherited through Notifications (0 actual):**

Per the precedent established in Parts 7-11.1: every audit event with `fires_wf_064 = true` originates in its source module (Driver Finance forfeit, Accounting period close, Documents legal hold, etc.) and triggers the Notifications dispatcher per MUST 4.9.3.3 PG NOTIFY. The Notifications module is the dispatch channel — it does NOT inherit the WF-064 audit, it MERELY routes the notification. The originating module owns the WF-064 audit; Notifications creates `notification_log` rows that reference the source audit but do not constitute additional WF-064 events.

The dispatcher writing routine `notification_log` rows is NOT a WF-064 inheritance — it is the routine downstream consequence of the originating module's WF-064. This pattern is identical to Banking's relationship with Accounting in Part 10b.

**Total: Notifications participates in 3 WF-064 paths — 3 originated + 0 inherited.**

#### 11.2.7.2 Driver self-access scope

**MUST 11.2.7.2.1** — Drivers can read + update OWN preferences and read OWN notification_log via RLS scope (user_uuid match). Drivers cannot list other users' preferences or logs; cannot create suppression rules.

#### 11.2.7.3 Contract Profile Mapping (Cursor Part 7.3.4.1 pattern)

The Notifications module has 12 permission actions in §11.2.7. 2 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §11.2.4. The remaining 10 actions are mapped via Contract Profiles A/B/C and service-only entries per Part 7.3.4.1 pattern.

| Permission action | Contract artifact | Endpoint(s) | Profile-specific error set | Test IDs |
|---|---|---|---|---|

| `notifications.suppression_rule.cancel` ⚡ WF-064 | Profile C | `DELETE /api/notifications/suppression-rules/{uuid}` | `E_VALIDATION`, `E_SUPPRESSION_NOT_ACTIVE`, `E_PERMISSION_DENIED` | T-204.3, T-204.4 |

**Coverage summary:**
- 2 actions have FULL endpoint contracts in §11.2.4 (suppression rule create, bulk preference update)
- 10 additional permission actions are mapped via: Profile A read-only (6), Profile B CRUD non-WF-064 (2), Profile C WF-064 mutation (1), service-only internal (1)
- Distribution: 2 FULL + 6 Profile A + 2 Profile B + 1 Profile C + 1 service-only = **12 of 12 permission actions** in §11.2.7 now have an explicit API mapping artifact. No permission action is left without DoD closure.

### 11.2.8 Element 8: Notifications

This is itself the Notifications module — its own audit events route through itself per the PG NOTIFY chain. The notification routing for Notifications-originated events:

| Audit event | Channel | Recipient | Severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| `notifications.suppression_rule_created` | email | Owner + Administrator | warning + WF-064 |
| `notifications.suppression_rule_cancelled` | email | Owner + Administrator | warning + WF-064 |
| `notifications.preference_bulk_updated` | email | Owner + Administrator | warning + WF-064 |
| `notifications.delivery_provider_failed` (single occurrence) | none | n/a | info — sampled |
| `notifications.delivery_provider_failed` (multiple consecutive) | email | Owner + Administrator | critical (potential service outage) |
| `notifications.channel_auto_disabled` (3+ bounces) | email (to user via still-working channel) | the user + Owner + Administrator | warning |
| `notifications.dispatch_failure` (after retry exhausted) | email | Owner + Administrator | critical |

**MUST 11.2.8.1 — No SMS for routine Notifications events:** even though Notifications dispatches SMS for OTHER modules' critical events, Notifications-originated events themselves are email-only because they are operational/governance, not time-critical to the SMS bar.

**MUST 11.2.8.2 — Provider outage detection:** consecutive `delivery_provider_failed` events (>5 within 5 minutes) escalates to critical Owner notification — likely indicates Twilio/Resend/Expo service outage.

### 11.2.9 Element 9: Audit events

| Event class | Severity | Subject | Fires WF-064? | Symmetric pair |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `notifications.preference_set` | info (sampled) | the preference + user | no | none |
| `notifications.preference_bulk_updated` | warning | the bulk update + filter + count | yes | `notifications.preference_bulk_update_blocked` |
| `notifications.preference_bulk_update_blocked` | warning | the rejected request | yes | success: `notifications.preference_bulk_updated` |
| `notifications.suppression_rule_created` | warning | the rule + user + event_class + window | yes | `notifications.suppression_rule_create_blocked` |
| `notifications.suppression_rule_create_blocked` | warning | the rejected request | yes | success: `notifications.suppression_rule_created` |
| `notifications.suppression_rule_cancelled` | warning | the cancelled rule | yes | `notifications.suppression_rule_cancel_blocked` |
| `notifications.suppression_rule_cancel_blocked` | warning | the rejected request | yes | success: `notifications.suppression_rule_cancelled` |
| `notifications.notification_dispatched` (sampled) | info | the notification | no | none |
| `notifications.notification_delivered` (sampled) | info | the notification + provider_id | no | none |
| `notifications.notification_bounced` | warning | the notification + bounce_reason | no | none |
| `notifications.notification_failed` | warning | the notification + error | no | none |
| `notifications.channel_auto_disabled` | warning | the user + channel | no | none |
| `notifications.channel_reenabled` | info | the user + channel + reenabled_by | no | none |
| `notifications.delivery_provider_failed` | info / critical (per consecutive count) | the provider | no | none |

**MUST 11.2.9.1** — All event INSERTs go through `audit.append_event()` per Part 4.9.3.2.

**MUST 11.2.9.2** — Per Cursor's locked WF-064 success/blocked symmetry rule: 3 originated WF-064 actions × 2 = 6 paired audit events specific to Notifications, plus 8 audit events for non-symmetric routine operations = 14 audit event classes total.

#### 11.2.10.1 Notifications-specific error codes

| Code | HTTP eq | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `E_PREFERENCE_FORCED_ON` | 422 | Cannot disable critical+WF-064 events for Owner+Admin via preferences |
| `E_SUPPRESSION_OVERLAP` | 422 | New suppression rule overlaps with existing active rule |
| `E_SUPPRESSION_NOT_ACTIVE` | 422 | Cannot cancel inactive or expired suppression rule |
| `E_BULK_UPDATE_NO_USERS_MATCHED` | 422 | Bulk update filter matched zero users |
| `E_CHANNEL_NOT_DISABLED` | 422 | Cannot reenable a channel that is not currently auto-disabled |

# IH35 Master Blueprint v3 — Part 12: Workflow Index, Role Consolidation, Audit Taxonomy, Build Order

**Section in v3 hierarchy:** Part 12 of 13. Reads after Part 11 (Documents + Notifications). Where Parts 7-11 were per-module specs, Part 12 is **cross-cutting synthesis** — the closing materials that integrate everything that came before.

**Status (initial draft):** Awaiting Cursor strict review per the same 8-check acceptance gate. Plus 5 Owner-directed acceptance gates specific to Part 12:
- Single authoritative WF namespace clearly stated
- Exhaustive mapping table present (not sampled)
- No contradictory WF references between Parts 12/13 and Parts 7-11
- WF-059 / WF-062 / WF-014 / WF-041 / WF-060 / WF-063 explicitly dispositioned
- Traceability rollup remains consistent with approved REQ-* and test IDs

All Part 7-11 locks carried forward:
- 10-element module template (where applicable for cross-cutting Parts; Part 12 uses a different structure since it's not a module spec)
- WF-064 success/blocked symmetry
- No schema drift / no new namespaces (14-schema lock)
- Single canonical count statements
- Cross-part trace matrix consistency

**Normative language:** MUST · MUST NOT · SHOULD · SHOULD NOT · MAY (RFC 2119).

**Test ID range:** Part 12 reuses no test IDs from Parts 1-11 (max prior: T-209.5). Part 12 introduces no new tests directly — it traces tests from prior Parts.

**Trace matrix:** Part 12 does not introduce new REQ-* rows. It produces a **rollup view** that synthesizes existing rows. Trace matrix integrity verified — 247 requirement rows preserved.

## 12.1 — WORKFLOW NUMBERING RECONCILIATION (Legacy Parity → V3 Authoritative)

### 12.1.1 Background

During the v3 Parts 1-6 phase, the `WORKFLOW_PARITY_CHECKLIST.csv` was delivered as the master workflow index — 64 entries (WF-001..WF-064) covering domains from Maintenance through Audit, with Given/When/Then narratives. This checklist was Cursor-approved as the WF taxonomy reference for v3.

During the Parts 7-11 phase (the per-module spec phase), each module Part introduced its own "WFs implemented" enumeration. **For roughly half the WF IDs, the module Parts re-used the same WF numbers but assigned them to different workflows than the parity checklist had.** This divergence was caught during Part 12 pre-drafting and reported to the Owner as a Stop-The-Line issue.

**Owner decision (recorded in conversation register, Part 11 → Part 12 transition):**

> **Option A.** Use Parts 7-11 WF assignments as authoritative for v3, and treat the old parity checklist as legacy input. Mapping table is transitional documentation only — single authoritative namespace going forward.

This section implements that decision with an exhaustive WF-by-WF mapping. Going forward, **the V3 Authoritative column in §12.1.3 is the single source of truth for any WF reference in v3 spec, code, tests, or documentation.**

### 12.1.2 Disposition definitions

For each of WF-001..WF-064, one of three dispositions:

- **MATCHED** — The legacy parity meaning and v3 authoritative meaning describe substantively the same workflow. Same domain, same intent, equivalent given/when/then. No semantic change. The vast majority of MATCHED entries are concentrated in Maintenance, Safety, Fuel/Compliance, Equipment, and Insurance — the operational domains drafted earliest in Parts 7 + 9.

- **REMAPPED** — The v3 authoritative meaning is materially different from the legacy parity meaning, OR the v3 scope has narrowed/expanded relative to legacy. The legacy meaning is **superseded** by the v3 meaning. The legacy intent may live in a different workflow now (search the V3 Authoritative column for the concept), or may be dispositioned as DEPRECATED. Where a workflow is partially implemented or deferred to v2, the v3 column carries an explanatory note but the status remains REMAPPED — there is no separate "partial" tier in the locked taxonomy.

- **DEPRECATED** — Not referenced anywhere in Parts 7-11. Disposition per §12.1.4: either deferred-from-v1 with rationale, or absorbed into another workflow.
