### 12.1.4 Disposition of DEPRECATED workflows (Owner-directed explicit handling)

Per Owner directive: "Handle WF-059 and WF-062 explicitly as `not implemented in v1 authoritative scope` (or `deferred`), with rationale and where/if covered elsewhere." Below the same treatment is applied to ALL six DEPRECATED entries identified in the §12.1.3 sweep.

**WF-014 — Dispatch: Border Crossing Charge Routing**
- **Disposition:** Deferred — not implemented in v1 authoritative scope.
- **Rationale:** Border-crossing charges as a distinct workflow were superseded by the more general Catalog → Posting Template → Driver Liability flow established in Part 7 Catalogs + Part 8b Driver Finance. A border-crossing charge is recorded as a routine `accounting.expenses` row with a Catalog-bound charge code, then optionally creates a `driver_finance.driver_liabilities` row via `WF-035` (Company Paid Driver Expense Recovery) if the company-paid-then-recover pattern applies. No domain-specific border-crossing workflow needed in v1.
- **Coverage in v3:** Part 7 Catalogs (account/charge code setup) + Part 8b WF-035 (recovery flow) + Part 10a WF-009 (expense creation).

**WF-041 — Dispatch: Presettlement to Settlement Lifecycle**
- **Disposition:** Deferred — not implemented in v1 authoritative scope.
- **Rationale:** The v3 Driver Finance redesign (Part 8b) replaced the legacy "presettlement → settlement" two-stage UI with a single live-recompute settlement model per WF-046 (Driver Earnings Live Recompute) + WF-055 (Settlement Lock with Stale Debt Refresh). The presettlement intermediate state no longer exists as a first-class concept; what was "presettlement" is now the in-progress live recompute view, and "settlement" is the lock-and-pay action.
- **Coverage in v3:** Part 8b WF-046 + WF-055 jointly cover the end-to-end settlement lifecycle.

**WF-059 — Banking: Factoring Virtual Bank Visualization**
- **Disposition:** Deferred — not implemented in v1 authoritative scope.
- **Rationale:** Apex factoring is represented in v3 as a virtual bank account per Part 4.7.1 (`bank_accounts.account_type = 'other_current_asset'`) plus the dedicated `banking.factoring_transactions` table per Part 4.6.7 + Part 10b §10b.1.2.5. The legacy "virtual bank visualization" UI was a presentation-layer enhancement on top of these primitives. Part 10b §10b.1.2.5 implements the factoring-tab list view; the dashboard-style "virtual bank account in the regular bank-accounts list" visualization is deferred to v2 as a UI polish item. Functional capability is unchanged — Apex transactions are visible and reconcilable in v1.
- **Coverage in v3:** Part 10b §10b.1.2.5 (Factoring tab) + Part 4.6.7 (factoring_transactions schema) provide functional parity. Visualization upgrade is v2 scope.

**WF-060 — Banking: Manual Journal Entry and Reclassify Transaction**
- **Disposition:** Deferred — not implemented in v1 authoritative scope as a Banking workflow; the underlying capability IS implemented under Accounting.
- **Rationale:** The "manual JE" capability was relocated to its proper home in Accounting (Part 10a `accounting.journal_entry.create_manual` per WF-018). The "reclassify transaction" capability is implemented as the Banking recategorize flow under WF-013 (open-period recategorize) + the Owner-only WF-064 closed-period variant. There is no separate Banking-side manual-JE workflow needed.
- **Coverage in v3:** Part 10a WF-018 (manual JE) + Part 10b WF-013 (transaction recategorize, both open- and closed-period variants).

**WF-062 — Compliance: Driver Permit Lifecycle and Auto-Deduction**
- **Disposition:** Deferred — not implemented in v1 authoritative scope.
- **Rationale:** Driver permits as a tracked first-class entity require a `master_data.driver_permits` table, a permit-expiry alerting subsystem, and an auto-deduction trigger flow into Driver Finance. None of these exist in the v1 schema lock (Part 4 Rev 2 has no `driver_permits` table; Driver Finance §4.5 has no permit-deduction trigger). Implementing this would require a Part 4 schema amendment + Part 8b Driver Finance addition. **Owner-directed Q-to-resolve for v2 planning:** confirm whether driver permits need first-class tracking or whether they can ride on the existing `documents.evidence_records` (with permit_expiry retention metadata) + Driver Finance liability creation flow when an auto-deduction is triggered manually.
- **Coverage in v3:** Partial — `documents.evidence_records` can store permit PDFs; Driver Finance can create permit-related liabilities manually via WF-035. Auto-detection of expiry + auto-deduction is **not** in v1.

**WF-063 — Catalogs: QBO Bidirectional Catalog Sync**
- **Disposition:** Deferred — not implemented in v1 authoritative scope as a fully bidirectional sync. v1 implements **outbound** sync (IH35 → QBO) per Part 3.12.4.8 outbox pattern. **Inbound** sync (QBO → IH35) is not implemented in v1.
- **Rationale:** Per Part 3 + Part 4 governance lock + Option C+ accounting strategy: IH35 is the local-authoritative system; QBO is a downstream mirror. Bidirectional sync would require conflict resolution rules, an inbound-change reconciliation pipeline, and a UI for conflict adjudication. None of these are in v1 scope. The trade-off was accepted at the Part 1-3 architecture phase (Option C+ = local authoritative, QBO mirror).
- **Coverage in v3:** Part 3.12.4.8 (outbox-driven outbound sync) + Part 7 Catalogs (chart-of-accounts setup that QBO mirrors). Inbound sync deferred to v2 if business need emerges.

### 12.1.5 Note on WF-047 (Equipment Dual Confirmation)

WF-047 is REMAPPED in v3 to "Notifications: Suppression Rules" (Part 11) — but the **legacy intent** of "Equipment Dual Confirmation Transfer Workflow" is preserved in v3 under a different anchor:

- Part 7 Master Data §7.2 implements equipment-transfer dual confirmation as an **invariant** on `master_data.equipment_log` rows (transferring custody requires both releasing-driver and receiving-driver acknowledgment).
- The implementing audit events are `master_data.equipment_transferred` + `master_data.equipment_transfer_blocked` (per Part 7 §7.2.9).
- This is the equivalent capability — the workflow is implemented, just not under WF-047's number.

If a future spec amendment wishes to restore a dedicated "Equipment Dual Confirmation" WF ID, the candidate is **WF-065+** (a new ID outside the legacy 1-64 range to prevent collision). Per Owner directive: "Do not introduce dual active namespaces going forward (mapping table is transitional documentation only)." So this is not done in v3 — recorded here only as a forward-planning note.

### 12.1.6 §12.1 Summary statement

**MUST 12.1.6.1 — Single authoritative WF namespace:** the V3 Authoritative column in §12.1.3 is the **only** valid WF reference for v3 spec, code, tests, documentation, audit logs, and traceability matrices going forward. The legacy parity meaning column is **retained for historical context only** and MUST NOT be referenced from any v3 normative material.

**MUST 12.1.6.2 — Mapping table is transitional:** §12.1.3 exists as transitional documentation per Owner directive. It is **not** a runtime translation table; it is a one-time disambiguation aid for stakeholders who saw both numbering schemes during the v3 development cycle. New code, tests, and reviewers MUST work exclusively from the V3 Authoritative column.

**Counts:**
- 64 WF IDs total (WF-001..WF-064)
- 21 MATCHED — legacy and v3 align: WF-001, 002, 003, 004, 005, 006, 007, 011, 019, 020, 022, 024, 026, 027, 035, 036, 044, 049, 050, 058, 064
- 37 REMAPPED — includes WF-061 Reports, partially implemented as cross-cut narrative in Part 7 Catalogs; Reports module deferred to v2 per §12.1.4 forward-planning notes
- 6 DEPRECATED — WF-014, 041, 059, 060, 062, 063

**Verification:** 21 + 37 + 6 = 64 ✓

## 12.3 — ROLE CONSOLIDATION MATRIX

This section synthesizes the per-module permission action matrices from Parts 7-11 into a single cross-cutting view. Per Part 5 + Part 7.1.5, the v3 role taxonomy is fixed at 8 roles:

**Roles (canonical):** Owner, Administrator (Admin), Manager, Accountant, Dispatcher, Safety, Driver, Mechanic.

### 12.3.1 Per-role permission action count

| Role | Approx. permission actions | Primary scope |
|---|---|---|
| Owner | ~165 | Full system; sole role for highest-risk WF-064 actions (force purge, period reopen, manual JE create, suppression rule create, bulk preference update) |
| Administrator | ~140 | Full operational + most WF-064; excludes Owner-only (force purge, period reopen, suppression create, bulk update, manual JE create) |
| Manager | ~95 | Read-everywhere office role; limited write (no banking write, no accounting void after payment) |
| Accountant | ~100 | Accounting + Banking write authority; no role/permission management; no driver-finance overrides |
| Dispatcher | ~50 | Dispatch + Maintenance read; create driver-related operational evidence |
| Safety | ~45 | Safety + DVIR + accident management |
| Driver | ~25 | Self-service: own settlement, own evidence, own preferences, own permits/licenses (read-only of most) |
| Mechanic | ~30 | Maintenance work-order completion; own assigned WO read/update |

**MUST 12.3.1.1** — These approximate counts are derived from the per-module role matrices in §7.1.7 (Identity), §7.2.7 (Master Data), §7.3.7 (Catalogs), §8b.1.7 (Driver Finance), §9.1.7 (Maintenance), §9.2.7 (Safety), §9.3.7 (Fuel), §10a.1.7 (Accounting), §10b.1.7 (Banking), §11.1.7 (Documents), §11.2.7 (Notifications). The exact totals will be machine-derived during implementation from the consolidated permission catalog (Part 13 will deliver the exact catalog as an authoritative artifact).

### 12.3.2 Cross-module role invariants

**MUST 12.3.2.1 — Owner is unique:** at any point in time there MUST be exactly 1 active user with role=owner. Per MUST 7.1.7 last-owner-cannot-deactivate enforcement.

**MUST 12.3.2.2 — Driver self-access scope:** every module that has driver-touching data implements RLS-based self-access (Part 7 §7.2.10, Part 8b §8b.1.7.2, Part 9 §9.2.7 DVIR, Part 11 §11.1.7.2). Drivers MUST NOT SELECT cross-driver data.

**MUST 12.3.2.3 — Office vs operational roles:** Manager + Accountant are office-finance roles with broad read access. Dispatcher + Safety + Mechanic are operational roles with narrow access scoped to their domain. Driver is self-only.

**MUST 12.3.2.4 — Service-only actions:** every module has at least one "service-only" action (called via internal service functions, not user-facing API) — e.g., `documents.create_evidence`, `accounting.post`, `notifications.dispatch_event`, `banking.factoring_transaction.create`. These actions MUST NOT be exposed via direct user API; only the originating module's user-facing endpoints invoke the service function.

### 12.3.3 WF-064 role gating consolidation

Per WF-064 + Part 6.8.3: actions that "fire WF-064" require Owner OR (sometimes) Administrator role. Below is the cross-module consolidation:

| WF-064 action | Required role | Module |
|---|---|---|
| `accounting.period.close` | Owner | Accounting (Part 10a) |
| `accounting.period.reopen` | Owner | Accounting |
| `accounting.bill.void.after_payment` | Owner | Accounting |
| `accounting.invoice.void.after_payment_or_factoring` | Owner | Accounting |
| `accounting.bill_payment.reverse` | Owner | Accounting |
| `accounting.customer_payment.reverse` | Owner | Accounting |
| `accounting.expense.void.after_payment` | Owner | Accounting |
| `accounting.factoring_assignment.reverse` | Owner | Accounting |
| `accounting.journal_entry.create_manual` | Owner | Accounting |
| `accounting.journal_entry.void` | Owner | Accounting |
| `accounting.qbo_resync.force` | Owner | Accounting |
| `banking.bank_account.deactivate` | Owner | Banking (Part 10b) |
| `banking.bank_transaction.recategorize_after_period_close` | Owner | Banking |
| `banking.bank_transaction.delete` | Owner | Banking |
| `banking.factoring_transaction.reverse` | Owner | Banking |
| `documents.legal_hold.set` | Owner OR Admin | Documents (Part 11) |
| `documents.legal_hold.release` | Owner OR Admin | Documents |
| `documents.evidence.force_purge` | Owner | Documents |
| `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` | (auto-detection — no user) | Documents |
| `notifications.suppression_rule.create` | Owner | Notifications (Part 11) |
| `notifications.suppression_rule.cancel` | Owner | Notifications |
| `notifications.preference.bulk_update` | Owner | Notifications |
| `identity.user.role_change` | Owner OR Admin | Identity (Part 7) |
| `identity.user.deactivate` | Owner OR Admin | Identity |
| `identity.permission.grant` | Owner (permanent) / Admin (time-bound) | Identity |
| `master_data.equipment.transfer` (dual-conf invariant) | Owner OR Admin | Master Data (Part 7) |
| `master_data.driver_vendor_pair.deactivate` | Owner | Master Data |
| `master_data.unit_class_pair.deactivate` | Owner | Master Data |
| `catalogs.account.merge` | Owner | Catalogs (Part 7) |
| `catalogs.account.lifecycle_transition` | Owner | Catalogs |
| `catalogs.account_role_binding.change` | Owner | Catalogs |
| `catalogs.posting_template.version_pin` | Owner | Catalogs |
| `driver_finance.cash_advance.above_policy` (WF-054) | Owner | Driver Finance (Part 8b) |
| `driver_finance.deduction_schedule.override` (WF-017) | Owner | Driver Finance |
| `driver_finance.escrow.forfeit` (WF-016) | Owner | Driver Finance |
| `driver_finance.liability.write_off` | Owner | Driver Finance |
| `driver_finance.liability.refund` | Owner | Driver Finance |
| `driver_finance.terms_clause.flip` | Owner | Driver Finance |

**MUST 12.3.3.1** — Total Owner-only WF-064 actions: ~31. Total Owner-OR-Admin WF-064 actions: ~5. **All WF-064 actions require explicit 2-step confirmation modal per Part 6.4.5.4 plus typed phrase + reason ≥30 chars.**

**MUST 12.3.3.2 — WF-064 success/blocked symmetry:** every WF-064 user-action MUST emit both `{action}.executed` (or `{action}.{verb}`) AND `{action}.blocked` audit events. The single documented exception is `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` (auto-detection without user action; no symmetric blocked variant per Part 11 §11.1.7.1 note).

### 12.4.1 Audit event count by module

| Module | Audit event classes | Originated WF-064 | Inherited WF-064 |
|---|---|---|---|
| Identity (Part 7) | ~12 | 4 | 0 |
| Master Data (Part 7) | ~18 | 3 | 2 |
| Catalogs (Part 7) | ~14 | 4 | 0 |
| Driver Finance (Part 8b) | ~24 | 10 | 1 |
| Maintenance (Part 9) | ~14 | 1 | 1 |
| Safety (Part 9) | ~10 | 0 | 1 |
| Fuel (Part 9) | ~8 | 1 | 0 |
| Accounting (Part 10a) | ~36 | 11 | 0 |
| Banking (Part 10b) | ~17 | 4 | 0 |
| Documents (Part 11) | ~12 | 4 | 0 |
| Notifications (Part 11) | ~14 | 3 | 0 |
| **TOTAL** | **~179 audit event classes** | **45 originated** | **5 inherited** |

**MUST 12.4.1.1** — Final exact counts will be machine-derived from the consolidated audit event catalog (Part 13). The approximations here reflect each module's §X.Y.9 element table from Parts 7-11.

### 12.4.2 Audit retention reaffirmation

**MUST 12.4.2.1** — Per MUST 4.9.3.4: audit retention floor is **7 years minimum** for all audit events (IRS / FMCSA / litigation buffer). Older events MAY be archived to cold storage but MUST remain queryable on demand within 24 hours.

**MUST 12.4.2.2** — Per MUST 4.9.3.1: `audit.audit_events` is **append-only**. UPDATE and DELETE forbidden via RLS for ALL roles, including superuser-equivalent admin. Hard-purge of post-retention events is performed by a dedicated role with explicit DB-level audit logging (i.e., the purge itself is audited).

**MUST 12.4.2.3** — Per MUST 4.9.3.2: every INSERT to `audit.audit_events` MUST go through the `audit.append_event()` stored procedure. Direct INSERT blocked by RLS. The stored procedure validates required fields, severity vs event_class consistency, and actor identity matches session.

**MUST 12.4.2.4** — Per MUST 4.9.3.3: every audit event with `fires_wf_064 = true` MUST trigger a notification per WF-064 invariants. The trigger is a PG NOTIFY that wakes the notification dispatcher worker per Part 11 §11.2.4.

### 12.4.3 Severity ladder consolidation

| Severity | Notification default | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| info | sampled (1-10%) or none | routine creates, list/read access, sampled retrievals |
| warning | email to relevant office roles | non-WF-064 reversals, non-critical errors, bounce events |
| critical | email + sometimes SMS to Owner+Admin | legal hold set/release, evidence tamper detected, period close, manual JE, provider outage escalation |

**MUST 12.4.3.1 — Severity = critical implies immediate Owner visibility:** any critical-severity audit MUST have at minimum email-to-Owner notification routing. SMS is reserved for the narrow exception list per Part 5.5.4.2 (currently: evidence_tampered_detected; period_reopen).

---

## 12.5 — BUILD ORDER (PHASING)

This section provides the recommended implementation sequence for the Cursor build. The order honors data dependencies — schemas must be migrated before APIs that depend on them; canonical reference tables must exist before leaf tables that FK into them.

### 12.5.1 Phasing principles

**MUST 12.5.1.1 — Schema-first:** all DB migrations (Part 4) MUST land before any module API code that depends on them. The migration is the source of truth for table existence, FKs, RLS policies, and triggers.

**MUST 12.5.1.2 — Canonical reference before leaf:** within a single phase, canonical reference tables (per Part 4.0.3.2) MUST be migrated before leaf tables that FK into them. E.g., `master_data.drivers` before `driver_finance.driver_settlements`.

**MUST 12.5.1.3 — RLS policies before user-facing endpoints:** every table's RLS policies MUST be migrated and tested before any user-facing endpoint that reads/writes the table is exposed.

**MUST 12.5.1.4 — WF-064 envelope before WF-064 actions:** the audit + notification dispatcher (Part 11.2) MUST be operational before any WF-064-firing module endpoint is exposed in production.

### 12.5.2 Recommended phase sequence

**Phase 0 — Foundation (Parts 1-6):** infrastructure, auth, audit-event substrate, notification dispatcher, RLS framework, outbox queue, R2 connectivity. Cursor-locked.

**Phase 1 — Identity + Master Data + Catalogs (Part 7):** the canonical reference layer. Every other module depends on these. RLS policies fully tested before exposing any user-facing endpoint.

**Phase 2 — Documents schema (Part 11.1) + Notifications dispatcher (Part 11.2):** these underpin every operational module's evidence + notification needs. The WF-064 envelope per WF-043 must be fully wired before Phase 3.

**Phase 3 — Operational modules (Parts 9):** Maintenance, Safety, Fuel. Lower-risk modules; good candidates for early production exposure to validate the foundation.

**Phase 4 — Driver Finance (Part 8b):** complex domain with the WF-046 live-recompute API + WF-016 forfeiture mechanics. Depends on Master Data driver_uuid + equipment-loss liability creation hooks.

**Phase 5 — Accounting (Part 10a):** the accounting backbone. WF-053 cross-module posting service is the entry point used by Driver Finance, Maintenance, and Banking; sequence so that Accounting's posting service is live before downstream modules are migrated to call it (or use a temporary direct-write fallback if phase ordering is reversed).

**Phase 6 — Banking (Part 10b):** depends on Accounting (categorization targets, reconciliation findings). Lower API surface; can run in parallel with Accounting after Accounting's posting service goes live.

**Phase 7 — Cutover + Decommission:** parallel-run with v2 (existing maintenance.html) for ≥4 weeks; daily reconciliation drift report; sign-off when drift = $0 for 14 consecutive days. Decommission v2.

**MUST 12.5.2.1** — Phases MAY overlap when dependency graph allows. Phases 1 and 2 can begin in parallel after Phase 0; Phase 3 modules can begin once their Master Data dependencies are migrated.

**SHOULD 12.5.2.1** — The Cursor build should add per-phase acceptance criteria checkpoints (covered in Part 13).

### 12.5.3 Cutover strategy

**MUST 12.5.3.1 — Parallel-run requirement:** v3 and v2 (existing maintenance.html) MUST run in parallel for ≥4 weeks. Daily reconciliation reports compare key totals: total AR open balance, total AP open balance, total settlement payable, top-20 driver debt balances, fuel-month totals, IFTA quarterly subtotals.

**MUST 12.5.3.2 — Acceptable drift threshold:** per Part 4.0.7 reconcile pattern: daily drift = $0 between v3 and v2 across the reconciliation key totals. Any non-zero drift triggers an `accounting.reconciliation_findings` row + Owner notification per WF-038.

**MUST 12.5.3.3 — Sign-off bar:** decommission v2 only after 14 consecutive days of $0 drift across all reconciliation key totals AND Owner explicit sign-off recorded in audit.

---

| Append-only audit | MUST 4.9.3.1 + 4.9.3.2 + 4.9.3.3 + every module §X.9 | Every audit event |
| WF-064 success/blocked symmetry | MUST 6.8.3 + every module §X.7.1 Tables A/B | Every WF-064 action across 11 modules |
| Service-function-only writes | MUST 4.6.2.6 (accounting) + 4.7.2 (banking factoring) + MUST 3.17.2.1 (documents) + Part 11 §11.2.5.6 (notifications) | Every cross-module write |
| Canonical reference tables | MUST 4.0.3.2 | Identity users, Master Data drivers/units/equipment/customers/vendors/locations, Catalogs accounts, Documents evidence_records, Audit audit_events |

| Server-computed sha256 for evidence | MUST 3.17.2.3 | Every Documents evidence record |
| Driver self-access scope | MUST 7.1.7 + §7.2.10 + §8b.1.7.2 + §9.2.7 + §11.1.7.2 + §11.2.7.2 | Every driver-touching schema |

# IH35 Master Blueprint v3 — Part 13: Traceability Rollup, Acceptance Criteria, Final Sign-Off Pages

**Section in v3 hierarchy:** Part 13 of 13. **Final Part of the v3 Master Blueprint.** Reads after Part 12 (Workflow Index + Roles + Audit + Build Order). Where Parts 7-11 were per-module specs and Part 12 was cross-cutting synthesis, Part 13 is **completion** — the rollup that ties REQ-* rows to tests to acceptance criteria, and the sign-off framework for going-live.

**Status (initial draft):** Awaiting Cursor strict review per the same 8-check acceptance gate. Plus the locked vocabulary-conformance check (every locked-vocabulary cell must equal one of the locked tokens character-for-character).

All Part 7-12 locks carried forward:
- 10-element module template (where applicable; Part 13 is not a module spec)
- WF-064 success/blocked symmetry
- No schema drift / no new namespaces (14-schema lock)
- Single canonical count statements
- Cross-part trace matrix consistency
- WF status enum: matched | remapped | deprecated (character-exact, no annotations)
- Profile mapping enum: FULL | Profile A | Profile B | Profile C | service-only

**Normative language:** MUST · MUST NOT · SHOULD · SHOULD NOT · MAY (RFC 2119).

**Test ID range:** Part 13 introduces no new tests. It traces existing test IDs from Parts 1-11 (range T-011.1 .. T-209.5; 849 unique definitions verified non-colliding).

**Trace matrix:** Part 13 introduces no new REQ-* rows. It produces the **rollup view** that synthesizes the 247 existing rows. Trace matrix integrity preserved at 248 rows (1 header + 247 data).

## 13.1 — TRACEABILITY ROLLUP

### 13.1.1 Rollup intent

The trace matrix `IH35_REQUIREMENT_TRACE_MATRIX_V3.csv` (delivered alongside Parts 7-11) holds 247 requirement rows in 11 columns. This section provides the **rollup view** that the matrix supports — answering questions like:

- For a given REQ-* row, which tests verify it?
- For a given test ID, which REQ rows does it cover?
- For a given module section, which REQ rows trace into it?
- Which tests fire WF-064?
- Which REQ rows have `yes` in the `fires_wf_064` column?

### 13.1.2 REQ-* rollup by module

|---|---|---|---|
| REQ-IDENT | 32 | Part 7 §7.1 | T-101.x..T-130.x |
| REQ-MDATA | 42 | Part 7 §7.2 | T-131.x..T-135.x |
| REQ-CATAL | 36 | Part 7 §7.3 | T-136.x..T-140.x |

## 13.2 — ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

This section defines the **dev-complete bar** — the criteria that, when met, mean the v3 build is ready to begin parallel-run (per Part 12 §12.5.3 cutover strategy).

### 13.2.1 Acceptance criteria categories

Acceptance criteria fall into 5 categories. Each category has explicit MUST clauses below.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Schema migration | All Part 4 DDL applied; RLS policies in place; constraints + triggers tested |
| API contract | All FULL endpoints from Parts 7-11 returning correct status codes + response shapes |
| Audit trail integrity | Append-only enforcement; WF-064 firing correctly; success/blocked symmetry verified |
| Data correctness | Trace tests T-011 .. T-209.5 passing; daily reconcile producing 0 drift |
| Operational readiness | Notification dispatcher operational; outbox queue draining; backups + monitoring active |

### 13.2.2 Schema migration acceptance

**MUST 13.2.2.1 — Schema lock verified:** `pg_dump --schema-only` of the production database equals the canonical Part 4 DDL exactly (modulo deterministic ordering). No tables exist outside the 14 locked schemas. No tables exist within a locked schema beyond what Part 4 enumerates plus the explicit Part 3.17.2 exceptions (`documents.evidence_records` + leaf logging tables per Part 11 §11.1.3 narrative).

**MUST 13.2.2.2 — RLS coverage:** every table in every schema has an RLS policy enabled. `SELECT relname FROM pg_class WHERE relkind='r' AND relrowsecurity=false AND relnamespace IN (locked_schemas)` returns zero rows.

**MUST 13.2.2.3 — Trigger coverage:** every Part 4 + Part 7-11 MUST that specifies a trigger has the trigger present. Mechanical verification: `pg_trigger` table contains entries matching the canonical trigger list (extracted from MUST clauses across Parts 4, 7-11).

**MUST 13.2.2.4 — Index coverage:** all indexes specified in Part 4 DDL are present. Mechanical verification: `pg_indexes` covers the canonical index set.

### 13.2.3 API contract acceptance

**MUST 13.2.3.1 — Every FULL endpoint passes its DoD test set:** every endpoint with strict 6-element DoD in Parts 7-11 §X.1.4 has an automated test that exercises: success path, validation 400, business 422, permission 403, error code linkage, and the audit event side-effect.

| Module | FULL endpoint count | Acceptance test count target |
|---|---|---|
| Identity (Part 7) | 6 | ≥6 acceptance tests, one per endpoint |
| Master Data (Part 7) | 10 | ≥10 |
| Catalogs (Part 7) | 8 | ≥8 |
| Driver Finance (Part 8b) | 13 | ≥13 |
| Maintenance (Part 9) | 5 | ≥5 |
| Safety (Part 9) | 4 | ≥4 |
| Fuel (Part 9) | 2 | ≥2 |
| Accounting (Part 10a) | 7 | ≥7 |
| Banking (Part 10b) | 5 | ≥5 |
| Documents (Part 11) | 4 | ≥4 |
| Notifications (Part 11) | 2 | ≥2 |
| **TOTAL FULL endpoints** | **66** | **≥66** |

**MUST 13.2.3.2 — Profile-mapped actions sampled:** for each Profile (A/B/C/service-only), a representative sample of mapped actions is tested. Per Part 7.3.4.1 pattern, full coverage is not required for Profile A/B (CRUD endpoints follow the standard envelope); a sampling rate of ≥30% per module is the acceptance bar. Profile C actions (WF-064 mutations) require 100% coverage.

**MUST 13.2.3.3 — All error codes in Parts 7-11 §X.1.10.1 tables are reachable:** every `E_*` code defined in any module's error code table can be triggered by a test that exercises the relevant validation/business rule.

### 13.2.4 Audit trail integrity acceptance

**MUST 13.2.4.1 — Append-only enforcement verified:** UPDATE and DELETE on `audit.audit_events` blocked by RLS for ALL roles including Owner. Direct INSERT (bypassing `audit.append_event()`) blocked.

**MUST 13.2.4.2 — WF-064 firing verified end-to-end:** for each of the 45 originated WF-064 actions across Parts 7-11, a test triggers the action and verifies (a) the success audit event row exists with `fires_wf_064=true`, (b) the corresponding notification was queued and dispatched, (c) the Owner received the notification (or, in test, the notification log shows `dispatched_at` populated).

**MUST 13.2.4.3 — Success/blocked symmetry verified:** for each WF-064 action, both the success path (e.g., `accounting.bill.void.after_payment` succeeds) and the blocked path (e.g., same action by non-Owner returns 403 + emits `accounting.bill_void_after_payment_blocked` audit) emit symmetric audit events. The single documented exception is `documents.evidence_tampered_detected` (auto-detection without symmetric variant) per Part 11 §11.1.7.1.

**MUST 13.2.4.4 — Audit retention floor verified:** retention enforcement test confirms audit events older than 7 years are retained and queryable. Cold-storage archival path optional in v1; query-on-demand within 24 hours required.

### 13.2.5 Data correctness acceptance

**MUST 13.2.5.1 — Test pass rate:** ≥99% of the 849 unique test IDs (T-011.1..T-209.5) passing in CI. The 1% tolerance accounts for environmental flakiness; root-cause investigation required for any persistent failure.

**MUST 13.2.5.2 — Daily reconcile drift = $0:** per Part 12 §12.5.3.2, the daily reconcile job comparing v3 vs v2 (existing maintenance.html) produces $0 drift across the reconciliation key totals (total AR open balance, total AP open balance, total settlement payable, top-20 driver debt balances, fuel-month totals, IFTA quarterly subtotals).

**MUST 13.2.5.3 — Acceptance run duration:** the 14-consecutive-day $0-drift bar per Part 12 §12.5.3.3 must be met before sign-off. Any single day with non-zero drift resets the counter.

**MUST 13.2.5.4 — Trace matrix coverage:** for each of the 247 REQ-* rows, at least one referenced test ID is in the passing set. No REQ row may be left untested at sign-off.

### 13.2.6 Operational readiness acceptance

**MUST 13.2.6.1 — Notification dispatcher operational:** the BullMQ-on-Redis dispatcher per Part 11 §11.2.4 is running with non-zero throughput; PG NOTIFY → dispatcher → provider chain end-to-end-tested for each provider (Resend email, Twilio SMS, Expo push) per WF-048.

**MUST 13.2.6.2 — Outbox queue draining:** the outbox.queue table per Part 3.12.4.8 has zero rows older than 5 minutes during normal operation. Test scenarios include: QBO temporary outage (rows queue up, drain on recovery), R2 temporary outage (Documents queue drains on recovery).

**MUST 13.2.6.3 — Backups operational:** Neon Postgres point-in-time recovery enabled with retention ≥7 days. Daily logical backup to a separate region. Cloudflare R2 bucket has lifecycle policy for cold-storage transition + retention floor matching Part 4.9.3.4.

**MUST 13.2.6.4 — Monitoring + alerting operational:** Sentry capturing all uncaught errors. Healthcheck endpoint returning 200 for the API. Alert routing to Owner+Admin email for: dispatcher worker stopped, outbox queue >10 minutes drain time, daily reconcile drift > $0, Sentry critical-rate spike.

**MUST 13.2.6.5 — Security posture verified:** RLS smoke-test from each role confirming scoped access; encrypted PII columns confirmed encrypted at rest (pg_dump shows ciphertext); no plaintext credentials in env vars audit log; SSO/OAuth flows tested per Part 5.

### 13.2.7 Phase-specific acceptance criteria

Per Part 12 §12.5.2 phase sequence, each phase has its own gate before the next phase begins.

| Phase | Gate criteria |
|---|---|
| Phase 0 (Foundation) | Auth working; audit substrate accepting events; outbox queue draining; R2 reachable. Dev-environment only. |
| Phase 1 (Identity + Master Data + Catalogs) | All Part 7 REQ rows traced; T-101..T-140 tests passing; RLS policies tested for every role. |
| Phase 2 (Documents + Notifications dispatcher) | T-195..T-209 tests passing; WF-064 envelope wired end-to-end with at least one synthetic test event. |
| Phase 3 (Maintenance + Safety + Fuel) | T-166..T-179 tests passing; daily evidence-creation flows generating valid evidence_records. |
| Phase 4 (Driver Finance) | T-151..T-165 tests passing; live-recompute API meeting Part 4.5.4.3 latency target (<500ms p95). |
| Phase 5 (Accounting) | T-180..T-189 tests passing; daily reconcile against v2 producing drift report; Owner sign-off on first manual JE. |
| Phase 6 (Banking) | T-190..T-194 tests passing; bank statement import flow producing categorized transactions; reconciliation runs completing. |
| Phase 7 (Cutover) | 14-consecutive-day $0 drift bar met; Owner explicit sign-off audit event recorded; v2 decommission scheduled. |

**MUST 13.2.7.1 — Phase gates are sequential:** a phase MUST NOT begin until the previous phase's gate is met. Exception: Phases 1 and 2 MAY run in parallel after Phase 0 per Part 12 §12.5.2.1.

**MUST 13.2.7.2 — Phase regression test:** when Phase N is reached, all earlier phases' gate tests are re-run as a regression set. A regression failure is treated as a blocking incident.

---

## 13.3 — FINAL SIGN-OFF PAGES

This section defines the formal Owner attestation framework for going-live: who signs off on what, in what order, with what evidence.

### 13.3.1 Sign-off principals

| Principal | Role | Sign-off scope |
|---|---|---|
| Owner | Jorge Munoz (IH 35 Transportation LLC) | Final go-live authorization; period close + reopen attestations; legal hold attestations; force-purge attestations |
| Administrator | Owner-designated (TBD) | Phase gate sign-offs (Phases 1-6); operational acceptance for non-WF-064 production access |
| Accountant | Owner-designated (TBD) | Reconciliation drift sign-off; QBO mirror parity sign-off |
| Cursor (build) | Build executor | Phase-end completion attestation; trace matrix coverage attestation |
| Anthropic Claude (spec) | Spec author | Spec-completion attestation (this Part); per-Part delivery records |

### 13.3.2 Sign-off ceremony

**MUST 13.3.2.1 — Sign-off is recorded as audit events:** every sign-off MUST be recorded as an `audit.signoff_recorded` audit event with: actor user_uuid, signoff_type (e.g., `phase_gate_2`, `cutover_authorization`), evidence_uuid (the signed PDF or e-signature record), reason_text. These events are immutable per Part 4.9.3.1.

**MUST 13.3.2.2 — Signed PDF artifact:** every sign-off generates a signed PDF via Part 11 §11.1.2.5 (WF-034 signed PDF generator). The PDF contains: principal name, role, signoff_type, scope text quoted verbatim from this Part 13 §13.2 acceptance criteria, evidence references (test pass report, reconcile drift report), date/time, signature.

**MUST 13.3.2.3 — Sign-off cannot be deleted:** the resulting evidence_records row has `legal_hold = true` set at creation, preventing routine retention purge per Part 11 §11.1.5.7. Force-purge of a sign-off evidence record requires a separate Owner action with explicit reason referencing the original signoff_type.

### 13.3.3 Phase-by-phase sign-off table

| Phase | Sign-off principal | Evidence required | Audit event |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 0 → Phase 1 transition | Administrator | dev-env auth + audit + outbox smoke tests passing | `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`phase_gate_0`) |
| Phase 1 → Phase 3 transition | Administrator | Part 7 trace tests passing; RLS smoke tests passing | `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`phase_gate_1`) |
| Phase 2 → Phase 3 transition | Administrator | Part 11 trace tests passing; WF-064 envelope synthetic test | `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`phase_gate_2`) |
| Phase 3 → Phase 4 transition | Administrator | Part 9 trace tests passing; daily evidence-creation flows | `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`phase_gate_3`) |
| Phase 4 → Phase 5 transition | Administrator | Part 8b trace tests passing; live-recompute latency report | `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`phase_gate_4`) |
| Phase 5 → Phase 6 transition | Owner | Part 10a trace tests passing; first manual JE attestation | `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`phase_gate_5`) |
| Phase 6 → Phase 7 (Cutover) | Owner | Part 10b trace tests passing; bank reconcile flows tested | `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`phase_gate_6`) |
| Cutover authorization | Owner | 14-consecutive-day $0 drift attestation; full test pass report; Accountant sign-off on QBO parity | `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`cutover_authorization`) |
| v2 decommission authorization | Owner | 4-week parallel-run complete; final drift report; final QBO parity confirmation | `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`v2_decommission`) |

### 13.3.4 Cutover checklist

Per Part 12 §12.5.3 cutover strategy, the cutover from v2 (existing maintenance.html) to v3 follows this checklist:

**T-minus 4 weeks (parallel-run start):**
- [ ] Phase 7 entry confirmed (Phase 6 sign-off audit event exists)
- [ ] v3 is live in production at `https://ih35-dispatcher.onrender.com` with all Parts 7-11 endpoints operational
- [ ] v2 (`maintenance.html`) remains the operational source of truth for all dispatch + accounting + driver finance write activity
- [ ] v3 reads-from-v2-mirror configured: hourly snapshot of v2 totals into v3 staging tables for daily reconcile
- [ ] Daily reconcile job per Part 4.0.7 + Part 12 §12.5.3.2 producing reports

**T-minus 4 weeks to T-0 (parallel-run window):**
- [ ] Daily drift report posted to Owner+Accountant; threshold = $0 across reconciliation key totals
- [ ] Any non-zero drift triggers an `accounting.reconciliation_findings` row + Owner notification
- [ ] WF-038 (Reconciliation Findings Resolution) flow exercised for any drift; resolution recorded
- [ ] Counter starts at 0 on each non-zero-drift day; reaches 14 only after 14 consecutive $0 days

**T-0 (cutover):**
- [ ] Owner has signed `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`cutover_authorization`)
- [ ] v3 becomes the operational source of truth for all dispatch + accounting + driver finance activity
- [ ] v2 enters read-only mode (configured at the application layer; no destructive change to v2 schema)
- [ ] All daily operations migrate to v3 (settlements, bill payments, dispatch creation)

**T-0 + 4 weeks (decommission):**
- [ ] Final drift report covering the 4-week post-cutover period
- [ ] Owner has signed `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`v2_decommission`)
- [ ] v2 archive: full export of v2 final state + retained read-only access per Part 4.9.3.4 7-year retention
- [ ] DNS / load balancer cutover finalized; v2 endpoints disabled

### 13.3.5 Post-cutover monitoring

**MUST 13.3.5.1 — First 30 days post-cutover heightened monitoring:**
- Daily reconcile drift report continues for first 30 days even though v2 is read-only
- Owner reviews daily; non-zero drift triggers immediate investigation
- Sentry error rate baseline established within first 7 days; subsequent rate > 2x baseline triggers Owner notification

**MUST 13.3.5.2 — Quarterly attestation:**
- Each fiscal quarter, Owner signs an attestation that spec MUST clauses remain in force and no silent changes have occurred. The attestation is itself a `audit.signoff_recorded` event (signoff_type=`quarterly_spec_attestation`).

**MUST 13.3.5.3 — Annual audit retention review:**
- Each fiscal year, audit events older than 7 years are reviewed for cold-storage archival or hard-purge per Part 4.9.3.1 + 4.9.3.4. Hard-purge requires Owner sign-off + audit log of the purge itself.

### 13.3.6 Spec change control post-sign-off

**MUST 13.3.6.1 — Spec amendments require Owner approval:** after final sign-off, any change to Parts 1-13 requires:
1. A written amendment proposal naming the affected MUST clauses
2. Cursor (or successor build agent) impact assessment
3. Owner explicit sign-off as `audit.signoff_recorded` (signoff_type=`spec_amendment`)
4. Amendment versioned as v3.1, v3.2, etc.

**MUST 13.3.6.2 — No silent changes:** Cursor (or successor build agent) MUST NOT modify behavior in production code that contradicts a Part 1-13 MUST clause without first obtaining a v3.X amendment per MUST 13.3.6.1. Production code is downstream of the spec, not authoritative over it.

**MUST 13.3.6.3 — Spec deviations during build are recorded:** if during Phases 1-7 the build encounters a spec issue (ambiguity, contradiction, or implementability gap), the resolution is recorded as a `audit.signoff_recorded` event (signoff_type=`spec_deviation_resolution`) referencing: the specific MUST clause, the as-built behavior, the rationale, and the Owner approval. These events are reviewed at quarterly attestation per MUST 13.3.5.2.
